αβαθής.definition: Not having great depth; lacking depth of thought or character. Can refer to physical depth or intellectual/emotional depth.
αβαθής.example_greek: Το νερό στη λίμνη είναι αβαθές σε αυτό το σημείο.
αβαθής.example_english: The water in the lake is shallow at this point.

αβεβαιότητα.definition: The state of being uncertain, doubtful, or not confident about something. A lack of certainty or clarity regarding a matter.
αβεβαιότητα.example_greek: Η αβεβαιότητα για το μέλλον τον ανησυχούσε πολύ.
αβεβαιότητα.example_english: The uncertainty about the future worried him greatly.

αγαθός.definition: Possessing positive qualities; morally upright or virtuous. Can describe a person's character or the quality of something positive.
αγαθός.example_greek: Είναι ένας αγαθός άνθρωπος που βοηθά τους άλλους.
αγαθός.example_english: He is a good person who helps others.

αγοραπωλησία.definition: The act or practice of buying and selling goods; commercial trade or business transactions. It refers to commercial activity or mercantile dealings.
αγοραπωλησία.example_greek: Η αγοραπωλησία των ακινήτων είναι μια δημοφιλής επιχείρηση στην Αθήνα.
αγοραπωλησία.example_english: The buying and selling of real estate is a popular business in Athens.

αγύριστος.definition: Describing something that has not been returned or brought back to its original place or owner. It can also mean something that has not been reciprocated.
αγύριστος.example_greek: Το βιβλίο του παραμένει αγύριστο μετά από τρία χρόνια.
αγύριστος.example_english: His book remains unreturned after three years.

αγωνίζομαι.translation: to struggle, to fight, to compete
αγωνίζομαι.definition: to make a great effort or exert oneself against difficulty or opposition; to engage in competition or conflict.
αγωνίζομαι.example_greek: Αγωνίζομαι κάθε μέρα για να πετύχω τα όνειρά μου.
αγωνίζομαι.example_english: I struggle every day to achieve my dreams.

αγωνιστής.definition: A person who participates in competitions, struggles, or fights; an athlete or combatant engaged in physical or intellectual contests.
αγωνιστής.example_greek: Ο αγωνιστής προετοιμάστηκε για τον αγώνα.
αγωνιστής.example_english: The athlete prepared for the competition.

άδεια.definition: An official document or authorization that permits someone to do something, or a period of absence from work granted by an employer.
άδεια.example_greek: Πήρα άδεια από την δουλειά για μια εβδομάδα.
άδεια.example_english: I took a week off from work.

αδειοδοτικός.definition: Relating to or characterized by the granting of licenses or permits. Describing something that has the nature or function of issuing official permissions or authorizations.
αδειοδοτικός.example_greek: Η αδειοδοτική διαδικασία είναι πολύπλοκη και χρονοβόρα.
αδειοδοτικός.example_english: The licensing procedure is complex and time-consuming.

αδιάλειπτος.definition: Occurring or continuing without interruption or pause; ceaseless and constant in nature.
αδιάλειπτος.example_greek: Άκουσα αδιάλειπτο θόρυβο όλη τη νύχτα.
αδιάλειπτος.example_english: I heard incessant noise all night long.

αδιάφορος.definition: Not caring or interested in something; showing a lack of concern or enthusiasm about a matter. It can also mean neutral or not mattering.
αδιάφορος.example_greek: Φαίνεται αδιάφορος για το αποτέλεσμα του αγώνα.
αδιάφορος.example_english: He seems indifferent about the result of the match.

αδιαφορώ.definition: To be indifferent or unconcerned about something; to not care about a matter or situation.
αδιαφορώ.example_greek: Αδιαφορώ για τις γνώμες των άλλων.
αδιαφορώ.example_english: I don't care about other people's opinions.

αδιέξοδος.definition: Describing a situation that has no way out or resolution; an impasse where no progress can be made. It can also mean a street or passage with no exit.
αδιέξοδος.example_greek: Η κατάσταση φαίνεται αδιέξοδη και δεν υπάρχει λύση.
αδιέξοδος.example_english: The situation appears deadlocked and there is no solution.

αδιευκρίνιστος.definition: Not clearly defined or explained; remaining unclear or ambiguous. Something that has not been clarified or whose meaning is not distinct.
αδιευκρίνιστος.example_greek: Η θέση της κυβέρνησης για το θέμα παραμένει αδιευκρίνιστη.
αδιευκρίνιστος.example_english: The government's position on the issue remains unclear.

αδικαιολόγητα.definition: In a manner that is not justified or cannot be explained by valid reasons. Used to describe actions, claims, or behaviors that lack proper justification.
αδικαιολόγητα.example_greek: Η απόφασή του ήταν αδικαιολόγητα αυστηρή.

αδύναμος.definition: Lacking physical strength or power; incapable of doing something. Can refer to physical weakness or inability to accomplish a task.
αδύναμος.example_greek: Ο ασθενής ήταν πολύ αδύναμος μετά την επέμβαση.
αδύναμος.example_english: The patient was very weak after the surgery.

αδυνατώ.definition: To lack the ability or power to do something; to be incapable of accomplishing a task or action.
αδυνατώ.example_greek: Αδυνατώ να σας βοηθήσω με αυτό το πρόβλημα.
αδυνατώ.example_english: I am unable to help you with this problem.

αδυσώπητος.definition: Describing someone who is shameless, lacks conscience, or shows no respect for social norms. Can also mean persistent or unrelenting in a negative sense.
αδυσώπητος.example_english: He was shameless in his demands.

αέναος.definition: Continuing or lasting forever without interruption; eternal and unceasing in nature or duration.
αέναος.example_greek: Το αέναο ρεύμα του ποταμού ρέει διαρκώς προς τη θάλασσα.
αέναος.example_english: The perpetual flow of the river continuously flows toward the sea.

αέριο.definition: A state of matter that has no fixed shape or volume, consisting of particles that move freely. Commonly used to refer to gasoline or fuel in modern Greek.

αεροσκάφος.definition: A powered flying vehicle designed to carry passengers or cargo through the air. It is a general term for any type of aircraft including airplanes, helicopters, and other flying machines.
αεροσκάφος.example_greek: Το αεροσκάφος απογειώθηκε από το αεροδρόμιο στις 8 το πρωί.
αεροσκάφος.example_english: The aircraft took off from the airport at 8 in the morning.

αετώμα.definition: The triangular section of a wall at the end of a pitched roof, or in classical architecture, the triangular gable above a colonnade.
αετώμα.example_greek: Το αετώμα του κτιρίου διακοσμείται με γλυπτά.
αετώμα.example_english: The pediment of the building is decorated with sculptures.

αθέατος.definition: Not watched, observed, or seen by others. Something that happens without being noticed or without an audience present.
αθέατος.example_greek: Έκανε την αθέατη δουλειά του κρυμμένος από τα βλέμματα του κόσμου.
αθέατος.example_english: He did his unseen work hidden from the eyes of the world.

αθεράπευτος.definition: Unable to be cured or treated successfully; describing a disease, condition, or situation that cannot be remedied or healed.
αθεράπευτος.example_greek: Η ασθένεια θεωρήθηκε αθεράπευτη από τους γιατρούς.
αθεράπευτος.example_english: The disease was considered incurable by the doctors.

άθροισμα.definition: The result of adding numbers or quantities together; a total amount obtained by combining individual elements.
άθροισμα.example_greek: Το άθροισμα των δύο αριθμών είναι δέκα.
άθροισμα.example_english: The sum of the two numbers is ten.

αθώνος.definition: Free from guilt, blame, or responsibility for a wrongdoing. Describes someone who has not committed an offense or is not responsible for something bad that happened.
αθώνος.example_greek: Ο κατηγορούμενος αποδείχθηκε αθώνος στο δικαστήριο.
αθώνος.example_english: The accused was proven innocent in court.

αθωότητα.definition: The state or quality of being innocent; freedom from guilt or blame for a wrongdoing. It can also refer to naivety or lack of experience.
αθωότητα.example_greek: Απέδειξε την αθωότητά του στο δικαστήριο.
αθωότητα.example_english: He proved his innocence in court.

αίμα.definition: The red fluid that circulates through the veins and arteries of the body, carrying oxygen and nutrients to cells.
αίμα.example_greek: Ο γιατρός έλεγχε το αίμα του ασθενή.
αίμα.example_english: The doctor was checking the patient's blood.

αίρεση.definition: A religious or philosophical belief that contradicts the established doctrine of a church or faith. In broader usage, it can also mean a choice or selection.
αίρεση.example_greek: Η αίρεση αυτή θεωρούνταν επικίνδυνη από την Εκκλησία.
αίρεση.example_english: This heresy was considered dangerous by the Church.

αιρετός.definition: Worthy of being chosen or preferred; in grammar, describing a word that can be omitted without changing the essential meaning of a sentence.
αιρετός.example_greek: Η θέση αυτή είναι αιρετή για όσους ενδιαφέρονται.
αιρετός.example_english: This position is elective for those who are interested.

αισιόδοξος.definition: Inclined to have a positive outlook on life and expect favorable outcomes. Someone who believes things will turn out well.
αισιόδοξος.example_greek: Είναι πολύ αισιόδοξος για το μέλλον του.
αισιόδοξος.example_english: He is very optimistic about his future.

αίτημα.definition: A formal or official request for something, or a claim made by someone. It can refer to a petition, application, or demand for action.
αίτημα.example_greek: Το αίτημα του για αύξηση μισθού εγκρίθηκε από τη διεύθυνση.
αίτημα.example_english: His request for a salary increase was approved by management.

αιτία.definition: The reason or cause for something happening; responsibility or fault for an action or event.
αιτία.example_greek: Η αιτία του ατυχήματος ήταν το υψηλό ταχύτητα.
αιτία.example_english: The cause of the accident was high speed.

αιτιολογώ.translation: to justify, to give reasons for
αιτιολογώ.definition: To provide reasons, explanations, or justifications for something; to account for or explain the causes of something.
αιτιολογώ.example_greek: Πρέπει να αιτιολογήσει τις απουσίες του.
αιτιολογώ.example_english: He needs to justify his absences.

αιφνίδια.definition: In a sudden or unexpected manner; without warning or prior notice. Often used to describe something that happens quickly and without preparation.
αιφνίδια.example_greek: Αιφνίδια άρχισε να βρέχει πολύ δυνατά.
αιφνίδια.example_english: Suddenly it started raining very heavily.

αιφνιδιάζω.translation: to surprise, to catch off guard
αιφνιδιάζω.definition: To surprise someone or catch them in an unexpected manner. It means to come upon someone suddenly or to shock them with something unexpected.
αιφνιδιάζω.example_english: The news surprised me very much.

αιφνιδιαστικός.definition: Occurring or happening without warning or preparation; causing surprise due to being unforeseen or abrupt.
αιφνιδιαστικός.example_greek: Η αιφνιδιαστική επίσκεψη του προέδρου προκάλεσε μεγάλη αναστάτωση.
αιφνιδιαστικός.example_english: The sudden visit of the president caused great upheaval.

αιχμάλωτος.definition: A person who has been taken prisoner or held in captivity, especially during war. Can be used as both a noun and adjective meaning 'captured' or 'imprisoned'.
αιχμάλωτος.example_greek: Ο αιχμάλωτος απελευθερώθηκε μετά τη σύμφωνία ειρήνης.
αιχμάλωτος.example_english: The prisoner was released after the peace agreement.

ακατάλληλος.definition: Not suitable or appropriate for a particular purpose, person, or situation. Lacking the necessary qualities or characteristics required.
ακατάλληλος.example_greek: Αυτά τα ρούχα είναι ακατάλληλα για το γραφείο.
ακατάλληλος.example_english: These clothes are unsuitable for the office.

ακατανίκητος.definition: Unable to be defeated, conquered, or overcome; possessing supreme strength or power that cannot be vanquished.
ακατανίκητος.example_greek: Ο ακατανίκητος πολεμιστής δεν έχασε ποτέ μάχη.
ακατανίκητος.example_english: The invincible warrior never lost a battle.

ακέραιος.definition: Not broken, damaged, or divided; complete and in original condition. Also used in mathematics to describe whole numbers without fractions.
ακέραιος.example_greek: Το πιάτο έπεσε αλλά παρέμεινε ακέραιο.
ακέραιος.example_english: The plate fell but remained intact.

ακονίζω.definition: To make a blade or cutting tool sharp by rubbing it against a stone or other abrasive surface. Can also mean to sharpen one's skills or abilities.
ακονίζω.example_greek: Ακονίζω το μαχαίρι μου πριν μαγειρέψω.
ακονίζω.example_english: I sharpen my knife before I cook.

άκρα.definition: The plural form of 'άκρο', referring to the outermost or extreme points of something, such as the tips of fingers or toes, or the edges of an object.
άκρα.example_greek: Τα παιδιά έτρεξαν στις άκρες του δρόμου.
άκρα.example_english: The children ran to the edges of the road.

ακραίος.definition: Situated at the extreme end or edge; representing the highest degree or most extreme form of something.
ακραίος.example_greek: Αυτή είναι μια ακραία περίπτωση που απαιτεί ειδική προσοχή.
ακραίος.example_english: This is an extreme case that requires special attention.

ακρίβεια.definition: The quality of being exact, precise, and free from errors. It refers to the degree to which something conforms to a standard or truth.
ακρίβεια.example_greek: Η ακρίβεια είναι πολύ σημαντική στα μαθηματικά.
ακρίβεια.example_english: Accuracy is very important in mathematics.

ακροδεξία.definition: The art or practice of performing acrobatic feats, particularly walking on a tightrope or performing dangerous balancing acts. It can also refer to skillful maneuvering or clever tactics.
ακροδεξία.example_greek: Ο ακροβάτης εκτέλεσε μια καταπληκτική ακροδεξία πάνω από το πλήθος.
ακροδεξία.example_english: The acrobat performed an amazing tightrope act above the crowd.

άκρος.definition: Situated at the extremity or end of something; reaching the highest degree or intensity. Can also mean sharp or pointed.
άκρος.example_greek: Ήταν σε άκρη ανάγκη και ζήτησε βοήθεια.
άκρος.example_english: He was in extreme need and asked for help.

ΑΚΤΩΡ.definition: A person who performs in theatrical productions, films, or television shows, portraying fictional characters or real people.
ΑΚΤΩΡ.example_greek: Ο ακτώρ έδωσε μια εξαιρετική ερμηνεία στην ταινία.
ΑΚΤΩΡ.example_english: The actor gave an exceptional performance in the film.

ακυρώνω.translation: to cancel, to annul, to invalidate
ακυρώνω.definition: To declare something null and void, to revoke or nullify a decision, agreement, or action. To make something no longer valid or effective.
ακυρώνω.example_greek: Η αεροπορική εταιρεία ακύρωσε την πτήση λόγω καιρού.
ακυρώνω.example_english: The airline cancelled the flight due to weather.

αλαζονικός.definition: Characterized by excessive pride, arrogance, or a tendency to boast about one's accomplishments or qualities. Describing someone who acts in a conceited or self-important manner.
αλαζονικός.example_greek: Η αλαζονική του στάση τον έκανε να χάσει πολλούς φίλους.
αλαζονικός.example_english: His boastful attitude caused him to lose many friends.

άλαλος.definition: Unable or unwilling to speak; struck silent by emotion or circumstance. Often used to describe someone rendered speechless by shock, amazement, or emotion.
άλαλος.example_greek: Έμεινε άλαλος όταν άκουσε τα νέα.
άλαλος.example_english: He remained speechless when he heard the news.

αλληλεγγύη.definition: A principle of unity and mutual responsibility among members of a group, characterized by shared interests and common goals. It represents the willingness to support others in a collective effort.
αλληλεγγύη.example_greek: Η αλληλεγγύη μεταξύ των εργαζομένων ήταν κρίσιμη για την απεργία.
αλληλεγγύη.example_english: Solidarity among the workers was critical for the strike.

αλληλεπίδραση.definition: The process by which two or more things have an effect on each other; reciprocal action or influence between entities, objects, or people.
αλληλεπίδραση.example_greek: Η αλληλεπίδραση μεταξύ των δύο χημικών ουσιών δημιούργησε ένα νέο προϊόν.
αλληλεπίδραση.example_english: The interaction between the two chemical substances created a new product.

αλληλοκατηγορητικός.definition: Describing a situation or relationship characterized by mutual or reciprocal accusations between parties. It refers to the exchange of blame or charges back and forth.
αλληλοκατηγορητικός.example_greek: Η αλληλοκατηγορητική συζήτηση δεν οδήγησε σε καμία λύση.
αλληλοκατηγορητικός.example_english: The mutually accusatory discussion did not lead to any solution.

αλληλοσπαραγμός.definition: A state of reciprocal provocation, irritation, or tension between two or more parties. It describes a situation where people provoke or irritate each other mutually.
αλληλοσπαραγμός.example_greek: Ο αλληλοσπαραγμός μεταξύ των δύο χωρών οδήγησε σε διπλωματικές εντάσεις.
αλληλοσπαραγμός.example_english: The mutual provocation between the two countries led to diplomatic tensions.

αλληλουχία.definition: The act or state of following one after another in a continuous or regular order. A series of things arranged in sequence or succession.
αλληλουχία.example_greek: Η αλληλουχία των εποχών είναι φυσική και αναπόφευκτη.
αλληλουχία.example_english: The succession of seasons is natural and inevitable.

αλλού.definition: In or to a different place; somewhere else. Indicates location or direction away from the current or mentioned place.
αλλού.example_english: He is not here, he is elsewhere.

άλλωστε.definition: An adverb used to introduce an additional argument or point, often dismissing a previous objection. It means 'in any case' or 'anyway' and is commonly used in conversational Greek.
άλλωστε.example_greek: Δεν θέλω να πάω σήμερα. Άλλωστε, είμαι κουρασμένος.
άλλωστε.example_english: I don't want to go today. Besides, I'm tired.

άλμα.definition: A sudden movement upward or forward by pushing off the ground with one's feet. It can also mean a sudden change or transition from one state to another.
άλμα.example_greek: Έκανε ένα μεγάλο άλμα για να φτάσει το μήλο.
άλμα.example_english: He made a big jump to reach the apple.

αλυσίδα.definition: A series of connected metal links or rings forming a flexible fastening or securing device. It can also refer to a sequence of connected things or events.
αλυσίδα.example_greek: Ο σκύλος ήταν δεμένος με μια σιδερένια αλυσίδα.
αλυσίδα.example_english: The dog was tied with an iron chain.

αμάξη.definition: A four-wheeled motor vehicle designed primarily for carrying passengers on roads. It is the most common word for 'car' in Modern Greek.

αμβλύνω.translation: to dull, to blunt, to weaken
αμβλύνω.definition: To make something less sharp, less intense, or less effective. It can refer to dulling the edge of a tool or reducing the intensity of an emotion or sensation.
αμβλύνω.example_greek: Η πόνος αμβλύνεται με τα φάρμακα.
αμβλύνω.example_english: The pain is dulled by the medication.

αμετάκλητος.definition: Unable to be revoked, recalled, or reversed; absolute and permanent. Describes something that cannot be changed or undone once decided or declared.
αμετάκλητος.example_greek: Η απόφαση του δικαστηρίου είναι αμετάκλητη και δεν μπορεί να αλλαχθεί.
αμετάκλητος.example_english: The court's decision is irrevocable and cannot be changed.

αμέτοχος.definition: Not taking part in something; excluded or distant from participation in an activity, group, or event.
αμέτοχος.example_greek: Ήμασταν αμέτοχοι στις πολιτικές συζητήσεις της οικογένειας.
αμέτοχος.example_english: We were uninvolved in the family's political discussions.

αμοιβή.definition: Money or something of value given in return for work, services, or goods. A form of remuneration or recompense.
αμοιβή.example_greek: Έλαβε καλή αμοιβή για την σκληρή του δουλειά.
αμοιβή.example_english: He received good compensation for his hard work.

αμυνίτες.definition: Plural form of αμυνίτης (aminítis), referring to people who defend or protect something, particularly used in historical or political contexts to describe defenders of a cause or territory.
αμυνίτες.example_greek: Οι αμυνίτες της πόλης αντιστάθηκαν στην επίθεση.
αμυνίτες.example_english: The defenders of the city resisted the attack.

αμφισβήτηση.definition: The act of questioning, disputing, or challenging the validity or accuracy of something. It refers to an expression of doubt or disagreement about a statement, claim, or decision.
αμφισβήτηση.example_greek: Η αμφισβήτηση των αποτελεσμάτων της εκλογής προκάλεσε πολλές συζητήσεις.
αμφισβήτηση.example_english: The challenging of the election results caused many discussions.

αμφισβητώ.translation: to dispute, to question, to challenge
αμφισβητώ.definition: To express doubt about something or to argue against a claim. To contest or dispute the validity or truth of something.

αναβαθμίζω.definition: to raise something to a higher level, standard, or degree; to improve or enhance the quality or capacity of something.
αναβαθμίζω.example_greek: Η εταιρεία αναβαθμίζει τα συστήματα ασφαλείας της.
αναβαθμίζω.example_english: The company is upgrading its security systems.

αναβάθμιση.definition: The process of improving something to a higher standard or level, such as upgrading equipment, skills, or qualifications. It can also refer to a promotion in rank or status.
αναβάθμιση.example_greek: Η αναβάθμιση του λογισμικού έγινε επιτυχώς.
αναβάθμιση.example_english: The software upgrade was completed successfully.

αναβαπτισμός.definition: The practice of baptizing again someone who has already been baptized, or the religious movement emphasizing adult baptism rather than infant baptism.
αναβαπτισμός.example_greek: Ο αναβαπτισμός ήταν ένα σημαντικό κίνημα κατά τη διάρκεια της Μεταρρύθμισης.
αναβαπτισμός.example_english: Anabaptism was a significant movement during the Reformation.

αναβιώνω.translation: to revive, to come back to life
αναβιώνω.definition: To return to life or consciousness; to be revived or restored to a previous state of activity or existence.
αναβιώνω.example_greek: Η παλιά παραδοσιμη τέχνη αναβιώνει στην περιοχή.
αναβιώνω.example_english: The old traditional art is being revived in the region.

αναβολή.definition: The act of putting something off to a later time; a postponement or deferment of an event or decision.
αναβολή.example_greek: Η αναβολή της συνεδρίασης ήταν αναγκαία λόγω του απρόσμενου προβλήματος.
αναβολή.example_english: The postponement of the meeting was necessary due to the unexpected problem.

αναγέννηση.definition: The revival or renewal of something, particularly referring to the historical Renaissance period or a spiritual or cultural rebirth. It can also mean the process of being born again or regenerated.
αναγέννηση.example_greek: Η αναγέννηση ήταν μια περίοδος μεγάλης πολιτιστικής ανάπτυξης στην Ευρώπη.
αναγέννηση.example_english: The Renaissance was a period of great cultural development in Europe.

αναγεννώ.translation: to regenerate, to rebirth, to renew
αναγεννώ.definition: To cause something to be born again or to come into existence anew; to restore or revive something to a better state.
αναγεννώ.example_greek: Η φύση αναγεννά τα δάση μετά από την καταστροφή.
αναγεννώ.example_english: Nature regenerates the forests after the destruction.

αναγκαιότητα.definition: The state of being necessary or unavoidable; something that is required or essential for a particular purpose or situation.
αναγκαιότητα.example_greek: Η αναγκαιότητα του ύπνου είναι σημαντική για την υγεία μας.
αναγκαιότητα.example_english: The necessity of sleep is important for our health.

ανάγλυφος.definition: Having a design or image raised above the surface; created by carving, molding, or pressing so that the subject stands out from the background.
ανάγλυφος.example_greek: Το ανάγλυφο σχέδιο στον τοίχο ήταν πολύ όμορφο.
ανάγλυφος.example_english: The embossed design on the wall was very beautiful.

αναδεικνύω.translation: to reveal, to bring to light, to highlight
αναδεικνύω.definition: To make something known or visible that was previously hidden or not widely recognized. To demonstrate or reveal the true nature or importance of something.
αναδεικνύω.example_greek: Η έρευνα αναδείχνει τα προβλήματα του συστήματος.
αναδεικνύω.example_english: The research brings to light the problems of the system.

ανάδειξη.definition: The act of bringing something to light, revealing, or making something prominent or apparent. It can also refer to the appointment or designation of someone to a position.
ανάδειξη.example_greek: Η ανάδειξη του νέου προέδρου έγινε χθες.
ανάδειξη.example_english: The appointment of the new president took place yesterday.

αναδιανέμω.definition: to distribute or allocate something again in a different way, often used in contexts of resources, funds, or responsibilities being shared out anew.
αναδιανέμω.example_greek: Η κυβέρνηση αποφάσισε να αναδιανείμει τα κεφάλαια στις περιφέρειες.
αναδιανέμω.example_english: The government decided to redistribute the funds to the regions.

αναδιπλώνομαι.translation: to double back, to fold back
αναδιπλώνομαι.definition: A reflexive verb meaning to fold or bend something back upon itself, or to double back on one's path. It can be used literally for physical objects or figuratively for actions.
αναδιπλώνομαι.example_greek: Το φίδι αναδιπλώθηκε και κινήθηκε προς την αντίθετη κατεύθυνση.
αναδιπλώνομαι.example_english: The snake doubled back and moved in the opposite direction.

ανάδοχος.definition: A person or company that undertakes a contract or project, or someone who takes responsibility for another person's obligations. In construction, it refers to a general contractor.
ανάδοχος.example_greek: Ο ανάδοχος του έργου ολοκλήρωσε την κατασκευή του κτιρίου.
ανάδοχος.example_english: The contractor of the project completed the construction of the building.

ανάδυση.definition: The act of emerging, rising up, or coming to the surface from below. It can refer to physical emergence (such as surfacing from water) or metaphorical emergence (such as the rise of a new idea or movement).
ανάδυση.example_greek: Η ανάδυση του ήλιου από τον ορίζοντα ήταν πολύ όμορφη.
ανάδυση.example_english: The emergence of the sun from the horizon was very beautiful.

αναζωογονιτικός.definition: Relating to or capable of restoring life, vitality, or vigor; having the quality of reviving or regenerating something that has diminished or died.
αναζωογονιτικός.example_greek: Ο αναζωογονιτικός ρόλος της φύσης είναι σημαντικός για την υγεία μας.
αναζωογονιτικός.example_english: The revitalizing role of nature is important for our health.

ανάθεση.definition: The act of assigning or delegating a task, responsibility, or contract to someone. It can refer to the official awarding of a project or duty to a person or organization.
ανάθεση.example_greek: Η ανάθεση του έργου έγινε στην καλύτερη εταιρεία.
ανάθεση.example_english: The assignment of the project was given to the best company.

αναθέτω.definition: To give a task, responsibility, or duty to someone else. To hand over or commit something to another person's care or management.
αναθέτω.example_greek: Ο διευθυντής ανέθεσε το έργο στον νέο υπάλληλο.
αναθέτω.example_english: The manager assigned the project to the new employee.

αναθεώρηση.definition: The act of examining or reconsidering something carefully, often to make corrections or updates. It can refer to a formal review process or a critical reassessment of ideas or decisions.
αναθεώρηση.example_greek: Η αναθεώρηση του συντάγματος είναι μια σημαντική διαδικασία.
αναθεώρηση.example_english: The revision of the constitution is an important process.

αναθηματικός.definition: Relating to or constituting a votive offering made to a deity or saint as fulfillment of a vow or in gratitude. Used to describe religious objects, gifts, or monuments dedicated in this manner.
αναθηματικός.example_greek: Το αναθηματικό αγαλμάτιο ήταν αφιερωμένο στον Άγιο Γεώργιο.
αναθηματικός.example_english: The votive statue was dedicated to Saint George.

αναιτιολόγητα.definition: In a manner that lacks proper reason, cause, or justification; without adequate explanation or grounds.
αναιτιολόγητα.example_english: He hit me unjustifiably without any reason.

αναιτιολόγητος.definition: Not justified, explained, or supported by valid reasons; done without proper cause or explanation.
αναιτιολόγητος.example_greek: Η απόφασή του ήταν αναιτιολόγητη και προκάλεσε πολλές αντιδράσεις.
αναιτιολόγητος.example_english: His decision was unjustified and provoked many reactions.

ανακάμπτω.translation: to turn back, to bend back
ανακάμπτω.definition: To change direction and go back the way one came, or to bend or curve something backward. It can be used both literally for physical movement and figuratively.
ανακάμπτω.example_greek: Ανακάμψαμε στο σπίτι όταν συνειδητοποιήσαμε ότι ξεχάσαμε τα κλειδιά.
ανακάμπτω.example_english: We turned back to the house when we realized we had forgotten the keys.

ανάκαμψη.definition: The process of returning to a normal or better state after illness, difficulty, or decline. Can refer to physical recovery or economic/social improvement.
ανάκαμψη.example_greek: Η ανάκαμψη του ασθενή ήταν αργή αλλά σταθερή.
ανάκαμψη.example_english: The patient's recovery was slow but steady.

ανακατάληφη.definition: The act of capturing, seizing, or taking back something that was previously lost or taken away. It refers to the recovery or reclamation of territory, property, or control.
ανακατάληφη.example_greek: Η ανακατάληφη της πόλης ήταν ένα σημαντικό στρατιωτικό επίτευγμα.
ανακατάληφη.example_english: The recapture of the city was an important military achievement.

ανακόπτω.definition: To stop or interrupt someone or something abruptly, breaking continuity or preventing completion of an action.
ανακόπτω.example_greek: Δεν πρέπει να ανακόπτεις τον άλλο όταν μιλά.
ανακόπτω.example_english: You shouldn't interrupt the other person when they're speaking.

ανακούφιση.definition: The act or feeling of being relieved from pain, worry, or difficulty; a sense of comfort or easing of burden.
ανακούφιση.example_greek: Ήταν μεγάλη ανακούφιση να μάθω ότι όλα είναι καλά.
ανακούφιση.example_english: It was a great relief to learn that everything is fine.

ανάκτηση.definition: The act of getting something back or recovering it after loss or damage. It can refer to physical recovery of objects, recovery from illness, or restoration of something lost.
ανάκτηση.example_greek: Η ανάκτηση του χαμένου περιστατικού ήταν δύσκολη.
ανάκτηση.example_english: The recovery of the lost data was difficult.

αναλαμβάνομαι.definition: To voluntarily accept or take on a task, responsibility, or role. It can also mean to recover or regain composure.
αναλαμβάνομαι.example_greek: Αναλαμβάνομαι την ευθύνη για το έργο.
αναλαμβάνομαι.example_english: I undertake the responsibility for the project.

αναλημματιλκός.definition: An adjective describing something related to an analemma, which is a figure-eight curve showing the position of the sun in the sky at a fixed time each day throughout the year, or a type of sundial using this principle.
αναλημματιλκός.example_greek: Το αναλημματικό ηλιακό ρολόι δείχνει την ακριβή ώρα καθ' όλο το έτος.
αναλημματιλκός.example_english: The analemmatic sundial shows the exact time throughout the year.

αναμενόμενος.definition: Describes something that is looked forward to, anticipated, or expected to happen or arrive. It can also mean 'pending' in official contexts.
αναμενόμενος.example_greek: Η αναμενόμενη άφιξη του τρένου ήταν στις 8 το πρωί.
αναμενόμενος.example_english: The expected arrival of the train was at 8 in the morning.

αναμένω.definition: To wait in anticipation of something or someone. To expect something to happen or someone to arrive.
αναμένω.example_english: I am waiting for the exam results.

αναμέτρηση.definition: A direct meeting or competition between two opponents or groups. It can refer to a sports match, a confrontation, or any face-to-face encounter between opposing sides.
αναμέτρηση.example_greek: Η αναμέτρηση ανάμεσα στις δύο ομάδες ήταν πολύ συναρπαστική.
αναμέτρηση.example_english: The match between the two teams was very exciting.

αναμετρώ.translation: to measure, to confront, to face off
αναμετρώ.definition: To measure again or to confront someone in competition or conflict. It can mean to meet as competitors or to take measurements of something.
αναμετρώ.example_greek: Οι δύο ομάδες θα αναμετρηθούν στον τελικό.
αναμετρώ.example_english: The two teams will face off in the final.

αναμονή.definition: The state of waiting for something or someone to arrive; the period of time spent in expectation or anticipation.
αναμονή.example_greek: Η αναμονή για τα αποτελέσματα ήταν πολύ δύσκολη.
αναμονή.example_english: The waiting for the results was very difficult.

αναντίρρητα.definition: In a manner that cannot be contradicted or disputed; used to emphasize that something is clearly true or obvious.
αναντίρρητα.example_greek: Αυτό είναι αναντίρρητα το καλύτερο προϊόν στην αγορά.
αναντίρρητα.example_english: This is undeniably the best product on the market.

αναξιοπιστία.definition: The quality or state of being unreliable, untrustworthy, or not worthy of confidence. It describes a lack of dependability or integrity.
αναξιοπιστία.example_greek: Η αναξιοπιστία του συναδέλφου του προκάλεσε σοβαρά προβλήματα στο έργο.
αναξιοπιστία.example_english: His colleague's unreliability caused serious problems on the project.

αναπλητώνω.definition: To make up for something, to compensate for a loss or deficiency, or to repair damage caused by past actions.
αναπλητώνω.example_greek: Προσπάθησε να αναπληρώσει το λάθος του με σκληρή δουλειά.
αναπλητώνω.example_english: He tried to compensate for his mistake with hard work.

αναπολώ.translation: to regret, to repent, to wish
αναπολώ.definition: To feel regret or sorrow about something that happened in the past, or to wish that something had been different. It can also mean to lament or mourn.
αναπολώ.example_greek: Αναπολώ που δεν πήγα στο πανεπιστήμιο που ήθελα.
αναπολώ.example_english: I regret that I didn't go to the university I wanted.

αναπτυξιακός.definition: Relating to or promoting development, growth, or progress, particularly in economic, social, or child development contexts.
αναπτυξιακός.example_greek: Το αναπτυξιακό πρόγραμμα της χώρας στοχεύει στη βελτίωση της οικονομίας.
αναπτυξιακός.example_english: The country's developmental program aims at improving the economy.

ανάρτηση.definition: Content shared on social media platforms or websites; the act of publishing or uploading something online.
ανάρτηση.example_greek: Η ανάρτησή του στα μέσα κοινωνικής δικτύωσης έλαβε πολλά likes.
ανάρτηση.example_english: His post on social media received many likes.

ανάσα.definition: The air that is inhaled and exhaled during respiration. Also used metaphorically to mean a moment of relief or respite.
ανάσα.example_greek: Πήρε μια βαθιά ανάσα πριν μιλήσει.
ανάσα.example_english: He took a deep breath before speaking.

αναστοχασμός.definition: The act of thinking again about something, reconsidering a previous decision or opinion. It implies a deliberate process of reviewing and reassessing one's thoughts or beliefs.
αναστοχασμός.example_greek: Μετά από τον αναστοχασμό του, άλλαξε γνώμη για το θέμα.
αναστοχασμός.example_english: After his reconsideration, he changed his opinion on the matter.

ανασυγκρότηση.definition: The process of rebuilding, reorganizing, or restructuring something that has been damaged, dissolved, or disorganized. It refers to putting something back together in a new or improved form.
ανασυγκρότηση.example_greek: Η ανασυγκρότηση της οικονομίας είναι μια μακρά διαδικασία.
ανασυγκρότηση.example_english: The reconstruction of the economy is a long process.

ανασυγκροτώ.translation: to reorganize, to reconstruct, to rebuild
ανασυγκροτώ.definition: To put together again or in a new way; to reorganize or reconstruct something that has been damaged, dispersed, or dismantled.
ανασυγκροτώ.example_greek: Η κυβέρνηση προσπάθησε να ανασυγκροτήσει την οικονομία μετά την κρίση.
ανασυγκροτώ.example_english: The government tried to reorganize the economy after the crisis.

ανασχηματισμός.definition: The process of changing the structure or form of something, typically an organization or system, to make it function better or differently. It involves a comprehensive redesign or reorganization.
ανασχηματισμός.example_greek: Η κυβέρνηση ανακοίνωσε ένα σχέδιο ανασχηματισμού του δημόσιου τομέα.
ανασχηματισμός.example_english: The government announced a plan for restructuring of the public sector.

ανατέμνω.definition: To cut or divide something into parts, especially in a surgical or anatomical context. It can also mean to analyze or examine something in detail.
ανατέμνω.example_greek: Οι γιατροί ανατέμνουν το δείγμα για να μελετήσουν την ασθένεια.
ανατέμνω.example_english: The doctors dissect the sample to study the disease.

ανατίμηση.definition: An increase in the price of a product or service. It refers to raising the cost of something above its previous price.
ανατίμηση.example_greek: Η ανατίμηση των τιμών των καυσίμων επηρέασε τα έξοδα μεταφοράς.
ανατίμηση.example_english: The price increase in fuel costs affected transportation expenses.

ανατρέπω.definition: To turn something upside down or cause it to tip over. Can also mean to overthrow or refute an argument or belief.
ανατρέπω.example_greek: Το κύμα ανέτρεψε το μικρό σκάφος.
ανατρέπω.example_english: The wave overturned the small boat.

ανάφλεξη.definition: The process of catching fire or bursting into flame; the act of igniting something or the state of being ignited. In medical contexts, it can refer to inflammation of tissues.
ανάφλεξη.example_greek: Η ανάφλεξη του καυσίμου συμβαίνει σε υψηλή θερμοκρασία.
ανάφλεξη.example_english: The ignition of fuel occurs at a high temperature.

ανάχωμα.definition: An artificial ridge or wall built to hold back water or prevent flooding, typically constructed along rivers or coasts.
ανάχωμα.example_greek: Το ανάχωμα του ποταμού προστατεύει το χωριό από τις πλημμύρες.
ανάχωμα.example_english: The river embankment protects the village from floods.

αναψηλάφηση.definition: The act of examining or touching something again, particularly in medical contexts when a physician re-examines a patient by touch or palpation to reassess a condition.
αναψηλάφηση.example_greek: Ο γιατρός ζήτησε αναψηλάφηση του πάσχοντα για να επιβεβαιώσει την διάγνωση.
αναψηλάφηση.example_english: The doctor requested a re-examination of the patient to confirm the diagnosis.

ανδρεϊκός.definition: Characterized by courage, bravery, or bold behavior; showing the qualities traditionally associated with a strong man.
ανδρεϊκός.example_greek: Ήταν μια ανδρεϊκή απόφαση να αντιμετωπίσει τον κίνδυνο.
ανδρεϊκός.example_english: It was a brave decision to face the danger.

ανεβώ.translation: to go up, to climb, to ascend
ανεβώ.definition: To move upward or to a higher position. Can refer to climbing stairs, mountains, or any upward movement.
ανεβώ.example_greek: Ανέβηκα στο σκαλοπάτι για να δω την θέα.
ανεβώ.example_english: I climbed the stairs to see the view.

ανέλκυση.definition: The act of pulling or lifting something up from a lower position to a higher one, commonly used in nautical or mechanical contexts.
ανέλκυση.example_greek: Η ανέλκυση του σκάφους από το νερό ήταν δύσκολη.
ανέλκυση.example_english: The hauling up of the boat from the water was difficult.

ανεμίζω.definition: To move something back and forth through the air to create a breeze, or to wave something in the air. Can also mean to air out or ventilate.
ανεμίζω.example_greek: Ανέμιζε το χαρτί για να δει τι ήταν γραμμένο.
ανεμίζω.example_english: She fanned the paper to see what was written on it.

ανεμπόδιστος.definition: Not blocked, impeded, or prevented from proceeding. Describing something that can move or continue without obstacles or restrictions.
ανεμπόδιστος.example_english: He has unobstructed access to the building.

ανενδοίαστα.definition: In a manner that shows no hesitation, doubt, or uncertainty; with complete confidence and conviction.
ανενδοίαστα.example_greek: Συμφώνησε ανενδοίαστα με την πρόταση του διευθυντή.
ανενδοίαστα.example_english: He agreed unhesitatingly with the director's proposal.

ανεπίλυτος.definition: Describes something that cannot be solved, resolved, or explained. Used for problems, mysteries, or conflicts that remain unresolved.
ανεπίλυτος.example_greek: Το ανεπίλυτο μυστήριο της υπόθεσης ταλαιπωρούσε τους ερευνητές.
ανεπίλυτος.example_english: The unsolved mystery of the case troubled the investigators.

ανερμήνευτος.definition: Not explained or interpreted; lacking clarification or understanding. Describes something that remains unclear or cannot be easily understood.
ανερμήνευτος.example_greek: Η ανερμήνευτη συμπεριφορά του τον καθιστούσε ύποπτο.
ανερμήνευτος.example_english: His unexplained behavior made him suspicious.

άνευ.definition: A formal or literary preposition meaning 'without' or 'in the absence of'. It is used to indicate the lack or absence of something.
άνευ.example_greek: Άνευ αμφιβολίας, αυτό είναι η καλύτερη λύση.
άνευ.example_english: Without doubt, this is the best solution.

ανήλικος.definition: A person who has not reached the legal age of adulthood; also used as an adjective describing someone who is under the age of legal maturity.
ανήλικος.example_greek: Ο ανήλικος δεν μπορεί να υπογράψει νόμιμα έγγραφα χωρίς τη συναίνεση του κηδεμόνα του.
ανήλικος.example_english: A minor cannot legally sign documents without the consent of their guardian.

ανησυχητικός.definition: Causing worry, anxiety, or concern; tending to make someone uneasy or troubled. Something that is unsettling or causes apprehension.
ανησυχητικός.example_greek: Τα νέα της ήταν πολύ ανησυχητικά για την οικογένεια.
ανησυχητικός.example_english: Her news was very disturbing for the family.

ανησυχία.definition: A state of feeling worried, nervous, or uneasy about something uncertain or feared. It describes an emotional condition characterized by mild to moderate stress or concern.
ανησυχία.example_greek: Η ανησυχία του για τις εξετάσεις ήταν μεγάλη.
ανησυχία.example_english: His anxiety about the exams was great.

ανθεκτικός.definition: Capable of withstanding damage, wear, or adverse conditions; able to endure physical or environmental stress without breaking or deteriorating.
ανθεκτικός.example_greek: Αυτό το υλικό είναι πολύ ανθεκτικό στη θερμότητα.
ανθεκτικός.example_english: This material is very resistant to heat.

ανθεκτικότητα.definition: The quality or capacity of something to withstand stress, damage, or adverse conditions without breaking or failing. It can refer to physical durability or the ability to resist external forces.
ανθεκτικότητα.example_greek: Η ανθεκτικότητα του υλικού είναι πολύ σημαντική για την κατασκευή γεφυρών.
ανθεκτικότητα.example_english: The durability of the material is very important for bridge construction.

ανίκανος.definition: Lacking the ability, skill, or qualification to do something; not capable of performing a task or duty.
ανίκανος.example_greek: Είναι ανίκανος να διαχειριστεί αυτό το πρόγραμμα.
ανίκανος.example_english: He is incapable of managing this program.

ανισότητα.definition: The state or quality of being unequal or uneven in size, amount, or value. In mathematics, it refers to a statement that two quantities are not equal.
ανισότητα.example_greek: Η κοινωνική ανισότητα είναι ένα σοβαρό πρόβλημα.
ανισότητα.example_english: Social inequality is a serious problem.

άνοδος.definition: The act of going up or climbing; an upward movement or slope. In electronics, it refers to the positive electrode of a battery or diode.
άνοδος.example_greek: Η άνοδος του βουνού ήταν πολύ δύσκολη.
άνοδος.example_english: The ascent of the mountain was very difficult.

ανοησία.definition: The quality or state of being foolish, lacking sense, or acting without intelligence. It describes actions or thoughts that are senseless or lacking proper judgment.
ανοησία.example_greek: Ήταν ανοησία να πάει μόνος του στη νύχτα.
ανοησία.example_english: It was foolishness to go alone at night.

ανόμοιος.definition: Not similar or alike in appearance, character, or quality. Characterized by lack of resemblance.
ανόμοιος.example_greek: Οι δύο αδελφές είναι ανόμοιες στη προσωπικότητά τους.
ανόμοιος.example_english: The two sisters are dissimilar in their personalities.

ανρηθώ.definition: To speak openly or express one's opinion, particularly about something that has been kept silent or hidden. It means to break silence and articulate what one thinks or feels.
ανρηθώ.example_greek: Τελικά ανρήθησα για τα προβλήματα που αντιμετωπίζουμε.
ανρηθώ.example_english: Finally, I spoke up about the problems we are facing.

ανταλάσσω.translation: to exchange, to swap, to barter
ανταλάσσω.definition: To give something in return for something else of equal value; to exchange or trade items between two parties.
ανταλάσσω.example_greek: Ανταλάσσαμε τα παλιά βιβλία με καινούρια.
ανταλάσσω.example_english: We exchanged the old books for new ones.

ανταλλαγή.definition: The act of exchanging or trading one thing for another; a reciprocal giving and receiving of goods or services.
ανταλλαγή.example_greek: Η ανταλλαγή των δώρων ήταν μια όμορφη στιγμή.
ανταλλαγή.example_english: The exchange of gifts was a beautiful moment.

αντάλλαγμα.definition: Something given or received in return for something else; a form of payment, compensation, or exchange in a transaction or agreement.
αντάλλαγμα.example_greek: Το αντάλλαγμα για την πώλησή του ήταν πολύ καλό.
αντάλλαγμα.example_english: The compensation for its sale was very good.

ανταλλακτικός.definition: Relating to or used as a replacement part or component. Can also mean related to exchange or barter.
ανταλλακτικός.example_greek: Χρειάζομαι ανταλλακτικά για το αυτοκίνητό μου.
ανταλλακτικός.example_english: I need spare parts for my car.

ανταποκρίνομαι.translation: to respond, to correspond, to meet
ανταποκρίνομαι.definition: to respond or react to something; to be equal to or meet expectations or requirements; to correspond or match.
ανταποκρίνομαι.example_greek: Η απόδοσή του ανταποκρίνεται στις προσδοκίες του προπονητή.
ανταποκρίνομαι.example_english: His performance meets the coach's expectations.

αντεπανάσταση.definition: A revolution or uprising that opposes and seeks to reverse a previous revolution. It represents an organized political movement against revolutionary changes.
αντεπανάσταση.example_greek: Η αντεπανάσταση ήταν μια προσπάθεια να ανατραπούν οι μεταρρυθμίσεις της επανάστασης.
αντεπανάσταση.example_english: The counter-revolution was an attempt to overturn the reforms of the revolution.

αντεπίθεση.definition: A military or strategic attack made in response to an enemy's attack. It is an offensive action taken to oppose or neutralize an opposing force's initial assault.
αντεπίθεση.example_greek: Ο στρατός σχεδίασε μια αντεπίθεση για να ανακτήσει τα χαμένα εδάφη.
αντεπίθεση.example_english: The army planned a counterattack to reclaim the lost territory.

αντιδιαδήλωση.definition: A public protest or demonstration organized in opposition to another protest or demonstration. It represents a contrasting viewpoint expressed through organized public action.
αντιδιαδήλωση.example_greek: Χιλιάδες άνθρωποι συμμετείχαν στην αντιδιαδήλωση ενάντια στην αρχική συγκέντρωση.
αντιδιαδήλωση.example_english: Thousands of people participated in the counter-protest against the original rally.

αντικατοπτρίζω.definition: To reflect or mirror something, either literally (light, image) or figuratively (ideas, feelings). It can also mean to respond in kind or reciprocate.
αντικατοπτρίζω.example_greek: Το καθρέφτη αντικατοπτρίζει το φως του ήλιου.

αντίκρισμα.definition: The side or place that is directly across from something; the opposite or facing direction. Often used to describe something located directly opposite or in front of something else.
αντίκρισμα.example_greek: Το σπίτι είναι στο αντίκρισμα του πάρκου.
αντίκρισμα.example_english: The house is on the opposite side of the park.

αντικρούομενος.definition: Describing something that opposes, contradicts, or clashes with something else. Often used to describe conflicting statements, interests, or forces.
αντικρούομενος.example_greek: Οι αντικρούομενες απόψεις των δύο πολιτικών προκάλεσαν σύγχυση.
αντικρούομενος.example_english: The conflicting views of the two politicians caused confusion.

αντίκτυπος.definition: The effect or consequence of an action or event; a reaction or rebound resulting from something. It can also refer to a physical echo or reverberation.
αντίκτυπος.example_greek: Η απόφαση του κυβέρνησης είχε μεγάλο αντίκτυπο στην οικονομία.
αντίκτυπος.example_english: The government's decision had a major impact on the economy.

αντίκυπτος.definition: Situated or facing opposite; visible from the other side or from a distance. Often used in literature to describe something that appears or stands opposite to the viewer.
αντίκυπτος.example_greek: Από το παράθυρο έβλεπε τα αντίκυπτα βουνά.
αντίκυπτος.example_english: From the window, she could see the mountains opposite/facing them.

αντιλαμβάνομαι.definition: To become aware of something through perception or understanding; to grasp or comprehend something mentally or sensually.
αντιλαμβάνομαι.example_greek: Αντιλήφθηκα ότι κάτι δεν ήταν σωστό.
αντιλαμβάνομαι.example_english: I realized that something was not right.

αντιληπτός.definition: Capable of being perceived or noticed by the senses or mind. Able to be understood or comprehended.
αντιληπτός.example_greek: Η διαφορά στην ποιότητα είναι αντιληπτή ακόμα και για τους αρχάριους.
αντιληπτός.example_english: The difference in quality is noticeable even to beginners.

αντίληψη.definition: The way something is understood, perceived, or interpreted by someone. It refers to a person's impression or comprehension of something.
αντίληψη.example_greek: Η αντίληψή μου για τη κατάσταση είναι διαφορετική από τη δική σου.
αντίληψη.example_english: My perception of the situation is different from yours.

αντιμαχόμενος.definition: One who opposes or resists something; currently engaged in opposition or struggle against something or someone.
αντιμαχόμενος.example_greek: Ο αντιμαχόμενος πληθυσμός δεν παραιτήθηκε ποτέ.

αντιμετώπηση.definition: The act of facing or dealing with a problem, challenge, or situation. It can mean direct confrontation or the process of handling something difficult.
αντιμετώπηση.example_greek: Η αντιμετώπηση του προβλήματος απαιτεί γρήγορη δράση.
αντιμετώπηση.example_english: Dealing with the problem requires quick action.

αντιμετωπίζω.translation: to face, to confront, to deal with
αντιμετωπίζω.definition: To face or confront a problem, challenge, or person directly. To deal with or handle a difficult situation.
αντιμετωπίζω.example_greek: Πρέπει να αντιμετωπίσουμε τα προβλήματα με θάρρος.
αντιμετωπίζω.example_english: We must face our problems with courage.

αντιμετώπιση.definition: The act of facing, confronting, or dealing with a problem, challenge, or opponent. It refers to the manner or approach taken to handle a difficult situation.
αντιμετώπιση.example_greek: Η αντιμετώπιση της κρίσης απαιτεί γρήγορες αποφάσεις.
αντιμετώπιση.example_english: Facing the crisis requires quick decisions.

αντιπαιδαγωγικός.definition: Describing something that is opposed to or works against sound educational principles and practices; contrary to good pedagogy.
αντιπαιδαγωγικός.example_greek: Αυτές οι μέθοδοι διδασκαλίας είναι αντιπαιδαγωγικές και δεν ωφελούν τους μαθητές.
αντιπαιδαγωγικός.example_english: These teaching methods are anti-educational and do not benefit the students.

αντίπαλος.definition: A person who opposes or competes against another in a contest, debate, or conflict. Can be used as both noun and adjective meaning opposing or rival.
αντίπαλος.example_greek: Ο αντίπαλός του ήταν πολύ δυνατός στο ποδόσφαιρο.
αντίπαλος.example_english: His opponent was very strong in football.

αντιπαλότητα.definition: The state or quality of being an opponent or rival; mutual hostility or opposition between competing parties or interests.
αντιπαλότητα.example_greek: Η αντιπαλότητα μεταξύ των δύο ομάδων ήταν έντονη.
αντιπαλότητα.example_english: The rivalry between the two teams was intense.

αντιπαράθεση.definition: The act of placing two things side by side for the purpose of comparing or contrasting them, or a state of direct opposition or disagreement between ideas, positions, or elements.
αντιπαράθεση.example_greek: Η αντιπαράθεση μεταξύ του παλιού και του νέου ήταν πολύ εντυπωσιακή.
αντιπαράθεση.example_english: The contrast between the old and the new was very striking.

αντιπαραθέτω.translation: to juxtapose, to place in contrast
αντιπαραθέτω.definition: To place two things side by side in order to compare or contrast them, or to set something in opposition to another thing.
αντιπαραθέτω.example_greek: Ο καθηγητής αντιπάραθεσε τις δύο διαφορετικές απόψεις για το πρόβλημα.
αντιπαραθέτω.example_english: The teacher juxtaposed the two different viewpoints on the issue.

αντίποινο.definition: An action taken in return for an injury or offense; a retaliatory measure taken against someone who has caused harm.
αντίποινο.example_greek: Η κυβέρνηση απείλησε με αντίποινα εναντίον του εχθρικού κράτους.
αντίποινο.example_english: The government threatened with retaliatory measures against the hostile state.

αντιπολιτευόμενος.definition: Referring to a member of the political opposition or someone who opposes the government in power. Can function as both an adjective describing oppositional activity and as a noun referring to opposition politicians.
αντιπολιτευόμενος.example_english: The opposition leader called for elections.

αντιπολίτευση.definition: The political opposition; the party or parties that oppose the government in power. In parliamentary systems, it refers to the organized political force that contests the ruling government's policies and decisions.
αντιπολίτευση.example_greek: Η αντιπολίτευση κριτίκαρε τα νέα μέτρα της κυβέρνησης.
αντιπολίτευση.example_english: The opposition criticized the government's new measures.

αντιπρόεδρος.definition: A person who holds the second-highest office in an organization, government, or institution, typically assuming leadership responsibilities in the absence of the president or chairman.
αντιπρόεδρος.example_greek: Ο αντιπρόεδρος της εταιρείας παρουσίασε το νέο σχέδιο.
αντιπρόεδρος.example_english: The vice president of the company presented the new plan.

αντιπροσώπευση.definition: The act of representing or acting on behalf of another person, group, or organization. It can also refer to a group of representatives sent to represent a larger body.
αντιπροσώπευση.example_greek: Η αντιπροσώπευση της χώρας στη διεθνή συνδιάσκεψη ήταν πολύ δυνατή.
αντιπροσώπευση.example_english: The country's representation at the international conference was very strong.

αντίρρηση.definition: A statement or action expressing disagreement or disapproval of something. An expression of opposition to a proposal, decision, or statement.
αντίρρηση.example_greek: Υπήρξε αντίρρηση από πολλούς συναδέλφους στο νέο σχέδιο.
αντίρρηση.example_english: There was objection from many colleagues to the new plan.

αντιστάθμισμα.definition: Something given or done to balance or compensate for something else; a counterweight or equivalent exchange to neutralize a loss or imbalance.
αντιστάθμισμα.example_greek: Η αύξηση των φόρων ήταν αντιστάθμισμα για τη μείωση των δαπανών.
αντιστάθμισμα.example_english: The tax increase was compensation for the reduction in spending.

αντιστέκομαι.translation: to resist, to oppose, to stand against
αντιστέκομαι.definition: To actively oppose or resist something, to stand firm against pressure or aggression. It conveys determination to not yield or comply with opposition.
αντιστέκομαι.example_greek: Οι κάτοικοι αντιστέκονται στα σχέδια κατεδάφισης του κτιρίου.
αντιστέκομαι.example_english: The residents resist the plans to demolish the building.

αντίστοιχος.definition: Matching or corresponding to something else; occupying a similar position or having similar properties. Often used to indicate the thing or person that matches another in a pair or series.
αντίστοιχος.example_greek: Κάθε χώρα έχει τις αντίστοιχες νόμιμες διαδικασίες.
αντίστοιχος.example_english: Each country has its corresponding legal procedures.

αντιστοίχως.definition: In a manner that corresponds to or matches something else; used to indicate that items mentioned correlate or correspond to each other in the same order.
αντιστοίχως.example_greek: Ο Γιάννης και η Μαρία είναι δάσκαλος και νοσοκόμα, αντιστοίχως.
αντιστοίχως.example_english: Giannis and Maria are a teacher and a nurse, respectively.

αντίστροφος.definition: Going in the opposite direction or having the opposite order, quality, or effect. Used to describe something that is contrary or inverted in relation to something else.
αντίστροφος.example_greek: Η αντίστροφη σειρά των αριθμών είναι 5, 4, 3, 2, 1.
αντίστροφος.example_english: The reverse order of the numbers is 5, 4, 3, 2, 1.

αντισυμβατικός.definition: Describing something or someone that goes against established norms, customs, or conventional standards. It refers to behavior, ideas, or approaches that deviate from what is traditionally or socially accepted.
αντισυμβατικός.example_greek: Έχει αντισυμβατικές απόψεις για την εκπαίδευση.
αντισυμβατικός.example_english: He has unconventional views about education.

αντιτάσσω.translation: to oppose, to resist, to set against
αντιτάσσω.definition: To arrange or position something in opposition to something else, or to resist and oppose actively. It can be used in military contexts or metaphorically for ideological opposition.
αντιτάσσω.example_greek: Η αντιπολίτευση αντιτάσσεται στις νέες πολιτικές του κυβερνήματος.
αντιτάσσω.example_english: The opposition resists the government's new policies.

αντίτισμο.definition: A payment made in return or as a response to another payment; compensation or retaliation through financial means.
αντίτισμο.example_greek: Το αντίτισμο για τις ζημιές έγινε γρήγορα.
αντίτισμο.example_english: The compensation payment for the damages was made quickly.

αντίφαση.definition: A situation in which two statements or ideas are opposed to each other and cannot both be true at the same time. It can also refer to a logical inconsistency or paradox.
αντίφαση.example_greek: Υπάρχει μια σαφή αντίφαση μεταξύ αυτών που λέει και αυτών που κάνει.
αντίφαση.example_english: There is a clear contradiction between what he says and what he does.

αντλώ.definition: To extract or draw up liquid (especially water) using a pump or similar device. Can also mean to draw upon or utilize resources.
αντλώ.example_greek: Αντλούμε νερό από το πηγάδι κάθε πρωί.
αντλώ.example_english: We pump water from the well every morning.

ανυποληψία.definition: The state or quality of being disregarded or not taken into consideration; neglect or lack of respect shown toward someone or something.
ανυποληψία.example_greek: Η ανυποληψία του προς τις απόψεις των άλλων τον έκανε απρόσφορο συνεργάτη.
ανυποληψία.example_english: His disregard for the opinions of others made him an unsuitable collaborator.

ανυπομονώ.translation: to be impatient, to lose patience
ανυπομονώ.definition: to feel or show impatience; to be unable to wait calmly or tolerate a delay. Often used to express frustration or eagerness.
ανυπομονώ.example_greek: Ανυπομονώ να δω το νέο του ταινία.
ανυπομονώ.example_english: I can't wait to see his new film.

ανώτερος.definition: Located at a higher position or level; of greater rank, quality, or importance than something else.
ανώτερος.example_greek: Το ανώτερο όροφο του κτιρίου είναι πιο σύγχρονο.
ανώτερος.example_english: The upper floor of the building is more modern.

ανωτέρω.definition: An adverb indicating a position higher up, or referring to something mentioned earlier in a text or conversation.
ανωτέρω.example_greek: Όπως αναφέρθηκε ανωτέρω, η συνάντηση είναι στις τρεις.
ανωτέρω.example_english: As mentioned above, the meeting is at three o'clock.

ανώφελα.definition: In a manner that is useless, fruitless, or without purpose; without achieving the desired result or effect.
ανώφελα.example_greek: Προσπάθησε ανώφελα να πείσει τον φίλο του.
ανώφελα.example_english: He tried in vain to convince his friend.

αξίζω.definition: To have value or merit; to be deserving of something. This verb is used to express that someone or something is worthy of a certain treatment, reward, or consideration.
αξίζω.example_greek: Αυτό το βιβλίο αξίζει να διαβαστεί.

αξιοθαύμαστος.definition: Deserving or worthy of wonder, amazement, or admiration; remarkable and impressive in a positive way.
αξιοθαύμαστος.example_greek: Η αξιοθαύμαστη επίδοση του αθλητή εντυπωσίασε όλους τους θεατές.
αξιοθαύμαστος.example_english: The athlete's admirable performance impressed all the spectators.

αξιοθρήνητος.definition: Deserving of lamentation or regret; worthy of being mourned or pitied due to being unfortunate or contemptible.
αξιοθρήνητος.example_greek: Η αξιοθρήνητη κατάσταση της οικονομίας προκάλεσε μεγάλη ανησυχία.
αξιοθρήνητος.example_english: The deplorable state of the economy caused great concern.

αξιολίγιση.definition: The process of assessing or evaluating the value, quality, or importance of something. It involves making a judgment based on examination and analysis.
αξιολίγιση.example_greek: Η αξιολίγιση των μαθητών γίνεται στο τέλος του σχολικού έτους.
αξιολίγιση.example_english: The assessment of students takes place at the end of the school year.

αξιολογικός.definition: Relating to or used in evaluation, assessment, or appraisal. Describing something that involves making judgments about value or worth.
αξιολογικός.example_greek: Η αξιολογική διαδικασία είναι σημαντική για τη βελτίωση της εκπαίδευσης.
αξιολογικός.example_english: The evaluative process is important for improving education.

αξιόπιστος.definition: Worthy of trust or confidence; able to be relied upon. Describes someone or something that can be depended on to do what is expected.
αξιόπιστος.example_greek: Ο Γιάννης είναι ένας αξιόπιστος φίλος που μπορώ να εμπιστευθώ.
αξιόπιστος.example_english: Giannis is a reliable friend I can trust.

αξιοποιώ.translation: to make use of, to utilize
αξιοποιώ.definition: To use something effectively or to its full potential; to take advantage of an opportunity or resource in a beneficial way.
αξιοποιώ.example_greek: Πρέπει να αξιοποιήσουμε τις διαθέσιμες πόρους.
αξιοποιώ.example_english: We must make use of the available resources.

αξιοπρέπεια.definition: The quality of being worthy of respect and honor; dignified and proper behavior that commands admiration and esteem.
αξιοπρέπεια.example_greek: Διατήρησε την αξιοπρέπειά του παρά τις δύσκολες περιστάσεις.
αξιοπρέπεια.example_english: He maintained his dignity despite the difficult circumstances.

αξιοσημείωτος.definition: Worthy of attention or notice; deserving to be remarked upon. Something significant or impressive enough to be remembered or pointed out.
αξιοσημείωτος.example_greek: Η απόδοσή του ήταν αξιοσημείωτη και έλαβε πολλά συγχαρητήρια.
αξιοσημείωτος.example_english: His performance was remarkable and he received many congratulations.

αξιωματική αντιπολίτευση.definition: The main opposition party in a parliamentary system that is formally recognized as the principal challenger to the governing party, with specific rights and parliamentary privileges.
αξιωματική αντιπολίτευση.example_greek: Η αξιωματική αντιπολίτευση έχει δικαίωμα να ελέγχει τις πράξεις της κυβέρνησης.
αξιωματική αντιπολίτευση.example_english: The official opposition has the right to scrutinize the government's actions.

αξιωματικός.definition: A military personnel of commissioned rank, typically holding authority over enlisted soldiers. Can also mean 'axiomatic' when used as an adjective.
αξιωματικός.example_greek: Ο αξιωματικός διέταξε τους στρατιώτες να σταθούν στη σειρά.
αξιωματικός.example_english: The officer ordered the soldiers to stand in line.

αξίωση.definition: A formal or assertive request for something that one believes they are entitled to; a requirement or condition that must be met.
αξίωση.example_greek: Η αξίωση του εργατικού συνδικάτου ήταν η αύξηση των μισθών.
αξίωση.example_english: The labor union's demand was a wage increase.

άξονας.definition: A straight line around which something rotates, or a central reference line in a coordinate system. It can also refer to the rod or shaft that allows wheels to turn.
άξονας.example_greek: Ο άξονας του τροχού είναι σπασμένος.
άξονας.example_english: The axle of the wheel is broken.

απαγωγή.definition: The act of taking someone away unlawfully or by force; abduction or kidnapping. It can also refer to the removal or carrying away of something.
απαγωγή.example_greek: Η απαγωγή του παιδιού ήταν ένα σοβαρό έγκλημα.
απαγωγή.example_english: The abduction of the child was a serious crime.

απαιτητικός.definition: Characterized by high or strict demands; requiring much effort, skill, or attention. Can describe a person who sets high standards or a task that is difficult.
απαιτητικός.example_greek: Αυτό το έργο είναι πολύ απαιτητικό και χρειάζεται πολλές ώρες δουλειάς.
απαιτητικός.example_english: This project is very demanding and requires many hours of work.

απαιτώ.definition: To insist on having something or demand that something be done; to require as a necessary condition.
απαιτώ.example_greek: Απαιτώ μια εξήγηση για αυτή τη συμπεριφορά.
απαιτώ.example_english: I demand an explanation for this behavior.

απαλλαγή.definition: The state of being freed or released from something burdensome, such as a duty, obligation, or pain. It can refer to relief from suffering or exemption from responsibility.
απαλλαγή.example_greek: Η απαλλαγή από τα προβλήματα ήταν μεγάλη ανακούφιση.
απαλλαγή.example_english: The relief from the problems was a great comfort.

απαλλάσσω.translation: to free, to relieve, to release
απαλλάσσω.definition: To free someone or something from a burden, obligation, or unwanted situation. It can mean to relieve, exempt, or discharge from responsibility.
απαλλάσσω.example_greek: Η δικαιοσύνη τον απάλλαξε από τις κατηγορίες.
απαλλάσσω.example_english: The court freed him from the charges.

απαλλάσω.translation: to free, to relieve, to rid
απαλλάσω.definition: To set someone free from something burdensome or unwanted; to relieve or liberate from a duty, obligation, or difficult situation.
απαλλάσω.example_greek: Η δικαστήριο απάλλαξε τον κατηγορούμενο από τις κατηγορίες.
απαλλάσω.example_english: The court freed the defendant from the charges.

απαλλοτριώνω.definition: To cause someone to feel isolated or separated from others, or to deprive someone of property or rights. It can mean making someone feel like an outsider or removing their sense of belonging.
απαλλοτριώνω.example_greek: Η συνεχής κριτική απαλλοτρίωσε τον από την οικογένειά του.
απαλλοτριώνω.example_english: The constant criticism alienated him from his family.

απάνθρωπος.definition: Lacking human compassion or kindness; brutal and inhumane in behavior or character.
απάνθρωπος.example_greek: Η απάνθρωπη συμπεριφορά του τον έκανε διάσημο για τα λάθος λόγια.
απάνθρωπος.example_english: His inhuman behavior made him infamous for the wrong reasons.

απαξιώνω.translation: to disdain, to treat with contempt
απαξιώνω.definition: To regard or treat someone or something as unworthy of respect or consideration; to show disdain or contempt toward.
απαξιώνω.example_greek: Δεν πρέπει να απαξιώνεις τις απόψεις των άλλων.
απαξιώνω.example_english: You should not disdain the opinions of others.

απαξίωση.definition: A feeling of not considering someone or something as worthy of respect or attention; the act of treating someone with disrespect or scorn.
απαξίωση.example_greek: Η απαξίωση του προς τις προτάσεις ήταν εμφανής.
απαξίωση.example_english: His contempt for the proposals was evident.

απαξιωτικός.definition: Describing something that is insulting, disrespectful, or causes loss of dignity. It refers to treatment or behavior that diminishes someone's worth or respect.
απαξιωτικός.example_greek: Αυτά τα σχόλια είναι απαξιωτικά και δεν θα τα ανεχτώ.
απαξιωτικός.example_english: These comments are degrading and I won't tolerate them.

απαπόσπαστος.definition: Unable to be separated or divided; firmly connected or attached in a way that cannot be broken apart.

απαραίτητα.definition: In a manner that is necessary or essential; something that must happen or be done. Can also function as an adjective in plural neuter form meaning 'necessary things'.

άπας.definition: Refers to the totality of something without exception; encompasses all individuals or items in a group. Often used in formal or literary contexts.
άπας.example_greek: Άπας ο κόσμος ήταν εκεί για να τον δει.
άπας.example_english: All the people were there to see him.

απάταλα.definition: Describing someone or something that is free from care, worry, or trouble; carefree and lighthearted.
απάταλα.example_greek: Τα παιδιά έπαιζαν απάταλα στο πάρκο.
απάταλα.example_english: The children played carefree in the park.

απατεώνας.definition: A person who deliberately deceives or defrauds others for personal gain. Someone who cheats or tricks people.
απατεώνας.example_greek: Ο απατεώνας έκλεψε τα χρήματα των ανθρώπων.

απατηλός.definition: Describing something that is intended to deceive or mislead; not truthful or honest. Something that gives a false impression or appearance.
απατηλός.example_greek: Το απατηλό του χαμόγελο κρύβει κακές προθέσεις.
απατηλός.example_english: His deceptive smile hides bad intentions.

ΑΠΕ.definition: An acronym for Ανανεώσιμες Πηγές Ενέργειας (Anavesimes Piges Energeias), referring to renewable energy sources such as solar, wind, and hydroelectric power.
ΑΠΕ.example_greek: Η χώρα επενδύει σε ΑΠΕ για να μειώσει τις εκπομπές άνθρακα.
ΑΠΕ.example_english: The country is investing in renewable energy sources to reduce carbon emissions.

απειλή.definition: An expression of intent to cause harm or damage to someone or something. A statement or action that conveys danger or danger.
απειλή.example_greek: Η απειλή του πολέμου ανησύχησε όλους τους πολίτες.
απειλή.example_english: The threat of war worried all the citizens.

απειλητικός.definition: Expressing or conveying a threat; having a quality that suggests danger or harm. Often used to describe behavior, tone, or appearance that intimidates or warns of potential danger.
απειλητικός.example_greek: Ο άνδρας είχε απειλητικό ύφος και μας κοίταγε με απειλητικό βλέμμα.
απειλητικός.example_english: The man had a threatening demeanor and looked at us with a menacing gaze.

απειλώ.definition: to express an intention to harm or punish someone, or to be a source of danger or harm. It can also mean to loom or impend as a threat.
απειλώ.example_greek: Δεν θα σας απειλώ, αλλά πρέπει να είστε προσεκτικοί.
απειλώ.example_english: I won't threaten you, but you need to be careful.

άπειρος.definition: Lacking experience or skill in something; alternatively, boundless or unlimited in extent or quantity.
άπειρος.example_greek: Ο νέος εργάτης είναι άπειρος στη δουλειά.
άπειρος.example_english: The new worker is inexperienced at the job.

απελευθερώνω.translation: to liberate, to free, to release
απελευθερώνω.definition: To set someone or something free from imprisonment, bondage, or constraint. To emancipate or grant freedom.
απελευθερώνω.example_greek: Η επανάσταση απελευθέρωσε τον λαό από την κατοχή.
απελευθερώνω.example_english: The revolution liberated the people from occupation.

απελευθέρωση.definition: The act of freeing someone or something from captivity, oppression, or constraint. It can refer to political independence, personal freedom, or the emancipation of enslaved people.
απελευθέρωση.example_greek: Η απελευθέρωση της χώρας από την κατοχή ήταν ένα σημαντικό γεγονός.
απελευθέρωση.example_english: The liberation of the country from occupation was a significant event.

απεριόριστος.definition: Without limits, boundaries, or restrictions; having no constraints or conditions imposed upon it.
απεριόριστος.example_greek: Έχει απεριόριστη πρόσβαση στα έγγραφα της εταιρείας.
απεριόριστος.example_english: He has unlimited access to the company's documents.

απευθύνομαι.definition: to direct speech or communication toward someone; to appeal to or consult someone for help or advice.
απευθύνομαι.example_greek: Απευθύνομαι στον δάσκαλο για βοήθεια με τα μαθηματικά.
απευθύνομαι.example_english: I turn to the teacher for help with mathematics.

απευθύνω.translation: to address, to direct, to turn
απευθύνω.definition: To direct one's attention, speech, or action toward someone or something. To speak to or appeal to a person or group.
απευθύνω.example_greek: Απευθύνομαι στον κόσμο με ένα σημαντικό μήνυμα.
απευθύνω.example_english: I am addressing the world with an important message.

άπιαστος.definition: Unable to be caught, grasped, or seized; something that remains out of reach or difficult to capture physically or conceptually.
άπιαστος.example_greek: Το όνειρό του ήταν άπιαστο, πάντα μακριά από την πραγματικότητα.
άπιαστος.example_english: His dream was elusive, always far from reality.

απίθανος.definition: Not likely to happen or be true; implausible or unbelievable. It describes something that seems impossible or highly unlikely to occur.
απίθανος.example_greek: Είναι απίθανο να κερδίσει το λαχείο.
απίθανος.example_english: It is unlikely that he will win the lottery.

απληστία.definition: An excessive and insatiable desire for wealth, possessions, or power. It refers to a character flaw of being unable to be satisfied with what one has.
απληστία.example_greek: Η απληστία του τον οδήγησε στη χρεοκοπία.
απληστία.example_english: His greed led him to bankruptcy.

απογοητευμένος.definition: Feeling sad or unhappy because something did not happen as expected or was not as good as hoped. Can also mean discouraged or let down.
απογοητευμένος.example_greek: Ήμουν απογοητευμένος με το αποτέλεσμα του αγώνα.
απογοητευμένος.example_english: I was disappointed with the result of the match.

απογοήτευση.definition: The feeling of sadness or dissatisfaction when something does not meet one's expectations or hopes. A state of being let down or disillusioned.
απογοήτευση.example_greek: Η απογοήτευσή του ήταν εvidently όταν έμαθε ότι δεν κέρδισε το βραβείο.
απογοήτευση.example_english: His disappointment was evident when he learned that he didn't win the prize.

αποδεικνύω.definition: To establish the truth or validity of something through evidence, reasoning, or logical argument. To show or demonstrate conclusively.
αποδεικνύω.example_greek: Ο μαθηματικός απέδειξε το θεώρημα με σαφήνεια.
αποδεικνύω.example_english: The mathematician proved the theorem clearly.

αποδεκτός.definition: Worthy of being accepted or approved; suitable and satisfactory according to set standards or requirements.
αποδεκτός.example_greek: Η πρόταση του είναι αποδεκτή για όλους.
αποδεκτός.example_english: His proposal is acceptable to everyone.

αποδίδω.translation: to return, to give back, to render
αποδίδω.definition: To give something back to its owner or to restore something to its original state. It can also mean to produce or yield a result.
αποδίδω.example_greek: Του απέδωσα το βιβλίο που του χρωστούσα.
αποδίδω.example_english: I returned the book that I owed him.

αποδοτικός.definition: Capable of producing good results or profits; describing something that yields substantial output or return. Often used to describe work, investments, or systems that are effective and yield positive outcomes.
αποδοτικός.example_greek: Η νέα μέθοδος παραγωγής είναι πολύ αποδοτική.
αποδοτικός.example_english: The new production method is very productive.

αποδοχή.definition: The act of accepting or approving something; the state of being accepted or recognized by others. It can refer to both the action of accepting and the favorable reception of something.
αποδοχή.example_greek: Η αποδοχή του έργου του από το κοινό ήταν τεράστια.
αποδοχή.example_english: The acceptance of his work by the public was enormous.

αποδυναμώνω.definition: To make someone or something weaker, less powerful, or less effective. To reduce the strength, vigor, or authority of a person or entity.
αποδυναμώνω.example_greek: Η συνεχής κριτική αποδυναμώνει την αυτοπεποίθησή του.

αποζιμιώση.definition: The biochemical process in which microorganisms break down sugars, typically producing alcohol and carbon dioxide. It is commonly used in winemaking and beer production.
αποζιμιώση.example_greek: Η αποζιμιώση του κρασιού διαρκεί περίπου δύο εβδομάδες.
αποζιμιώση.example_english: The fermentation of the wine lasts approximately two weeks.

αποθηκεύω.translation: to store, to save, to keep
αποθηκεύω.definition: To put something away in a safe place for future use, or to keep data in a computer system. It can refer to physical storage or digital storage.
αποθηκεύω.example_english: I stored the documents in the cabinet.

αποθήκη.definition: A building or room used for storing goods, materials, or merchandise. In Modern Greek, it can refer to a warehouse, storage facility, or small shop/store.
αποθήκη.example_greek: Τα εμπορεύματα είναι αποθηκευμένα στην αποθήκη.
αποθήκη.example_english: The goods are stored in the warehouse.

αποικία.definition: A territory or settlement established and controlled by a foreign country. In ancient Greece, it referred to settlements founded by Greek city-states in other regions.
αποικία.example_greek: Η αποικία αυτή ιδρύθηκε από Αθηναίους εμπόρους.
αποικία.example_english: This colony was founded by Athenian merchants.

αποικιακός.definition: Relating to or characteristic of a colony or colonialism. Describes things connected to the establishment and rule of colonies by a foreign power.
αποικιακός.example_greek: Η αποικιακή περίοδος άφησε βαθιά σημάδια στην ιστορία του κόσμου.
αποικιακός.example_english: The colonial period left deep marks on the history of the world.

αποικιοκράτης.definition: A person who supports or practices colonialism; one who advocates for or participates in the establishment and control of colonies by a foreign power.
αποικιοκράτης.example_greek: Οι αποικιοκράτες του 19ου αιώνα κατέκτησαν πολλές περιοχές της Αφρικής.
αποικιοκράτης.example_english: The colonialists of the 19th century conquered many regions of Africa.

αποκαλόκαιρο.definition: The period at the end of summer, typically late August and early September, when summer is transitioning toward autumn.
αποκαλόκαιρο.example_greek: Στο αποκαλόκαιρο, οι θάλασσες είναι ακόμα ζεστές.
αποκαλόκαιρο.example_english: In late summer, the seas are still warm.

αποκαλύπτω.definition: To make something known that was previously hidden or secret; to unveil or expose something.
αποκαλύπτω.example_greek: Ο δημοσιογράφος αποκάλυψε τη σκευωρία των κυβερνητικών αξιωματούχων.
αποκαλύπτω.example_english: The journalist revealed the conspiracy of the government officials.

αποκάλυψη.definition: The act of revealing or uncovering something hidden or secret. In religious context, it refers to a divine revelation or the apocalypse.
αποκάλυψη.example_greek: Η αποκάλυψη της αλήθειας ήταν σοκαριστική για όλους.
αποκάλυψη.example_english: The revelation of the truth was shocking for everyone.
