νοικοκυριό.example_english: Her household is very large with five children.

νομιμοποιήση.definition: The process of making something legitimate or legal according to law. It can refer to officially recognizing the validity of something or bringing it into compliance with legal requirements.
νομιμοποιήση.example_greek: Η νομιμοποιήση του νέου νόμου απαιτεί την υπογραφή του προέδρου.
νομιμοποιήση.example_english: The legitimation of the new law requires the president's signature.

νομιμοποιώ.definition: To make something legal or socially acceptable; to give official or formal approval to something that was previously illegal or illegitimate.
νομιμοποιώ.example_greek: Η κυβέρνηση νομιμοποίησε τη χρήση ορισμένων φαρμάκων.
νομιμοποιώ.example_english: The government legitimized the use of certain medications.

νόμισμα.definition: A medium of exchange in the form of coins or banknotes used in a country's financial system. It can refer to the official currency of a nation or to coins in general.
νόμισμα.example_greek: Το ευρώ είναι το νόμισμα της Ελλάδας.
νόμισμα.example_english: The euro is the currency of Greece.

νομοθεσία.definition: The process of making or enacting laws, or the body of laws created by a legislative body. It refers to the legislative activity and the laws that result from it.
νομοθεσία.example_greek: Η νέα νομοθεσία για την προστασία του περιβάλλοντος εγκρίθηκε από το κοινοβούλιο.
νομοθεσία.example_english: The new legislation on environmental protection was approved by parliament.

νομοσχέδιο.definition: A proposed law or legislation that has been drafted and is presented to parliament for consideration and voting. It is the preliminary version of a law before its official enactment.
νομοσχέδιο.example_greek: Το νομοσχέδιο για την εκπαίδευση θα συζητηθεί στη Βουλή αύριο.
νομοσχέδιο.example_english: The bill on education will be discussed in Parliament tomorrow.

νοσταλγώ.translation: to feel nostalgic, to yearn for
νοσταλγώ.definition: To feel a sentimental longing or yearning for the past, for a person, or for a place that is distant or no longer accessible.
νοσταλγώ.example_greek: Νοσταλγώ τα παιδικά μου χρόνια στο χωριό.
νοσταλγώ.example_english: I feel nostalgic for my childhood years in the village.

νους.definition: The faculty of understanding, intelligence, or mental capacity. It refers to the mind's ability to think, reason, and comprehend.
νους.example_greek: Χρησιμοποίησε το νου του για να λύσει το πρόβλημα.
νους.example_english: He used his mind to solve the problem.

ντροπή.definition: A feeling of humiliation or self-consciousness caused by something considered dishonorable or improper. It can also mean disgrace or dishonor.
ντροπή.example_greek: Αισθάνθηκε ντροπή όταν έπεσε μπροστά σε όλους.
ντροπή.example_english: He felt embarrassment when he fell in front of everyone.

νυν.definition: At the present time or moment; indicating the current point in time.
νυν.example_greek: Τώρα δεν έχω χρόνο, αλλά νυν θα σε δω.
νυν.example_english: I don't have time now, but I will see you presently.

ξανανιώνω.translation: to feel young again, to rejuvenate
ξανανιώνω.definition: to experience a renewal of youth or youthful energy; to feel rejuvenated or invigorated as if becoming young again.
ξανανιώνω.example_greek: Όταν ταξιδεύω, ξανανιώνω και ξεχνώ τα προβλήματά μου.
ξανανιώνω.example_english: When I travel, I feel young again and forget my problems.

ξεθωριάζω.translation: to fade away, to become pale
ξεθωριάζω.definition: To gradually lose color, brightness, or vitality; to become faded or washed out over time through exposure to sunlight or wear.
ξεθωριάζω.example_greek: Το χρώμα του τοίχου ξεθωριάζει από τον ήλιο.
ξεθωριάζω.example_english: The color of the wall fades away from the sun.

ξεκαθ΄θρισμα.definition: The act of making something clear or explaining something that was confusing or misunderstood. It refers to removing confusion or ambiguity from a situation.
ξεκαθ΄θρισμα.example_greek: Χρειαζόμαστε ένα ξεκαθάρισμα για αυτό το θέμα.
ξεκαθ΄θρισμα.example_english: We need a clarification on this matter.

ξεκάθαρα.definition: In a clear and unambiguous manner; without any doubt or confusion. Used to emphasize that something is obvious or stated without reservation.
ξεκάθαρα.example_greek: Της είπε ξεκάθαρα ότι δεν θα παντρευόταν ποτέ.
ξεκάθαρα.example_english: He told her plainly that he would never marry.

ξεκαθαρίζω.definition: To make something clear or explicit, to remove confusion or ambiguity. It can also mean to tidy up or to put things in order.
ξεκαθαρίζω.example_greek: Πρέπει να ξεκαθαρίσουμε τα πράγματα ανάμεσά μας.
ξεκαθαρίζω.example_english: We need to clarify things between us.

ξεκάθαρος.definition: Completely clear, transparent, or easy to understand with no confusion or doubt. Often used to describe something obvious, transparent, or explicitly stated.
ξεκάθαρος.example_greek: Η απάντησή του ήταν ξεκάθαρη και χωρίς αμφιβολίες.
ξεκάθαρος.example_english: His answer was crystal clear and without any doubts.

ξεκίνημα.definition: The act of starting or beginning something; the point at which something begins or someone departs from a place.
ξεκίνημα.example_greek: Το ξεκίνημα του νέου έτους ήταν πολύ ενθουσιαστικό.
ξεκίνημα.example_english: The start of the new year was very exciting.

ξεμένω.translation: to stay, to remain, to be left
ξεμένω.definition: To remain in a place or condition, or to be left behind without something. It can also mean to stay in a location for a period of time.
ξεμένω.example_greek: Ξέμεινα στο σπίτι όλη την ημέρα.
ξεμένω.example_english: I stayed at home all day.

ξεπερνάω.translation: to surpass, to exceed, to overcome
ξεπερνάω.definition: To go beyond a limit, surpass someone or something in ability or performance, or to overcome a difficulty or obstacle.
ξεπερνάω.example_greek: Η εταιρεία ξεπέρασε τους στόχους πωλήσεων της.
ξεπερνάω.example_english: The company exceeded its sales targets.

ξεπηδώ.translation: jump out, leap out, spring out
ξεπηδώ.definition: To jump or leap out suddenly from a place or position. It can be used literally for physical jumping or figuratively for sudden emergence or action.
ξεπηδώ.example_greek: Ο σκύλος ξεπήδησε από το αυτοκίνητο όταν άνοιξα την πόρτα.
ξεπηδώ.example_english: The dog jumped out of the car when I opened the door.

ξέσπασμα.definition: A sudden and violent occurrence or expression of something, such as violence, emotion, or disease. It can refer to an explosion of feeling, an outbreak of conflict, or a sudden appearance of symptoms.
ξέσπασμα.example_greek: Το ξέσπασμα της βίας στην πόλη ήταν απροσδόκητο.
ξέσπασμα.example_english: The outbreak of violence in the city was unexpected.

ξεσπάω.translation: to break out, to burst forth
ξεσπάω.definition: To suddenly break free or burst out with force or emotion. Can refer to physical breaking or emotional outburst.
ξεσπάω.example_greek: Ξέσπασε σε κλάματα όταν άκουσε τα νέα.
ξεσπάω.example_english: She burst into tears when she heard the news.

ξετυλίγω.translation: to unroll, to unfold, to unwind
ξετυλίγω.definition: To unfold or unwind something that is rolled or coiled, or figuratively to reveal or explain something gradually. The prefix 'κσε-' indicates reversal or undoing of the action.
ξετυλίγω.example_greek: Ξετύλιξε το κόκκινο σχοινί που ήταν τυλιγμένο.
ξετυλίγω.example_english: She unrolled the red rope that was wound up.

ξεφορτώνομαι.translation: to unload, to get rid of
ξεφορτώνομαι.definition: To remove cargo or goods from a vehicle, ship, or container. Can also mean to rid oneself of something burdensome or to offload responsibility.
ξεφορτώνομαι.example_english: The ship unloaded at the port.

ξεφτίζω.definition: To eject saliva forcefully from the mouth. Can also mean to express contempt or disgust through spitting.
ξεφτίζω.example_greek: Ο σκύλος ξέφτισε όταν είδε τη γάτα.
ξεφτίζω.example_english: The dog spat when it saw the cat.

ξεχειλίζω.definition: to flow or spill over the edges of a container; to exceed normal limits or boundaries. Can also be used metaphorically to describe emotions or expressions that cannot be contained.
ξεχειλίζω.example_greek: Το ποτήρι ξεχειλίζει από το νερό.
ξεχειλίζω.example_english: The glass is overflowing with water.

ξεχόντρισμα.definition: The act or process of removing or stripping cartilage from meat, typically during butchering or food preparation. It can also refer to the cartilage itself that has been removed.
ξεχόντρισμα.example_greek: Το ξεχόντρισμα του κοτόπουλου είναι σημαντικό για τη σωστή προετοιμασία του.
ξεχόντρισμα.example_english: The cartilage removal from the chicken is important for proper preparation.

ξεχρεώνω.translation: to pay off a debt, to settle
ξεχρεώνω.definition: To pay off or settle a debt completely. It can also mean to discharge someone from an obligation or to clear a financial liability.
ξεχρεώνω.example_greek: Τέλος πάντων, ξεχρέωσα όλα μου τα χρέη.
ξεχρεώνω.example_english: Finally, I paid off all my debts.

ξεχωριστά.definition: In a manner that is separate or distinct from others; set apart or individually. It can also mean clearly or distinctly.
ξεχωριστά.example_greek: Θέλω να μιλήσω με εσάς ξεχωριστά.
ξεχωριστά.example_english: I want to speak with you separately.

ξορκίζω.translation: to exorcise, to cast out demons
ξορκίζω.definition: To drive out or expel evil spirits or demons from a person or place through ritual or incantation. It can also mean to conjure or call upon spirits.
ξορκίζω.example_greek: Ο ιερέας ξόρκισε το κτίριο που ήταν στοιχειωμένο.
ξορκίζω.example_english: The priest exorcised the building that was haunted.

ογκόλιθος.definition: A large rounded rock or stone, typically found naturally in fields, riverbeds, or mountainous areas. Often used to describe massive isolated rocks in the landscape.
ογκόλιθος.example_greek: Ο ογκόλιθος ήταν τόσο μεγάλος που δεν μπορούσαν να τον μετακινήσουν.
ογκόλιθος.example_english: The boulder was so large that they couldn't move it.

οδική.definition: Relating to roads, traffic, or road safety. Often used in compound terms like 'οδική ασφάλεια' (road safety) or 'οδική βοήθεια' (roadside assistance).
οδική.example_greek: Η οδική ασφάλεια είναι πολύ σημαντική για όλους τους οδηγούς.
οδική.example_english: Road safety is very important for all drivers.

οδικός.definition: Adjective describing something that pertains to or is related to roads, streets, or road traffic. Often used in contexts of traffic regulations and road safety.
οδικός.example_greek: Το οδικό δίκτυο της χώρας είναι πολύ καλό.
οδικός.example_english: The road network of the country is very good.

οικεία.definition: Belonging to or characteristic of one's home or family; familiar or intimate. Can also refer to domestic affairs or household matters.
οικεία.example_greek: Η οικεία ατμόσφαιρα του σπιτιού τον έκανε να νιώθει ασφάλεια.
οικεία.example_english: The familiar atmosphere of the house made him feel safe.

οικισμός.definition: A small community or settlement, typically a group of houses or buildings established in a particular area. It can refer to informal settlements or residential districts.
οικισμός.example_greek: Ο νέος οικισμός κτίστηκε κοντά στην πόλη.
οικισμός.example_english: The new settlement was built near the city.

οικονομοτεχνικός.definition: Relating to both economic and technical aspects; combining economic considerations with technical or engineering factors in analysis or planning.
οικονομοτεχνικός.example_greek: Η οικονομοτεχνική μελέτη του έργου έδειξε ότι είναι βιώσιμο.
οικονομοτεχνικός.example_english: The techno-economic study of the project showed that it is viable.

οικόπεδο.definition: A piece of land intended for building purposes, typically residential. It is an undeveloped parcel of property with defined boundaries.
οικόπεδο.example_greek: Αγόρασε ένα οικόπεδο στην εξοχή για να χτίσει σπίτι.
οικόπεδο.example_english: He bought a plot of land in the countryside to build a house.

οικτρός.definition: Deserving or evoking pity; in a deplorable or miserable condition. Often used to describe someone or something that is pathetic or pitiable.
οικτρός.example_greek: Ήταν μια οικτρή κατάσταση που δεν μπορούσε κανείς να δει.
οικτρός.example_english: It was a pitiful condition that no one could bear to see.

οίστρος.definition: A flying insect that bites livestock, or metaphorically, an irresistible urge, impulse, or state of frenzy that drives someone to action.
οίστρος.example_greek: Ο οίστρος της δημιουργίας τον κυρίευσε και δεν μπορούσε να σταματήσει.
οίστρος.example_english: The creative frenzy seized him and he couldn't stop.

ΟΛΘ.definition: ΟΛΘ does not form a recognizable word in Modern Greek. It may be an abbreviation, acronym, or typographical error.

ολίγος.definition: Small in quantity or amount; not much or not many. Used to describe something that exists in limited numbers or small measure.
ολίγος.example_greek: Έχω ολίγο χρόνο για να ολοκληρώσω το έργο.
ολίγος.example_english: I have little time to finish the work.

ολοένα.definition: in a manner that increases gradually or continuously; used to express progressive intensification of something over time.
ολοένα.example_english: The weather is becoming increasingly worse.

ολοκαύτωμα.definition: The systematic, state-sponsored persecution and murder of six million Jews by the Nazi regime and its collaborators between 1933 and 1945. More broadly, any large-scale destruction or massacre of a group of people.
ολοκαύτωμα.example_greek: Το ολοκαύτωμα είναι ένα από τα πιο σκοτεινά κεφάλαια της ιστορίας της ανθρωπότητας.
ολοκαύτωμα.example_english: The Holocaust is one of the darkest chapters in the history of humanity.

ολοκληρωτισμός.definition: A system of government that exercises total control over all aspects of citizens' lives and permits no individual freedom or private institutions independent of the state.
ολοκληρωτισμός.example_greek: Ο ολοκληρωτισμός είναι ένα καθεστώς που καταδυναστεύει την ελευθερία των ανθρώπων.
ολοκληρωτισμός.example_english: Totalitarianism is a regime that oppresses human freedom.

ολομέλεια.definition: A meeting or session in which all members of an organization, parliament, or body are present and participating. It refers to a full gathering as opposed to a committee or partial meeting.
ολομέλεια.example_greek: Η ολομέλεια του κοινοβουλίου θα συνεδριάσει αύριο για τη ψήφιση του νέου νόμου.
ολομέλεια.example_english: The plenary session of parliament will meet tomorrow to vote on the new law.

ολότητα.definition: The state or quality of being complete, whole, or entire. The sum total of all parts or elements considered together.
ολότητα.example_greek: Η ολότητα του έργου είναι εντυπωσιακή.
ολότητα.example_english: The totality of the work is impressive.

ομνύω.translation: to swear, to take an oath
ομνύω.definition: to make a solemn promise or declaration, typically invoking a deity or something sacred as a witness. It can also mean to curse or use profanity.
ομνύω.example_greek: Ομνύω στον Θεό ότι λέω την αλήθεια.
ομνύω.example_english: I swear to God that I am telling the truth.

ομολογήσω.definition: First person singular future tense of ομολογώ, meaning to confess, admit, or acknowledge something, often used when one accepts responsibility or tells the truth about something.
ομολογήσω.example_english: I will admit that I made a mistake.

ομολογώ.definition: To acknowledge or declare something to be true, often something one has done wrong or a fact one previously denied. It can also mean to confess or admit guilt.
ομολογώ.example_english: He admitted that he made a mistake.

ομότιμος.definition: Holding an honorary position or status equivalent in rank to another. Often used in academic or professional contexts to denote honorary membership or emeritus status.
ομότιμος.example_greek: Ο καθηγητής διορίστηκε ως ομότιμος καθηγητής του πανεπιστημίου.
ομότιμος.example_english: The professor was appointed as an honorary professor of the university.

ομόφυλος.definition: Referring to individuals of the same sex or things of the same nature or kind. In modern usage, commonly used to describe same-sex relationships or attractions.
ομόφυλος.example_greek: Ένας ομόφυλος σχέση είναι όταν δύο άτομα του ίδιου φύλου είναι ερωτικά ενδιαφερόμενα.
ομόφυλος.example_english: A same-sex relationship is when two people of the same sex are romantically interested in each other.

ομόφωνος.definition: Referring to words or sounds that are pronounced the same way but have different meanings and/or spellings. In music, it describes texture where all parts move together with the same rhythm.
ομόφωνος.example_greek: Τα λόγια «θάλασσα» και «θέληση» δεν είναι ομόφωνα.
ομόφωνος.example_english: The words 'thalassa' (sea) and 'thélisi' (will) are not homophonous.

ΟΝΕ.definition: A series of images, thoughts, and sensations occurring in a person's mind during sleep. In Greek culture, dreams hold significant symbolic and spiritual meaning.
ΟΝΕ.example_english: I had a strange dream last night.

όντως.definition: Used to emphasize that something is true or to confirm a statement with certainty. It expresses agreement or acknowledgment of reality.
όντως.example_greek: Αυτό είναι όντως ένα όμορφο σπίτι.
όντως.example_english: This is really a beautiful house.

όπλο.definition: An instrument or device designed or used for causing harm or damage, such as a gun, knife, or sword. It can also refer figuratively to any tool or means used to achieve a goal.
όπλο.example_greek: Η αστυνομία βρήκε ένα όπλο στο σημείο του εγκλήματος.
όπλο.example_english: The police found a weapon at the crime scene.

οπορτουνιστής.definition: A person who takes advantage of circumstances to gain profit or benefit, especially without regard for principles or ethics.
οπορτουνιστής.example_greek: Ο οπορτουνιστής άλλαξε την άποψή του για να κερδίσει ψήφους.
οπορτουνιστής.example_english: The opportunist changed his opinion to gain votes.

οπορτουνιστικός.definition: Characterized by taking advantage of opportunities without regard for principles or consequences. Describing someone or something that exploits situations for personal gain.
οπορτουνιστικός.example_greek: Η οπορτουνιστική του στάση τον αποξένωσε από τους φίλους του.
οπορτουνιστικός.example_english: His opportunistic attitude alienated him from his friends.

οπωροκηπευτικά.definition: Agricultural products consisting of fruits and vegetables, typically fresh produce sold in markets or shops.
οπωροκηπευτικά.example_greek: Αγόρασα φρέσκα οπωροκηπευτικά από τη λαϊκή αγορά.
οπωροκηπευτικά.example_english: I bought fresh fruits and vegetables from the farmers market.

ορατός.definition: Able to be seen or perceived by the eye; visible or apparent to observation.
ορατός.example_greek: Ο ήλιος είναι ορατός στον ουρανό.
ορατός.example_english: The sun is visible in the sky.

οργή.definition: A strong feeling of displeasure and hostility; intense anger or fury.
οργή.example_greek: Η οργή του ήταν φοβερή όταν άκουσε τα νέα.
οργή.example_english: His anger was terrible when he heard the news.

ορθός.definition: Meaning correct, accurate, or proper in judgment or action. Also can mean physically straight or upright in position or direction.

ορθώς.definition: In a correct or proper manner; accurately or appropriately. Used to indicate that something is done or said in the right way.

ορίζω.translation: to define, to set, to arrange
ορίζω.definition: To establish or determine the boundaries, limits, or meaning of something; to fix or arrange something in advance.
ορίζω.example_greek: Ορίζουμε τις προθεσμίες για το έργο.
ορίζω.example_english: We set the deadlines for the project.

όριο.definition: A line or point that marks the edge of something, or the maximum amount allowed. It can refer to a physical boundary or a numerical/conceptual limit.
όριο.example_greek: Το όριο ταχύτητας είναι 50 χιλιόμετρα την ώρα.
όριο.example_english: The speed limit is 50 kilometers per hour.

οριοθετώ.definition: To establish or mark the limits, boundaries, or definitions of something; to delineate clearly the extent or scope of an entity, concept, or area.
οριοθετώ.example_greek: Πρέπει να οριοθετήσουμε τα όρια της περιοχής.
οριοθετώ.example_english: We need to demarcate the boundaries of the area.

ορισμένος.definition: Referring to something that is fixed, determined, or clearly identified rather than vague or indefinite. Can also mean 'a certain' or 'some' in the sense of a particular but unnamed thing.
ορισμένος.example_greek: Θέλω να μιλήσω με ορισμένο άτομο στο γραφείο.
ορισμένος.example_english: I want to speak with a certain person at the office.

οριστικός.definition: Serving as a final decision or statement that cannot be changed; in grammar, referring to the definite article or tense that expresses a completed action.
οριστικός.example_greek: Αυτή είναι η οριστική απόφαση του δικαστηρίου.
οριστικός.example_english: This is the court's final decision.

όρομα.definition: A line or mark that indicates the edge or limit of an area, region, or property. It can refer to geographical boundaries or the limits of something.
όρομα.example_greek: Το όρομα μεταξύ των δύο χωρών είναι αυστηρά φυλαγμένο.
όρομα.example_english: The border between the two countries is strictly guarded.

όρος.definition: A large natural elevation of the earth's surface, significantly higher than surrounding terrain. Can also mean 'term' or 'condition' in different contexts.
όρος.example_greek: Το όρος Όλυμπος είναι το ψηλότερο βουνό της Ελλάδας.
όρος.example_english: Mount Olympus is the highest mountain in Greece.

όρυγμα.definition: A hole, pit, or excavated area in the ground, typically created by digging or mining operations.
όρυγμα.example_greek: Το όρυγμα ήταν πολύ βαθύ και επικίνδυνο.
όρυγμα.example_english: The excavation was very deep and dangerous.

ορυμαγδός.definition: A loud, confused noise or commotion; a state of noisy disorder or disturbance.
ορυμαγδός.example_greek: Ο ορυμαγδός στο στάδιο ήταν τόσο δυνατός που δεν μπορούσαμε να ακούσουμε τους εαυτούς μας.
ορυμαγδός.example_english: The uproar in the stadium was so loud that we couldn't hear ourselves.

οσμή.definition: A distinctive smell or odor, whether pleasant or unpleasant. It refers to the quality perceived by the sense of smell.
οσμή.example_greek: Η οσμή του καφέ είναι πολύ ωραία.
οσμή.example_english: The smell of coffee is very pleasant.

όσον.translation: as long as, while, insofar as
όσον.definition: A conjunction used to indicate a condition or time period, meaning 'as long as' or 'while.' It can also mean 'insofar as' when expressing a qualified statement.
όσον.example_greek: Όσον αφορά τη δουλειά, είμαι πολύ ικανοποιημένος.
όσον.example_english: As far as work is concerned, I am very satisfied.

ούδεις.definition: An indefinite pronoun meaning no person or not anyone. It is used to refer to the absence of any individual.
ούδεις.example_english: No one came to the party.

ουδέποτε.definition: At no time; not on any occasion. An emphatic negation indicating something has not and will not happen.
ουδέποτε.example_greek: Δεν έχω ουδέποτε δει τέτοιο πράγμα.
ουδέποτε.example_english: I have never seen such a thing.

ουδέτερος.definition: Not taking sides in a conflict or dispute; maintaining a position of neutrality or impartiality. Can also refer to the grammatical gender that is neither masculine nor feminine.
ουδέτερος.example_greek: Η Ελβετία παραμένει σε ουδέτερη θέση στις διεθνείς συγκρούσεις.
ουδέτερος.example_english: Switzerland maintains a neutral position in international conflicts.

ουδετερότητα.definition: The state or policy of not supporting either side in a conflict or dispute. The quality of being impartial or unbiased.
ουδετερότητα.example_greek: Η χώρα διατήρησε την ουδετερότητά της κατά τη διάρκεια του πολέμου.
ουδετερότητα.example_english: The country maintained its neutrality throughout the war.

ουσία.definition: The fundamental nature or most important quality of something; also refers to material substance or property. In philosophy, it denotes the essential being of an entity.
ουσία.example_greek: Η ουσία του προβλήματος είναι ότι δεν έχουμε χρόνο.
ουσία.example_english: The essence of the problem is that we don't have time.

ουσιαστικός.definition: Describing something that is fundamental, important, or constituting the essence of something. It can also refer to grammar, specifically nouns.
ουσιαστικός.example_greek: Αυτό είναι ένα ουσιαστικό σημείο στη συζήτηση.
ουσιαστικός.example_english: This is an essential point in the discussion.

ουσιώδης.definition: Of great importance or significance; forming the essential part of something. Relating to the basic nature or substance of something.
ουσιώδης.example_greek: Η υγεία είναι ουσιώδης για την ευτυχία.

οφείλω.translation: to owe, to be obliged to
οφείλω.definition: to be indebted to someone for money or a favor, or to have a moral or legal obligation to do something.
οφείλω.example_english: I owe money to my friend.

όχθη.definition: The sloping land beside a river, stream, or other body of water. Can also refer to an embankment or raised edge along a watercourse.
όχθη.example_english: We walked along the bank of the river.

π.χ..definition: An abbreviation of 'παραδείγματος χάριν' (paradigmatos charin) meaning 'for example' or 'for instance', used to introduce examples in written or spoken Greek.
π.χ..example_greek: Μερικά φρούτα, π.χ. τα μήλα και τα πορτοκάλια, είναι πολύ υγιεινά.
π.χ..example_english: Some fruits, for example apples and oranges, are very healthy.

πάγωμα.definition: The process or result of liquid turning into ice due to cold temperatures. It can also refer to frost formation on surfaces.
πάγωμα.example_greek: Το πάγωμα του νερού συμβαίνει στους 0 βαθμούς Κελσίου.
πάγωμα.example_english: The freezing of water occurs at 0 degrees Celsius.

παγώνω.definition: To turn into ice or become solid due to cold; to freeze something or someone in place.
παγώνω.example_greek: Το νερό παγώνει όταν η θερμοκρασία πέσει κάτω από το μηδέν.
παγώνω.example_english: Water freezes when the temperature drops below zero.

ΠΑΘΕ.definition: Second person singular aorist imperative or third person singular aorist indicative of the verb πάσχω (páscho), meaning to suffer, endure, or experience something negative.
ΠΑΘΕ.example_greek: Πάθε πολλά για να φτάσει εκεί που είναι τώρα.
ΠΑΘΕ.example_english: He suffered a lot to reach where he is now.

παθογένεια.definition: The process by which a disease or pathological condition develops and originates. It refers to the sequence of events that lead to the development of a disease or disorder in an organism.
παθογένεια.example_greek: Η παθογένεια της νόσου είναι πολύπλοκη και δεν είναι πλήρως κατανοητή.
παθογένεια.example_english: The pathogenesis of the disease is complex and not fully understood.

παλεύω.definition: To engage in wrestling or physical struggle with another person. Can also mean to struggle against difficulties or obstacles.
παλεύω.example_greek: Οι δύο αθλητές παλεύουν στο ματ.
παλεύω.example_english: The two athletes are wrestling on the mat.

πάλη.definition: A sport or physical contest where two opponents grapple with each other to throw or pin one another to the ground. It can also mean a metaphorical struggle or conflict.
πάλη.example_greek: Ο αθλητής εκπαιδεύεται για τον αγώνα πάλης.
πάλη.example_english: The athlete is training for the wrestling competition.

παλινωδία.definition: A formal or public withdrawal or retraction of a previously stated opinion, belief, or statement. The term originates from ancient Greek literature where poets would retract earlier declarations.
παλινωδία.example_greek: Ο πολιτικός αναγκάστηκε να κάνει παλινωδία για τις προηγούμενες δηλώσεις του.
παλινωδία.example_english: The politician was forced to make a recantation of his previous statements.

παλλαπλάσιος.definition: Describing something that is many times greater or larger than something else; having a multiplied quantity or magnitude. A mathematical or comparative term indicating multiplication.
παλλαπλάσιος.example_greek: Η νέα έκδοση του βιβλίου είναι παλλαπλάσια σε μέγεθος από την παλιά.
παλλαπλάσιος.example_english: The new edition of the book is manifold times larger than the old one.

παλτό.definition: A long outer garment worn over other clothes for warmth or protection from the weather.
παλτό.example_greek: Φόρεσα το παλτό μου γιατί έκανε κρύο.
παλτό.example_english: I put on my coat because it was cold.

πανανθρώπινος.definition: Relating to or affecting all human beings; universal in scope or application to humanity as a whole.
πανανθρώπινος.example_greek: Η πανανθρώπινη αξία της δικαιοσύνης είναι αναγνωρισμένη σε όλες τις κουλτούρες.
πανανθρώπινος.example_english: The universal value of justice is recognized in all cultures.

πανελλαδικός.definition: Relating to or involving all of Greece or all Greek people; concerning the entire nation rather than a specific region.
πανελλαδικός.example_greek: Οι πανελλαδικές εξετάσεις είναι πολύ σημαντικές για τους μαθητές.
πανελλαδικός.example_english: The nationwide exams are very important for students.

πανηγυρικός.definition: Relating to or characteristic of a public festival or celebration; grand and formal in manner or style, often used to describe speeches or ceremonies.
πανηγυρικός.example_greek: Η πανηγυρική ομιλία του προέδρου ήταν εντυπωσιακή.
πανηγυρικός.example_english: The president's festive speech was impressive.

παρά της.translation: beside/next to her; at her place
παρά της.definition: A combination of the preposition 'παρά' (beside, at, near) with the feminine genitive article 'της' (of the/her). Used to indicate proximity to a female person or location at someone's place.
παρά της.example_greek: Μένω παρά της φίλης μου για μια εβδομάδα.
παρά της.example_english: I am staying at my friend's place for a week.

παραβίαση.definition: The act of breaking, violating, or infringing upon a law, rule, agreement, or right. It refers to a transgression or failure to comply with established norms or regulations.
παραβίαση.example_greek: Η παραβίαση του συμβολαίου έχει σοβαρές συνέπειες.
παραβίαση.example_english: The breach of the contract has serious consequences.

παράγοντας.definition: A circumstance, element, or agent that contributes to or influences a particular result or situation. In mathematics, a number or expression that divides evenly into another number.
παράγοντας.example_greek: Ο χρόνος είναι ένας σημαντικός παράγοντας για την επιτυχία.
παράγοντας.example_english: Time is an important factor for success.

παράγω.translation: to produce, to generate, to manufacture
παράγω.definition: To create, manufacture, or generate something. It can refer to the production of goods, energy, or other outputs.
παράγω.example_greek: Το εργοστάσιο παράγει χιλιάδες προϊόντα κάθε μέρα.
παράγω.example_english: The factory produces thousands of products every day.

παραδείγματος χάρη.definition: A phrase used to introduce an example or illustration of something previously mentioned. It literally means 'for the sake of an example.'
παραδείγματος χάρη.example_greek: Υπάρχουν πολλές ασθένειες, παραδείγματος χάρη, η γρίπη και το κρυολόγημα.
παραδείγματος χάρη.example_english: There are many illnesses, for example, the flu and the common cold.

παραδίδω.definition: To give something to someone, to deliver or pass on; also means to betray or surrender someone or something to another party.
παραδίδω.example_greek: Ο δάσκαλος παραδίδει τα γράμματα στους μαθητές.
παραδίδω.example_english: The teacher delivers the letters to the students.

παραδοχή.definition: The act of accepting or receiving something; an assumption or supposition that is accepted as a basis for reasoning.
παραδοχή.example_greek: Η παραδοχή αυτή δεν είναι σωστή.

παραθέτω.translation: to present, to cite, to set forth
παραθέτω.definition: To present, exhibit, or cite something for consideration or as evidence. Commonly used in academic or formal contexts to refer to quoting, citing sources, or presenting arguments.
παραθέτω.example_greek: Ο ερευνητής παρατίθεται πολλές μελέτες για να υποστηρίξει την άποψή του.
παραθέτω.example_english: The researcher cites many studies to support his opinion.

παραισία.definition: The quality of being bold, daring, or impudent; a lack of shame or restraint in one's behavior or speech.
παραισία.example_greek: Είχε την παραισία να μιλήσει έτσι στον δάσκαλο.
παραισία.example_english: He had the audacity to speak that way to the teacher.

παραίτηση.definition: The act of formally giving up or resigning from a position, office, or responsibility. It can also mean the withdrawal from a claim or right.
παραίτηση.example_greek: Υπέβαλε παραίτηση από τη θέση του διευθυντή.
παραίτηση.example_english: He submitted his resignation from the position of director.

παρακινώ.translation: to incite, to instigate, to urge on
παρακινώ.definition: To encourage or persuade someone to do something, often something inappropriate or harmful. It can also mean to motivate or spur someone into action.
παρακινώ.example_greek: Δεν πρέπει να παρακινείς τα παιδιά να κάνουν άσχημα πράγματα.
παρακινώ.example_english: You should not incite the children to do bad things.

παράκτιος.definition: Relating to or situated near the coast or seashore. Describes areas, regions, or activities that are adjacent to or characteristic of coastal environments.
παράκτιος.example_greek: Οι παράκτιες περιοχές της Ελλάδας είναι πολύ δημοφιλείς για τουρισμό.
παράκτιος.example_english: The coastal regions of Greece are very popular for tourism.

παραλαβή.definition: The act of receiving, collecting, or taking possession of something, particularly goods or responsibility. It is commonly used in commercial and administrative contexts.
παραλαβή.example_greek: Η παραλαβή του πακέτου έγινε χθες το πρωί.
παραλαβή.example_english: The receipt of the package took place yesterday morning.

παραλυτικός.definition: Relating to or causing paralysis; affecting the nerves or muscles in a way that results in loss of movement or sensation. Can also describe something that is extremely limiting or incapacitating.
παραλυτικός.example_greek: Ο παραλυτικός φόβος τον εμπόδισε να μιλήσει.
παραλυτικός.example_english: Paralytic fear prevented him from speaking.

παραμένω.definition: To stay in a place or condition; to continue to exist or be present without change or movement.
παραμένω.example_greek: Παραμένω στο σπίτι λόγω της βροχής.
παραμένω.example_english: I remain at home because of the rain.

παραμύθι.definition: A fictional narrative, typically involving magical or fantastical elements, told for entertainment or moral instruction. Often refers to traditional tales passed down through generations.
παραμύθι.example_greek: Η μητέρα διάβαζε ένα παραμύθι στο παιδί πριν κοιμηθεί.
παραμύθι.example_english: The mother was reading a fairy tale to the child before sleep.

παράνομα.definition: Actions or behaviors that violate the law or are contrary to legal regulations. The plural form of παράνομο.
παράνομα.example_greek: Η αστυνομία διακόπησε τα παράνομα που συνέβαιναν στην περιοχή.
παράνομα.example_english: The police stopped the illegal activities that were occurring in the area.

παραπαίω.translation: to stumble, to falter, to make a mistake
παραπαίω.definition: To trip or stumble while walking, or figuratively to make an error or fail in one's duty. It can also mean to transgress or go astray.
παραπαίω.example_english: He stumbled on the step and fell.

παραπέμπω.translation: to refer, to direct, to send on
παραπέμπω.definition: To direct someone to another person or place for help, information, or further action. It can also mean to postpone or adjourn a matter to another time.
παραπέμπω.example_greek: Ο γιατρός με παραπέμπει σε έναν ειδικό.
παραπέμπω.example_english: The doctor is referring me to a specialist.

παραπλάνηση.definition: The act of deceiving someone or leading them astray through false information or dishonest means. It refers to intentional misrepresentation or trickery.
παραπλάνηση.example_greek: Η παραπλάνηση των καταναλωτών είναι παράνομη.
παραπλάνηση.example_english: The deception of consumers is illegal.

παράπονο.definition: An expression of dissatisfaction or annoyance about something; a complaint or grievance regarding a perceived wrong or problem.
παράπονο.example_greek: Έχω ένα παράπονο για την υπηρεσία του καταστήματος.
παράπονο.example_english: I have a complaint about the service at the store.

παράρτητα.definition: Additional documents or items attached or appended to something main, such as appendices to a book or attachments to an email.
παράρτητα.example_greek: Τα παράρτητα του συμβολαίου περιέχουν σημαντικές λεπτομέρειες.
παράρτητα.example_english: The attachments of the contract contain important details.

παρασκηνιακά.definition: Activities, dealings, or arrangements that happen privately or unofficially, away from public view; often refers to political or professional machinations conducted in secret.
παρασκηνιακά.example_greek: Τα παρασκηνιακά της πολιτικής είναι συχνά πολύ περίπλοκα.
παρασκηνιακά.example_english: The behind-the-scenes dealings of politics are often very complicated.

παρασκήνιο.definition: The area behind the stage in a theater where actors prepare and wait before performances, or the hidden workings behind any public activity.
παρασκήνιο.example_greek: Οι ηθοποιοί περιμένουν στο παρασκήνιο πριν την παράσταση.
παρασκήνιο.example_english: The actors wait backstage before the performance.

παρασύρω.translation: drag along, carry away, sweep along
παρασύρω.definition: To pull or drag someone/something along forcefully, or to persuade someone to do something they might not otherwise do. Can also mean to be swept away by emotion or circumstances.
παρασύρω.example_english: The current sweeps away the leaves.

παράταξη.definition: A military formation or arrangement of troops in battle order. More generally, any organized arrangement or lineup of people or things.
παράταξη.example_greek: Η παράταξη των στρατιωτών ήταν πολύ καλά οργανωμένη.
παράταξη.example_english: The battle formation of the soldiers was very well organized.

παράταση.definition: The act of extending or prolonging something in time or space, or the extended part itself. It can refer to an extension of a deadline, contract, or a physical extension of an object.
παράταση.example_greek: Ζήτησα παράταση της προθεσμίας για την παράδοση του έργου.
παράταση.example_english: I requested an extension of the deadline for submitting the project.

παρατείνω.definition: to make something longer in duration or physical length; to extend or stretch something beyond its original limit.
παρατείνω.example_greek: Η κυβέρνηση αποφάσισε να παρατείνει την προθεσμία για τις αιτήσεις.
παρατείνω.example_english: The government decided to extend the deadline for applications.

παρατεταμένος.definition: Stretched out over a long period of time; lasting longer than usual or expected. Can describe a duration, state, or condition that is extended or drawn out.
παρατεταμένος.example_greek: Η παρατεταμένη ξηρασία προκάλεσε σοβαρά προβλήματα στη γεωργία.
παρατεταμένος.example_english: The prolonged drought caused serious problems in agriculture.

παρατήρηση.definition: The act of observing or noticing something carefully, or a comment or remark made about something. It can refer to both the process of watching attentively and the statement made as a result.
παρατήρηση.example_greek: Η παρατήρησή του ήταν πολύ χρήσιμη για το έργο.
παρατήρηση.example_english: His observation was very useful for the project.

παρατηρώ.definition: To watch carefully or pay close attention to something; to notice or remark upon a particular detail or behavior.
παρατηρώ.example_greek: Παρατήρησα ότι ήταν πολύ ήσυχος σήμερα.
παρατηρώ.example_english: I noticed that he was very quiet today.

παραφράζω.definition: to express the meaning of a text or statement using different words, typically to clarify or simplify it.
παραφράζω.example_greek: Μπορείς να παραφράσεις αυτή την πρόταση με πιο απλά λόγια;
παραφράζω.example_english: Can you paraphrase this sentence with simpler words?

παραχώρηση.definition: The act of granting, yielding, or surrendering something, such as rights, territory, or privileges to another party. It can also refer to a commercial concession or the right granted to operate a business.
παραχώρηση.example_greek: Η κυβέρνηση συμφώνησε σε μια παραχώρηση των εδαφών στη γειτονική χώρα.
παραχώρηση.example_english: The government agreed to a cession of territories to the neighboring country.

παραχωρησιούχος.definition: A person or company that holds or operates a concession granted by an authority, typically for a specific business activity or right in exchange for fees or royalties.
παραχωρησιούχος.example_greek: Ο παραχωρησιούχος του αεροδρομίου είναι υπεύθυνος για τις καταστάσεις λιανικής.
παραχωρησιούχος.example_english: The airport concessionaire is responsible for the retail shops.

παραχωρίο.definition: A small settlement or village, typically located in a rural or remote area, smaller than a regular town or village.
παραχωρίο.example_greek: Το παραχωρίο είναι κρυμμένο στα βουνά.
παραχωρίο.example_english: The small village is hidden in the mountains.

παραχωρούμενος.definition: Describing something that is being handed over, conceded, or granted to someone else. It can refer to territory, rights, or positions being given up or transferred.
παραχωρούμενος.example_greek: Ο παραχωρούμενος χώρος θα χρησιμοποιηθεί για δημόσιες εκδηλώσεις.
παραχωρούμενος.example_english: The conceded space will be used for public events.

παρέα.definition: A group of people who spend time together socially, typically friends. It can also mean the act of spending time together in company.
παρέα.example_greek: Πήγα στο σινεμά με τη παρέα μου.
παρέα.example_english: I went to the cinema with my group of friends.

παρέκκλιση.definition: A departure from a rule, standard, or expected course of action; an exception granted from a requirement or procedure.
παρέκκλιση.example_greek: Η παρέκκλιση από τους κανονισμούς εγκρίθηκε από την επιτροπή.
παρέκκλιση.example_english: The exemption from the regulations was approved by the committee.

παρέλευση.definition: The act of passing by or moving past something or someone. It can also refer to the passage of time or a procession passing through a location.
παρέλευση.example_greek: Η παρέλευση του χρόνου είναι αναπόφευκτη.
παρέλευση.example_english: The passage of time is inevitable.

παρεμβαίνω.definition: To come between or interrupt; to take action in a situation in which one is not directly involved. It can mean to intervene in a conflict or to interfere in someone's affairs.
παρεμβαίνω.example_greek: Ο δάσκαλος παρεμβαίνει όταν τα παιδιά μαλώνουν.
παρεμβαίνω.example_english: The teacher intervenes when the children quarrel.

παρέμβαση.definition: The act of intervening or interfering in a situation, or an instance of interrupting someone or something. It can also refer to a medical or military intervention.
παρέμβαση.example_greek: Η παρέμβαση του κυβέρνησης στην οικονομία ήταν απαραίτητη.
παρέμβαση.example_english: The government's intervention in the economy was necessary.

παρευρίσκομαι.translation: to be present, to happen to be there
παρευρίσκομαι.definition: A passive or middle voice verb meaning to be present at a place or event, or to happen to find oneself somewhere at a particular time.
παρευρίσκομαι.example_greek: Παρευρίσκομαι στη συνάντηση και άκουσα όλα τα νέα.
παρευρίσκομαι.example_english: I happened to be present at the meeting and heard all the news.

παρεχώμενος.definition: Describing something that is being provided, offered, or made available. It is the present passive participle form of the verb παρέχω (to provide).
παρεχώμενος.example_greek: Οι υπηρεσίες που παρεχώμενες από την εταιρεία είναι εξαιρετικές.
παρεχώμενος.example_english: The services being provided by the company are excellent.

παρεών.definition: Describing someone or something that is present or in attendance at a particular place or event. It is the present participle form of the verb 'παρείμαι' (pareímai).
παρεών.example_greek: Οι παρεόντες στη συνέλευση ψήφισαν για το νέο σχέδιο.
παρεών.example_english: The people present at the assembly voted on the new plan.

παριανός.definition: Relating to or originating from Paros, a Greek island in the Aegean Sea. Often refers to the famous white marble quarried from the island in ancient times.
παριανός.example_greek: Το παριανό μάρμαρο ήταν πολύ ζητούμενο στην αρχαιότητα.
παριανός.example_english: Parian marble was highly sought after in antiquity.

παριστάμενος.definition: Present participle of the verb 'παρίσταμαι' meaning someone who is standing by, present, or assisting. Can also mean someone who pretends or feigns.
παριστάμενος.example_greek: Ο παριστάμενος δικηγόρος ήταν πολύ έμπειρος.
παριστάμενος.example_english: The present lawyer was very experienced.

πάροδος.definition: A narrow passage or corridor, often between buildings or through a structure. It can also refer to a passageway in an ancient Greek theater where the chorus entered.
πάροδος.example_greek: Η πάροδος μεταξύ των δύο κτηρίων είναι πολύ στενή.
πάροδος.example_english: The passage between the two buildings is very narrow.

παρότι.definition: A conjunction used to introduce a clause that contrasts with or contradicts the main clause, expressing concession or opposition.
παρότι.example_greek: Παρότι ήταν κουρασμένος, συνέχισε να δουλεύει.
παρότι.example_english: Although he was tired, he continued working.

παρουσία.definition: The state or fact of being present in a place or at an event. It can also refer to someone's physical or noticeable presence.
παρουσία.example_greek: Η παρουσία του στη συνάντηση ήταν πολύ σημαντική.
παρουσία.example_english: His presence at the meeting was very important.

παροχολογία.definition: The act of flattering or pleasing someone excessively in order to gain favor or advantage; insincere praise or compliments given for personal benefit.
παροχολογία.example_greek: Δε θέλω παροχολογία, θέλω ειλικρινή κριτική.
παροχολογία.example_english: I don't want flattery, I want honest criticism.

παρυφή.definition: The outer edge or margin of something, particularly the border or hem of a garment. It can also refer to the outer regions or periphery of a place.
παρυφή.example_greek: Η παρυφή του φορέματος ήταν διακοσμημένη με δαντέλα.
παρυφή.example_english: The edge of the dress was decorated with lace.

παρών.definition: Currently present or in attendance; existing or occurring at the present time. Can also function as a present participle meaning 'being' or 'existing'.
παρών.example_greek: Οι παρόντες μαθητές ήταν πολύ προσεκτικοί.
παρών.example_english: The students present were very attentive.

πάσης φύσεως.translation: of all kinds, of every nature
πάσης φύσεως.definition: A prepositional phrase meaning 'of every type' or 'of all sorts.' It combines the genitive form of 'all' (πάσης) with 'nature/kind' (φύσεως) to indicate comprehensive variety or diversity.
πάσης φύσεως.example_greek: Το κατάστημα πουλάει προϊόντα πάσης φύσεως.
πάσης φύσεως.example_english: The store sells products of all kinds.

πατάσσω.definition: To hit or strike someone or something with force. It can also mean to defeat or overcome in a figurative sense.
πατάσσω.example_greek: Ο παίκτης πάτησε την μπάλα με δύναμη.
πατάσσω.example_english: The player struck the ball with force.

παύω.translation: to stop, to pause, to cease
παύω.definition: To bring an action or process to an end; to discontinue or halt something. Can be used transitively (to stop something) or intransitively (to stop doing something).

πάχος.definition: The measurement of how thick something is, or the distance between two opposite surfaces. It can also refer to body fatness or corpulence.
πάχος.example_greek: Το πάχος του τοίχου είναι 30 εκατοστά.
πάχος.example_english: The thickness of the wall is 30 centimeters.

παχύτερος.definition: Comparative form of παχύς (pachýs), meaning more thick or more fat. Used to describe something with greater thickness or density, or a person who is heavier.
παχύτερος.example_greek: Αυτό το σχοινί είναι παχύτερο από εκείνο.
παχύτερος.example_english: This rope is thicker than that one.

πεδίο.definition: An open area of land, or a sphere of activity, knowledge, or influence. It can refer to a physical field or an abstract domain.
πεδίο.example_english: He works in the field of medicine.

πείθω.definition: To cause someone to believe something or to agree to do something through reasoning or argument. It can also mean to obey or to trust.
πείθω.example_greek: Ο δάσκαλος πείθει τους μαθητές να μελετήσουν περισσότερο.
πείθω.example_english: The teacher persuades the students to study more.

πειρασμός.definition: The act of trying to persuade someone to do something wrong or unwise; also a difficult situation that tests one's character or resolve.
πειρασμός.example_greek: Ο πειρασμός να φάει γλυκά είναι πολύ δυνατός.
πειρασμός.example_english: The temptation to eat sweets is very strong.

πειστικός.definition: Capable of persuading or convincing someone; having the power to make someone believe or accept something as true.
πειστικός.example_greek: Η ομιλία του ήταν πολύ πειστική και πείσε όλους τους παρευρισκομένους.
πειστικός.example_english: His speech was very convincing and persuaded everyone present.

πεπραγμένος.definition: Describing something that has been finished, accomplished, or completed. Often used to refer to deeds or actions that have been carried out.

πέρα.definition: On or to the other side of something; indicating movement or position beyond a boundary or obstacle. Can function as a preposition when followed by 'από' or as an adverb indicating direction.
πέρα.example_greek: Το σπίτι είναι πέρα από το δάσος.
πέρα.example_english: The house is beyond the forest.

περαιτέρω.definition: To a greater distance or extent; moreover or furthermore. Used to indicate continuation or progression to something additional or more distant.
περαιτέρω.example_greek: Δεν θέλω να συζητήσω περαιτέρω αυτό το θέμα.
περαιτέρω.example_english: I don't want to discuss this matter any further.

περαίωση.definition: The act or process of finishing or completing something; the state of being finished or concluded.
περαίωση.example_greek: Η περαίωση του έργου ήταν επιτυχής.
περαίωση.example_english: The completion of the project was successful.

πέραν.translation: beyond, on the other side of
πέραν.definition: On or to the further side of something; beyond a particular point or boundary. It can function as both a preposition (when followed by a noun) and an adverb (when used independently).
πέραν.example_greek: Το χωριό είναι πέραν του ποταμού.
πέραν.example_english: The village is beyond the river.

πέρασμα.definition: A way or route that allows passage from one place to another, or the act of passing through. Can also refer to a narrow waterway connecting two larger bodies of water.
πέρασμα.example_greek: Το πέρασμα από την παλιά πόλη ήταν στενό και σκοτεινό.
πέρασμα.example_english: The passage through the old city was narrow and dark.

περήφανος.definition: Having or displaying excessive pride in one's achievements or qualities; showing arrogance or haughtiness toward others.
περήφανος.example_greek: Ήταν περήφανος για την επιτυχία του και δεν άκουγε κανέναν.
περήφανος.example_english: He was proud of his success and didn't listen to anyone.

περιβόητος.definition: Well-known and talked about by many people; having widespread fame or notoriety. Often used to describe someone or something that is famous or infamous.
περιβόητος.example_greek: Ο περιβόητος σκηνοθέτης κέρδισε το βραβείο του Φεστιβάλ.
περιβόητος.example_english: The renowned director won the Festival award.

περιέρχομαι.definition: To move around or circulate through a place, or to visit multiple locations. It can also mean to go about or travel in a circular manner.
περιέρχομαι.example_greek: Περιήλθε όλα τα σπίτια της γειτονιάς για να συγκεντρώσει χρήματα.
περιέρχομαι.example_english: He went around all the houses in the neighborhood to collect money.

περιεχόμενο.definition: The material or information contained in something, such as a book, website, or document. It refers to what is included or expressed within a form or container.
περιεχόμενο.example_greek: Το περιεχόμενο του βιβλίου είναι πολύ ενδιαφέρον.
περιεχόμενο.example_english: The content of the book is very interesting.

περιζήτητος.definition: Highly wanted or in great demand; something or someone that is greatly sought by many people.
περιζήτητος.example_greek: Ο περιζήτητος ηθοποιός έδωσε μια εξαιρετική παράσταση.
περιζήτητος.example_english: The much sought-after actor gave an exceptional performance.

περίθαλψη.definition: The act of caring for or attending to someone or something with concern and thoughtfulness. It refers to devoted care and protective attention.
περίθαλψη.example_greek: Η περίθαλψη της μητέρας για τα παιδιά της ήταν αξιοσημείωτη.
περίθαλψη.example_english: The mother's care for her children was remarkable.

περιθώριο.definition: The space or area around the edge of something, or a narrow space between two things. It can also refer to a difference or advantage in a competitive situation.
περιθώριο.example_greek: Το περιθώριο του βιβλίου είναι πολύ στενό.
περιθώριο.example_english: The margin of the book is very narrow.

περίκλειστος.definition: Completely surrounded or enclosed by boundaries; shut in or confined within limits. Often used to describe areas that are isolated or cut off from external access.
περίκλειστος.example_greek: Το περίκλειστο χωριό ήταν δύσκολο να προσπελαστεί κατά τους χειμώνες.
περίκλειστος.example_english: The enclosed village was difficult to reach during the winters.

περικομμένος.definition: Describing something that has been cut or trimmed around the edges, typically referring to hair that has been neatly cut short.
περικομμένος.example_greek: Έχει περικομμένα μαλλιά που του ταιριάζουν πολύ.
περικομμένος.example_english: He has trimmed hair that suits him very well.

περιορίζομαι.translation: to be limited, to restrict oneself
περιορίζομαι.definition: A passive/middle voice verb meaning to be confined, restricted, or limited within certain boundaries or constraints. It can also mean to limit oneself voluntarily.
περιορίζομαι.example_greek: Δεν μπορώ να περιοριστώ μόνο σε αυτό το θέμα.
περιορίζομαι.example_english: I cannot limit myself to just this topic.

περιορίζω.definition: To set boundaries or constraints on something; to prevent something from expanding beyond certain limits. It can apply to physical spaces, actions, freedom, or abstract concepts.
περιορίζω.example_greek: Η κυβέρνηση περιόρισε τις ώρες λειτουργίας των καταστημάτων.
περιορίζω.example_english: The government restricted the operating hours of the shops.

περιουσία.definition: The totality of valuable possessions, money, and property that a person or entity owns. It refers to accumulated material wealth or fortune.
περιουσία.example_greek: Η περιουσία του αυξήθηκε σημαντικά μετά την επιτυχία της επιχείρησης.
περιουσία.example_english: His wealth increased significantly after the success of his business.

περιουσιακός.definition: Relating to or concerning assets, property, or possessions. Used in legal and financial contexts to describe matters pertaining to personal or organizational wealth and holdings.
περιουσιακός.example_greek: Η περιουσιακή κατάστασή του βελτιώθηκε σημαντικά.

περιποιούμαι.translation: to look after, to care for
περιποιούμαι.definition: A middle voice verb meaning to take care of, maintain, or preserve something or someone with attention and effort.
περιποιούμαι.example_greek: Περιποιούμαι τον κήπο μου με μεγάλη φροντίδα.
περιποιούμαι.example_english: I look after my garden with great care.

περιρρέουσα.definition: Present participle feminine form of περιρρέω (perirreo), meaning flowing around or surrounding something. It describes something that encircles or moves around an object.
περιρρέουσα.example_greek: Η περιρρέουσα ενέργεια του νερού δημιουργεί μια ηρεμιστική ατμόσφαιρα.
περιρρέουσα.example_english: The flowing water surrounding the area creates a calming atmosphere.

περισσεύω.translation: to abound, to have in excess
περισσεύω.definition: To exist or be present in large quantities or abundance. To have more than enough of something.
περισσεύω.example_greek: Στο τραπέζι περισσεύουν τα φρούτα και τα λαχανικά.
περισσεύω.example_english: There is an abundance of fruits and vegetables on the table.

περιφερειακός.definition: Relating to or located on the outer edge or periphery of something; also used to describe things pertaining to a region or local area rather than central areas.
περιφερειακός.example_greek: Οι περιφερειακές περιοχές χρειάζονται περισσότερη ανάπτυξη.

περιφερειάρχης.definition: An administrative official who governs a periphery (administrative region) in Greece. This is a high-ranking position responsible for managing regional affairs and coordinating local government functions.
περιφερειάρχης.example_greek: Ο περιφερειάρχης της Αττικής συγκάλεσε σύσκεψη με τους δημάρχους.
περιφερειάρχης.example_english: The prefect of Attica called a meeting with the mayors.

περίφημα.definition: Plural form referring to famous or renowned matters, deeds, or things that are well-known and celebrated. It can also mean 'gossip' or 'talk about someone' in modern usage.
περίφημα.example_greek: Τα περίφημα του πολιτικού γύρισαν σε όλη την πόλη.
περίφημα.example_english: The rumors about the politician spread throughout the entire city.

περσινός.definition: Referring to something that happened or existed during the previous year; from or belonging to the year before the current one.
περσινός.example_greek: Η περσινή συγκομιδή ήταν πολύ καλή.
περσινός.example_english: Last year's harvest was very good.

πεσσός.definition: A medical device inserted into the vagina to provide contraception or support pelvic structures. In ancient Greece, it also referred to a stone or pebble used in games.
πεσσός.example_greek: Η γυναίκα χρησιμοποίησε έναν πεσσό ως μέσο αντισύλληψης.
πεσσός.example_english: The woman used a pessary as a method of contraception.

πέσω.definition: to move downward rapidly or involuntarily due to gravity, or to drop from a standing or elevated position to the ground.
πέσω.example_greek: Το παιδί έπεσε από το ποδήλατο.
πέσω.example_english: The child fell off the bicycle.

πεταχτός.definition: Describing something that has been thrown or flung forcefully. Often used to describe objects that have been cast aside or thrown away.
πεταχτός.example_greek: Το πεταχτό καπέλο ήταν στο δρόμο.
πεταχτός.example_english: The thrown hat was on the road.

πετάω.definition: To move through the air with wings, or to throw something forcefully through the air.
πετάω.example_greek: Τα πουλιά πετάνε ψηλά στον ουρανό.
πετάω.example_english: The birds fly high in the sky.

πετυχαίνω.definition: To accomplish or achieve a goal successfully, or to manage to do something. It can also mean to happen to meet someone or to catch someone.
πετυχαίνω.example_greek: Τελικά πέτυχε να περάσει τις εξετάσεις.
πετυχαίνω.example_english: He finally managed to pass the exams.

πετυχημένος.definition: Describing someone or something that has achieved success or favorable results. It indicates that goals have been accomplished or outcomes have been positive.
πετυχημένος.example_greek: Ο πετυχημένος επιχειρηματίας έχει αποκτήσει μεγάλο πλούτο.
πετυχημένος.example_english: The successful businessman has acquired great wealth.

πευτικός.definition: Relating to or promoting digestion; affecting the digestive system or process of breaking down food.
πευτικός.example_greek: Τα πευτικά προβλήματα είναι πολύ συχνά στη σημερινή εποχή.
πευτικός.example_english: Digestive problems are very common in today's times.

πηγάζω.definition: To flow or gush out from a source, especially referring to water from a spring. Figuratively, it means to originate or stem from something.
πηγάζω.example_greek: Το νερό πηγάζει από το βουνό.
πηγάζω.example_english: The water springs forth from the mountain.

πηγαινοέρχομαι.definition: A reflexive verb meaning to go and come repeatedly between two places, or to commute regularly. It combines the concepts of going (πηγαίνω) and coming (έρχομαι).
πηγαινοέρχομαι.example_greek: Πηγαινοέρχομαι στη δουλειά κάθε μέρα με το αυτοκίνητο.
πηγαινοέρχομαι.example_english: I commute to work every day by car.

πηλός.definition: A soft, wet earth material composed of fine particles of rock and organic matter. It can refer to either mud or clay used in pottery and construction.
πηλός.example_greek: Το παιδί έπαιζε με πηλό στο πάρκο.
πηλός.example_english: The child was playing with mud in the park.

πήχη.definition: The part of the arm between the elbow and the wrist. In ancient times, it was also a unit of measurement roughly equal to 45 cm.
πήχη.example_greek: Έσπασε τη πήχη του όταν έπεσε από τη σκάλα.
πήχη.example_english: He broke his forearm when he fell from the ladder.

πίεση.definition: The force exerted per unit area on a surface, or the state of being pushed or squeezed. It can also refer to psychological stress or tension.
πίεση.example_greek: Η πίεση του αέρα είναι πολύ υψηλή σήμερα.
πίεση.example_english: The air pressure is very high today.

πιεστήριο.definition: A machine or apparatus used to apply pressure or force, commonly referring to a printing press or oil press used in industrial or agricultural contexts.
πιεστήριο.example_greek: Το πιεστήριο λαδιού είναι ένα σημαντικό μηχάνημα στην Ελλάδα.
πιεστήριο.example_english: The oil press is an important machine in Greece.

πιηούνι.definition: A colloquial or informal term referring to the buttocks or rear end of a person's body.
πιηούνι.example_greek: Το παιδί έπεσε και χτύπησε το πιηούνι του.
πιηούνι.example_english: The child fell and hit their backside.

πιθανή.definition: Having a reasonable chance of happening or being true; probable or plausible.

πιθανός.definition: Having a reasonable chance of being true or happening; seeming probable or believable based on available evidence.
πιθανός.example_greek: Είναι πιθανός ότι θα βρέξει αύριο.
πιθανός.example_english: It is likely that it will rain tomorrow.

πιθανότητα.definition: The quality or state of being probable or likely to occur; the extent to which something is likely to happen or be true.
πιθανότητα.example_greek: Η πιθανότητα να βρέξει αύριο είναι πολύ υψηλή.
πιθανότητα.example_english: The probability that it will rain tomorrow is very high.

πιθανώς.definition: An adverb indicating that something is probable or plausible; expressing likelihood or possibility rather than certainty.
πιθανώς.example_greek: Πιθανώς θα έρθει αύριο στη συνάντηση.
πιθανώς.example_english: He will probably come to the meeting tomorrow.

πικρόχολα.definition: A state of deep bitterness, resentment, or malice. It refers to a bitter and spiteful attitude or feeling toward someone or something.
πικρόχολα.example_greek: Μίλησε με πικρόχολα για τον παλιό του φίλο.
πικρόχολα.example_english: He spoke with bitterness about his old friend.

πίστη.definition: A strong belief or confidence in someone or something, or a system of religious beliefs. It can refer to trust in a person, faith in an idea, or religious faith.
πίστη.example_english: I have faith in your abilities.

πιστός.definition: Reliable and deserving of trust; remaining loyal and devoted to someone or something.
πιστός.example_greek: Ο Γιάννης είναι ένας πιστός φίλος.

πιστωτικός.definition: Relating to or involving credit, especially in financial or commercial contexts. Describes something that pertains to the extension of credit or borrowing arrangements.
πιστωτικός.example_greek: Η πιστωτική κάρτα είναι ένα χρήσιμο εργαλείο για τις αγορές.
πιστωτικός.example_english: The credit card is a useful tool for shopping.

πλαίσιο.definition: A structure that surrounds or contains something, or the general context within which something exists or operates.
πλαίσιο.example_greek: Το πλαίσιο του παραθύρου είναι ξύλινο.
πλαίσιο.example_english: The window frame is made of wood.

πλάνη.definition: A state of being deceived or misled; false belief or misconception. Can also mean the act of wandering or straying from the correct path.
πλάνη.example_greek: Η πλάνη του ότι θα πλούταινε εύκολα τον κόστισε πολλά.
πλάνη.example_english: His delusion that he would become rich easily cost him dearly.

πλανώμαι.translation: to wander, to roam, to be mistaken
πλανώμαι.definition: A passive or middle voice verb meaning to walk aimlessly or to travel without a specific destination. It can also mean to be confused or to make a mistake.
πλανώμαι.example_greek: Πλανιόμαστε στα βουνά όλη την ημέρα.
πλανώμαι.example_english: We wandered in the mountains all day long.

πλατφόρμα.definition: A raised, flat surface or structure; also used for digital platforms or social media services.
πλατφόρμα.example_greek: Η πλατφόρμα του σταθμού είναι πολύ πλατιά.
πλατφόρμα.example_english: The platform of the station is very wide.

πλειοψηφία.definition: The greater number or portion of something, especially the number of votes greater than half of the total votes cast in an election or assembly.
πλειοψηφία.example_greek: Η πλειοψηφία των ψηφοφόρων ψήφισε υπέρ της νέας πολιτικής.
πλειοψηφία.example_english: The majority of voters voted in favor of the new policy.

πλειοψηφικός.definition: Relating to or constituting a majority; decided by or resulting from a majority of votes or support.
πλειοψηφικός.example_greek: Η απόφαση περάστηκε με πλειοψηφική ψήφο στη βουλή.
πλειοψηφικός.example_english: The decision was passed by a majority vote in parliament.

πλεονέκτημα.definition: A favorable circumstance or condition that gives one a superior position or better outcome compared to others. A benefit or gain obtained from something.
πλεονέκτημα.example_greek: Το μεγάλο μέγεθος της εταιρείας είναι ένα σημαντικό πλεονέκτημα.
πλεονέκτημα.example_english: The large size of the company is a significant advantage.

πλευρικός.definition: Relating to or located on the side of something, particularly used in anatomical contexts to describe structures situated on the sides of the body.
πλευρικός.example_greek: Ο πλευρικός πόνος ήταν έντονος μετά το ατύχημα.
πλευρικός.example_english: The lateral pain was intense after the accident.

πληγή.definition: A physical injury to the body caused by violence or accident, or a blow delivered to someone. It can also refer to emotional harm or suffering.
πληγή.example_greek: Ο ασθενής είχε μια σοβαρή πληγή στο πόδι του.
πληγή.example_english: The patient had a serious wound on his leg.

πλήγμα.definition: A forceful hit or impact with a physical object, or the injury resulting from such a strike. It can also refer to a misfortune or calamity.
πλήγμα.example_greek: Δέχτηκε ένα δυνατό πλήγμα στο κεφάλι.
πλήγμα.example_english: He received a strong blow to the head.

πλήθος.definition: A large number of people or things gathered together; a multitude or mass of something.
πλήθος.example_greek: Ένα πλήθος ανθρώπων συγκεντρώθηκε στην πλατεία.
πλήθος.example_english: A crowd of people gathered in the square.

πληθυσμιακός.definition: Relating to or concerning a population or the study of population statistics. Used to describe data, trends, or characteristics associated with demographic analysis.
πληθυσμιακός.example_greek: Η πληθυσμιακή αύξηση είναι ένα σημαντικό ζήτημα για πολλές χώρες.
πληθυσμιακός.example_english: Population growth is an important issue for many countries.

πλημμυρικός.definition: Relating to or caused by floods; characteristic of or pertaining to an overflow of water that inundates land.
πλημμυρικός.example_greek: Η πλημμυρική καταστροφή προκάλεσε μεγάλες ζημιές στο χωριό.
πλημμυρικός.example_english: The flood disaster caused major damage to the village.

πληρέστερος.definition: Comparative form of πλήρης (plíris), meaning more full, more complete, or more comprehensive in quantity or content.
πληρέστερος.example_greek: Αυτή η έκδοση είναι πληρέστερη από την προηγούμενη.
πληρέστερος.example_english: This edition is fuller than the previous one.

πλήρης.definition: Containing or holding as much as possible; having no empty space. Also means complete or whole in every part.
πλήρης.example_english: The glass is full of water.

πλήρως.definition: In a complete or thorough manner; to the full extent or degree. Used to indicate that something is done without reservation or incompleteness.
πλήρως.example_english: I completely understood what you mean.

πλήρωση.definition: The act of paying money owed or the amount of money paid to settle a debt or complete a transaction. It can also refer to fulfillment or completion of a task.
πλήρωση.example_greek: Η πλήρωση του λογαριασμού γίνεται κάθε μήνα.
πλήρωση.example_english: The payment of the bill is made every month.

πλήττω.definition: To hit or strike someone or something physically, or to affect someone emotionally or mentally. It can also mean to plague or afflict.
πλήττω.example_greek: Η ασθένεια πλήττει πολλούς ανθρώπους κάθε χρόνο.
πλήττω.example_english: The disease strikes many people every year.

πνευματικός.definition: Relating to the spirit or mind rather than physical matter; also can mean pertaining to air or pneumatic systems. In religious contexts, it refers to spiritual or divine matters.
πνευματικός.example_greek: Η πνευματική ζωή είναι σημαντική για τον άνθρωπο.
πνευματικός.example_english: Spiritual life is important for humans.

πνίγω.translation: to choke, to strangle, to suffocate
πνίγω.definition: To deprive someone of air or oxygen, causing difficulty in breathing. Can be used literally (physical strangulation) or figuratively (emotional distress).
πνίγω.example_greek: Το καπνό με πνίγει στο δωμάτιο.
πνίγω.example_english: The smoke is choking me in the room.

πογκρόμ.definition: A planned series of activities, events, or broadcasts arranged in a specific order. A schedule or agenda for entertainment or organizational purposes.
πογκρόμ.example_greek: Το πογκρόμ της συναυλίας είναι πολύ ενδιαφέρον.
πογκρόμ.example_english: The program of the concert is very interesting.

ποθώ.definition: To have a strong desire or yearning for something or someone that is absent or unattainable. It expresses a deep emotional longing or craving.
ποθώ.example_greek: Ποθώ να είμαι πάλι στην Ελλάδα.
ποθώ.example_english: I long to be in Greece again.

ποινή.definition: A penalty or consequence imposed for wrongdoing, or a fine imposed by law. It can refer to any form of punishment, whether legal, disciplinary, or social.
ποινή.example_greek: Ο κατάδικος δέχτηκε μια σκληρή ποινή για το έγκλημά του.
ποινή.example_english: The convicted person received a harsh punishment for his crime.

ποινική.definition: Relating to or involving punishment, crime, or the criminal justice system. Used to describe laws, courts, or proceedings concerned with criminal matters.
ποινική.example_greek: Η ποινική δικογραφία είναι πολύ σημαντική για την ασφάλεια της κοινωνίας.
ποινική.example_english: Criminal law is very important for society's safety.

πολεοδομικός.definition: Relating to the planning, design, and development of cities and urban areas. Describes policies, projects, or regulations concerned with organizing urban space.
πολεοδομικός.example_greek: Το πολεοδομικό σχέδιο της πόλης περιλαμβάνει νέα πάρκα και δρόμους.
πολεοδομικός.example_english: The town planning scheme of the city includes new parks and roads.

πολίτευμα.definition: The system of government or constitutional structure of a state. It refers to the organized form of political authority and the principles by which a state is governed.
πολίτευμα.example_greek: Το πολίτευμα της χώρας είναι δημοκρατικό.
πολίτευμα.example_english: The country's form of government is democratic.

πολιτισμένος.definition: Describing someone who is well-educated, refined in manner, and demonstrates cultural sophistication. It can also refer to a society that has reached an advanced level of cultural and social development.
πολιτισμένος.example_greek: Είναι ένας πολιτισμένος άνθρωπος που αγαπά την τέχνη και τη λογοτεχνία.
πολιτισμένος.example_english: He is a cultured man who loves art and literature.

πολιτισμικός.definition: Relating to or characteristic of the culture, customs, and intellectual achievements of a society or civilization. It describes anything connected to cultural activities, values, and heritage.
πολιτισμικός.example_greek: Το μουσείο διοργανώνει πολιτισμικές εκδηλώσεις κάθε μήνα.
πολιτισμικός.example_english: The museum organizes cultural events every month.

πολιτιστικός.definition: Relating to or characteristic of the culture, arts, customs, and intellectual achievements of a society or group. Describes things associated with civilization and cultural development.
πολιτιστικός.example_greek: Το μουσείο διοργανώνει πολιτιστικές εκδηλώσεις κάθε μήνα.
πολιτιστικός.example_english: The museum organizes cultural events every month.

πολλαπλασιαστικός.definition: Relating to or denoting multiplication; involving the process of multiplying numbers or quantities.
πολλαπλασιαστικός.example_greek: Η πολλαπλασιαστική ιδιότητα είναι σημαντική στα μαθηματικά.
πολλαπλασιαστικός.example_english: The multiplicative property is important in mathematics.

πολλοίς.definition: Dative plural form of πολύς (polús), meaning 'many' or 'much'. Used to describe a large quantity of things or people in the dative case.
πολλοίς.example_greek: Έδωσε δώρα σε πολλούς φίλους του.
πολλοίς.example_english: He gave gifts to many of his friends.

πόλος.definition: Either of the two points on a sphere (such as Earth or a magnet) where the axis of rotation intersects the surface. Also refers to an extreme point or end of something.
πόλος.example_greek: Ο Βόρειος Πόλος είναι το πιο κρύο μέρος της Γης.
πόλος.example_english: The North Pole is the coldest place on Earth.

πολυδιάστατος.definition: Having many dimensions or aspects; complex and involving multiple perspectives or layers. Used to describe something that cannot be understood or addressed from a single viewpoint.
πολυδιάστατος.example_greek: Η κρίση είναι ένα πολυδιάστατο πρόβλημα που απαιτεί συνδυασμένες λύσεις.
πολυδιάστατος.example_english: The crisis is a multidimensional problem that requires combined solutions.

πολύπλευρος.definition: Having many sides or angles; involving multiple parties or perspectives. Used in geometry to describe polygons and figuratively to describe complex situations with multiple dimensions.
πολύπλευρος.example_greek: Η συζήτηση ήταν πολύπλευρη και κάλυψε πολλά θέματα.
πολύπλευρος.example_english: The discussion was multifaceted and covered many topics.

πολύπλοκος.definition: Difficult to understand or deal with because of having many interconnected parts or elements. Characterized by complexity and intricacy.
πολύπλοκος.example_greek: Η λύση του προβλήματος ήταν πολύπλοκη και απαιτούσε πολύ χρόνο.
πολύπλοκος.example_english: The solution to the problem was complex and required a lot of time.

πολυπόθητος.definition: An adjective describing something that is intensely wanted or eagerly anticipated by many people. It conveys a strong sense of desire and anticipation.
πολυπόθητος.example_greek: Η πολυπόθητη ημέρα της διακοπής έφτασε επιτέλους.
πολυπόθητος.example_english: The long-awaited day of the vacation finally arrived.

πολύπτυχο.definition: A document, booklet, or leaflet with multiple pages or folds, often used for informational or promotional purposes.
πολύπτυχο.example_greek: Η τουριστική υπηρεσία μας έδωσε ένα πολύπτυχο με πληροφορίες για τα αξιοθέατα.
πολύπτυχο.example_english: The tourism service gave us a pamphlet with information about the sights.

πολυτάρχη.definition: A person who rules over multiple territories or domains; a ruler with extensive power over many regions or subjects.
πολυτάρχη.example_greek: Ο πολυτάρχη διοικούσε πολλές επαρχίες με αυθεντικότητα.
πολυτάρχη.example_english: The polyarch governed many provinces with authority.

πολυτέλεια.definition: The state of living luxuriously with expensive and unnecessary items; a costly or extravagant way of life.
πολυτέλεια.example_greek: Η πολυτέλεια δεν είναι απαραίτητη για την ευτυχία.
πολυτέλεια.example_english: Luxury is not necessary for happiness.

πολυτραυματίας.translation: severely injured person, multiple trauma victim
πολυτραυματίας.definition: A person who has sustained multiple serious injuries, typically used in medical contexts to describe a patient with injuries to multiple body systems or regions.
πολυτραυματίας.example_greek: Ο πολυτραυματίας μεταφέρθηκε στο νοσοκομείο με ασθενοφόρο.
πολυτραυματίας.example_english: The severely injured patient was transported to the hospital by ambulance.

πονηρός.definition: Describing someone or something that is morally bad, wicked, or crafty and deceitful. Can also mean mischievous or troublesome.
πονηρός.example_greek: Αυτός είναι ένας πονηρός άνθρωπος που δεν μπορείς να εμπιστευθείς.
πονηρός.example_english: He is a wicked person whom you cannot trust.

ποντάρω.translation: to bet, to wager, to stake
ποντάρω.definition: To place a bet or wager money on the outcome of something, typically a game or sporting event. It can also mean to risk or stake something of value.
ποντάρω.example_greek: Ποντάρω 10 ευρώ στο ματς του ποδοσφαίρου.
ποντάρω.example_english: I'm betting 10 euros on the football match.

πόρος.definition: A tiny opening in a surface, or a way/means of achieving something. In ancient Greek, it also referred to a strait or passage between bodies of water.
πόρος.example_greek: Η ιδρώτας διέρχεται από τους πόρους του δέρματος.
πόρος.example_english: Sweat passes through the pores of the skin.

πόρός.definition: A small opening or hole in a surface, or a way/means of achieving something. In ancient contexts, it can also refer to a strait or passage between two bodies of water.
πόρός.example_greek: Τα πόρια του δέρματος επιτρέπουν την αναπνοή.
πόρός.example_english: The pores of the skin allow it to breathe.

ποσοστό.definition: A proportion or share expressed as a part of a whole, typically represented as a number out of 100. It indicates what fraction or amount something represents relative to the total.
ποσοστό.example_greek: Το ποσοστό των φοιτητών που έδωσαν τις εξετάσεις ήταν 85%.
ποσοστό.example_english: The percentage of students who took the exams was 85%.

πραγματοποίηση.definition: The act of making something real or actual; the process of turning plans or ideas into concrete reality through execution and completion.
πραγματοποίηση.example_greek: Η πραγματοποίηση του προγράμματος ήταν πολύ επιτυχημένη.
πραγματοποίηση.example_english: The implementation of the program was very successful.

πρακτορείο.definition: A business office or establishment that provides services such as ticketing, travel arrangements, or representation for a company. It can refer to a travel agency, ticket office, or similar service provider.
πρακτορείο.example_greek: Πήγα στο πρακτορείο του ταξιδιού για να κάνω κράτηση.
πρακτορείο.example_english: I went to the travel agency to make a reservation.

πράξη.definition: Something done or performed; a deed or action, especially one that is notable or significant. In philosophy, it refers to an act or practice.
πράξη.example_greek: Η πράξη του ήταν πολύ γενναία.

πρασινίζω.translation: to turn green, to become green
