πρασινίζω.definition: To become or make something green in color, or to adopt green/environmental principles. It can also mean to become verdant or lush with vegetation.
πρασινίζω.example_english: The trees turn green in spring.

προαναγγέλλω.definition: A verb meaning to announce, declare, or make known in advance; to predict or foretell something before it happens.
προαναγγέλλω.example_greek: Ο προφήτης προαναγγέλλει τα μελλοντικά γεγονότα.

προαναφερθείς.definition: An adjective describing something that has been mentioned or referred to before in a text or conversation. It is a formal, literary term commonly used in legal and official documents.
προαναφερθείς.example_greek: Ο προαναφερθείς κανόνας ισχύει για όλες τις περιπτώσεις.
προαναφερθείς.example_english: The aforementioned rule applies to all cases.

προανεφερώ.translation: to mention beforehand, to refer previously
προανεφερώ.definition: A verb meaning to mention, reference, or allude to something that was stated or discussed earlier. It combines the prefix 'προ-' (before) with 'ανεφερώ' (to refer/mention).
προανεφερώ.example_greek: Όπως προανέφερα στην προηγούμενη συνάντηση, πρέπει να αλλάξουμε τη στρατηγική.
προανεφερώ.example_english: As I mentioned before in the previous meeting, we need to change our strategy.

προαπαιτούμενος.definition: Something that is required or necessary as a condition before something else can happen or be obtained. A prerequisite or prior requirement.
προαπαιτούμενος.example_greek: Η γνώση των μαθηματικών είναι προαπαιτούμενη για τη φυσική.
προαπαιτούμενος.example_english: Knowledge of mathematics is a prerequisite for physics.

προασπιακός.translation: pre-Aspis, relating to before the Aspis
προασπιακός.definition: An adjective describing something that existed or occurred before the Aspis period or organization, or relating to the historical period preceding it. This is a specialized historical or political term.
προασπιακός.example_greek: Η προασπιακή περίοδος της ιστορίας ήταν σημαντική για την ανάπτυξη της πολιτείας.
προασπιακός.example_english: The pre-Aspis period of history was important for the development of the city-state.

προαστικός σιδηρόδρομος.definition: A railway system that connects a city center with its surrounding suburban areas, typically used for commuter transportation. It usually operates with frequent stops and shorter distances compared to long-distance trains.
προαστικός σιδηρόδρομος.example_greek: Παίρνω τον προαστικό σιδηρόδρομο για να πάω στη δουλειά μου κάθε πρωί.
προαστικός σιδηρόδρομος.example_english: I take the suburban railway to go to work every morning.

προβάδισμα.definition: A favorable position or advantage gained in advance, particularly in competition or conflict. It refers to getting ahead of others or having an edge.
προβάδισμα.example_greek: Ο ποδοσφαιρικός σύλλογος είχε ένα προβάδισμα δύο γκόλ στο δεύτερο ημίχρονο.
προβάδισμα.example_english: The football club had a two-goal advantage in the second half.

πρόβλεψη.definition: The act of predicting or anticipating something in the future, or a measure taken beforehand to prepare for something. It can also mean a clause or stipulation in a document.
πρόβλεψη.example_greek: Η πρόβλεψη του καιρού λέει ότι θα βρέξει αύριο.
πρόβλεψη.example_english: The weather forecast says it will rain tomorrow.

προβληματισμός.definition: A state of worry, concern, or uncertainty about something. It refers to the feeling of being troubled or having doubts about a situation or matter.
προβληματισμός.example_greek: Ο προβληματισμός του για το μέλλον ήταν εμφανής.
προβληματισμός.example_english: His concern about the future was evident.

προβλήτα.definition: A structure extending into water, used for mooring boats and ships or for loading and unloading cargo. It is a common feature in ports and harbors.
προβλήτα.example_greek: Το καράβι είναι δεμένο στην προβλήτα.
προβλήτα.example_english: The ship is moored at the pier.

προβολέας.definition: A device that projects light, images, or films onto a surface. It can refer to a cinema projector, a spotlight used in theaters or outdoor events, or a searchlight.
προβολέας.example_greek: Ο προβολέας του σινεμά έδειξε την ταινία στη μεγάλη οθόνη.
προβολέας.example_english: The cinema projector showed the film on the big screen.

προεδοποιώ.translation: to prepare the way, to pave the way
προεδοποιώ.definition: To prepare conditions or circumstances in advance; to make preliminary arrangements or create favorable conditions for something that will follow.
προεδοποιώ.example_greek: Η καλή εκπαίδευση προεδοποιεί την επιτυχία στη ζωή.
προεδοποιώ.example_english: Good education paves the way for success in life.

προεδρία.definition: The office, position, or term of a president or chair of an organization, institution, or country. It can also refer to the body or office that holds this position.
προεδρία.example_greek: Κατά τη διάρκεια της προεδρίας του, έγιναν πολλές σημαντικές αλλαγές.
προεδρία.example_english: During his presidency, many important changes were made.

προεκλογικός.definition: Relating to or occurring before an election. Used to describe events, campaigns, or periods that precede an electoral process.
προεκλογικός.example_greek: Η προεκλογική περίοδος είναι πολύ σημαντική για τα πολιτικά κόμματα.
προεκλογικός.example_english: The pre-election period is very important for political parties.

προέλαση.definition: The act of moving forward, advancing in position or rank, or making progress in a field or career. It can refer to military advancement or professional advancement.
προέλαση.example_greek: Η προέλαση του στρατού ήταν γρήγορη και αποτελεσματική.
προέλαση.example_english: The army's advance was quick and effective.

προέλευση.definition: The place, source, or point from which something comes or originates. It refers to the beginning or derivation of something.
προέλευση.example_greek: Η προέλευση αυτού του προϊόντος είναι ιταλική.
προέλευση.example_english: The origin of this product is Italian.

προέρχομαι.definition: To come or originate from a particular source, place, or person. It can also mean to proceed or advance forward.
προέρχομαι.example_greek: Αυτή η παράδοση προέρχεται από την αρχαία Ελλάδα.
προέρχομαι.example_english: This tradition comes from ancient Greece.

προηγηθεί.definition: Third person singular future tense of προηγούμαι (proigúme), meaning to go ahead of something or someone, or to precede in time or order.
προηγηθεί.example_greek: Ο δάσκαλος θα προηγηθεί των μαθητών στο μουσείο.
προηγηθεί.example_english: The teacher will go ahead of the students in the museum.

προηγούμαι.definition: To come or go before someone or something in time, order, or position. It can mean to lead the way or to happen earlier than something else.
προηγούμαι.example_greek: Ο επικεφαλής της αποστολής προηγείται των άλλων.
προηγούμαι.example_english: The head of the mission goes before the others.

πρόθεση.definition: A word that shows the relationship between a noun/pronoun and other words in a sentence (grammatical meaning), or a plan or intention to do something (general meaning).
πρόθεση.example_greek: Η πρόθεσή του ήταν να μελετήσει σκληρά για τις εξετάσεις.
πρόθεση.example_english: His intention was to study hard for the exams.

προθεσμία.definition: A specified period of time by which something must be completed or a decision must be made. It refers to the final date or moment before which an action must be taken.
προθεσμία.example_greek: Η προθεσμία για την υποβολή των εγγράφων είναι την επόμενη Παρασκευή.
προθεσμία.example_english: The deadline for submitting the documents is next Friday.

πρόθυμος.definition: Readily willing or enthusiastic to do something; showing willingness and eagerness to act or help.
πρόθυμος.example_greek: Ήμουν πρόθυμος να σε βοηθήσω με το έργο.
πρόθυμος.example_english: I was willing to help you with the project.

προϊδεάζω.translation: to warn in advance, to forewarn
προϊδεάζω.definition: To inform someone beforehand about something that may happen, typically as a caution or preparation. It means to give advance notice or warning.
προϊδεάζω.example_greek: Του προϊδεάζω ότι θα έχει δύσκολη εξέταση.
προϊδεάζω.example_english: I'm warning him in advance that he will have a difficult exam.

προίκα.definition: Money or property that a bride brings to her husband at marriage. Historically, a financial gift given by a family when their daughter marries.
προίκα.example_greek: Η προίκα της νύφης ήταν πολύ μεγάλη.
προίκα.example_english: The bride's dowry was very large.

προικοδοτημένος.definition: Describing something that has been provided with or equipped with certain features, qualities, or resources. It can refer to someone or something naturally endowed with particular traits or artificially equipped with necessary items.
προικοδοτημένος.example_greek: Το όχημα είναι προικοδοτημένο με σύγχρονες τεχνολογίες.
προικοδοτημένος.example_english: The vehicle is equipped with modern technologies.

προκαλώ.translation: to provoke, to challenge, to cause
προκαλώ.definition: To deliberately anger or irritate someone; to challenge someone to do something; or to cause something to happen as a result.
προκαλώ.example_greek: Μην με προκαλείς με τα σχόλιά σου.
προκαλώ.example_english: Don't provoke me with your comments.

προκατάληψη.definition: A preconceived opinion or judgment formed before having adequate knowledge or experience. An unfair attitude based on assumptions rather than facts.
προκατάληψη.example_greek: Η προκατάληψη του κατά των μεταναστών είναι ένα σοβαρό πρόβλημα.
προκατάληψη.example_english: Prejudice against immigrants is a serious problem.

προκείμενος.translation: lying ahead, in question, at hand
προκείμενος.definition: Positioned or located ahead; being the subject under discussion or consideration. Often used in formal or legal contexts to refer to something that is the focus of attention or decision.
προκείμενος.example_greek: Στην προκείμενη περίπτωση, πρέπει να ληφθούν σοβαρές αποφάσεις.
προκείμενος.example_english: In the case at hand, serious decisions must be taken.

προκειμένου.translation: in order to, so that, for the purpose of
προκειμένου.definition: A conjunction used to introduce a clause indicating purpose or intention. It expresses the goal or objective of an action.
προκειμένου.example_greek: Σπούδασε σκληρά προκειμένου να περάσει τις εξετάσεις.
προκειμένου.example_english: He studied hard in order to pass the exams.

προκήρυξη.definition: A formal public announcement or declaration, typically made by an official authority. It can refer to the act of proclaiming something publicly or the document containing such a proclamation.
προκήρυξη.example_greek: Η προκήρυξη των εκλογών έγινε από το κυβερνητικό γραφείο.
προκήρυξη.example_english: The proclamation of the elections was made by the government office.

προκηρύσσω.translation: to announce in advance, to proclaim
προκηρύσσω.definition: To announce or declare something publicly beforehand; to make a prior announcement or proclamation about an event or decision.
προκηρύσσω.example_greek: Η κυβέρνηση προκήρυξε τις εκλογές για το Μάιο.
προκηρύσσω.example_english: The government announced the elections in advance for May.

προκύπτει.definition: Third person singular present tense of προκύπτω. Means to emerge, come to light, or result from something as a logical consequence.
προκύπτει.example_greek: Από τα δεδομένα προκύπτει ότι υπάρχει πρόβλημα.
προκύπτει.example_english: From the data it emerges that there is a problem.

προκύπτω.translation: to emerge, to arise, to result
προκύπτω.definition: To come into view or existence; to become apparent or evident as a result of something. Often used to indicate that something emerges from a situation or analysis.
προκύπτω.example_greek: Από την έρευνα προκύπτει ότι υπάρχει πρόβλημα.
προκύπτω.example_english: From the research, it emerges that there is a problem.

προλαβαίνω.translation: to forestall, to anticipate, to get ahead
προλαβαίνω.definition: To do something before someone else or before a certain time; to anticipate or prevent something by acting first. It can also mean to have time or opportunity to do something.
προλαβαίνω.example_greek: Προλαβαίνω να τελειώσω το έργο πριν φύγω.
προλαβαίνω.example_english: I manage to finish the work before I leave.

προμετωπίδα.definition: The front brim or peak of a cap, hat, or helmet that projects over the eyes to provide shade or protection.
προμετωπίδα.example_greek: Φόρεσε ένα καπέλο με μια μεγάλη προμετωπίδα για να προστατευθεί από τον ήλιο.
προμετωπίδα.example_english: He wore a cap with a large visor to protect himself from the sun.

προμήθεια.definition: The quality of being able to foresee and make provision for future needs; careful planning and preparation in advance. It can also refer to provisions or supplies arranged beforehand.
προμήθεια.example_greek: Με προμήθεια και σχεδιασμό, κατάφεραν να ολοκληρώσουν το έργο εγκαίρως.
προμήθεια.example_english: With foresight and planning, they managed to complete the project on time.

προμηθεύω.translation: to supply, to provide, to furnish
προμηθεύω.definition: To supply or provide someone with something they need. It can also mean to prepare or make arrangements for something in advance.
προμηθεύω.example_greek: Η εταιρεία προμηθεύει τα σχολεία με βιβλία και υλικά.
προμηθεύω.example_english: The company supplies schools with books and materials.

προνομία.definition: A special right, advantage, or privilege granted to a person or group. It refers to an exclusive benefit or immunity that sets someone apart from others.
προνομία.example_greek: Τα παιδιά των διπλωματών έχουν ορισμένες προνομίες.
προνομία.example_english: The children of diplomats have certain privileges.

προνομιούχος.definition: A person who holds or enjoys special rights, advantages, or privileges that are not available to others. It can also refer to someone who is favored or given preferential treatment.
προνομιούχος.example_greek: Οι προνομιούχοι της κοινωνίας έχουν περισσότερα πλεονεκτήματα από τους άλλους.
προνομιούχος.example_english: The privileged people in society have more advantages than others.

προοδευτικός.definition: Characterized by progress, reform, or innovation; moving forward or advancing in a positive direction. Can describe ideas, policies, or people with modern or liberal views.
προοδευτικός.example_greek: Η προοδευτική κυβέρνηση εφάρμοσε νέες πολιτικές για την εκπαίδευση.
προοδευτικός.example_english: The progressive government implemented new policies for education.

πρόοφατα.definition: In the recent past; not long before the present time. Indicates something that happened shortly before now.
πρόοφατα.example_english: I bought a new car recently.

πρόπερσι.definition: An adverb indicating the year that came two years before the present year. It refers to a time period that is more distant than 'persi' (last year).
πρόπερσι.example_greek: Πρόπερσι ταξίδεψα στην Ελλάδα και ήταν υπέροχο.
πρόπερσι.example_english: The year before last I traveled to Greece and it was wonderful.

προσανατολισμός.definition: The action or process of orienting or aligning something in a particular direction. It can also refer to a person's tendency, inclination, or position toward a particular ideology, group, or goal.
προσανατολισμός.example_greek: Ο προσανατολισμός του στη πολιτική είναι σαφής.

προσάπτω.translation: to attach, to affix, to accuse
προσάπτω.definition: To attach or fasten something to another object, or to direct blame or an accusation toward someone. It can mean both a physical attachment and an abstract attribution of responsibility.
προσάπτω.example_greek: Του προσάπτουν ότι έκλεψε τα χρήματα.
προσάπτω.example_english: They accuse him of stealing the money.

προσατιακός.definition: Pertaining to begging, alms-giving, or charitable donations. This is a rare or archaic term derived from 'προσάτης' (beggar).
προσατιακός.example_greek: Η προσατιακή δραστηριότητα ήταν κοινή στο Βυζάντιο.
προσατιακός.example_english: Begging activity was common in Byzantine times.

πρόσβαση.definition: The ability or right to enter, reach, or use something. It can refer to physical access to a place or digital access to information.
πρόσβαση.example_english: Do you have access to the internet?

προσβεύω.definition: To treat someone with disrespect or rudeness; to cause offense or hurt someone's feelings through insulting remarks or behavior.
προσβεύω.example_greek: Δεν θα έπρεπε να με προσβάλεις με αυτά τα λόγια.
προσβεύω.example_english: You shouldn't have insulted me with those words.

προσδιορισμός.definition: The act of determining, defining, or specifying something precisely. It refers to the process of establishing exact limits, characteristics, or meanings of a concept or object.
προσδιορισμός.example_greek: Ο προσδιορισμός των όρων της συμφωνίας ήταν πολύ σημαντικός.
προσδιορισμός.example_english: The specification of the terms of the agreement was very important.

προσδοκία.definition: The feeling of looking forward to something or expecting something to happen. A state of anticipatory waiting for an event or outcome.
προσδοκία.example_greek: Η προσδοκία του για το ταξίδι ήταν πολύ μεγάλη.
προσδοκία.example_english: His expectation for the trip was very great.

προσδοκώμενος.definition: Describing something or someone that is expected to arrive, happen, or be received. It refers to what is looked forward to or anticipated.
προσδοκώμενος.example_greek: Ο προσδοκώμενος επισκέπτης έφτασε στις πέντε το απόγευμα.
προσδοκώμενος.example_english: The expected visitor arrived at five in the afternoon.

προσεγγίζω.definition: To move closer to something or someone physically or metaphorically. It can also mean to approximate or come close to a value or idea.
προσεγγίζω.example_greek: Καθώς προσεγγίζουμε το καλοκαίρι, ο καιρός γίνεται πιο ζεστός.
προσεγγίζω.example_english: As we approach summer, the weather becomes warmer.

προσέγγιση.definition: The act of moving closer to something or someone; a method or way of dealing with something; an estimate or approximation of a value.
προσέγγιση.example_greek: Η προσέγγιση του προβλήματος από διαφορετική γωνία ήταν πολύ χρήσιμη.
προσέγγιση.example_english: The approach to the problem from a different angle was very useful.

προσέλαση.definition: The act of coming towards or approaching something; access or entry to a place or person. Can also refer to admission or approach in a figurative sense.
προσέλαση.example_greek: Η προσέλαση στο κτίριο είναι δύσκολη για τα άτομα με αναπηρία.
προσέλαση.example_english: Access to the building is difficult for people with disabilities.

προσέλευση.definition: The act of coming towards or approaching someone or something; an arrival or advent. It can also refer to access or admittance in formal contexts.
προσέλευση.example_greek: Η προσέλευση των επισκεπτών στο μουσείο ήταν πολυάριθμη.
προσέλευση.example_english: The arrival of visitors to the museum was numerous.

προσελκύω.definition: To pull or draw something towards oneself, either physically or figuratively. In a figurative sense, it means to attract attention, interest, or affection.
προσελκύω.example_greek: Η όμορφη μουσική προσελκύει πολλούς ανθρώπους.

προσεχής.definition: Occurring or coming immediately after the present time or mentioned event. Used to refer to something that will happen in the near future.
προσεχής.example_greek: Την προσεχή εβδομάδα θα έχουμε εξετάσεις.
προσεχής.example_english: Next week we will have exams.

πρόσημο.definition: A mark, sign, or symbol used to indicate something or convey meaning. In mathematics, it refers to the positive or negative sign before a number.
πρόσημο.example_greek: Το πρόσημο του αριθμού είναι αρνητικό.
πρόσημο.example_english: The sign of the number is negative.

προσθέτω.definition: To put something additional to something else; to append or attach one thing to another. Commonly used in mathematics, writing, and general contexts.
προσθέτω.example_english: I add sugar to my coffee.

πρόσκαιρος.definition: Lasting only for a short time; not permanent or enduring. Something that exists or is valid only temporarily.
πρόσκαιρος.example_greek: Η πρόσκαιρη λύση δεν θα λύσει το πρόβλημα μακροπρόθεσμα.
πρόσκαιρος.example_english: The temporary solution will not solve the problem in the long term.

προσπάθεια.definition: The act of making an effort to achieve something; a serious attempt or try at accomplishing a goal.
προσπάθεια.example_greek: Με μεγάλη προσπάθεια κατάφερε να περάσει το διαγώνισμα.
προσπάθεια.example_english: With great effort, he managed to pass the exam.

προσποιούμαι.definition: to act as if something is true when it is not; to simulate or fake an emotion, illness, or condition. It is typically used in the middle voice.
προσποιούμαι.example_greek: Προσποιείται ότι δεν άκουσε το όνομά του.
προσποιούμαι.example_english: He pretends that he didn't hear his name.

προστασία.definition: The act of protecting someone or something from harm, danger, or loss. It can also refer to patronage or sponsorship.
προστασία.example_greek: Η προστασία του περιβάλλοντος είναι πολύ σημαντική.

προστίθεμαι.definition: A passive or middle voice verb meaning to be added to something or to join a group. It can also mean to be attached or to come in addition.
προστίθεμαι.example_greek: Πολλοί περισσότεροι άνθρωποι προστίθενται στο κίνημα κάθε μέρα.
προστίθεμαι.example_english: Many more people are joining the movement every day.

πρόσφατος.definition: Occurring or made not long ago; of recent origin or date. Can also mean fresh or newly made.
πρόσφατος.example_greek: Αυτή είναι μια πρόσφατη ειδήσεως που έμαθα χτες.
πρόσφατος.example_english: This is recent news that I learned yesterday.

προσφορά.definition: A proposal or proposal to sell or provide something at a certain price. It can also refer to the supply of goods or services in the market.
προσφορά.example_greek: Η προσφορά του προϊόντος είναι πολύ καλή αυτή την εποχή.
προσφορά.example_english: The supply of the product is very good at this time of year.

πρόταγμα.definition: An authoritative command or decree issued by someone in power. It refers to an official instruction or mandate that must be followed.
πρόταγμα.example_greek: Το πρόταγμα του διευθυντή ήταν να κλείσουν όλα τα γραφεία στις πέντε το απόγευμα.
πρόταγμα.example_english: The director's order was to close all offices at five in the afternoon.

προταγωνηστής.definition: The principal character in a literary work, film, or drama who drives the main action of the story. Can also refer to a leading figure or advocate in a real-world context.
προταγωνηστής.example_greek: Ο προταγωνηστής του ρομάν είναι ένας νεαρός δημοσιογράφος.
προταγωνηστής.example_english: The protagonist of the novel is a young journalist.

πρόταση.definition: A sequence of words that forms a complete grammatical unit, or a formal proposal or suggestion made to someone for consideration.
πρόταση.example_greek: Η πρόταση του δασκάλου ήταν πολύ καλή.
πρόταση.example_english: The teacher's suggestion was very good.

προτάσσω.definition: To put something or someone in front or in a position of priority; to arrange in advance. It can also mean to prefer one thing over another.
προτάσσω.example_greek: Προτάσσω την υγεία μου πριν από τα χρήματα.
προτάσσω.example_english: I prioritize my health before money.

προτεινόμενος.definition: Describing something that has been put forward, suggested, or recommended for consideration or acceptance. It is the passive voice participle form of the verb 'προτείνω' (propose).
προτεινόμενος.example_greek: Ο προτεινόμενος υποψήφιος έλαβε πολλές ψήφους.
προτεινόμενος.example_english: The proposed candidate received many votes.

προτείνω.definition: To put forward an idea, plan, or person for consideration or acceptance. To recommend something to someone.
προτείνω.example_greek: Σας προτείνω να πάμε στο εστιατόριο απέναντι.
προτείνω.example_english: I suggest that we go to the restaurant across the street.

προτεραιότητα.definition: The fact or condition of being regarded or treated as more important than others. Something that deserves to be dealt with first.
προτεραιότητα.example_greek: Η υγεία είναι η πρώτη μου προτεραιότητα.

προτού.definition: A conjunction indicating that one action occurs before another action in time. It introduces a temporal clause and is typically followed by a verb in the subjunctive mood.
προτού.example_greek: Θα φύγω προτού έρθει ο δάσκαλος.
προτού.example_english: I will leave before the teacher comes.

προτρέχω.definition: To run in front of someone or something, or to move forward more quickly than others. It can also mean to surpass or excel beyond expectations.
προτρέχω.example_greek: Ο γιος μου προτρέχει των άλλων παιδιών στο σχολείο.
προτρέχω.example_english: My son outruns the other children at school.

προϋποθεση.definition: A condition that must be fulfilled beforehand or an assumption made in advance. It refers to something that is assumed or required to exist before something else can happen.
προϋποθεση.example_greek: Η γνώση της αγγλικής είναι προϋπόθεση για αυτή τη δουλειά.
προϋποθεση.example_english: Knowledge of English is a prerequisite for this job.

προϋποθέτω.definition: To assume or require something as a necessary condition beforehand; to suppose or assume something in advance as a basis for something else.

προϋπολογισμός.definition: A financial plan that estimates expected income and expenses for a specific period, or a preliminary calculation of costs before implementation.
προϋπολογισμός.example_greek: Ο προϋπολογισμός του κράτους για το 2024 εγκρίθηκε από τη Βουλή.
προϋπολογισμός.example_english: The state budget for 2024 was approved by Parliament.

προύχοντας.definition: Present participle of the verb 'προέχω' (proékhō), meaning to stick out, protrude, or project beyond a surface. It describes something that extends outward from a main body.
προύχοντας.example_greek: Το καρφί προύχοντας από τον τοίχο είναι επικίνδυνο.
προύχοντας.example_english: The nail sticking out from the wall is dangerous.

προφανώς.definition: In a way that is easily seen, understood, or recognized; manifestly or evidently.
προφανώς.example_greek: Προφανώς δεν ήθελε να έρθει στο πάρτι.
προφανώς.example_english: Obviously, he didn't want to come to the party.

πρόφαση.definition: A false reason or explanation given to hide the real reason for something. An excuse or pretense used to justify an action.
πρόφαση.example_greek: Έδωσε μια πρόφαση για να φύγει νωρίς από το γραφείο.
πρόφαση.example_english: He gave an excuse to leave the office early.

προφτεία.definition: A state of being ahead or in a better position than someone else. An advantage gained in competition or a lead in a race or game.
προφτεία.example_greek: Ο ομάδα μας έχει μια καλή προφτεία στο πρωτάθλημα.
προφτεία.example_english: Our team has a good lead in the championship.

προχωρημένος.definition: Describing someone or something that has progressed far along in development, age, or skill level. Can refer to advanced stage, age, or degree of complexity.
προχωρημένος.example_greek: Είναι προχωρημένος ηλικίας και δεν μπορεί να ταξιδέψει.
προχωρημένος.example_english: He is advanced in age and cannot travel.

προχωρώ.translation: to proceed, to advance, to go forward
προχωρώ.definition: To move forward or make progress in a physical, temporal, or abstract sense. It can also mean to go ahead with a plan or action.
προχωρώ.example_greek: Προχωρούμε με το έργο παρά τις δυσκολίες.
προχωρώ.example_english: We are proceeding with the project despite the difficulties.

προωθώ.definition: To move something forward or advance it, either physically or metaphorically. It can mean to promote a product, idea, or person's career.
προωθώ.example_greek: Η εταιρεία προωθεί ένα νέο προϊόν στην αγορά.
προωθώ.example_english: The company is promoting a new product in the market.

πρόωρος.definition: Occurring or happening before the expected or usual time; arriving or existing earlier than is natural, proper, or desired.
πρόωρος.example_greek: Ο πρόωρος θάνατός του ήταν μια μεγάλη απώλεια για την οικογένεια.
πρόωρος.example_english: His premature death was a great loss for the family.

πρώην.definition: At a time in the past; indicating something that happened or existed before the present. Often used to describe a former status or condition.
πρώην.example_greek: Ο πρώην πρόεδρος επισκέφθηκε την πόλη.
πρώην.example_english: The former president visited the city.

πρώιμος.definition: Occurring or happening before the usual or expected time; taking place in the beginning stages of a period or process.
πρώιμος.example_greek: Η πρώιμη διάγνωση της ασθένειας είναι πολύ σημαντική.
πρώιμος.example_english: Early diagnosis of the disease is very important.

πρωταγωνιστής.definition: The main character or central figure in a story, film, or play. Also refers to the leading actor in a theatrical production.
πρωταγωνιστής.example_greek: Ο πρωταγωνιστής του φιλμ ήταν εξαιρετικός.
πρωταγωνιστής.example_english: The protagonist of the film was exceptional.

πρωτοβουλία.definition: The ability or action of taking the first step or making the first move in a situation. It refers to the quality of being proactive and introducing new ideas or actions.
πρωτοβουλία.example_greek: Πήρε την πρωτοβουλία να οργανώσει το event.
πρωτοβουλία.example_english: He took the initiative to organize the event.

πρωτόγνωρος.definition: Describing someone or something encountered or known for the first time; meeting someone for the first time. Often used to describe a person you've just met.
πρωτόγνωρος.example_greek: Αυτός ήταν πρωτόγνωρος για εμένα όταν τον συνάντησα στο πάρτι.
πρωτόγνωρος.example_english: He was a first acquaintance for me when I met him at the party.

πρωτοκαθεδρία.definition: The position of highest honor, authority, or precedence in a hierarchy or organization. It refers to the right to sit first or hold the most important position.
πρωτοκαθεδρία.example_greek: Ο Πατριάρχης κατέχει την πρωτοκαθεδρία στην Εκκλησία.
πρωτοκαθεδρία.example_english: The Patriarch holds primacy in the Church.

πρωτοκλασάτος.definition: Referring to the firstborn child or offspring, particularly the eldest son in a family. This term combines 'proto-' (first) with 'klasatos' (born).
πρωτοκλασάτος.example_greek: Ο πρωτοκλασάτος γιος έχει μεγαλύτερη ευθύνη στην οικογένεια.
πρωτοκλασάτος.example_english: The firstborn son has greater responsibility in the family.

πρωτολογία.definition: The official record or minutes of proceedings, meetings, or legal documents. It can also refer to a formal set of rules governing ceremonies or diplomatic procedures.
πρωτολογία.example_greek: Η πρωτολογία της συνεδρίασης περιέχει όλες τις αποφάσεις που λήφθησαν.
πρωτολογία.example_english: The protocol of the meeting contains all the decisions that were made.

πρωτοστατώ.translation: to lead, to be in the forefront
πρωτοστατώ.definition: To take the lead or be at the forefront of something; to stand out as a leader or pioneer in a particular effort or movement.
πρωτοστατώ.example_greek: Ο σύλλογος πρωτοστατεί στην προστασία του περιβάλλοντος.
πρωτοστατώ.example_english: The association leads the way in environmental protection.

πρωτότηπος.definition: Something that is the first of its kind, novel, or authentic rather than a copy or imitation. It can also mean creative or inventive.
πρωτότηπος.example_greek: Αυτή είναι μια πρωτότυπη ιδέα που δεν έχω ξανακούσει.
πρωτότηπος.example_english: This is an original idea that I have never heard before.

πρωτοφανής.definition: Something that has never been seen or known before; completely novel or original. An adjective describing something that is appearing or happening for the first time.
πρωτοφανής.example_greek: Αυτή η πρωτοφανής κατάσταση προκάλεσε μεγάλη ανησυχία στην κοινωνία.
πρωτοφανής.example_english: This unprecedented situation caused great concern in society.

πτυχή.definition: A fold or crease in a surface, such as cloth or skin. It can also refer to a section or aspect of something.

πτυχωμένος.definition: Having folds, wrinkles, or creases; bent or folded over. Can describe fabric, paper, skin, or other materials that have been folded or have natural creases.
πτυχωμένος.example_greek: Το πτυχωμένο χαρτί ήταν δύσκολο να λυθεί.
πτυχωμένος.example_english: The folded paper was difficult to unfold.

πτώση.definition: The act of falling or dropping from a higher position; a decline or decrease in level, amount, or quality; in grammar, one of the inflectional forms of nouns, pronouns, and adjectives.
πτώση.example_greek: Η πτώση της θερμοκρασίας ήταν απότομη.
πτώση.example_english: The fall in temperature was sudden.

πτωτικός.definition: Describing something that falls, descends, or is in decline. In grammar, it can refer to cases involving declension (ptosis in classical terms).
πτωτικός.example_greek: Η πτωτική τάση της οικονομίας ανησύχησε τους αναλυτές.
πτωτικός.example_english: The declining trend of the economy worried the analysts.

πυλώνας.definition: A tall structure or gateway, often referring to an entrance portal, a monumental gateway, or a large tower-like structure used for electricity transmission or supporting cables.
πυλώνας.example_greek: Οι πυλώνες της γέφυρας ήταν πολύ ψηλοί.
πυλώνας.example_english: The pylons of the bridge were very tall.

πυρινικός.definition: Relating to or involving the nucleus of an atom, or relating to nuclear energy and nuclear weapons.
πυρινικός.example_greek: Η πυρηνική ενέργεια είναι μια σημαντική πηγή ενέργειας.
πυρινικός.example_english: Nuclear energy is an important source of energy.

πυρκαγιά.definition: An uncontrolled fire that spreads rapidly, especially a large fire in a forest or natural area. In broader use, it refers to any significant fire outbreak.
πυρκαγιά.example_greek: Η μεγάλη πυρκαγιά κατέστρεψε χιλιάδες στρέμματα δάσους.
πυρκαγιά.example_english: The large wildfire destroyed thousands of acres of forest.

πυρομαχικός.definition: Relating to or designed to cause fire or burning. Used to describe weapons, devices, or materials that use fire as their primary mechanism of action.
πυρομαχικός.example_greek: Η αστυνομία κατασχέθηκε πυρομαχικά όπλα από τον ύποπτο.
πυρομαχικός.example_english: The police confiscated incendiary weapons from the suspect.

πώληση.definition: The act of selling goods or services, or a period when products are offered at reduced prices.
πώληση.example_greek: Η πώληση των εισιτηρίων ξεκινά αύριο.
πώληση.example_english: The sale of tickets starts tomorrow.

ράφτης.definition: A person whose occupation is making or repairing clothes by sewing. The term can refer to both male and female tailors, though ράφτρα is the feminine form.
ράφτης.example_greek: Ο ράφτης έφτιαξε ένα όμορφο κοστούμι για τον γάμο.
ράφτης.example_english: The tailor made a beautiful suit for the wedding.

ρεάμα.definition: A body of flowing water; a stream or creek. Can also refer to an electric current or any continuous flow of something.
ρεάμα.example_greek: Το ρεάμα του ποταμού ήταν πολύ δυνατό μετά τις βροχές.
ρεάμα.example_english: The river's current was very strong after the rain.

ρέστα.definition: The money returned to a customer after payment, or small coins and bills remaining after a transaction. It can also refer to leftover items or remainder.
ρέστα.example_greek: Το καφέ κόστισε τρία ευρώ και μου έδωσε τα ρέστα του πέντε.
ρέστα.example_english: The coffee cost three euros and he gave me the change from five.

ρευστοποίηση.definition: The process of converting assets or resources into liquid form, typically cash or easily tradable securities. In finance, it refers to the conversion of non-liquid assets into money.
ρευστοποίηση.example_greek: Η ρευστοποίηση των περιουσιακών στοιχείων της εταιρείας ήταν απαραίτητη για την αποπληρωμή των χρεών.
ρευστοποίηση.example_english: The liquification of the company's assets was necessary to pay off the debts.

ρήτρα.definition: A specific condition, stipulation, or section within a contract, agreement, or legal document that outlines particular terms or requirements.
ρήτρα.example_greek: Η σύμβαση περιέχει μια ρήτρα που απαγορεύει την πώληση του ακινήτου.
ρήτρα.example_english: The contract contains a clause that prohibits the sale of the property.

ριψοκίνδυνα.definition: In a manner that is reckless, careless, or without proper consideration; throwing caution to the wind. The term literally derives from the idea of 'throwing dice' (taking chances).
ριψοκίνδυνα.example_greek: Δεν μπορείς να ρίχνεις ριψοκίνδυνα τα χρήματά σου.
ριψοκίνδυνα.example_english: You cannot throw your money away recklessly.

ροπή.definition: A natural tendency or inclination toward something; in physics, the rotational effect of a force applied at a distance from a pivot point.
ροπή.example_greek: Έχει ροπή να αργοπορεί στα ραντεβού.
ροπή.example_english: He has a tendency to be late for appointments.

σαθρός.definition: Describing something that is decomposed, deteriorated, or in a state of decay. Can be used literally for rotting material or figuratively for weak or unsound things.
σαθρός.example_greek: Το παλιό ξύλο ήταν σαθρό και έσπασε εύκολα.
σαθρός.example_english: The old wood was rotten and broke easily.

σαφής.definition: Easy to perceive, understand, or see; clearly defined or expressed without ambiguity.
σαφής.example_greek: Η σαφής εξήγηση του δασκάλου βοήθησε όλους τους μαθητές.
σαφής.example_english: The teacher's clear explanation helped all the students.

ΣΕΒ.definition: Abbreviation for Σώμα Επιθεώρησης Βάσεων (Soma Epitheorissis Vaseon), referring to Greek social security inspection bodies or services.
ΣΕΒ.example_greek: Πρέπει να επικοινωνήσεις με τη ΣΕΒ για τα ζητήματα ασφάλισης.
ΣΕΒ.example_english: You need to contact the social security agency about insurance matters.

σέβω.definition: To hold in high regard or show respect for someone or something; to treat with reverence or honor.
σέβω.example_english: I respect my parents very much.

σεξουαλικός.definition: Relating to or involving sex, sexuality, or sexual activity. Used to describe matters pertaining to physical intimacy or sexual characteristics.
σεξουαλικός.example_greek: Η σεξουαλική υγεία είναι σημαντικό μέρος της συνολικής υγείας.
σεξουαλικός.example_english: Sexual health is an important part of overall health.

σέρνω.translation: to drag, to pull, to crawl
σέρνω.definition: To pull or drag something along a surface, or to move slowly by crawling or dragging oneself. It can also mean to trail or haul.
σέρνω.example_greek: Το παιδί σέρνει την κούκλα του στο πάτωμα.
σέρνω.example_english: The child drags her doll along the floor.

σημαία.definition: A piece of fabric or banner, typically rectangular, with distinctive colors or symbols, used as a symbol of a nation, organization, or signal.
σημαία.example_greek: Η σημαία της Ελλάδας είναι γαλάζια και λευκή.
σημαία.example_english: The flag of Greece is blue and white.

σημειωτέον.definition: Something that should be noted or is worthy of attention; a formal term indicating that a particular point deserves special consideration.
σημειωτέον.example_greek: Σημειωτέον ότι η απόφαση αυτή είναι πολύ σημαντική για το μέλλον.
σημειωτέον.example_english: It should be noted that this decision is very important for the future.

σιγοντάρω.translation: to silence, to shush, to hush
σιγοντάρω.definition: To tell someone to be quiet or to make someone stop talking. It can also mean to suppress or conceal something.
σιγοντάρω.example_greek: Σιγόνταρε τα παιδιά γιατί έκλαιγαν πολύ.
σιγοντάρω.example_english: She hushed the children because they were crying a lot.

σιδηρόδρομος.definition: A transportation system consisting of steel rails and trains that carry passengers or cargo. It can also refer to the railroad company or the railroad line itself.
σιδηρόδρομος.example_greek: Πήρα το τρένο από τον σιδηρόδρομο για να πάω στη θεσσαλονίκη.
σιδηρόδρομος.example_english: I took the train from the railway station to go to Thessaloniki.

σιμπίπτω.definition: To occur at the same time as something else; to coincide or happen simultaneously with another event.
σιμπίπτω.example_greek: Τα δύο γεγονότα σιμπίπτουν στην ίδια ημερομηνία.
σιμπίπτω.example_english: The two events coincide on the same date.

σιωπηλός.definition: Characterized by silence or not speaking much; quiet and reserved in nature or demeanor.
σιωπηλός.example_greek: Ο σιωπηλός άνδρας κάθισε στη γωνία και δεν είπε λέξη.
σιωπηλός.example_english: The silent man sat in the corner and didn't say a word.

σκαλιομένος.definition: Past participle form describing something that has been dug up, excavated, or scratched. Often used to describe ground that has been disturbed or a surface that has been scratched.
σκαλιομένος.example_greek: Το σκαλιομένο χώμα ήταν σκληρό και αφθονό.
σκαλιομένος.example_english: The dug-up soil was hard and abundant.

σκαπακός.definition: Having legs that curve outward at the knees, creating a distinctive bowed appearance when standing with feet together.
σκαπακός.example_greek: Ο παλιός κυρ Γιάννης ήταν σκαπακός και περπατούσε δύσκολα.
σκαπακός.example_english: Old Mr. Yiannis was bowlegged and walked with difficulty.

σκάω.definition: To break up and turn over soil or earth, typically with a spade or similar tool. It can also mean to dig or excavate in a literal or figurative sense.
σκάω.example_greek: Ο κηπουρός σκάβει το χώμα για να φυτέψει λουλούδια.
σκάω.example_english: The gardener digs the soil to plant flowers.

σκέλος.definition: The lower limb of the human body extending from the hip to the foot. Also used metaphorically to refer to a leg of furniture or other objects.

σκιά.definition: A dark area or shape produced by an object blocking light. The absence of light cast by an object when light shines on it.
σκιά.example_greek: Η σκιά του δέντρου ήταν πολύ μεγάλη το απόγευμα.
σκιά.example_english: The shadow of the tree was very large in the afternoon.

σκληρός.definition: Having a firm, solid consistency; difficult to break or penetrate. Can also mean severe, harsh, or rigorous in manner or treatment.
σκληρός.example_greek: Αυτό το ξύλο είναι πολύ σκληρό.

σκληρότερος.definition: Comparative form of σκληρός (hard/harsh), indicating something that is harder in texture, more difficult, or more severe than something else.
σκληρότερος.example_greek: Αυτό το ξύλο είναι σκληρότερο από εκείνο.
σκληρότερος.example_english: This wood is harder than that one.

σκοπιμότητα.definition: The quality of being suitable, appropriate, or advantageous for a particular purpose or situation. It refers to what is practical or wise to do under specific circumstances.
σκοπιμότητα.example_greek: Η σκοπιμότητα της απόφασης δεν είναι σαφής.
σκοπιμότητα.example_english: The expediency of the decision is not clear.

σκοτεινός.definition: Lacking light or brightness; characterized by darkness or shadow. Can also mean unclear or difficult to understand.
σκοτεινός.example_greek: Το δωμάτιο ήταν πολύ σκοτεινό χωρίς φώτα.
σκοτεινός.example_english: The room was very dark without lights.

σκούρα.definition: Describing a color that is dark or dim in appearance. Can refer to dark shades of any color, such as dark blue, dark brown, or dark hair.
σκούρα.example_english: She is wearing a dark blue dress.

σοφός.definition: Having or showing good judgment, knowledge, and understanding. Possessing wisdom or intellectual capability.
σοφός.example_greek: Ο σοφός δάσκαλος έδωσε καλές συμβουλές στους μαθητές του.
σοφός.example_english: The wise teacher gave good advice to his students.

σπαράζω.definition: To tear or rip something violently, or to wrench apart with force. Can also mean to lacerate or cause sharp emotional pain.
σπαράζω.example_greek: Ο άνεμος σπάραξε το πανί του ιστιοπλοΐου.
σπαράζω.example_english: The wind tore the sail of the sailboat.

σπαράσσω.translation: to tear, to rend, to rip
σπαράσσω.definition: To pull apart or tear something forcefully into pieces. It can also mean to distress or torment emotionally.
σπαράσσω.example_greek: Ο αέρας σπάραξε το ύφασμα του χαρταιού.
σπαράσσω.example_english: The wind tore the fabric of the kite.

σπάργανα.definition: Strips of cloth used to wrap and swaddle infants, traditionally used to keep babies warm and secure. The term is often associated with the biblical image of the newborn Jesus wrapped in swaddling clothes.
σπάργανα.example_greek: Το μωρό ήταν τυλιγμένο σε σπάργανα.
σπάργανα.example_english: The baby was wrapped in swaddling clothes.

σπάω.translation: to break, to crack, to snap
σπάω.definition: to cause something to break into pieces or to become broken; to fracture or split something apart.
σπάω.example_greek: Ο παιδί έσπασε το παιχνίδι του.

σπεύδω.definition: To move or act with great speed or urgency; to hasten towards something. It conveys a sense of moving quickly and eagerly.
σπεύδω.example_greek: Σπεύδω να φτάσω στο σπίτι πριν νυχτώσει.
σπεύδω.example_english: I hurry to get home before it gets dark.

σπινθηροβόλος.definition: An adjective describing something that throws off sparks or sparkles brightly. It is a compound word combining 'spinthir' (spark) and 'volos' (throwing).
σπινθηροβόλος.example_greek: Ο σπινθηροβόλος σίδερος έπεσε στο έδαφος.
σπινθηροβόλος.example_english: The sparking metal fell to the ground.

σπολλύω.translation: to spoil, to ruin, to waste
σπολλύω.definition: To damage, destroy, or cause something to deteriorate; to waste time, money, or resources. In Modern Greek, it means to ruin or mar something's condition or quality.
σπολλύω.example_greek: Η βροχή σπόλλυσε το πικνίκ μας.

σπουδαίος.definition: Of great importance or significance; worthy of attention or respect. Can also mean serious in demeanor or excellent in quality.
σπουδαίος.example_greek: Αυτό είναι ένα σπουδαίο θέμα που πρέπει να συζητήσουμε.
σπουδαίος.example_english: This is an important matter that we need to discuss.

σπουδαστής.definition: A person who is enrolled in an educational institution or studying a subject formally. This term typically refers to university students or advanced learners.
σπουδαστής.example_greek: Ο σπουδαστής διαβάζει πολλά βιβλία για την εξέταση.
σπουδαστής.example_english: The student reads many books for the exam.

σταδιακός.definition: Occurring or developing slowly in stages over time rather than suddenly or all at once. Characterized by gradual progression through stages or steps.
σταδιακός.example_greek: Η σταδιακή αύξηση της θερμοκρασίας ήταν ανησυχητική.
σταδιακός.example_english: The gradual increase in temperature was worrying.

σταθερός.definition: Not changing or fluctuating; remaining in the same state or condition. Firmly fixed or established.
σταθερός.example_english: He has a steady job at the company.

σταθερότητα.definition: The quality or state of being stable, firm, and not changing or moving. It refers to permanence and reliability in various contexts such as emotions, relationships, or physical objects.
σταθερότητα.example_greek: Η σταθερότητα της σχέσης τους ήταν ένα παράδειγμα για όλους.
σταθερότητα.example_english: The stability of their relationship was an example to everyone.

σταθμίζω.translation: to weigh, to balance, to ponder
σταθμίζω.definition: To determine the weight of something using a scale, or figuratively, to carefully consider and evaluate different aspects of a matter.
σταθμίζω.example_greek: Πρέπει να σταθμίσω όλα τα υπέρ και τα κατά πριν πάρω μια απόφαση.
σταθμίζω.example_english: I need to weigh all the pros and cons before making a decision.

σταθώ.definition: First person singular future tense of the verb 'στέκομαι' (stékomai), meaning to stand or to take a stand. It expresses an action that will happen in the future.
σταθώ.example_greek: Θα σταθώ δίπλα σου στη δύσκολη στιγμή.
σταθώ.example_english: I will stand by your side in the difficult moment.

σταχυολογώ.definition: To collect leftover grain or crops after harvesting, or more broadly, to gather small amounts of something scattered or remaining.
σταχυολογώ.example_greek: Μετά τη συγκομιδή, οι φτωχοί σταχυολογούσαν στα χωράφια.
σταχυολογώ.example_english: After the harvest, the poor would glean in the fields.

στέγαση.definition: The provision of a place to live or shelter; accommodation or housing provided to someone, especially in social contexts.
στέγαση.example_greek: Η κυβέρνηση προσφέρει στέγαση στους άστεγους.
στέγαση.example_english: The government provides housing to the homeless.

στεγαστικός.definition: Relating to or concerning housing, dwellings, or residential accommodation. Often used in administrative or official contexts.
στεγαστικός.example_greek: Η στεγαστική κρίση είναι ένα σημαντικό πρόβλημα στις μεγάλες πόλεις.
στεγαστικός.example_english: The housing crisis is a significant problem in large cities.

στείρος.definition: Unable to produce offspring or vegetation; lacking fertility or the ability to reproduce or bear fruit.
στείρος.example_greek: Το στείρο έδαφος δεν μπορούσε να καλλιεργηθεί.
στείρος.example_english: The barren soil could not be cultivated.

στελεχικός.definition: Relating to or characteristic of management, administrators, or executive personnel in an organization. Describes positions, roles, or matters pertaining to organizational leadership and administration.
στελεχικός.example_greek: Η στελεχική ομάδα της εταιρείας ανέλαβε τη διοίκηση του νέου έργου.
στελεχικός.example_english: The managerial team of the company took over the administration of the new project.

στελέχος.definition: The main trunk or stem of a plant; also refers to a key personnel member or cadre in an organization, particularly in political or military contexts.
στελέχος.example_greek: Το στελέχος του φυτού ήταν πολύ ισχυρό.
στελέχος.example_english: The stem of the plant was very strong.

στέλεχος.definition: The main trunk or stem of a plant, tree, or plant structure. In modern usage, it can also refer to a branch or division of an organization.
στέλεχος.example_greek: Το στέλεχος του δέντρου είναι πολύ παχύ.
στέλεχος.example_english: The trunk of the tree is very thick.

στελεχωμένος.definition: Having adequate staff or personnel assigned to it; furnished with the necessary people to operate or function properly.
στελεχωμένος.example_greek: Το νοσοκομείο είναι καλά στελεχωμένο με ειδικευμένους γιατρούς.
στελεχωμένος.example_english: The hospital is well-staffed with specialized doctors.

στιγμιότυπο.definition: A digital image captured from a screen, or a moment frozen in time. It refers to a single frame or instant representation of something.
στιγμιότυπο.example_greek: Έκανα ένα στιγμιότυπο της συνομιλίας για αποδείξεις.
στιγμιότυπο.example_english: I took a screenshot of the conversation as proof.

στοά.definition: A covered walkway with a roof supported by columns, typically found in ancient Greek architecture. In ancient Greece, stoas were common public structures used for commerce, meetings, and shelter.
στοά.example_greek: Η στοά του Αττάλου είναι ένα σημαντικό μνημείο της αρχαίας Αθήνας.
στοά.example_english: The Stoa of Attalos is an important monument of ancient Athens.

στοιχείο.definition: A fundamental part or component of something; in chemistry, a substance that cannot be broken down into simpler substances. It can also refer to a single piece of information or a characteristic feature.
στοιχείο.example_greek: Το χημικό στοιχείο του οξυγόνου είναι απαραίτητο για τη ζωή.
στοιχείο.example_english: The chemical element of oxygen is essential for life.

στόλος.definition: A group of ships sailing together, typically under military command, or the naval forces of a country.
στόλος.example_greek: Ο στόλος της χώρας είναι ένας από τους ισχυρότερους στον κόσμο.
στόλος.example_english: The country's fleet is one of the most powerful in the world.

στορογή.definition: The quality of being unwilling to spend money or give generously; extreme frugality or stinginess.
στορογή.example_greek: Η στορογή του δεν του επιτρέπει να βοηθήσει τους φτωχούς.
στορογή.example_english: His stinginess does not allow him to help the poor.

στόχαστρο.definition: A device attached to a firearm that is used to aim and sight targets. It helps the shooter align the weapon with the intended target.
στόχαστρο.example_greek: Ο κυνηγός ρύθμισε το στόχαστρο του τουφεκιού του.
στόχαστρο.example_english: The hunter adjusted the sight of his rifle.

στοχευμένος.definition: Intended or designed with a specific purpose or target in mind; characterized by being aimed at something particular.

στόχευση.definition: The act of aiming at or directing something toward a specific target or goal. It can refer to both physical aiming (as in shooting) and metaphorical targeting (as in marketing or strategy).
στόχευση.example_greek: Η στόχευση της διαφημιστικής καμπάνιας ήταν τα νεαρά άτομα.
στόχευση.example_english: The targeting of the advertising campaign was young people.

στραγγίζω.definition: To remove liquid from something by pouring it out or pressing it, or to extract liquid by wringing or squeezing.
στραγγίζω.example_greek: Στραγγίζω τα λαχανικά μετά το πλύσιμο.
στραγγίζω.example_english: I drain the vegetables after washing them.

στράτευμα.definition: An organized group of soldiers or armed personnel; a military force or army unit deployed for combat or defense.
στράτευμα.example_greek: Το στράτευμα ετοιμάστηκε για την αποστολή.
στράτευμα.example_english: The army was prepared for the mission.

στρατόπεδο.definition: A temporary or permanent military installation where soldiers are stationed, trained, or housed. It can refer to both active military camps and permanent barracks facilities.
στρατόπεδο.example_greek: Το στρατόπεδο βρίσκεται στα περίχωρα της πόλης.
στρατόπεδο.example_english: The military camp is located on the outskirts of the city.

στρεβλός.definition: Bent or twisted out of the normal shape; not straight. Can also mean distorted or perverted in meaning or interpretation.
στρεβλός.example_greek: Το παιδί έσπασε το κλάδο και έγινε στρεβλός.
στρεβλός.example_english: The child broke the branch and it became twisted.

στρέφω.definition: To cause something to move around an axis or point; to rotate or pivot. Can also mean to turn one's body or face in a different direction.
στρέφω.example_greek: Στρέφω το κλειδί για να ανοίξω την πόρτα.
στρέφω.example_english: I turn the key to open the door.

στρογγυλός.definition: Having a circular or spherical shape; lacking sharp angles or corners. Can be used literally for physical objects or figuratively for descriptions.
στρογγυλός.example_greek: Ένα στρογγυλό τραπέζι είναι πιο πρακτικό για συνομιλίες.
στρογγυλός.example_english: A round table is more practical for conversations.

στρώμα.definition: A thick padded surface used for sleeping on, or more generally, a horizontal layer of material or substance.
στρώμα.example_greek: Το στρώμα του κρεβατιού είναι πολύ άνετο.
στρώμα.example_english: The mattress of the bed is very comfortable.

συγκεκριμένος.definition: Clearly defined or identified; not vague or general. Refers to something that is precise, concrete, or clearly determined.
συγκεκριμένος.example_greek: Θέλω να μιλήσω για ένα συγκεκριμένο πρόβλημα.
συγκεκριμένος.example_english: I want to talk about a specific problem.

συγκρατώ.translation: to hold back, to contain, to restrain
συγκρατώ.definition: To hold something firmly in place or to prevent something from happening or spreading. It can also mean to keep or maintain control over something.
συγκρατώ.example_greek: Προσπάθησε να συγκρατήσει τα δάκρυά της κατά τη διάρκεια της ταινίας.
συγκρατώ.example_english: She tried to hold back her tears during the movie.

σύγκρια.definition: The act of comparing two or more things to examine their similarities and differences. A juxtaposition or relative estimation of worth or quality between things.
σύγκρια.example_greek: Η σύγκρια μεταξύ των δύο προϊόντων δείχνει ότι το πρώτο είναι καλύτερης ποιότητας.
σύγκρια.example_english: The comparison between the two products shows that the first one is of better quality.

συγκρίσιμος.definition: Capable of being compared or suitable for comparison with something else. Describes something that has sufficient similarity or common characteristics to allow meaningful comparison.
συγκρίσιμος.example_greek: Αυτά τα δύο προϊόντα είναι συγκρίσιμα στην τιμή και την ποιότητα.
συγκρίσιμος.example_english: These two products are comparable in price and quality.

συγκρότηση.definition: The action or process of forming, establishing, or organizing something, such as a committee, team, or structure. It can also refer to the composition or arrangement of constituent parts.
συγκρότηση.example_greek: Η συγκρότηση της νέας επιτροπής θα γίνει την επόμενη εβδομάδα.
συγκρότηση.example_english: The formation of the new committee will take place next week.

συγκροτώ.translation: to form, to constitute, to assemble
συγκροτώ.definition: To bring together or organize elements into a unified whole; to establish or create a structured entity by combining components.
συγκροτώ.example_greek: Η κυβέρνηση συγκρότησε μια νέα επιτροπή για τη μελέτη του προβλήματος.
συγκροτώ.example_english: The government formed a new committee to study the problem.

σύγκρουση.definition: A violent encounter between two or more things; a disagreement or dispute between people or groups.
σύγκρουση.example_greek: Υπήρξε σύγκρουση μεταξύ των δύο αυτοκινήτων στη διασταύρωση.
σύγκρουση.example_english: There was a collision between the two cars at the intersection.

συγκυριακός.definition: Happening by chance or coincidence; occurring at the same time by accident rather than by design or intention.
συγκυριακός.example_greek: Ήταν μια συγκυριακή συνάντηση που άλλαξε τη ζωή του.
συγκυριακός.example_english: It was a coincidental meeting that changed his life.

συλλαγικός.definition: Relating to or characteristic of a collective group or action involving multiple parties working together. Often used in legal, commercial, or organizational contexts.
συλλαγικός.example_greek: Η συλλαγική απόφαση ελήφθη από όλα τα μέλη της επιτροπής.
συλλαγικός.example_english: The collective decision was made by all members of the committee.

σύλληψη.definition: The act of arresting or capturing someone; also refers to conception or the grasping of an idea. It can mean the apprehension of a person by law enforcement or the mental act of understanding something.
σύλληψη.example_greek: Η σύλληψη του διακινητή ήταν αποτέλεσμα της αστυνομικής έρευνας.
σύλληψη.example_english: The arrest of the trafficker was the result of the police investigation.

συμβάλλω.definition: To contribute or help toward a goal, or to come together and meet. It can also mean to combine or merge elements together.
συμβάλλω.example_greek: Όλοι συμβάλλουν στην επιτυχία του έργου.
συμβάλλω.example_english: Everyone contributes to the success of the project.

συμβιβασμός.definition: A mutual agreement where both parties make concessions to reach a settlement. An adjustment or accommodation between conflicting positions or interests.
συμβιβασμός.example_greek: Οι δύο πλευρές κατέληξαν σε έναν συμβιβασμό που ικανοποίησε και τους δύο.
συμβιβασμός.example_english: The two parties reached a compromise that satisfied both of them.

συμβολαίο.definition: A formal written agreement between two or more parties that outlines terms, conditions, and obligations. It is legally binding and enforceable.
συμβολαίο.example_greek: Υπέγραψα ένα συμβολαίο με τη νέα εταιρεία.
συμβολαίο.example_english: I signed a contract with the new company.

σύμβουλος.definition: A person who provides professional advice or guidance in a particular field. Can refer to a business consultant, legal advisor, or counselor.
σύμβουλος.example_greek: Ο σύμβουλος μας βοήθησε να λάβουμε τη σωστή απόφαση.
σύμβουλος.example_english: Our advisor helped us make the right decision.

συμμαχία.definition: A formal agreement or union between two or more parties, especially countries or political groups, for mutual support and common goals.
συμμαχία.example_greek: Η συμμαχία μεταξύ των δύο χωρών ήταν πολύ σημαντική κατά τον Δεύτερο Παγκόσμιο Πόλεμο.
συμμαχία.example_english: The alliance between the two countries was very important during World War II.

σύμμαχος.definition: A person or country that is united with another for a common purpose, especially in war or military alliance. It refers to a partner in a strategic or military agreement.
σύμμαχος.example_greek: Η Ελλάδα και η Γαλλία είναι σύμμαχοι στο ΝΑΤΟ.
σύμμαχος.example_english: Greece and France are allies in NATO.

συμμαχώ.translation: to ally with, to be an ally
συμμαχώ.definition: To form an alliance or partnership with someone; to act as an ally or confederate in a cause or conflict.
συμμαχώ.example_greek: Οι δύο χώρες συμμάχησαν ενάντια στον κοινό εχθρό.
συμμαχώ.example_english: The two countries allied against the common enemy.

συμμετέχω.definition: To engage in or be involved in an activity, event, or group. To have a share or role in something.
συμμετέχω.example_greek: Θέλω να συμμετέχω στο μάθημα της τέχνης.
συμμετέχω.example_english: I want to participate in the art class.

συμπαιγνία.definition: A playful exchange of witty remarks or teasing between people; light-hearted joking or banter. It refers to clever, amusing conversation characterized by mutual teasing.
συμπαιγνία.example_greek: Η συμπαιγνία ανάμεσα στους δύο φίλους ήταν πολύ διασκεδαστική.
συμπαιγνία.example_english: The playful banter between the two friends was very entertaining.

συμπέρασμα.definition: A judgment or decision reached by reasoning; the final outcome or result of a process or argument.
συμπέρασμα.example_greek: Το συμπέρασμα της μελέτης ήταν ότι η άσκηση είναι σημαντική για την υγεία.
συμπέρασμα.example_english: The conclusion of the study was that exercise is important for health.

συμπεριφορά.definition: The way a person acts or conducts themselves in social situations or environments. It refers to one's actions, mannerisms, and overall deportment.
συμπεριφορά.example_greek: Η συμπεριφορά του παιδιού ήταν πολύ καλή στο σχολείο.
συμπεριφορά.example_english: The child's behavior was very good at school.

συμπίπτω.definition: To occur at the same time as something else; to happen simultaneously or to agree with something. It can also mean to collide or come together physically.
συμπίπτω.example_english: The two events coincide in time.

σύμπλευση.definition: The act of sailing or traveling together by sea. It can also refer to a joint voyage or nautical journey undertaken by multiple parties.
σύμπλευση.example_greek: Η σύμπλευση των δύο πλοίων ήταν επιτυχημένη.
σύμπλευση.example_english: The joint voyage of the two ships was successful.

συμπρόεδρος.definition: A person who shares the position of chairperson or president with one or more other people. It refers to joint leadership of an organization, committee, or institution.
συμπρόεδρος.example_greek: Ο Γιάννης και η Μαρία είναι συμπρόεδροι του συλλόγου.
συμπρόεδρος.example_english: Yannis and Maria are co-chairs of the association.

συμπρωτευόυσα.definition: A city that shares capital status with another city in a country or region. It is a city that has equal or joint status as a capital.
συμπρωτευόυσα.example_greek: Η Θεσσαλονίκη θεωρείται συμπρωτευούσα της Ελλάδας.
συμπρωτευόυσα.example_english: Thessaloniki is considered a co-capital of Greece.

συμπρωτεύουσα.definition: A city that shares capital status with another city in the same country or region. It refers to one of two or more cities jointly designated as capitals.
συμπρωτεύουσα.example_greek: Η Θεσσαλονίκη θεωρείται συμπρωτεύουσα της Ελλάδας.
συμπρωτεύουσα.example_english: Thessaloniki is considered a co-capital of Greece.

σύμπτηξη.definition: The process or result of compressing or condensing something into a smaller space or more compact form. It can refer to physical compression or the act of summarizing information.
σύμπτηξη.example_greek: Η σύμπτηξη του αερίου αυξάνει την πίεση του.
σύμπτηξη.example_english: The compression of the gas increases its pressure.

σύμπτυξη.definition: The act or process of compressing, contracting, or folding something into a smaller space or form. It can refer to physical compression or condensation of matter.
σύμπτυξη.example_greek: Η σύμπτυξη του αερίου προκάλεσε αύξηση της θερμοκρασίας.
σύμπτυξη.example_english: The compression of the gas caused an increase in temperature.

συμπυκνώνω.translation: to condense, to compress, to concentrate
συμπυκνώνω.definition: To make something more compact, dense, or concentrated by removing unnecessary parts or reducing volume. It can be used literally (physical compression) or figuratively (condensing information or ideas).
συμπυκνώνω.example_greek: Ο συγγραφέας συμπύκνωσε το μεγάλο μυθιστόρημα σε μια σύντομη περίληψη.
συμπυκνώνω.example_english: The author condensed the long novel into a brief summary.

συμπύκνωση.definition: The process of making something more compact, dense, or concentrated. In physics, it refers to the phase transition of vapor into liquid; in general usage, it means the act of reducing volume or summarizing information.
συμπύκνωση.example_greek: Η συμπύκνωση του υδρατμού δημιουργεί σταγόνες νερού.
συμπύκνωση.example_english: The condensation of water vapor creates water droplets.

συμφέρον.definition: Something that is advantageous or beneficial to a person or group. It refers to what serves one's interests or brings profit or gain.
συμφέρον.example_greek: Το συμφέρον του κράτους είναι να διατηρήσει την ειρήνη.
συμφέρον.example_english: The interest of the state is to maintain peace.

συμφέρων.definition: Present participle of συμφέρω (symféro), meaning 'to be advantageous' or 'to benefit'. As an adjective, it describes something that is expedient or in someone's interest.
συμφέρων.example_greek: Αυτό το σχέδιο είναι συμφέρων για όλους.
συμφέρων.example_english: This plan is beneficial for everyone.

συμφιλιώνω.definition: To bring two people or groups back into agreement after a disagreement or conflict. To restore friendly relations between estranged parties.
συμφιλιώνω.example_greek: Ο δάσκαλος συμφιλίωσε τα δύο παιδιά που είχαν μαλώσει.
συμφιλιώνω.example_english: The teacher reconciled the two children who had quarreled.

συναγερμός.definition: A signal or announcement warning of danger or emergency; a mobilization or state of heightened readiness in response to a threat or urgent situation.
συναγερμός.example_greek: Ο συναγερμός ακούστηκε σε όλη την πόλη λόγω της πυρκαγιάς.
συναγερμός.example_english: The alarm was heard throughout the city because of the fire.

συναλλαγή.definition: A business transaction or exchange between two parties, often used in commercial and legal contexts to describe the agreement or act of exchanging goods, services, or money.
συναλλαγή.example_greek: Η συναλλαγή ολοκληρώθηκε με επιτυχία χθες.
συναλλαγή.example_english: The transaction was completed successfully yesterday.

συναρπαστικός.definition: Extremely interesting and engaging; capable of holding someone's attention completely. Often used to describe stories, movies, or events that are compelling and exciting.
συναρπαστικός.example_greek: Αυτό το βιβλίο είναι πολύ συναρπαστικό και δεν μπορώ να σταματήσω να το διαβάζω.
συναρπαστικός.example_english: This book is very captivating and I cannot stop reading it.

συνάρτηση.definition: A mathematical relationship between variables where one quantity depends on another. More broadly, a connection or relationship between things.

συνασπισμός.definition: A temporary or permanent alliance of political parties, groups, or nations formed to achieve common goals or oppose a common enemy.
συνασπισμός.example_greek: Ο συνασπισμός των αριστερών κομμάτων κέρδισε τις εκλογές.
συνασπισμός.example_english: The coalition of leftist parties won the election.

συνδεδεμένος.definition: Joined or attached to something else; in a state of connection or relationship with another thing. Can refer to physical, emotional, or logical connections.
συνδεδεμένος.example_greek: Οι δύο πόλεις είναι συνδεδεμένες με μια γέφυρα.
συνδεδεμένος.example_english: The two cities are connected by a bridge.

συνδικαλιστικός.definition: Relating to or characteristic of trade unions, labor unions, or union activities and principles. Describes things associated with organized labor movements.
συνδικαλιστικός.example_greek: Η συνδικαλιστική κίνηση ήταν πολύ δυνατή στις αρχές του 20ού αιώνα.
συνδικαλιστικός.example_english: The trade union movement was very strong in the early 20th century.

συνδικάτο.definition: An organized association of workers formed to protect their common interests, negotiate wages and working conditions, and advocate for workers' rights.
συνδικάτο.example_greek: Ο εργάτης είναι μέλος του συνδικάτου της εργοστασίου.
συνδικάτο.example_english: The worker is a member of the factory's trade union.

συνδρομή.definition: A collection of symptoms that occur together in a medical condition, or alternatively, a subscription or financial contribution to something.
συνδρομή.example_greek: Ο γιατρός διάγνωσε το σύνδρομο του καρπιαίου σωλήνα.
συνδρομή.example_english: The doctor diagnosed carpal tunnel syndrome.

συνδυασμός.definition: A joining together of different elements or parts to form a unified whole. The act or result of combining things in a particular way.
συνδυασμός.example_greek: Ο συνδυασμός των χρωμάτων είναι πολύ όμορφος.
συνδυασμός.example_english: The combination of colors is very beautiful.

σύνεγγας.definition: A person who is related by blood or marriage; a family member or relative. This is an archaic or literary term in Modern Greek.
σύνεγγας.example_greek: Ο σύνεγγας του ήρθε να τον επισκεφθεί.
σύνεγγας.example_english: His relative came to visit him.

συνεδριάζω.translation: to hold a meeting, to convene
συνεδριάζω.definition: To meet together as a group, council, or assembly for discussion or decision-making. It typically refers to formal gatherings of officials, committees, or legislative bodies.
συνεδριάζω.example_greek: Το διοικητικό συμβούλιο συνεδριάζει κάθε Δευτέρα.
συνεδριάζω.example_english: The board of directors meets every Monday.

συνεδρίαση.definition: A gathering of people, typically members of an organization or government body, to discuss matters and make decisions. It refers to a formal meeting or sitting of an official group.
συνεδρίαση.example_greek: Η συνεδρίαση του διοικητικού συμβουλίου θα γίνει αύριο.
συνεδρίαση.example_english: The board of directors' meeting will take place tomorrow.

συνείδηση.definition: The inner sense of right and wrong that guides moral behavior, or the state of being aware and awake. It refers to both moral conscience and conscious awareness.
συνείδηση.example_greek: Η συνείδησή του τον ενοχλούσε για αυτό που έκανε.
συνείδηση.example_english: His conscience troubled him for what he did.

συνειδητοποιώ.translation: to become aware of, to realize
συνειδητοποιώ.definition: To become conscious of something or to make someone aware of something; to realize or recognize the importance or existence of something.
συνειδητοποιώ.example_english: He realized that he made a mistake.

συνεκτικός.definition: Having all parts logically connected and forming a unified whole; characterized by clear logical connection between ideas or elements.
συνεκτικός.example_greek: Ο συνεκτικός λόγος του καθηγητή έκανε το μάθημα πολύ ενδιαφέρον.
συνεκτικός.example_english: The professor's coherent speech made the lesson very interesting.

συνεννόηση.definition: A mutual agreement or understanding reached between two or more parties through discussion or negotiation. It refers to a consensus or accord on a particular matter.
συνεννόηση.example_greek: Η συνεννόηση μεταξύ των δύο χωρών ήταν σημαντική για την ειρήνη.
συνεννόηση.example_english: The agreement between the two countries was important for peace.

συνεννούμαι.translation: to agree, to come to an understanding
συνεννούμαι.definition: A reflexive verb meaning to reach an agreement or mutual understanding with someone, often through discussion or negotiation. It implies a consensual arrangement between parties.
συνεννούμαι.example_greek: Συνεννοήθηκαν για την ώρα της συνάντησης.
συνεννούμαι.example_english: They agreed on the time of the meeting.

συνέπεια.definition: A result or effect that follows from an action or cause. Also refers to logical consistency or coherence in behavior and statements.
συνέπεια.example_greek: Οι κακές αποφάσεις έχουν σοβαρές συνέπειες.

συνεπής.definition: Following logically from something; demonstrating agreement between different parts or statements. Characterized by logical consistency and absence of contradiction.
συνεπής.example_greek: Οι απόψεις του ήταν συνεπής με τις προηγούμενες δηλώσεις του.
συνεπής.example_english: His opinions were consistent with his previous statements.

συνεπώς.definition: An adverb indicating a logical consequence or result following from a previous statement. It connects ideas to show cause-and-effect relationships.
συνεπώς.example_greek: Δεν έχει αρκετή εμπειρία, συνεπώς δεν μπορεί να πάρει τη θέση.
συνεπώς.example_english: He doesn't have enough experience, therefore he cannot get the position.

συνηθέστερα.definition: In a more frequent or habitual manner; more often than not. It is the comparative form of the adverb 'συνήθως' (usually).
συνηθέστερα.example_greek: Συνηθέστερα πηγαίνουμε στην παραλία το καλοκαίρι.
συνηθέστερα.example_english: We more commonly go to the beach in summer.

συνηθιζώ.translation: to accustom, to get used to
συνηθιζώ.definition: To become familiar with something through repeated experience or practice. Also means to accustom someone else to something.
συνηθιζώ.example_greek: Συνήθισα στο κρύο κλίμα μετά από έναν χρόνο.
συνηθιζώ.example_english: I got used to the cold climate after a year.

συνηθίζω.translation: to accustom, to get used to
συνηθίζω.definition: To make someone familiar with something through repeated exposure, or to become accustomed to a habit or situation. It means to develop a habit or become comfortable with something over time.
συνηθίζω.example_greek: Συνηθίζω τα παιδιά να τρώνε υγιεινά.
συνηθίζω.example_english: I accustom the children to eating healthily.

συνηθισμένος.definition: Describing someone who is familiar with or adapted to something through repeated experience or habit. It can also mean something that is done regularly or customarily.
συνηθισμένος.example_english: I am used to waking up early.

συνθήκη.definition: A formal agreement between two or more parties, or a circumstance or requirement that must be met. It can refer to international treaties, contracts, or general conditions.
συνθήκη.example_greek: Υπέγραψαν μια συνθήκη ειρήνης μεταξύ των δύο χωρών.
συνθήκη.example_english: They signed a peace treaty between the two countries.

συνθηκολογώ.translation: to negotiate a treaty or agreement
συνθηκολογώ.definition: To engage in diplomatic negotiations for the purpose of concluding a treaty, agreement, or peace settlement. It refers to the formal process of discussing and arranging terms between parties.
συνθηκολογώ.example_greek: Οι δύο χώρες συνθηκολογούν για να επιτύχουν ένα εμπορικό συμφωνίας.
συνθηκολογώ.example_english: The two countries are negotiating to reach a trade agreement.

συνιθίζω.definition: To make someone or oneself familiar or accustomed to something through repeated exposure or practice. It means to get used to a habit or situation.
συνιθίζω.example_greek: Με τον καιρό συνιθίστηκα στο κρύο κλίμα.
συνιθίζω.example_english: Over time, I became accustomed to the cold climate.

συνιστώ.definition: To suggest or propose something to someone as being good or suitable. It can also mean to establish or set up.
συνιστώ.example_greek: Σας συνιστώ να επισκεφθείτε αυτό το εστιατόριο.
συνιστώ.example_english: I recommend that you visit this restaurant.

σύννεφο.definition: A visible mass of water droplets or ice crystals suspended in the atmosphere. Clouds form when water vapor condenses in the air.

συνοδεύομαι.translation: to be accompanied, to go with
συνοδεύομαι.definition: A passive or middle voice verb meaning to travel or move together with someone, or to be accompanied by someone or something. It can also mean to be accompanied by a feeling or condition.
συνοδεύομαι.example_greek: Η επίσκεψή του συνοδεύεται από μεγάλη ανησυχία.
συνοδεύομαι.example_english: His visit is accompanied by great concern.

συνοδευόμενος.definition: Describing someone who is accompanied or escorted by another person or group. It is the passive participle form of the verb συνοδεύω (to accompany).
συνοδευόμενος.example_greek: Ο συνοδευόμενος προέδρος έφτασε στο αεροδρόμιο με τη δική του ασφάλεια.
συνοδευόμενος.example_english: The accompanied president arrived at the airport with his own security.

συνομιλητής.definition: A person who participates in a conversation or dialogue with another person. Someone you are speaking with.
συνομιλητής.example_greek: Ο συνομιλητής μου ήταν πολύ ενδιαφέρον άτομο.
συνομιλητής.example_english: My conversation partner was a very interesting person.

συνονθύλευμα.definition: A secret agreement or plot between two or more people to do something unlawful or harmful. A conspiracy or collusion to deceive or harm others.
συνονθύλευμα.example_greek: Οι αρχές αποκάλυψαν ένα συνονθύλευμα για την ανατροπή της κυβέρνησης.
συνονθύλευμα.example_english: The authorities uncovered a conspiracy to overthrow the government.

συνταγματολόγος.definition: A legal professional or scholar who specializes in constitutional law and the study of constitutions. This person has expertise in analyzing and interpreting constitutional matters.
συνταγματολόγος.example_greek: Ο συνταγματολόγος έδωσε την γνώμη του για το νέο νόμο.
συνταγματολόγος.example_english: The constitutional law expert gave his opinion on the new law.

συντάσσομαι.translation: to be arranged, to be organized
συντάσσομαι.definition: A passive or middle voice verb meaning to be arranged, organized, or composed in a structured way. It can also mean to agree or cooperate with others.
συντάσσομαι.example_greek: Οι παίκτες συντάσσονταν για το μεγάλο αγώνα.
συντάσσομαι.example_english: The players were organizing themselves for the big match.

συντήρηση.definition: The act of keeping something in good condition or preserving it. It refers to regular care and repair work needed to prevent deterioration.
συντήρηση.example_greek: Η συντήρηση του αυτοκινήτου είναι πολύ σημαντική.
συντήρηση.example_english: The maintenance of the car is very important.

συντηρώ.definition: To keep something in good condition by performing regular care and repairs. To preserve or maintain the existence or functioning of something.
συντηρώ.example_greek: Πρέπει να συντηρούμε το αυτοκίνητό μας τακτικά.
συντηρώ.example_english: We must maintain our car regularly.

σύντομα.definition: In a short time; in the near future. Also means in a concise or brief manner.

συντονίζω.translation: to tune, to coordinate, to synchronize
συντονίζω.definition: To adjust or align something to work together harmoniously; to tune an instrument or coordination between people or systems.
συντονίζω.example_greek: Πρέπει να συντονίσουμε τα σχέδιά μας για το έργο.
συντονίζω.example_english: We need to coordinate our plans for the project.

συντονισμός.definition: The process of adjusting or aligning something to work in harmony with something else. In physics, it refers to the state of resonance between vibrating systems.
συντονισμός.example_greek: Ο συντονισμός των χρωμάτων στο δωμάτιο είναι τέλειος.
συντονισμός.example_english: The coordination of the colors in the room is perfect.
