συνυπευθυνότητα.definition: The state or condition of being responsible together with others for something. It refers to shared liability or mutual accountability between two or more parties.
συνυπευθυνότητα.example_greek: Η συνυπευθυνότητα των εταίρων για τα χρέη της εταιρείας είναι νομικά δεσμευτική.
συνυπευθυνότητα.example_english: The joint responsibility of the partners for the company's debts is legally binding.

συνυπολογίζω.translation: to take into account together, to consider jointly
συνυπολογίζω.definition: To calculate or consider something together with other factors or elements as part of a comprehensive evaluation. It means to include something in a calculation or assessment along with other relevant items.
συνυπολογίζω.example_greek: Πρέπει να συνυπολογίσουμε και τις πρόσθετες δαπάνες στον προϋπολογισμό.
συνυπολογίζω.example_english: We need to take the additional expenses into account in the budget.

συνυποφήφιος.definition: A person who runs as a candidate alongside another candidate for the same position or office. Someone competing with others for the same post or nomination.
συνυποφήφιος.example_greek: Ο συνυποφήφιός του στις εκλογές ήταν ένας γνωστός πολιτικός.
συνυποφήφιος.example_english: His co-candidate in the elections was a well-known politician.

συνυφαίνω.definition: To weave or braid together; to combine or integrate different elements into a unified whole. In modern usage, it can mean to connect or link things together.
συνυφαίνω.example_greek: Ο συγγραφέας συνυφαίνει διάφορα νήματα της ιστορίας σε ένα συνεκτικό αφήγημα.
συνυφαίνω.example_english: The author weaves together various threads of the story into a coherent narrative.

συνυφασμένος.definition: Describing something that is closely interwoven or connected together as if by weaving. It can be used literally for physical threads or figuratively for concepts, relationships, or ideas that are deeply intertwined.
συνυφασμένος.example_greek: Η ιστορία και η πολιτική είναι συνυφασμένες στη σύγχρονη κοινωνία.
συνυφασμένος.example_english: History and politics are interwoven in modern society.

συνωμοσία.definition: A secret plan or agreement made by a group of people to do something harmful or illegal, often used historically to describe a solemn oath or pledge made collectively.
συνωμοσία.example_greek: Η συνωμοσία κατά του τυράννου αποκαλύφθηκε πριν εκτελεστεί.
συνωμοσία.example_english: The conspiracy against the tyrant was revealed before it could be carried out.

συνωμοσιολογία.definition: The study, discussion, or promotion of conspiracy theories; the belief in or tendency to attribute events to secret conspiracies rather than public causes.
συνωμοσιολογία.example_greek: Η συνωμοσιολογία έχει αυξηθεί με την εξάπλωση των κοινωνικών μέσων.
συνωμοσιολογία.example_english: Conspiracy theory has increased with the spread of social media.

συριζαϊκός.definition: An adjective describing something that pertains to or is associated with Syriza, the left-wing political party in Greece founded in 2004.
συριζαϊκός.example_greek: Ο συριζαϊκός κυβερνήτης υποστήριξε νέες πολιτικές.
συριζαϊκός.example_english: The Syriza-affiliated government supported new policies.

συρρικνώνω.definition: To become smaller or to make something smaller in size or volume. It can be used literally for physical objects or figuratively for situations and circumstances.
συρρικνώνω.example_greek: Το ύφασμα συρρικνώθηκε όταν το έπλυνα με ζεστό νερό.
συρρικνώνω.example_english: The fabric shrank when I washed it with hot water.

συρρίκνωση.definition: The process or result of becoming smaller in size, volume, or extent. It can refer to physical shrinkage of materials or metaphorical reduction in quantity, value, or importance.
συρρίκνωση.example_greek: Η συρρίκνωση του πληθυσμού είναι ένα σοβαρό πρόβλημα για τη χώρα.
συρρίκνωση.example_english: Population shrinkage is a serious problem for the country.

συσκευή.definition: A piece of equipment or machinery designed to perform a specific function. Can refer to any mechanical or electrical device used for practical purposes.
συσκευή.example_greek: Αγόρασα μια νέα συσκευή για την κουζίνα μου.
συσκευή.example_english: I bought a new device for my kitchen.

συσπειρώνω.translation: to coil up, to curl into a spiral
συσπειρώνω.definition: To wind or twist into a coil or spiral shape; to form a helical pattern by rolling or coiling something tightly upon itself.
συσπειρώνω.example_english: The snake coiled up in the bucket.

συσσώρευση.definition: The process or result of accumulating or gathering things together in large quantities over time. It refers to the gradual collection or concentration of materials, resources, or conditions.
συσσώρευση.example_greek: Η συσσώρευση των απορριμμάτων είναι ένα σοβαρό περιβαλλοντικό πρόβλημα.
συσσώρευση.example_english: The accumulation of waste is a serious environmental problem.

συσσωρεύω.definition: To gather or heap together in large quantities; to accumulate over time. Often used to describe the buildup of things like debt, trash, or other materials.
συσσωρεύω.example_greek: Τα χρέη συσσωρεύονται κάθε μήνα που δεν πληρώνουμε.
συσσωρεύω.example_english: The debts accumulate every month that we don't pay.

σύσταση.definition: A suggestion or endorsement of someone or something; alternatively, the way something is made up or organized.
σύσταση.example_greek: Έχω μια σύσταση για έναν καλό γιατρό.
σύσταση.example_english: I have a recommendation for a good doctor.

συστήνω.definition: To present or make known someone to another person, or to recommend something as worthy of consideration or use.
συστήνω.example_greek: Σας συστήνω τον φίλο μου Γιάννη.
συστήνω.example_english: I introduce to you my friend Yiannis.

συσχετισμός.definition: A mutual connection or relationship between two or more things; the degree to which two variables are related or dependent on each other.
συσχετισμός.example_greek: Υπάρχει ένας ισχυρός συσχετισμός μεταξύ της μελέτης και των καλών βαθμών.
συσχετισμός.example_english: There is a strong correlation between studying and good grades.

σφαγή.definition: The violent killing of many people or animals; a massacre or slaughter. It can also refer to the killing of an animal for food.
σφαγή.example_greek: Η σφαγή των αθώων ανθρώπων ήταν μια τραγική συμβάν.
σφαγή.example_english: The slaughter of innocent people was a tragic event.

σφικτός.definition: Describes something that is tightly compressed, firmly held together, or densely packed with little space between elements.
σφικτός.example_greek: Φόρεσα ένα σφικτό παντελόνι που δεν μπορώ να κινηθώ άνετα.
σφικτός.example_english: I wore tight pants that I cannot move comfortably in.

σφοδρότητα.definition: The quality or state of being intense, violent, or forceful. It refers to the degree of strength or severity with which something occurs or is expressed.
σφοδρότητα.example_greek: Η σφοδρότητα της καταιγίδας έκλεισε τα σχολεία.
σφοδρότητα.example_english: The intensity of the storm closed the schools.

σφυρί.definition: A tool with a heavy metal head mounted on a handle, used for driving nails, breaking things, or shaping materials.
σφυρί.example_greek: Χρησιμοποίησε ένα σφυρί για να καρφώσει τον καρφί.
σφυρί.example_english: He used a hammer to drive the nail.

σχετίζω.definition: To establish a connection or relationship between two or more things; to link or associate. Often used in the passive voice to indicate that something is related or connected to something else.
σχετίζω.example_greek: Αυτό το πρόβλημα σχετίζεται με την οικονομία.
σχετίζω.example_english: This problem is related to the economy.

σχήμα.definition: The external form or outline of something; a geometric figure or design. Can also refer to a schema or pattern in abstract contexts.
σχήμα.example_greek: Το σχήμα του τριγώνου είναι πολύ σημαντικό στη γεωμετρία.
σχήμα.example_english: The shape of the triangle is very important in geometry.

σχηματισμός.definition: The process or result of forming or shaping something; the way something is organized or structured. It can refer to physical formation or the organization of groups, concepts, or ideas.
σχηματισμός.example_greek: Ο σχηματισμός του νέου κόμματος έγινε πολύ γρήγορα.
σχηματισμός.example_english: The formation of the new party happened very quickly.

σχολιάζω.definition: To make comments or remarks about something, typically expressing an opinion or observation. It can also mean to annotate or provide explanatory notes.
σχολιάζω.example_greek: Δεν θέλω να σχολιάσω το γεγονός αυτό.
σχολιάζω.example_english: I don't want to comment on this fact.

σχόλιο.definition: A brief expression of opinion or explanation about something. An annotation or remark added to clarify or respond to a statement or text.
σχόλιο.example_greek: Έχεις κάποιο σχόλιο για την παρουσίασή μου;
σχόλιο.example_english: Do you have any comment on my presentation?

σωζόμενος.definition: Present middle participle of σώζω (sózo), meaning one who is being saved, rescued, or surviving. Can function as an adjective describing someone in the state of being rescued or preserved.
σωζόμενος.example_greek: Οι σωζόμενοι από το ναυάγιο περίμεναν τη βοήθεια.
σωζόμενος.example_english: The survivors from the shipwreck waited for help.

σώζω.definition: To rescue someone from danger or harm, or to preserve something from being lost or destroyed.
σώζω.example_greek: Ο πυροσβέστης έσωσε το παιδί από τη φωτιά.
σώζω.example_english: The firefighter saved the child from the fire.

σωρευτικός.definition: Relating to or characterized by accumulation or the process of gathering together over time. Describes something that increases or builds up gradually.
σωρευτικός.example_greek: Η σωρευτική επίδραση των μικρών αλλαγών ήταν σημαντική.
σωρευτικός.example_english: The cumulative effect of small changes was significant.

ΤΑΙΠΕΔ.definition: A woven or knitted fabric covering for floors, typically made from wool or synthetic materials and used for decoration and insulation.
ΤΑΙΠΕΔ.example_greek: Το ταπέδο του σαλονιού είναι πολύ όμορφο.
ΤΑΙΠΕΔ.example_english: The living room carpet is very beautiful.

τακτοποίηση.definition: The act of arranging, organizing, or settling something in order. It can refer to putting things in order, resolving a matter, or making practical arrangements.
τακτοποίηση.example_greek: Η τακτοποίηση των εγγράφων πήρε πολύ χρόνο.
τακτοποίηση.example_english: The organization of the documents took a lot of time.

ταλαιπωρώ.translation: to torment, to afflict, to trouble
ταλαιπωρώ.definition: To cause suffering or hardship to someone; to torment or distress. It can also mean to struggle or toil with difficulty.
ταλαιπωρώ.example_greek: Η ασθένεια τον ταλαιπωρεί για χρόνια.
ταλαιπωρώ.example_english: The illness has been tormenting him for years.

ταμειακά.definition: Plural form of ταμειακό referring to money, cash, or financial resources held in a treasury or account.
ταμειακά.example_greek: Τα ταμειακά της εταιρείας αυξήθηκαν αυτό το τρίμηνο.
ταμειακά.example_english: The company's cash reserves increased this quarter.

ταμείο.definition: A place where money is kept or exchanged, such as a cashier's counter in a shop or a government treasury office.
ταμείο.example_greek: Πήγαινε στο ταμείο για να πληρώσεις.
ταμείο.example_english: Go to the cashier to pay.

τάση.definition: A general direction or inclination toward something; a prevailing tendency or movement in a particular direction. Can also mean electrical or mechanical tension.
τάση.example_greek: Υπάρχει μια τάση για αύξηση των τιμών στην αγορά.
τάση.example_english: There is a tendency for prices to increase in the market.

τάσσομαι.translation: to be arranged, to be assigned
τάσσομαι.definition: A middle voice verb meaning to be arranged, positioned, or assigned to a particular place or role. It can also mean to be stationed or deployed.
τάσσομαι.example_greek: Οι στρατιώτες τάσσονται στη γραμμή άμυνας.
τάσσομαι.example_english: The soldiers are positioned on the defensive line.

τάσσω.translation: to arrange, to post, to assign
τάσσω.definition: To put in order, arrange, or assign someone to a position or task. It can also mean to station or post someone in a particular location.
τάσσω.example_greek: Ο δάσκαλος τάσσει τους μαθητές σε σειρές.
τάσσω.example_english: The teacher arranges the students in rows.

ταυτίζω.translation: to identify, to equate, to regard as the same
ταυτίζω.definition: To consider or treat two things as being identical or equivalent. To establish that something is the same as something else.
ταυτίζω.example_greek: Δεν πρέπει να ταυτίζουμε την επιτυχία με το χρήμα.
ταυτίζω.example_english: We should not equate success with money.

ταύτιση.definition: The process or act of identifying something or someone, or establishing the identity of a person or object. It can also refer to psychological identification with someone or something.
ταύτιση.example_greek: Η ταύτιση του ύπόπτου ήταν δύσκολη για την αστυνομία.
ταύτιση.example_english: The identification of the suspect was difficult for the police.

ταυτοχρονία.translation: simultaneity, occurrence at the same time
ταυτοχρονία.definition: The quality or state of happening, existing, or occurring at exactly the same time. The condition of being simultaneous.
ταυτοχρονία.example_greek: Η ταυτοχρονία των δύο γεγονότων ήταν καθαρά συμπτωματική.
ταυτοχρονία.example_english: The simultaneity of the two events was purely coincidental.

ταχύτερος.definition: Comparative form of ταχύς (fast). Describes something that moves or happens at greater speed than something else.
ταχύτερος.example_greek: Αυτό το αυτοκίνητο είναι ταχύτερο από το άλλο.
ταχύτερος.example_english: This car is faster than the other one.

τεθνεώς.definition: In a state of being dead; no longer alive. This is a literary or formal term, often used in official or legal contexts to refer to someone who has passed away.
τεθνεώς.example_greek: Ο τεθνεώς πατέρας του ήταν πολύ σημαντική προσωπικότητα.
τεθνεώς.example_english: His deceased father was a very important figure.

τεκταινόμενα.definition: Present participle form of τεκταίνω (tektaíno), meaning structures or buildings that are in the process of being built or constructed.
τεκταινόμενα.example_greek: Τα τεκταινόμενα κτίρια στο κέντρο της πόλης θα ολοκληρωθούν το επόμενο έτος.
τεκταινόμενα.example_english: The buildings being constructed in the city center will be completed next year.

τελειωμένος.definition: Describing something that has been brought to completion or is fully finished. Can also mean perfect or polished when referring to quality.

τελετουργικός.definition: Relating to or involving formal religious or ceremonial rituals and procedures. Describing something that is performed as part of a solemn ceremony or formal observance.
τελετουργικός.example_greek: Η τελετουργική διαδικασία της λειτουργίας είναι πολύ σημαντική στην ορθόδοξη παράδοση.
τελετουργικός.example_english: The ceremonial procedure of the liturgy is very important in the Orthodox tradition.

τέλος πάντων.translation: in any case, after all, anyway
τέλος πάντων.definition: An idiomatic Greek phrase meaning 'in the end' or 'anyway', used to conclude a thought or to indicate that something is true regardless of other considerations.
τέλος πάντων.example_greek: Τέλος πάντων, έκανε καλά τη δουλειά του.
τέλος πάντων.example_english: Anyway, he did his job well.

τερματίζω.translation: to finish, to end, to terminate
τερματίζω.definition: To bring something to an end or conclusion. In sports, to finish a race or competition by crossing the finish line.
τερματίζω.example_greek: Ο δρομέας τερμάτισε πρώτος στην αγωνία.
τερματίζω.example_english: The runner finished first in the race.

τετραετία.definition: A period of four years, commonly used to refer to a four-year term of office or a sports competition cycle. It is derived from the prefix 'tetra-' (four) and 'etia' (year).
τετραετία.example_greek: Η τετραετία του Προέδρου της Δημοκρατίας διαρκεί τέσσερα χρόνια.
τετραετία.example_english: The four-year term of the President of the Republic lasts four years.

τετραήμερος.definition: An adjective describing something that lasts for or occurs over a period of four days. It is a compound word derived from 'τέσσερα' (four) and 'ημέρα' (day).
τετραήμερος.example_greek: Ο τετραήμερος διαγωνισμός ήταν πολύ επίπονος.
τετραήμερος.example_english: The four-day competition was very demanding.

τεφρός.definition: Having the color of ash; grayish in appearance. Often used to describe a dull gray or ashen hue.
τεφρός.example_greek: Ο ουρανός ήταν τεφρός και συννεφιασμένος.
τεφρός.example_english: The sky was gray and cloudy.

τέχνασμα.definition: A clever or cunning device, trick, or stratagem used to deceive or outmaneuver someone. It refers to a skillfully devised plan or tactic.
τέχνασμα.example_greek: Χρησιμοποίησε ένα τέχνασμα για να ξεφύγει από τους εχθρούς του.
τέχνασμα.example_english: He used a trick to escape from his enemies.

τεχνοκρατικός.definition: Relating to or characteristic of technocracy, a system of governance by technical experts and specialists. It describes policies, approaches, or attitudes that prioritize technical and scientific expertise over political or democratic processes.
τεχνοκρατικός.example_greek: Η κυβέρνηση ακολούθησε μια τεχνοκρατική προσέγγιση στην επίλυση των οικονομικών προβλημάτων.
τεχνοκρατικός.example_english: The government followed a technocratic approach to solving economic problems.

τέως.definition: Up to the present time; until this moment. An archaic or formal adverb indicating a period extending to the current point in time.
τέως.example_greek: Τέως δεν είχε φανεί κανένα πρόβλημα.
τέως.example_english: Until now, no problem had appeared.

τζίρος.definition: The total amount of money taken in by a business from sales of goods or services over a specific period, regardless of profit or loss.
τζίρος.example_greek: Το κατάστημα έχει μεγάλο τζίρο κάθε μήνα.
τζίρος.example_english: The store has high turnover every month.

τηλεπικοινωνικός.definition: Relating to or involving the transmission of information over distance using electronic means such as telephone, radio, television, or internet.
τηλεπικοινωνικός.example_greek: Η τηλεπικοινωνιακή βιομηχανία αναπτύσσεται γρήγορα στη χώρα μας.
τηλεπικοινωνικός.example_english: The telecommunications industry is developing rapidly in our country.

τήραση.definition: The act of observing, keeping, or maintaining something; adherence to rules or customs. It can also mean custody or guardianship.
τήραση.example_greek: Η τήραση των κανόνων είναι σημαντική στο σχολείο.
τήραση.example_english: The observance of rules is important at school.

τήρηση.definition: The act of observing, keeping, or complying with rules, laws, or customs. It can also mean watching or monitoring something carefully.
τήρηση.example_greek: Η τήρηση των νόμων είναι υποχρεωτική για όλους.
τήρηση.example_english: The observance of laws is mandatory for everyone.

τηρώ.translation: to keep, to observe, to maintain
τηρώ.definition: To keep something in a certain state or condition; to observe rules, laws, or customs; to preserve or maintain something.
τηρώ.example_greek: Τηρώ τις παραδόσεις της οικογένειάς μου.

τθλίγω.translation: to grieve, to sadden, to distress
τθλίγω.definition: To cause sorrow, grief, or sadness to someone; to distress or upset emotionally.
τθλίγω.example_greek: Η είδηση θλίβει πολύ τους γονείς του.
τθλίγω.example_english: The news greatly saddens his parents.

τίθεμαι.definition: A middle voice verb meaning to place, put, or set something in a position. It can also mean to establish or propose something.
τίθεμαι.example_greek: Τίθεμαι τον στόχο να τελειώσω το έργο αύριο.
τίθεμαι.example_english: I set the goal to finish the work tomorrow.

τίμια.definition: Having great worth or value; deserving respect and esteem. Can describe both material worth and moral qualities.
τίμια.example_greek: Τα τίμια κοσμήματα ήταν κρυμμένα στο χρηματοκιβώτιο.
τίμια.example_english: The precious jewels were hidden in the safe.

τιμόνι.definition: The wheel or device used to steer a vehicle, ship, or aircraft. It can also refer to the rudder of a boat or the control mechanism for direction.
τιμόνι.example_greek: Ο οδηγός κρατούσε το τιμόνι με σταθερά χέρια.
τιμόνι.example_english: The driver held the steering wheel with steady hands.

τμήμα.definition: A distinct section, division, or part of something larger, such as a department in an organization or a section of a text. It can also refer to a portion or piece of a whole.
τμήμα.example_greek: Δουλεύω στο τμήμα πωλήσεων της εταιρείας.
τμήμα.example_english: I work in the sales department of the company.

ΤΟ.definition: The definite article used before neuter singular nouns in Modern Greek. It denotes a specific, previously mentioned, or known neuter noun.

τοίχος.definition: A vertical structure made of brick, stone, or other materials that forms the side of a building or encloses an area.
τοίχος.example_greek: Ο τοίχος του σπιτιού είναι άσπρος.
τοίχος.example_english: The wall of the house is white.

τόκος.definition: In financial contexts, it refers to interest charged on a loan or earned on an investment. Historically, it can also mean offspring or progeny.
τόκος.example_english: The loan has an annual interest rate of 5%.

τολμηλός.definition: Showing courage and willingness to take risks; characterized by boldness or daring behavior.
τολμηλός.example_greek: Ήταν ένα τολμηλό σχέδιο που δεν περίμενε κανείς.
τολμηλός.example_english: It was a bold plan that nobody expected.

τομή.definition: The act of cutting or dividing something into parts; also refers to a section or cross-section of an object.
τομή.example_greek: Ο γιατρός έκανε μια τομή για την εγχείρηση.
τομή.example_english: The doctor made an incision for the surgery.

τόμος.definition: A bound collection of printed pages forming a single book, especially one of several volumes in a larger work or series.
τόμος.example_greek: Αυτό το μυθιστόρημα έχει τρεις τόμους.

τοξικότητα.definition: The quality or state of being toxic or poisonous. The degree to which a substance can harm or damage living organisms.
τοξικότητα.example_greek: Η τοξικότητα του χημικού προϊόντος είναι πολύ υψηλή.
τοξικότητα.example_english: The toxicity of the chemical product is very high.

τοποθέτηση.definition: The act of placing or positioning something in a specific location or the expression of a viewpoint or opinion on a matter.
τοποθέτηση.example_greek: Η τοποθέτηση των επίπλων στο σαλόνι ήταν δύσκολη.
τοποθέτηση.example_english: The placement of the furniture in the living room was difficult.

τοποθετώ.translation: to place, to position, to put
τοποθετώ.definition: To put something in a specific location or position. Can also mean to express or state an opinion.
τοποθετώ.example_english: I placed the books on the shelf.

τόπος.definition: A physical space, area, or location where something is situated or happens. Can also refer to a topic or subject matter in abstract contexts.
τόπος.example_greek: Αυτός είναι ένας όμορφος τόπος για να κάνουμε πικνίκ.
τόπος.example_english: This is a beautiful place to have a picnic.

τόσο καίρο.translation: for so long, such a long time
τόσο καίρο.definition: A phrase expressing a prolonged duration or extended period of time. It indicates that something has lasted or will last for a considerable length of time.
τόσο καίρο.example_english: I haven't seen you for such a long time.

τούτος.definition: A demonstrative pronoun used to indicate something or someone near the speaker or already mentioned in discourse. It can function as a standalone pronoun or as an adjective modifying a noun.
τούτος.example_greek: Τούτος ο άνθρωπος είναι πολύ καλός.

τούτου.definition: Genitive form of the demonstrative pronoun 'τούτος' (this/that one). It indicates possession or relationship to something previously mentioned or nearby.

τραβήξω.translation: I will pull, I will draw
τραβήξω.definition: Future simple tense of τραβώ (travó). It means to pull, drag, draw, or attract something toward oneself.
τραβήξω.example_greek: Θα τραβήξω το σχοινί με δύναμη.
τραβήξω.example_english: I will pull the rope with force.

τραγελαφικός.definition: Describing something that is absurdly incongruous or ridiculous, combining incompatible or contradictory elements. The term derives from the mythical tragelaphus (a hybrid creature part goat, part deer).
τραγελαφικός.example_greek: Η ιδέα του να πετάξει χωρίς φτερά ήταν τραγελαφική.
τραγελαφικός.example_english: The idea of flying without wings was absurd.

τραγέλαφος.definition: A legendary creature from Greek mythology that is a hybrid of a goat and a deer, or more broadly, any bizarre or impossible combination of things.
τραγέλαφος.example_greek: Ο τραγέλαφος είναι ένα μυθικό όν που δεν υπάρχει στην πραγματικότητα.
τραγέλαφος.example_english: The tragelaphus is a mythical being that does not exist in reality.

τράπουλα.definition: A set of cards used for playing various card games, typically consisting of 52 cards divided into four suits.
τράπουλα.example_greek: Παίζουμε τράπουλα κάθε Παρασκευή το βράδυ.
τράπουλα.example_english: We play cards every Friday evening.

τρέχουσα.definition: Present participle form of the verb τρέχω (to run) used as an adjective, meaning actively running or currently flowing. Can also mean 'current' or 'ongoing' in modern context.
τρέχουσα.example_greek: Η τρέχουσα κατάσταση της οικονομίας είναι δύσκολη.
τρέχουσα.example_english: The current state of the economy is difficult.

τρέχων.definition: Present active participle of τρέχω (trécho), meaning someone or something that is running or in motion. Can also mean current or present in time.
τρέχων.example_greek: Ο τρέχων άνθρωπος είναι πολύ γρήγορος.
τρέχων.example_english: The running man is very fast.

τριανδρία.definition: A botanical term referring to a plant or flower that has three stamens or three male reproductive organs. It is a classification used in plant taxonomy, particularly in Linnaean classification systems.
τριανδρία.example_greek: Η τριανδρία είναι μια κατηγορία φυτών με τρία στήματα.
τριανδρία.example_english: Triandry is a category of plants with three stamens.

τριγωνικός.definition: Having the shape of a triangle; relating to or characterized by three angles or corners.
τριγωνικός.example_greek: Το τριγωνικό σχήμα είναι πολύ σημαντικό στη γεωμετρία.
τριγωνικός.example_english: The triangular shape is very important in geometry.

τριμηνιαίος.definition: Occurring or produced every three months; lasting for a period of three months.
τριμηνιαίος.example_greek: Η τριμηνιαία αναφορά της εταιρείας δείχνει καλή ανάπτυξη.
τριμηνιαίος.example_english: The company's quarterly report shows good growth.

τρολάρω.definition: To deliberately post inflammatory or off-topic messages on the internet to provoke reactions from others; to engage in trolling behavior.
τρολάρω.example_greek: Δεν πρέπει να τρολάρεις τους ανθρώπους στα social media.
τρολάρω.example_english: You shouldn't troll people on social media.

τρομακτικός.definition: Causing great fear or terror; extremely frightening or alarming in appearance or nature.
τρομακτικός.example_greek: Ήταν μια τρομακτική εμπειρία που δεν θα ξεχάσει ποτέ.
τρομακτικός.example_english: It was a terrifying experience that he will never forget.

τρομερός.definition: Causing fear or dread; of very poor quality or unpleasant. Can describe something terrifying or simply very bad.
τρομερός.example_greek: Είχε μια τρομερή εμπειρία στο αεροπλάνο.
τρομερός.example_english: He had a terrible experience on the airplane.

τροποποίηση.definition: The act or process of changing or adapting something to make it different or improved. A change made to an existing plan, document, or object.
τροποποίηση.example_greek: Η τροποποίηση του νόμου έγινε αναγκαία για να προστατευθούν τα δικαιώματα των πολιτών.
τροποποίηση.example_english: The amendment of the law became necessary to protect the rights of citizens.

τροποποιώ.translation: to modify, to change, to alter
τροποποιώ.definition: To change or adjust something by making alterations to its form, structure, or characteristics. It means to transform or adapt something from its original state.
τροποποιώ.example_greek: Πρέπει να τροποποιήσουμε το σχέδιο για να ταιριάζει στις νέες απαιτήσεις.
τροποποιώ.example_english: We need to modify the plan to fit the new requirements.

τρόπος.definition: A method or manner of doing something; a way in which something happens or is done. Also refers to a particular style or characteristic approach.
τρόπος.example_greek: Μου αρέσει ο τρόπος που μιλάει.
τρόπος.example_english: I like the way she speaks.

τροτσκιστές.definition: Followers of Trotskyism, a form of Marxist communism based on the theories of Leon Trotsky that emphasizes permanent revolution and international socialist revolution.
τροτσκιστές.example_greek: Οι τροτσκιστές υποστήριζαν τη διεθνή επανάσταση.

τροφοδοτώ.translation: to feed, to supply, to nourish
τροφοδοτώ.definition: To provide food or sustenance to someone or something; to supply with necessary materials or resources. It can be used both literally (feeding a person or animal) and figuratively (supplying information or ideas).
τροφοδοτώ.example_greek: Ο παππούς τροφοδοτεί τα πουλιά κάθε πρωί.
τροφοδοτώ.example_english: The grandfather feeds the birds every morning.

τροχιά.definition: The curved path or route followed by a moving object, such as a planet in orbit or a projectile in flight.
τροχιά.example_greek: Η τροχιά της Σελήνης γύρω από τη Γη είναι ελλειπτική.
τροχιά.example_english: The Moon's orbit around the Earth is elliptical.

τροχία.definition: The curved path followed by a moving object through space, such as a projectile, planet, or celestial body.
τροχία.example_greek: Η τροχία του δορυφόρου γύρω από τη Γη είναι σχεδόν κυκλική.
τροχία.example_english: The satellite's trajectory around Earth is almost circular.

τρυπημένος.definition: Having holes made through it; perforated or damaged by drilling or piercing. Can also mean figuratively 'hurt' or 'offended'.
τρυπημένος.example_greek: Η σακούλα ήταν τρυπημένη και έχυνε άμμο.
τρυπημένος.example_english: The bag was torn with holes and was leaking sand.

τρύπιος.definition: Having many holes or perforations; pierced or punctured in multiple places.
τρύπιος.example_greek: Το παλιό δίχτυ ήταν τρύπιο και δεν ήταν χρήσιμο πια.
τρύπιος.example_english: The old net was full of holes and was no longer useful.

τσέπη.definition: A small pouch sewn into or attached to clothing, typically used for carrying small items. Also can refer to any small compartment or cavity.
τσέπη.example_greek: Έβαλα το κινητό μου στην τσέπη μου.
τσέπη.example_english: I put my phone in my pocket.

τσιγκούνης.definition: A person who is unwilling to spend money; someone who is extremely frugal or miserly. Often used pejoratively to describe someone who is stingy or cheap.
τσιγκούνης.example_greek: Ο τσιγκούνης δεν ήθελε να πληρώσει για το φαγητό.
τσιγκούνης.example_english: The miser did not want to pay for the food.

τύφλα.definition: The condition of being unable to see; loss of vision or lack of sight. Can also refer to figurative blindness or inability to understand something.
τύφλα.example_greek: Η τύφλα δεν τον εμπόδισε να ζήσει μια ικανοποιητική ζωή.
τύφλα.example_english: Blindness did not prevent him from living a satisfying life.

τυχαίος.definition: Occurring by chance or without being planned or intended; happening unexpectedly or by coincidence rather than through deliberate action.
τυχαίος.example_greek: Η συνάντηση με τον παλιό μου φίλο ήταν εντελώς τυχαία.
τυχαίος.example_english: The meeting with my old friend was completely accidental.

τυχάρπαστος.definition: Describes something obtained or seized by chance or opportunity, often implying a hasty or unplanned acquisition. Can also refer to someone who grabs opportunities as they come.
τυχάρπαστος.example_greek: Η τυχάρπαστη νικη του ήταν αναπάντεχη για όλους.
τυχάρπαστος.example_english: His opportunistic victory was unexpected for everyone.

τύχη.definition: The occurrence of events that happen by chance rather than design; fate or destiny. In Greek mythology, Tyche is the goddess of fortune and prosperity.
τύχη.example_greek: Είχε μεγάλη τύχη να βρει δουλειά τόσο γρήγορα.
τύχη.example_english: He was very lucky to find a job so quickly.

τυχοδιώκτης.definition: A person who seeks wealth or success through luck or opportunistic means rather than through hard work or merit. Someone who pursues easy gains or takes advantage of circumstances.
τυχοδιώκτης.example_greek: Ο τυχοδιώκτης προσπάθησε να κερδίσει χρήματα από ένα επικίνδυνο σχέδιο.
τυχοδιώκτης.example_english: The fortune hunter tried to make money from a risky scheme.

τυχωδιώκτης.definition: A person who pursues opportunities or advantages, especially in an opportunistic or self-serving manner without regard for principles or others' interests.
τυχωδιώκτης.example_greek: Ο τυχωδιώκτης προσπάθησε να εκμεταλλευθεί την κατάσταση για προσωπικό όφελος.
τυχωδιώκτης.example_english: The opportunist tried to exploit the situation for personal gain.

τωρινός.definition: Relating to or existing at the present time; contemporary. Used to describe something that is happening or existing right now.
τωρινός.example_greek: Η τωρινή κυβέρνηση έχει πολλές προκλήσεις.
τωρινός.example_english: The current government faces many challenges.

ύβρις.definition: Excessive pride, arrogance, or disrespect toward others, especially toward gods or authority. In ancient Greek tragedy, it represents the overconfidence that leads to one's downfall.
ύβρις.example_greek: Η ύβρις του βασιλιά τον οδήγησε στην καταστροφή.
ύβρις.example_english: The king's hubris led him to destruction.

υδροδότηση.definition: The provision or distribution of water to households, communities, or areas. It refers to the system and process of delivering water for domestic, commercial, or industrial use.
υδροδότηση.example_greek: Η υδροδότηση του χωριού είναι ένα σημαντικό πρόβλημα που πρέπει να λυθεί.
υδροδότηση.example_english: The water supply of the village is an important problem that needs to be solved.

υιοθέτηση.definition: The legal process of taking a child into one's family and raising them as one's own. It can also refer metaphorically to the acceptance or adoption of ideas, practices, or beliefs.
υιοθέτηση.example_greek: Η υιοθέτηση του παιδιού ήταν ένα από τα ευτυχέστερα γεγονότα της ζωής τους.
υιοθέτηση.example_english: The adoption of the child was one of the happiest events of their lives.

υιοθετώ.definition: to legally take a child into one's family and become its parent. Can also mean to accept or take on an idea or practice as one's own.
υιοθετώ.example_greek: Αποφάσισαν να υιοθετήσουν ένα παιδί από το ορφανοτροφείο.
υιοθετώ.example_english: They decided to adopt a child from the orphanage.

ύλη.definition: Physical substance or material; also refers to a subject of study or topic of discussion.
ύλη.example_greek: Η ύλη του μαθήματος είναι πολύ δύσκολη.
ύλη.example_english: The subject matter of the lesson is very difficult.

υλικός.definition: Relating to matter or physical substance; concerned with material rather than spiritual or intellectual things.
υλικός.example_greek: Τα υλικά αγαθά δεν είναι το πιο σημαντικό στη ζωή.
υλικός.example_english: Material possessions are not the most important thing in life.

υλοποιώ.translation: to implement, to realize, to materialize
υλοποιώ.definition: To turn an idea, plan, or concept into reality or concrete form. To execute or carry out something that was previously only theoretical or planned.
υλοποιώ.example_greek: Θα υλοποιήσουμε το νέο έργο την επόμενη εβδομάδα.
υλοποιώ.example_english: We will implement the new project next week.

ύπαιθρος.definition: Describing something that is in the open air, not covered or indoors, exposed to the sky and elements.
ύπαιθρος.example_greek: Το πάρτι θα γίνει σε ένα ύπαιθρο χώρο κοντά στη θάλασσα.
ύπαιθρος.example_english: The party will take place in an open-air space near the sea.

υπαινιγμός.definition: An indirect or subtle suggestion or reference to something, often implying meaning without stating it directly. A veiled allusion or insinuation.
υπαινιγμός.example_greek: Ο δάσκαλος έκανε έναν υπαινιγμό για τη δύσκολη εξέταση που θα έρχονταν.
υπαινιγμός.example_english: The teacher made a hint about the difficult exam that would be coming.

υπαργορεύω.definition: To speak in a low, soft voice, often in a secretive or conspiratorial manner. It refers to quiet utterances that are barely audible.
υπαργορεύω.example_greek: Οι δύο φίλοι υπαργόρευαν κάτι μεταξύ τους κατά τη διάρκεια της συνάντησης.
υπαργορεύω.example_english: The two friends whispered something to each other during the meeting.

υπαρκτός.definition: Something that exists in reality or is genuine, not imaginary or theoretical. It describes something that has actual existence or presence.
υπαρκτός.example_greek: Αυτό είναι ένα υπαρκτό πρόβλημα που πρέπει να λύσουμε.
υπαρκτός.example_english: This is a real problem that we need to solve.

υπενθυμίζω.translation: to remind, to bring to mind
υπενθυμίζω.definition: To gently remind someone of something they may have forgotten or overlooked. It can also mean to hint at or subtly bring attention to something.
υπενθυμίζω.example_greek: Σας υπενθυμίζω ότι η συνάντηση είναι αύριο στις τρεις.
υπενθυμίζω.example_english: I would like to remind you that the meeting is tomorrow at three o'clock.

υπενθύμιση.definition: A subtle or gentle reminder of something that was previously mentioned or known. It can also refer to the act of reminding someone in a discreet way.
υπενθύμιση.example_greek: Ήθελα να κάνω μια φιλική υπενθύμιση ότι η συνάντηση είναι αύριο.
υπενθύμιση.example_english: I wanted to give you a friendly reminder that the meeting is tomorrow.

υπέρ.definition: A preposition indicating support, advocacy, or position above something. It can mean 'in favor of' or 'for the sake of' when followed by a genitive noun.
υπέρ.example_greek: Μιλούσε υπέρ των δικαιωμάτων των εργαζομένων.
υπέρ.example_english: He spoke in favor of workers' rights.

υπεράνω.definition: Positioned at or to a higher level than something else; also means beyond in terms of surpassing or exceeding something. Often used in formal or literary contexts.
υπεράνω.example_greek: Το πουλί πέταξε υπεράνω του σπιτιού.
υπεράνω.example_english: The bird flew above the house.

υπερασπιστής.definition: A person who defends, protects, or advocates strongly for someone or something. Often used to describe someone who champions a cause or stands up for the rights of others.
υπερασπιστής.example_greek: Ο δικηγόρος ήταν ένας σθεναρός υπερασπιστής των δικαιωμάτων του ανθρώπου.
υπερασπιστής.example_english: The lawyer was a staunch defender of human rights.

υπερβαίνω.translation: to exceed, to surpass, to go beyond
υπερβαίνω.definition: To go beyond a limit, boundary, or expectation; to exceed or surpass something in degree or measure.
υπερβαίνω.example_greek: Η κατανάλωση του ηλεκτρικού ρεύματος υπερβαίνει τα όρια που έχουμε θέσει.
υπερβαίνω.example_english: The electricity consumption exceeds the limits we have set.

υπερβολή.definition: The act of overstating or representing something as greater than it actually is; an extreme or excessive degree of something.
υπερβολή.example_greek: Η λέξη του ήταν μια υπερβολή, δεν ήταν τόσο σοβαρά τα πράγματα.
υπερβολή.example_english: His statement was an exaggeration; things weren't that serious.

υπερκείμενος.definition: Describing something that is positioned above something else or situated in a higher place. It can also mean superior in rank or status.
υπερκείμενος.example_greek: Ο υπερκείμενος στρώμα του εδάφους είναι πολύ εύφορος.
υπερκείμενος.example_english: The overlying layer of soil is very fertile.

υπερκέρδος.definition: An unusually large or excessive profit, especially one obtained unexpectedly or through favorable circumstances. It refers to earnings that significantly exceed normal or anticipated returns.
υπερκέρδος.example_greek: Η εταιρεία έκανε υπερκέρδος λόγω της αύξησης της ζήτησης.
υπερκέρδος.example_english: The company made excessive profits due to increased demand.

υπερξουσία.definition: Absolute or supreme power and authority, often used in religious or philosophical contexts to refer to transcendent power beyond normal authority. The term combines 'hyper' (over/beyond) with 'exousia' (authority/power).
υπερξουσία.example_greek: Η υπερξουσία του Θεού είναι απόλυτη και αναμφισβήτητη.
υπερξουσία.example_english: God's supreme authority is absolute and unquestionable.

υπερψήφιση.definition: The phenomenon where the total number of votes cast exceeds the number of eligible voters, or voting beyond one's constitutional right in a multi-preference voting system.
υπερψήφιση.example_greek: Η υπερψήφιση ήταν ένα σημαντικό ζήτημα στις προηγούμενες εκλογές.
υπερψήφιση.example_english: Overvoting was a significant issue in the previous elections.

υπήκοος.definition: Willing to obey or comply with rules, commands, or authority. Describes someone who shows respect and follows instructions without resistance.
υπήκοος.example_greek: Ο σκύλος είναι πολύ υπήκοος στο αφεντικό του.
υπήκοος.example_english: The dog is very obedient to his master.

υπηρετώ.definition: To work as a servant or employee for someone, or to perform a duty or function in service of something. Can also mean to be useful or beneficial.
υπηρετώ.example_greek: Υπηρέτησε στον στρατό για δέκα χρόνια.
υπηρετώ.example_english: He served in the army for ten years.

υπό.definition: A preposition indicating position below something, or used to show the agent performing an action in passive voice constructions.
υπό.example_greek: Το παιδί κάθεται υπό το δέντρο.
υπό.example_english: The child sits under the tree.

υποβάθμηση.definition: The action of reducing someone to a lower rank, status, or level, or the process of lowering the quality or importance of something.
υποβάθμηση.example_greek: Η υποβάθμηση του αξιωματικού ήταν αποτέλεσμα της διαδικασίας πειθαρχίας.
υποβάθμηση.example_english: The officer's demotion was the result of the disciplinary process.

υπόβαθρο.definition: A platform or base that supports something above it, such as a pedestal for a statue or the foundation of a structure.
υπόβαθρο.example_greek: Το άγαλμα στέκεται πάνω σε ένα όμορφο υπόβαθρο.
υπόβαθρο.example_english: The statue stands on a beautiful pedestal.

υποβάλλω.definition: To present or hand in something officially, such as a document, application, or proposal. It can also mean to subject someone to something.
υποβάλλω.example_greek: Πρέπει να υποβάλω την αίτησή μου μέχρι την Παρασκευή.
υποβάλλω.example_english: I need to submit my application by Friday.

υπόγεια.definition: Located or situated beneath the ground or surface. Can refer to underground spaces, passages, or structures.
υπόγεια.example_greek: Το μετρό είναι ένα υπόγειο δίκτυο μεταφοράς.
υπόγεια.example_english: The metro is an underground transportation network.

υπόγειος.definition: Located or situated beneath the ground or surface. Often used to describe underground structures, passages, or transportation systems.
υπόγειος.example_greek: Το μετρό είναι ένα υπόγειο σύστημα μεταφορών.
υπόγειος.example_english: The metro is an underground transportation system.

υποδηλώνω.translation: to imply, to suggest, to hint at
υποδηλώνω.definition: To indicate or express something indirectly or subtly, without stating it explicitly. To suggest something through implication rather than direct statement.
υποδηλώνω.example_greek: Η απάντησή του υποδηλώνει ότι δεν συμφωνεί με την απόφαση.
υποδηλώνω.example_english: His answer implies that he does not agree with the decision.

υποδοχέας.definition: A person who receives and welcomes visitors, clients, or guests, typically at the entrance of an office, hotel, or similar establishment.
υποδοχέας.example_greek: Ο υποδοχέας του ξενοδοχείου ήταν πολύ ευγενικός.
υποδοχέας.example_english: The hotel receptionist was very polite.

υποθαλάσσιος.definition: Located or occurring beneath the surface of the sea. Relating to or denoting things that exist or happen underwater.
υποθαλάσσιος.example_greek: Η υποθαλάσσια ζωή είναι πολύ διαφορετική από τη χερσαία.
υποθαλάσσιος.example_english: Underwater life is very different from terrestrial life.

υπόθεση.definition: A proposed explanation or theory based on limited evidence, or a tentative assumption made for the sake of argument. It can also refer to a specific case or situation.
υπόθεση.example_greek: Η υπόθεση του επιστήμονα ήταν ενδιαφέρουσα.

υποθετικός.definition: Based on a suggested idea or theory rather than proven fact; expressed as a condition or supposition rather than as something certain or actual.
υποθετικός.example_greek: Σε μια υποθετική περίπτωση, τι θα έκανες;
υποθετικός.example_english: In a hypothetical case, what would you do?

υποθέτω.definition: To assume something as true without proof, or to propose a theory or idea for consideration. It means to make an assumption or put forward a hypothesis.
υποθέτω.example_greek: Υποθέτω ότι θα έρθει αύριο στη συνάντηση.
υποθέτω.example_english: I assume that he will come to the meeting tomorrow.

υποκαθιστώ.definition: To put someone or something in place of another; to act as a substitute or replacement for someone or something else.
υποκαθιστώ.example_greek: Ο δάσκαλος υποκαθιστά τον συνάδελφό του στο μάθημα σήμερα.
υποκαθιστώ.example_english: The teacher is substituting for his colleague in today's lesson.

υπόκειμαι.translation: to be subject to, to underlie
υπόκειμαι.definition: To be placed under or subordinate to something; to be liable or exposed to something. Can also mean to form the basis or foundation of something.
υπόκειμαι.example_greek: Όλοι υπόκεινται στους νόμους της χώρας.
υπόκειμαι.example_english: Everyone is subject to the laws of the country.

υποκλοπή.definition: The act of stealing or taking something that belongs to another person without permission. It refers to larceny or petty theft.
υποκλοπή.example_greek: Η υποκλοπή είναι ένα εγκλημα που τιμωρείται από το νόμο.
υποκλοπή.example_english: Theft is a crime that is punished by law.

υπολειτουργώ.definition: To operate or function at a level below what is expected, normal, or optimal. To work with reduced efficiency or capacity.
υπολειτουργώ.example_greek: Το σύστημα υπολειτουργεί λόγω της έλλειψης πόρων.
υπολειτουργώ.example_english: The system is underperforming due to lack of resources.

υπονομευτήσ.definition: A person who deliberately sabotages or undermines something, such as an organization, plan, or system. The term can refer to someone who engages in subversive activities or deliberate obstruction.
υπονομευτήσ.example_greek: Ο υπονομευτής προσπάθησε να καταστρέψει το έργο από τα έσω.
υπονομευτήσ.example_english: The saboteur tried to destroy the project from within.

υπονομεύτης.definition: A person who deliberately sabotages or undermines something, particularly someone engaged in subversive activities against a government or organization.
υπονομεύτης.example_greek: Ο υπονομεύτης προσπάθησε να καταστρέψει τις υποδομές του εργοστασίου.
υπονομεύτης.example_english: The saboteur tried to destroy the factory's infrastructure.

υπονομεύω.definition: To weaken or destroy something gradually from within, or to deliberately damage or obstruct someone's efforts or plans.
υπονομεύω.example_greek: Η αρνητική δημοσιότητα υπονόμευσε την φήμη της εταιρείας.
υπονομεύω.example_english: The negative publicity undermined the company's reputation.

υποστιρικτής.definition: A person who supports, defends, or promotes a cause, idea, or person. Someone who provides backing or assistance.
υποστιρικτής.example_greek: Ο υποστηρικτής του έργου ήταν πολύ ενθουσιασμένος.
υποστιρικτής.example_english: The supporter of the project was very enthusiastic.

υπόστρωμα.definition: A layer of material that lies beneath or forms the base for something else. It can refer to a physical substrate in nature, soil composition, or a supporting foundation in various contexts.
υπόστρωμα.example_greek: Το υπόστρωμα του εδάφους είναι πολύ σημαντικό για τη ρίζες των φυτών.
υπόστρωμα.example_english: The soil substrate is very important for plant roots.

υποστύλωμα.definition: A structural element consisting of a row of columns supporting a roof or entablature, commonly found in classical architecture. It refers to the space or structure created by columns arranged in a line.
υποστύλωμα.example_greek: Το αρχαίο ναό είχε ένα όμορφο υποστύλωμα στην είσοδο.
υποστύλωμα.example_english: The ancient temple had a beautiful colonnade at the entrance.

υποστώ.translation: to endure, to withstand, to support
υποστώ.definition: To bear, sustain, or endure something difficult; to be able to withstand pressure, hardship, or opposition.
υποστώ.example_greek: Δεν μπορώ να υποστώ άλλο τη θερμοκρασία.
υποστώ.example_english: I cannot endure the temperature any longer.

υπόσχεση.definition: A declaration or guarantee that one will do something or that something will happen. An assurance given to someone.
υπόσχεση.example_greek: Έδωσε μου την υπόσχεση ότι θα έρθει στο πάρτι.
υπόσχεση.example_english: He gave me the promise that he would come to the party.

υπόσχομαι.definition: to make a commitment or pledge to do something in the future. It can also mean to appear to be or show signs of becoming something.
υπόσχομαι.example_greek: Σας υπόσχομαι ότι θα το τελειώσω αύριο.
υπόσχομαι.example_english: I promise you that I will finish it tomorrow.

υποτίθεται.definition: Third person singular present passive form of υποτίθημι, meaning it is assumed, supposed, or believed that something should happen or be true.
υποτίθεται.example_english: He is supposed to come tomorrow.

υποτιμώ.definition: To regard or represent something or someone as being of less importance, value, or quality than it actually is. Can also mean to depreciate in value or to treat with disrespect.
υποτιμώ.example_greek: Δεν πρέπει να υποτιμάς τις ικανότητές του.

υποχρεώνω.translation: to oblige, to compel, to force
υποχρεώνω.definition: to require someone to do something by legal, moral, or physical force; to make it necessary or mandatory for someone to act in a certain way.
υποχρεώνω.example_greek: Ο νόμος με υποχρεώνει να δηλώσω τα έσοδά μου.
υποχρεώνω.example_english: The law compels me to declare my income.

υποχρεωτικός.definition: Required by law, rule, or necessity; not optional. Something that must be done without choice.
υποχρεωτικός.example_greek: Η φοίτηση στο σχολείο είναι υποχρεωτική για όλα τα παιδιά.
υποχρεωτικός.example_english: School attendance is compulsory for all children.

υποχώρηση.definition: The act of moving back or withdrawing from a position, either physically or figuratively. It can also mean giving in or making a concession in a dispute.
υποχώρηση.example_greek: Η υποχώρηση του στρατού ήταν αναγκαία για την αποφυγή περισσότερων απωλειών.
υποχώρηση.example_english: The army's retreat was necessary to avoid further losses.

υποψία.definition: A feeling or belief that something is wrong or that someone is guilty without having clear proof. A state of doubt or distrust toward a person or situation.
υποψία.example_greek: Έχω υποψία ότι κάτι δεν είναι σωστό.
υποψία.example_english: I have a suspicion that something is not right.

υστέρηση.definition: The state of being late, behind schedule, or underdeveloped. It can refer to a time delay, a developmental gap, or a lack of progress compared to expected standards.
υστέρηση.example_greek: Η υστέρηση του τρένου ήταν τρία λεπτά.
υστέρηση.example_english: The delay of the train was three minutes.

ύφεση.definition: A period of temporary economic decline marked by reduced output, employment, and trade. It is less severe than a depression.
ύφεση.example_greek: Η χώρα αντιμετωπίζει μια οικονομική ύφεση που επηρεάζει τις επιχειρήσεις.
ύφεση.example_english: The country is facing an economic recession that is affecting businesses.

υφιστάμενος.definition: Describing something that exists or is present at the current time; existing in reality or fact.
υφιστάμενος.example_greek: Οι υφιστάμενες συνθήκες δεν είναι ικανοποιητικές.
υφιστάμενος.example_english: The existing conditions are not satisfactory.

υφίστομαι.translation: to undergo, to suffer, to endure
υφίστομαι.definition: To experience or be subjected to something, typically something negative or challenging. It means to go through or tolerate a difficult situation.
υφίστομαι.example_english: The patient is undergoing great pain.

ύφος.definition: The characteristic manner or style in which something is expressed or presented, particularly referring to the tone of voice, writing, or behavior in a social context.
ύφος.example_greek: Δεν μού άρεσε το ύφος της όταν μίλησε.
ύφος.example_english: I didn't like her tone when she spoke.

ύψος.definition: The vertical distance from the ground or base to the top of something. It can also refer to elevation or the state of being high.
ύψος.example_greek: Το ύψος του κτιρίου είναι 50 μέτρα.
ύψος.example_english: The height of the building is 50 meters.

φανερώνω.translation: to reveal, to disclose, to manifest
φανερώνω.definition: To make something known or visible; to reveal a hidden truth or secret. It can also mean to demonstrate or display something clearly.

φάντασμα.definition: A supernatural apparition or specter of a deceased person. Also used metaphorically to describe something illusory or insubstantial.
φάντασμα.example_greek: Το παιδί φοβόταν τα φαντάσματα τη νύχτα.
φάντασμα.example_english: The child was afraid of ghosts at night.

φάσμα.definition: An apparition or phantom; also the range of colors produced when light is separated into its component wavelengths.
φάσμα.example_greek: Το φάσμα του φωτός είναι όμορφο.
φάσμα.example_english: The spectrum of light is beautiful.

φατρία.definition: A group of people united by common ancestry, descent, or shared interests; historically, a subdivision of ancient Greek society or a political faction.
φατρία.example_greek: Η φατρία των αριστοκρατών είχε μεγάλη επιρροή στην πόλη.
φατρία.example_english: The aristocratic faction had great influence in the city.

ΦΗΑ.definition: An acronym for Φόρος Προστιθέμενης Αξίας, the Greek term for Value Added Tax, a consumption tax applied to goods and services.
ΦΗΑ.example_greek: Η ΦΗΑ στα τρόφιμα είναι 13% στην Ελλάδα.
ΦΗΑ.example_english: VAT on food products is 13% in Greece.

φήμη.definition: The state of being widely known or talked about; a widespread report or piece of gossip about someone or something.
φήμη.example_greek: Η φήμη του ως καλλιτέχνη εξαπλώθηκε σε όλο τον κόσμο.
φήμη.example_english: His fame as an artist spread throughout the world.

φθαρμένος.definition: Describes something that has been damaged, ruined, or morally corrupted. It can refer to physical decay or moral degradation.
φθαρμένος.example_greek: Το φθαρμένο φρούτο δεν ήταν ασφαλές να φάμε.
φθαρμένος.example_english: The spoiled fruit was not safe to eat.

φθείρω.translation: to corrupt, to destroy, to ruin
φθείρω.definition: To damage, spoil, or morally corrupt something or someone. In classical usage, it can mean to destroy or bring ruin.

φθορά.definition: The process of gradual destruction, decay, or deterioration of something; physical or moral corruption or decline.
φθορά.example_greek: Η φθορά του κτιρίου ήταν εμφανής μετά από χρόνια αμέλειας.
φθορά.example_english: The deterioration of the building was evident after years of neglect.

φιλέτο.definition: A boneless cut of meat or fish, typically from the loin or similar section. It is a premium cut often used in fine dining.
φιλέτο.example_greek: Παράγγειλα ένα φιλέτο σολομού στο εστιατόριο.
φιλέτο.example_english: I ordered a salmon fillet at the restaurant.

φιλοδοξία.definition: A strong desire to achieve success, distinction, or honor; the drive to accomplish great things and gain recognition or fame.
φιλοδοξία.example_greek: Η φιλοδοξία του τον οδήγησε να σπουδάσει σκληρά για να γίνει γιατρός.
φιλοδοξία.example_english: His ambition led him to study hard to become a doctor.

φιλοτεχνώ.translation: to be fond of art, to cultivate art
φιλοτεχνώ.definition: To have a love or passion for art and its cultivation; to pursue artistic interests or endeavors with dedication and enthusiasm.
φιλοτεχνώ.example_greek: Ο Γιάννης φιλοτεχνεί και ξοδεύει πολλές ώρες στο στούντιό του.
φιλοτεχνώ.example_english: Giannos is passionate about art and spends many hours in his studio.

φόβος.definition: A strong feeling of anxiety or apprehension caused by the presence or anticipation of danger. In Ancient Greek philosophy, it also referred to reverence or respect.
φόβος.example_greek: Έχει φόβο να μιλήσει μπροστά σε πολλούς ανθρώπους.
φόβος.example_english: He has a fear of speaking in front of many people.

φόνος.definition: The unlawful killing of a human being, or the act of killing someone. It is a serious crime in all legal systems.
φόνος.example_greek: Ο φόνος είναι ένα σοβαρό έγκλημα που τιμωρείται με κάθειρξη.
φόνος.example_english: Murder is a serious crime that is punished with imprisonment.

φόρας.definition: A weight or quantity of goods that is carried or transported; something that is borne or endured as a responsibility or obligation.
φόρας.example_greek: Ο φορτηγό μεταφέρει ένα βαρύ φόρα.
φόρας.example_english: The truck carries a heavy load.

φορεά.definition: A portable frame or device used to carry or transport a person, typically a patient or injured person. It can also refer to a ceremonial carrying platform.
φορεά.example_greek: Οι διασώστες μετέφεραν τον τραυματία με τη φορεά.
φορεά.example_english: The rescuers carried the injured person on a stretcher.

φορέας.definition: A person or entity that carries, bears, or wears something; can also refer to a carrier in medical or biological contexts (e.g., a disease carrier).
φορέας.example_greek: Ο φορέας της ασθένειας μεταδίδει το ιό σε άλλους ανθρώπους.
φορέας.example_english: The carrier of the disease transmits the virus to other people.

φοροδιαφυγή.definition: The illegal practice of not paying taxes owed to the government by concealing income or using fraudulent methods.
φοροδιαφυγή.example_greek: Η φοροδιαφυγή είναι ένα σοβαρό εγκλημα που τιμωρείται με αυστηρότητα.
φοροδιαφυγή.example_english: Tax evasion is a serious crime that is severely punished.

φορολόγηση.definition: The process of imposing or collecting taxes on income, property, or goods. It refers to the system or act of determining and levying taxes on individuals or businesses.
φορολόγηση.example_greek: Η φορολόγηση των εισοδημάτων είναι υψηλή φέτος.
φορολόγηση.example_english: Income taxation is high this year.

φορολογούμενος.definition: A person who is subject to taxation or liable to pay taxes; something that is being taxed. Can function as an adjective (taxable) or as a noun (taxpayer).
φορολογούμενος.example_greek: Ο φορολογούμενος πρέπει να υποβάλει τη δήλωση φόρου έως τις 30 Απριλίου.
φορολογούμενος.example_english: The taxpayer must submit their tax return by April 30th.

φορτηγός.definition: A large motor vehicle designed for carrying heavy loads and freight. It is commonly used for commercial transportation of goods.
φορτηγός.example_greek: Το φορτηγό παρκάρισε στο λιμάνι για να φορτώσει εμπορεύματα.
φορτηγός.example_english: The truck parked at the port to load cargo.

φουσκώνω.translation: to inflate, to swell, to puff up
φουσκώνω.definition: To fill with air or gas causing something to expand, or metaphorically to become swollen or puffed up. Can also mean to exaggerate or boast.
φουσκώνω.example_english: He inflated the balloon with air.

φράγκο.definition: A monetary unit formerly used in France, Switzerland, and other countries before the adoption of the euro. The term can also refer to any foreign currency or hard currency in colloquial usage.
φράγκο.example_greek: Το φράγκο ήταν το νόμισμα της Ελβετίας.
φράγκο.example_english: The franc was the currency of Switzerland.

φρένο.definition: A device used to slow down or stop a vehicle or machine. It can also refer to something that restrains or hinders progress.
φρένο.example_greek: Πάτησε το φρένο για να σταματήσει το αυτοκίνητο.
φρένο.example_english: He pressed the brake to stop the car.

φρικτός.definition: Causing horror or disgust; extremely unpleasant or offensive. Describes something that inspires fear or revulsion.
φρικτός.example_greek: Η φρικτή είδηση του ατυχήματος συγκλόνισε όλη την κοινότητα.
φρικτός.example_english: The gruesome news of the accident shocked the entire community.

φρουρά.definition: A group of soldiers stationed to protect a place, or the act of guarding and protecting something or someone. It can also refer to custody or safekeeping.
φρουρά.example_greek: Η φρουρά του κάστρου ήταν πολύ δυνατή.
φρουρά.example_english: The garrison of the castle was very strong.

φύλακα.definition: A person responsible for protecting, guarding, or watching over something or someone. Can refer to a security guard, prison guard, or guardian.
φύλακα.example_greek: Ο φύλακας της φυλακής είναι πολύ αυστηρός.
φύλακα.example_english: The prison guard is very strict.

φυλάω.translation: to guard, to watch, to keep
φυλάω.definition: to protect someone or something from danger or harm, or to keep watch over something. It can also mean to preserve or maintain something.

φυλή.definition: A social group sharing common ancestry, culture, or identity. In modern usage, it can also refer to a race or ethnicity.
φυλή.example_greek: Οι αρχαίες φυλές της Ελλάδας είχαν τις δικές τους παραδόσεις.
φυλή.example_english: The ancient tribes of Greece had their own traditions.

φυλλάδο.definition: A small printed booklet or brochure containing information about a product, service, or topic. It typically has a few pages and is distributed for promotional or informational purposes.
φυλλάδο.example_greek: Πήρα ένα φυλλάδο για τις διακοπές στο νησί.
φυλλάδο.example_english: I picked up a brochure about holidays on the island.

φωτίζω.definition: To provide light to something or to make something bright. It can also mean to enlighten or clarify something intellectually.
φωτίζω.example_greek: Ο ήλιος φωτίζει το δωμάτιο κάθε πρωί.
φωτίζω.example_english: The sun illuminates the room every morning.

φωτοδιαπερατός.definition: Allowing light to pass through; capable of transmitting light while diffusing it so that objects beyond cannot be seen clearly. Used to describe materials with partial transparency.
φωτοδιαπερατός.example_greek: Το φωτοδιαπερατό γυαλί χρησιμοποιείται στα μπάνια για λόγους ιδιωτικότητας.
φωτοδιαπερατός.example_english: Translucent glass is used in bathrooms for privacy reasons.

χαζός.definition: Describing someone who lacks intelligence or acts foolishly; can also mean absurd or ridiculous when applied to situations or ideas.
χαζός.example_greek: Αυτή η ιδέα είναι πολύ χαζή.

χαλαρός.definition: Not tight or tense; loose or slack in physical form. Can also mean relaxed in manner or atmosphere.
χαλαρός.example_greek: Φόρεσε χαλαρά ρούχα για να είναι άνετος.
χαλαρός.example_english: He wore loose clothes to be comfortable.

χάπι.definition: A small solid dose of medicine in tablet or capsule form, typically taken orally. It is a common form of pharmaceutical administration.
χάπι.example_greek: Πήρα ένα χάπι για τον πονοκέφαλό μου.
χάπι.example_english: I took a pill for my headache.

χάραμα.definition: The first light of day when the sun begins to rise; the early morning period just before sunrise.
χάραμα.example_greek: Ξύπνησα στο χάραμα και πήγα για περπάτημα.
χάραμα.example_english: I woke up at dawn and went for a walk.

χάραξη.definition: The act of cutting, scratching, or engraving marks or lines onto a surface, or the marks themselves produced by this action.
χάραξη.example_greek: Η χάραξη στο μέταλλο απαιτεί δεξιότητα και υπομονή.
χάραξη.example_english: Engraving on metal requires skill and patience.

χαράτοι.definition: Plural form of χάρατος (charatos), referring to visible marks, scratches, or lines made on a surface, typically from scratching or engraving.
χαράτοι.example_greek: Οι χαράτοι στο τραπέζι δείχνουν ότι είναι παλιό.
χαράτοι.example_english: The scratches on the table show that it is old.

χάρη.definition: A quality of elegance, beauty, or divine blessing; also means a favor or kindness shown to someone.
χάρη.example_greek: Η χάρη του Θεού είναι απεριόριστη.
χάρη.example_english: The grace of God is limitless.

χαρήγηση.definition: The act of granting, awarding, or providing something, such as a license, permission, or financial support. It refers to the formal conferment or bestowal of rights, privileges, or resources.
χαρήγηση.example_greek: Η χαρήγηση της άδειας έγινε μετά από μακρά διαδικασία.
χαρήγηση.example_english: The granting of the license took place after a lengthy process.

χαρίζω.translation: to give, to forgive, to grant
χαρίζω.definition: To give someone something as a gift or favor, or to forgive/pardon someone for a wrongdoing. It can also mean to grant or allow something to happen.
χαρίζω.example_greek: Του χάρισα ένα βιβλίο για τα γενέθλιά του.
χαρίζω.example_english: I gave him a book as a gift for his birthday.

χάριν.translation: for the sake of, in order to
χάριν.definition: A preposition or adverbial form meaning 'for the sake of' or 'in order to,' often used in formal or literary contexts to indicate purpose or reason.
χάριν.example_greek: Έκανε θυσίες χάριν της οικογένειας του.
χάριν.example_english: He made sacrifices for the sake of his family.

χάρις.definition: A quality of elegance, beauty, or kindness that attracts and pleases others. It can also mean a favor, blessing, or divine grace.
χάρις.example_greek: Η χάρις της ήταν εμφανής στον τρόπο που χορεύει.
χάρις.example_english: Her grace was evident in the way she danced.

χαρκοκρατία.definition: A form of government or rule by the common people or masses, often implying mob rule or government by the multitude without proper structure or law.
χαρκοκρατία.example_greek: Ορισμένοι φιλόσοφοι ανησυχούν ότι η χαρκοκρατία μπορεί να οδηγήσει σε αναρχία.
χαρκοκρατία.example_english: Some philosophers worry that ochlocracy can lead to anarchy.

χαρτί.definition: A thin material made from wood pulp, used for writing, printing, or wrapping. It is one of the most common materials in everyday life.
χαρτί.example_greek: Χρειάζομαι ένα φύλλο χαρτί για να γράψω.
χαρτί.example_english: I need a sheet of paper to write on.

χάσκω.translation: to gape, to yawn, to open wide
χάσκω.definition: to open the mouth wide, especially in yawning or from astonishment. It can also mean to gape or stand agape in wonder.
χάσκω.example_greek: Το παιδί χάσκει από την κούραση.

χάσμα.definition: A deep opening or gap in the earth's surface, or figuratively, a wide separation or divide between two things or groups.
χάσμα.example_greek: Υπάρχει ένα βαθύ χάσμα μεταξύ των δύο πολιτικών απόψεων.
χάσμα.example_english: There is a deep chasm between the two political views.

χασομερώ.translation: to waste time, to loaf around
χασομερώ.definition: To spend time idly or unproductively; to loaf, dawdle, or engage in time-wasting activities without purpose.
χασομερώ.example_greek: Χασομέρεψες όλη την ημέρα αντί να δουλέψεις.
χασομερώ.example_english: You wasted the whole day instead of working.

χείλος.definition: The fleshy part forming the upper or lower edge of the mouth in humans and animals.

χείμαρρος.definition: A swift-flowing mountain stream or torrent, typically dry in summer and flowing heavily during winter rains or after snowmelt.
χείμαρρος.example_greek: Ο χείμαρρος κατέρρευσε τη γέφυρα κατά τη διάρκεια της καταιγίδας.
χείμαρρος.example_english: The torrent destroyed the bridge during the storm.

χειραγώγηση.definition: The act of guiding or leading someone by the hand, often used metaphorically to mean guidance or instruction. It can also refer to the process of helping someone understand or navigate through something.
χειραγώγηση.example_greek: Η χειραγώγηση των νέων μαθητών στο σχολείο είναι σημαντική.
χειραγώγηση.example_english: The guidance of new students at school is important.

χειρίζομαι.translation: to handle, to operate, to manage
χειρίζομαι.definition: To use or control something with one's hands; to operate a device or manage a situation. Can also mean to treat or deal with someone in a particular way.

χειρισμός.definition: The action or process of handling, operating, or manipulating something with skill or control. It can refer to physical handling or the operation of equipment and systems.
χειρισμός.example_greek: Ο χειρισμός του νέου λογισμικού είναι πολύ εύκολος.
χειρισμός.example_english: The operation of the new software is very easy.

χειροκροτώ.definition: To strike the palms of the hands together repeatedly in approval or appreciation of a performance or speaker. It is a gesture of acclaim and encouragement.
χειροκροτώ.example_greek: Το κοινό χειροκροτούσε θερμά τον καλλιτέχνη.
χειροκροτώ.example_english: The audience applauded the artist warmly.

χειροπιαστός.definition: Something that can be touched or grasped by hand; something concrete or physical rather than abstract or theoretical.
χειροπιαστός.example_greek: Η απόδειξη ήταν χειροπιαστή και δεν μπορούσε κανείς να τη διαφωνήσει.
χειροπιαστός.example_english: The proof was tangible and no one could dispute it.

χειρότερος.definition: Comparative or superlative form meaning of lower quality, more unpleasant, or more severe than something else. It indicates a negative quality that is greater than another.
χειρότερος.example_greek: Αυτό το φιλμ είναι χειρότερο από το προηγούμενο.
χειρότερος.example_english: This film is worse than the previous one.

χειρόφρενο.definition: A brake operated by hand, typically found in vehicles to hold them in place when parked or to provide emergency braking.
χειρόφρενο.example_greek: Τίρησε το χειρόφρενο πριν κατέβει από το αυτοκίνητο.
χειρόφρενο.example_english: He pulled the handbrake before getting out of the car.

χεράκι.definition: A diminutive form of χέρι (hand), referring to a small handle, grip, or handheld object. It can also be used affectionately to refer to a child's small hand.
χεράκι.example_greek: Το παιδί κρατούσε το χεράκι της μητέρας του.
χεράκι.example_english: The child held onto his mother's hand.

χευδαίσθηση.definition: A sensory perception that occurs without an external stimulus, or a mistaken impression of reality. It refers to a false or deceptive sensation experienced by the senses.
χευδαίσθηση.example_greek: Η χευδαίσθηση του ασθενή περιορίστηκε μετά τη θεραπεία.
χευδαίσθηση.example_english: The patient's hallucination subsided after treatment.

χολή.definition: A bitter yellowish-green fluid produced by the liver that aids in digestion. In older or literary usage, it can also refer to anger or bitterness of spirit.
χολή.example_greek: Η χολή είναι απαραίτητη για την πέψη των λιπών.
χολή.example_english: Bile is essential for the digestion of fats.

χορδή.definition: A thin rope or cord made of twisted fibers, or a string on a musical instrument. In music, also refers to a harmonic chord.

χορήγηση.definition: The act of providing, supplying, or granting something. It can refer to the provision of funds, resources, or sponsorship in financial or administrative contexts.
χορήγηση.example_greek: Η χορήγηση των φαρμάκων γίνεται με συνταγή του γιατρού.
χορήγηση.example_english: The provision of medicines is done with a doctor's prescription.

χορηγώ.translation: to sponsor, to provide, to supply
χορηγώ.definition: To provide financial support or resources for an event, project, or activity; to sponsor or fund something.
χορηγώ.example_greek: Η εταιρεία χορηγεί το διεθνές φεστιβάλ μουσικής.
χορηγώ.example_english: The company sponsors the international music festival.

χοροπηδητό.definition: Describing music or a rhythm that naturally invites people to dance and jump; characterized by a lively, rhythmic quality that encourages movement.
χοροπηδητό.example_greek: Το χοροπηδητό τραγούδι έκανε όλους να σηκωθούν και να χορέψουν.
χοροπηδητό.example_english: The upbeat, danceable song made everyone get up and dance.

χρέος.definition: A sum of money that is owed or due to be paid. Also means a moral or social obligation that one has toward another person.
χρέος.example_greek: Έχει μεγάλο χρέος προς την τράπεζα.
χρέος.example_english: He has a large debt to the bank.

χρεωμένος.definition: Describing someone who owes money or is under obligation to someone else. It can also mean someone who is obligated or duty-bound.
χρεωμένος.example_greek: Είμαι χρεωμένος στον φίλο μου γιατί με βοήθησε πολύ.
χρεωμένος.example_english: I am indebted to my friend because he helped me a lot.

χρεώνω.translation: to charge, to debit, to owe
χρεώνω.definition: To record a debt against someone or to charge someone money for goods or services. It is commonly used in financial and commercial contexts.
χρεώνω.example_greek: Η τράπεζα χρέωσε τον λογαριασμό μου με 50 ευρώ για τις επιτόκια.
χρεώνω.example_english: The bank charged my account 50 euros for the interest.

χρέωση.definition: The act of charging or debiting an amount of money to an account. It refers to a financial charge or the process of billing someone for a service or product.
χρέωση.example_greek: Η χρέωση για την υπηρεσία εμφανίστηκε στον λογαριασμό μου.
χρέωση.example_english: The charge for the service appeared on my account.

χρηματοπιστωτικός.definition: Relating to or concerning financial institutions, credit systems, or monetary affairs. Used to describe sectors, systems, or services connected to banking and finance.
χρηματοπιστωτικός.example_greek: Ο χρηματοπιστωτικός κλάδος είναι σημαντικός για την οικονομία.
χρηματοπιστωτικός.example_english: The financial sector is important for the economy.

χρηματοστήριο.definition: A financial institution where stocks, bonds, and other securities are bought and sold. It serves as a marketplace for trading financial instruments and determining their market prices.
χρηματοστήριο.example_greek: Το χρηματοστήριο Αθηνών είναι ένας σημαντικός χρηματοοικονομικός σταθμός.
χρηματοστήριο.example_english: The Athens Stock Exchange is an important financial hub.

χρίζω.definition: To apply oil or ointment to someone or something as a religious or ceremonial act; to consecrate or sanctify by anointing.
χρίζω.example_greek: Ο ιερέας χρίζει τον βασιλιά με ιερό έλαιο.
χρίζω.example_english: The priest anoints the king with holy oil.

χρονικός.definition: Relating to time or duration; in medicine, referring to a disease or condition that lasts a long time or recurs frequently.
χρονικός.example_greek: Ο ασθενής έχει χρονικά προβλήματα υγείας.
χρονικός.example_english: The patient has chronic health problems.

χρυσοφόρος.definition: An adjective describing something that bears, carries, or contains gold. It can be used literally to describe objects or substances containing gold, or figuratively to describe something precious or valuable.
χρυσοφόρος.example_greek: Η χρυσοφόρος άμμος του ποταμού ήταν πολύ ζητητή.
χρυσοφόρος.example_english: The gold-bearing sand of the river was highly sought after.

χρωστάω.definition: To be obligated to pay or repay money or a debt to someone. Can also mean to owe something non-material, like gratitude or an explanation.
χρωστάω.example_greek: Του χρωστάω 50 ευρώ από το δάνειο που μου έκανε.
χρωστάω.example_english: I owe him 50 euros from the loan he gave me.

χυδαίος.definition: Lacking refinement or elegance; crude or offensive in manner or language. Describes something that is common, base, or lacking in sophistication.
χυδαίος.example_greek: Η χυδαία γλώσσα δεν είναι κατάλληλη για την τυπική συζήτηση.
χυδαίος.example_english: Vulgar language is not suitable for formal conversation.

χωλαίνω.definition: to walk with difficulty or unevenly, typically due to an injury or disability affecting one's legs or feet.
χωλαίνω.example_greek: Ο παππούς χωλαίνει λίγο μετά το ατύχημα.
χωλαίνω.example_english: The grandfather limps a little after the accident.

χωριουδάκι.definition: A diminutive form referring to a very small village or hamlet, typically used affectionately or descriptively to indicate a tiny rural settlement.
χωριουδάκι.example_greek: Ζούμε σε ένα χωριουδάκι κοντά στα βουνά.
χωριουδάκι.example_english: We live in a small village near the mountains.

ψευδώς.definition: In a false, dishonest, or deceptive manner; contrary to truth or fact. Used to describe actions or statements made with intent to deceive.
ψευδώς.example_greek: Μίλησε ψευδώς για τα γεγονότα του περιστατικού.
ψευδώς.example_english: He spoke falsely about the facts of the incident.

ψηφιακός.definition: Relating to or using digital technology, involving numerical digits, or operating with discrete values rather than continuous ones.
ψηφιακός.example_greek: Χρησιμοποιώ ένα ψηφιακό ρολόι στο χέρι μου.
ψηφιακός.example_english: I use a digital watch on my wrist.

ψηφίζω.definition: To cast a vote or participate in voting, either in an election or on a specific matter. It can also mean to decide or determine by vote.
ψηφίζω.example_greek: Θα ψηφίσω για τον νέο πρόεδρο αύριο.
ψηφίζω.example_english: I will vote for the new president tomorrow.

ψηφιοποιώ.translation: to digitize, to convert to digital
ψηφιοποιώ.definition: To convert analog information, documents, or physical materials into digital format using electronic means. This involves transforming data into a form that can be processed, stored, and transmitted by computers.
ψηφιοποιώ.example_greek: Η βιβλιοθήκη ψηφιοποιεί τα παλιά έγγραφα για να τα διατηρήσει.
ψηφιοποιώ.example_english: The library is digitizing old documents to preserve them.

ψήφιση.definition: The act of voting or casting a vote in an election or decision-making process. It can also refer to the vote itself or the voting system.
ψήφιση.example_greek: Η ψήφιση για τον νέο πρόεδρο θα γίνει την επόμενη εβδομάδα.
ψήφιση.example_english: The voting for the new president will take place next week.

ψήφος.definition: A vote cast in an election or decision-making process. Also refers to a small stone or pebble used historically for voting.
ψήφος.example_greek: Έδωσα την ψήφο μου στις εκλογές.
ψήφος.example_english: I cast my vote in the elections.

ψηφοφορία.definition: The process of casting votes in an election or decision-making process. It refers to the act of voting or the voting procedure itself.
ψηφοφορία.example_greek: Η ψηφοφορία για τον νέο πρόεδρο θα γίνει αύριο.
ψηφοφορία.example_english: The voting for the new president will take place tomorrow.

ψίθυρος.definition: A soft, hushed sound or speech made with the voice lowered to avoid being heard by others. It can also refer to rumors or gossip whispered among people.
ψίθυρος.example_greek: Άκουσα ψίθυρους στο διάδρομο του σχολείου.
ψίθυρος.example_english: I heard whispers in the school hallway.

ψιμύθιο.definition: A white powder made from lead carbonate, historically used as a cosmetic and pigment. It was commonly applied to the face as makeup in ancient and medieval times.
ψιμύθιο.example_greek: Οι αρχαίες γυναίκες χρησιμοποιούσαν ψιμύθιο για να λευκάνουν το δέρμα τους.
ψιμύθιο.example_english: Ancient women used white lead to whiten their skin.

ψιχάλα.definition: Light, fine rain or mist that falls gently. A soft, delicate rain with very small droplets.
ψιχάλα.example_greek: Έπεσε μια ψιχάλα το πρωί και όλα ήταν υγρά.
