ψιχάλα.example_english: Fine rain fell in the morning and everything was wet.

ψυχαναγκαστικός.definition: Relating to or characterized by psychological compulsions or obsessive thoughts and behaviors. Commonly used in psychological and psychiatric contexts to describe obsessive-compulsive disorders.
ψυχαναγκαστικός.example_greek: Ο ασθενής παρουσιάζει ψυχαναγκαστικά συμπτώματα που απαιτούν θεραπεία.
ψυχαναγκαστικός.example_english: The patient presents psychocompulsive symptoms that require treatment.

ψυχικό.definition: Relating to the mind, mental processes, or the human spirit. Can refer to psychological aspects of the mind or spiritual dimensions of human existence.
ψυχικό.example_greek: Η ψυχική υγεία είναι πολύ σημαντική.

ψυχρός.definition: Having a low temperature; characterized by coldness. Can also mean distant or unfriendly in manner.
ψυχρός.example_greek: Το νερό είναι πολύ ψυχρό σήμερα.
ψυχρός.example_english: The water is very cold today.

ωμός.definition: Not cooked or processed; in a natural or unrefined state. Can also mean harsh, rough, or crude in manner or tone.
ωμός.example_greek: Δεν μπορώ να φάω ωμό κοτόπουλο.

ώμος.definition: The upper joint of the arm where it connects to the body between the neck and the arm. A body part that typically appears rounded and is used for carrying or lifting.
ώμος.example_greek: Είχε έναν πόνο στον ώμο του.
ώμος.example_english: He had a pain in his shoulder.

ωριμότητα.definition: The state or quality of being mature, fully developed, or ripe. It can refer to both physical ripeness (of fruit) and psychological or emotional maturity.
ωριμότητα.example_greek: Η ωριμότητα του σταφυλιού φάνηκε από το χρώμα του.
ωριμότητα.example_english: The ripeness of the grape was evident from its color.

ως εκ του του.translation: so to speak, in a way, as it were
ως εκ του του.definition: An idiomatic expression used to indicate that something is being said in a qualified or approximate manner, often softening a statement or expressing uncertainty about the exact nature of something.
ως εκ του του.example_greek: Είναι ως εκ του του ένας ηγέτης της κοινότητας.
ως εκ του του.example_english: He is, so to speak, a leader of the community.

ανώτατος.definition: Superlative form of ανώ (high); refers to the highest point, rank, or degree. Used to describe the most superior position or level.
ανώτατος.example_greek: Το ανώτατο δικαστήριο αποφάσισε την υπόθεση.
ανώτατος.example_english: The supreme court decided the case.

νόμιμος.definition: In accordance with law or legal requirements; permitted or sanctioned by law. Can also mean legitimate or properly established.
νόμιμος.example_greek: Αυτή είναι μια νόμιμη επιχείρηση που λειτουργεί με όλες τις απαραίτητες άδειες.
νόμιμος.example_english: This is a legitimate business that operates with all necessary licenses.

κείμενος.definition: Describing something that is in a lying position or situated in a particular location. Often used in formal or literary contexts to indicate the position or state of something.
κείμενος.example_english: The text lies on the table.

αυστιρός.definition: Characterized by strictness, severity, or harshness in manner or appearance. Describing someone or something that is rigid, uncompromising, or austere.
αυστιρός.example_greek: Ο δάσκαλος είχε μια αυστιρή έκφραση στο πρόσωπό του.
αυστιρός.example_english: The teacher had a stern expression on his face.

βαθύτατα.definition: The superlative form of the adverb 'βαθιά' (deeply), expressing the greatest degree of depth either literally or figuratively.
βαθύτατα.example_greek: Η αλήθεια είναι βαθύτατα κρυμμένη στις σελίδες του βιβλίου.
βαθύτατα.example_english: The truth is hidden most deeply in the pages of the book.

αντιπαθητικός.definition: Describing someone or something that provokes dislike or aversion; not pleasant or agreeable in nature or character.
αντιπαθητικός.example_greek: Αυτό το άτομο έχει μια αντιπαθητική συμπεριφορά.
αντιπαθητικός.example_english: This person has an unpleasant behavior.

κολακεύω.definition: To praise someone excessively or insincerely in order to gain favor or advantage. It means to use flattery to please someone.
κολακεύω.example_greek: Δεν θέλω να με κολακεύεις, θέλω την αλήθεια.
κολακεύω.example_english: I don't want you to flatter me, I want the truth.

αξίωμα.definition: A fundamental principle or self-evident truth accepted without proof; also refers to a position of authority or dignity. In mathematics and logic, an axiom is a basic assumption from which other statements are derived.
αξίωμα.example_greek: Το αξίωμα της ίσης μεταχείρισης είναι θεμελιώδες για τη δημοκρατία.
αξίωμα.example_english: The principle of equal treatment is fundamental to democracy.

πας.definition: Refers to the whole amount or number of something without exception; used to indicate totality or universality. Can function as both an adjective and pronoun.
πας.example_greek: Όλοι οι μαθητές, πας ένας από αυτούς, πέρασε την εξέταση.
πας.example_english: All the students, every single one of them, passed the exam.

φύσις.definition: The essential qualities or characteristics of a person or thing; the physical world and everything in it that is not made by humans.
φύσις.example_greek: Η φύσις είναι πολύ όμορφη στο καλοκαίρι.
φύσις.example_english: Nature is very beautiful in summer.

δικαστήριο.definition: A building or institution where legal cases are heard and decided by a judge or judges. It is the place where justice is administered in a legal system.
δικαστήριο.example_greek: Πήγε στο δικαστήριο για την δίκη του.
δικαστήριο.example_english: He went to the court for his trial.

συγκινημένος.definition: Deeply affected emotionally; stirred or moved by strong feelings, typically sympathy or sentiment. Often used to describe someone who is emotionally touched by something meaningful.
συγκινημένος.example_greek: Ήταν συγκινημένος όταν άκουσε τα νέα για την επιτυχία του παιδιού του.
συγκινημένος.example_english: He was moved when he heard the news about his child's success.

πτυχίο.definition: An official document awarded by an educational institution certifying that a student has completed a course of study or earned an academic degree.
πτυχίο.example_greek: Έλαβα το πτυχίο μου από το πανεπιστήμιο πέρυσι.
πτυχίο.example_english: I received my diploma from the university last year.

άριστα.definition: In a manner that is excellent, perfect, or of the highest quality. It can also mean 'excellent' when used as an interjection or adjective.
άριστα.example_greek: Τα παιδιά πήγαν άριστα στο σχολείο.
άριστα.example_english: The children did excellently at school.

σκάλα.definition: A structure with steps for climbing up or down, or a graduated system of measurements or rankings.
σκάλα.example_greek: Ανέβηκε στη σκάλα για να καθαρίσει το παράθυρο.
σκάλα.example_english: He climbed the ladder to clean the window.

ψιθυριστά.definition: In a whispering manner; speaking very softly and quietly, often in a secretive or confidential way.
ψιθυριστά.example_greek: Μιλούσαν ψιθυριστά για να μην τους ακούσει κανείς.
ψιθυριστά.example_english: They spoke in whispers so that no one would hear them.

ωδείο.definition: An educational institution or building where music is taught and studied, typically offering instruction in various musical instruments and music theory.
ωδείο.example_greek: Η κόρη μου φοιτά σε ένα διάσημο ωδείο στο κέντρο της πόλης.
ωδείο.example_english: My daughter studies at a famous music conservatory in the city center.

εξάμηνο.definition: A period of six months, commonly used in academic contexts to refer to one half of an academic year in schools and universities.
εξάμηνο.example_greek: Το πρώτο εξάμηνο του ακαδημαϊκού έτους είναι πολύ απαιτητικό.
εξάμηνο.example_english: The first semester of the academic year is very demanding.

καλές τέχνες.definition: The visual and performing arts created primarily for aesthetic purposes, including painting, sculpture, music, and dance. This is a collective term for artistic disciplines valued for their beauty and cultural expression.
καλές τέχνες.example_english: She is studying fine arts at the university.

κουλτουριάρης.definition: A man who is well-educated, refined, and cultured; someone who appreciates art, literature, and intellectual pursuits. The term often carries a slightly ironic or colloquial tone.
κουλτουριάρης.example_greek: Ο κουλτουριάρης διαβάζει κλασικά βιβλία κάθε βράδυ.
κουλτουριάρης.example_english: The cultured man reads classical books every evening.

πλατύς.definition: Having great extent from side to side; not narrow. Can describe physical dimensions or figurative concepts.
πλατύς.example_greek: Το πλατύ δρόμο είναι πολύ όμορφο.
πλατύς.example_english: The wide road is very beautiful.

πλασάρω.definition: To shape or form something by hand or with tools, particularly in sculpting or pottery. Can also mean to create or fashion something.
πλασάρω.example_greek: Ο αγαλματοποιός πλασάρει το άγαλμα από το μάρμαρο.
πλασάρω.example_english: The sculptor shapes the statue from marble.

μεγαλεία.definition: The quality of being grand, impressive, or majestic in appearance or character. A state of impressive dignity and splendor.
μεγαλεία.example_greek: Η μεγαλεία του παλατιού εντυπωσίασε όλους τους επισκέπτες.
μεγαλεία.example_english: The magnificence of the palace impressed all the visitors.

παγκοσμοποίηση.definition: The process of integration of economies, societies, and cultures across the world through trade, technology, and communication networks. It refers to the worldwide spread of ideas, products, and services.
παγκοσμοποίηση.example_greek: Η παγκοσμοποίηση έχει αλλάξει τον τρόπο που ζούμε και εργαζόμαστε.
παγκοσμοποίηση.example_english: Globalization has changed the way we live and work.

τόθεμαι.translation: to place oneself, to be situated
τόθεμαι.definition: A middle voice verb meaning to position oneself in a particular location or to be stationed/situated somewhere. It is primarily used in formal or archaic contexts in Modern Greek.
τόθεμαι.example_english: The army is positioned at the borders.

συγκοινωνιές.definition: Systems and services for transporting people or facilitating communication between different places. Often refers to public transportation networks like buses, trains, and metro systems.
συγκοινωνιές.example_greek: Οι συγκοινωνιές της πόλης είναι πολύ καλές.
συγκοινωνιές.example_english: The city's public transport is very good.

διακρίνω.definition: To perceive the difference between two or more things; to recognize or identify as distinct. Can also mean to discriminate or to be distinguished.
διακρίνω.example_greek: Μπορώ να διακρίνω τη διαφορά ανάμεσα στα δύο προϊόντα.
διακρίνω.example_english: I can distinguish the difference between the two products.

κρατικός.definition: Relating to or belonging to the state or government. Used to describe institutions, services, or properties controlled or operated by the state.
κρατικός.example_greek: Το κρατικό σχολείο είναι δωρεάν για όλα τα παιδιά.
κρατικός.example_english: The public school is free for all children.

φυλ'η.definition: A social group sharing common ancestry, culture, or characteristics. In ancient Greece, it also referred to one of the traditional divisions of the population.
φυλ'η.example_greek: Η φυλή των Ελλήνων έχει μια πλούσια ιστορία.
φυλ'η.example_english: The Greek race has a rich history.

θρησκεία.definition: A system of faith and worship, including beliefs, practices, and moral values shared by a community of believers.
θρησκεία.example_greek: Η θρησκεία είναι σημαντική για πολλούς ανθρώπους.
θρησκεία.example_english: Religion is important for many people.

εκλαμβάνω.translation: to interpret, to understand, to construe
εκλαμβάνω.definition: To take or interpret something in a particular way; to understand or perceive something according to a specific meaning or viewpoint.
εκλαμβάνω.example_greek: Η δήλωσή του εκλήφθη ως απειλή.
εκλαμβάνω.example_english: His statement was interpreted as a threat.

ισοπέδωση.definition: The process of making something level or flat, or the act of demolishing a building or structure completely. It can also refer to eliminating differences or disparities.
ισοπέδωση.example_greek: Η ισοπέδωση του κτιρίου άρχισε το πρωί.
ισοπέδωση.example_english: The demolition of the building started in the morning.

υπόσταση.definition: The fundamental nature or essential quality of something; the underlying basis or support that gives something its existence or character.
υπόσταση.example_greek: Η υπόσταση του προβλήματος είναι πολύ σύνθετη.
υπόσταση.example_english: The essence of the problem is very complex.

πλανητάρχης.definition: In astrology and mythology, a deity or force that rules over a particular planet. The term combines 'planítis' (planet) and 'árchis' (ruler/governor).
πλανητάρχης.example_greek: Ο Αρης είναι ο πλανητάρχης του Άρη στην αρχαία ελληνική μυθολογία.
πλανητάρχης.example_english: Ares is the planetary ruler of the planet Mars in ancient Greek mythology.

μεγαλόπνοος.definition: Having a lofty spirit and noble character; displaying generosity and dignity in bearing and actions. An archaic or literary term describing someone of great moral worth.
μεγαλόπνοος.example_greek: Ο μεγαλόπνοος ήρωας χάρισε τη ζωή του για το καλό του λαού.
μεγαλόπνοος.example_english: The magnanimous hero sacrificed his life for the good of the people.

μίσος.definition: A strong feeling of intense dislike or animosity toward someone or something. It represents deep-seated enmity and hostility.
μίσος.example_greek: Το μίσος δεν λύνει τα προβλήματα.

πάθη.definition: Plural form of πάθος (pathos). Refers to intense emotions, sufferings, passions, or ailments that affect the soul or body.
πάθη.example_greek: Τα ανθρώπινα πάθη είναι σύνθετα και δύσκολα να κατανοήσουμε.
πάθη.example_english: Human passions are complex and difficult to understand.

αδήριτος.definition: Not harmed, damaged, or injured; remaining intact or unaffected by harm or damage.
αδήριτος.example_greek: Ο οδηγός βγήκε αδήριτος από το ατύχημα.
αδήριτος.example_english: The driver came out of the accident unharmed.

σιμβίωση.definition: The state of living together with another person, typically in a romantic relationship without being married. It can also refer to symbiosis in biological contexts.
σιμβίωση.example_greek: Η σιμβίωση τους διαρκούσε πέντε χρόνια πριν παντρευτούν.
σιμβίωση.example_english: Their cohabitation lasted five years before they got married.

αισθητός.definition: Capable of being perceived by the senses; noticeable or evident. Can also mean appreciable or considerable in degree.
αισθητός.example_greek: Η διαφορά στη θερμοκρασία ήταν αισθητή.
αισθητός.example_english: The difference in temperature was noticeable.

ρεύμα.definition: The continuous flow of water, air, or electricity in a particular direction. It can refer to an ocean current, a river's flow, or an electric current.

ερμηνευώ.definition: To explain the meaning of something or translate from one language to another. It can also mean to perform or present a work artistically.
ερμηνευώ.example_greek: Ο δάσκαλος ερμήνευσε το δύσκολο κείμενο για τους μαθητές.
ερμηνευώ.example_english: The teacher interpreted the difficult text for the students.

ποικιλοτρόπως.definition: An adverb meaning in a varied, diverse, or multifaceted manner. It describes actions or situations that occur in many different ways or from multiple perspectives.
ποικιλοτρόπως.example_greek: Το πρόβλημα αυτό μπορεί να επιλυθεί ποικιλοτρόπως.
ποικιλοτρόπως.example_english: This problem can be solved in various ways.

στολεύω.translation: to set out, to depart, to head towards
στολεύω.definition: To begin a journey or trip, to go in a particular direction, or to leave a place with a specific destination in mind.
στολεύω.example_greek: Στολεύω για το χωριό κάθε καλοκαίρι.
στολεύω.example_english: I set out for the village every summer.

πουθενά.definition: An adverb indicating the absence of a place or location; used to express that something is not found in any place or that someone is not anywhere.
πουθενά.example_greek: Δεν μπορώ να βρω το κλειδί μου πουθενά.
πουθενά.example_english: I cannot find my key anywhere.

ευλογιά.definition: A divine blessing or favor; good luck or fortune bestowed upon someone. It can also refer to a prayer of blessing.
ευλογιά.example_greek: Η ευλογιά του Θεού είναι πάνω σας.

κατάρα.definition: A solemn utterance intended to invoke evil or misfortune upon someone or something; a curse or malediction.
κατάρα.example_greek: Η κατάρα του ήταν πολύ δυνατή και φοβόταν όλοι.
κατάρα.example_english: His curse was very powerful and everyone feared it.

θίγω.definition: To make physical contact with something or someone, or to have an emotional or psychological effect on a person. It can also mean to mention or bring up a subject.

απόσπασμα.definition: A short extract or fragment taken from a longer text, work, or document. It refers to a piece that has been separated or pulled away from the whole.
απόσπασμα.example_greek: Διαβάστε αυτό το απόσπασμα από το βιβλίο του Καζαντζάκη.
απόσπασμα.example_english: Read this excerpt from Kazantzakis's book.

διαμαρτυρία.definition: A formal expression of disagreement, disapproval, or objection to something. It typically refers to a strong verbal or written complaint against an action, decision, or situation.
διαμαρτυρία.example_greek: Η διαμαρτυρία των πολιτών ήταν έντονη και σαφή.
διαμαρτυρία.example_english: The citizens' protest was intense and clear.

εναντόον.definition: A preposition or adverb indicating opposition, contrast, or facing direction. It can mean 'opposite to', 'against', or 'in front of' depending on context.
εναντόον.example_greek: Το σπίτι είναι εναντόον του πάρκου.
εναντόον.example_english: The house is opposite the park.

ειδηλώνω.translation: to manifest, to show, to display
ειδηλώνω.definition: To make something visible or apparent; to display or demonstrate a quality, feeling, or characteristic openly.
ειδηλώνω.example_greek: Η αγάπη του για την οικογένεια ειδηλώνεται σε κάθε του πράξη.
ειδηλώνω.example_english: His love for his family is manifested in every action he takes.

εκπρόσωπος.definition: A person who represents or acts on behalf of another person, organization, or group. Can also be used as an adjective meaning 'representative of' or 'characteristic of'.
εκπρόσωπος.example_greek: Ο εκπρόσωπος της κυβέρνησης δήλωσε την επίσημη θέση της χώρας.
εκπρόσωπος.example_english: The government representative stated the country's official position.

κυριαρχικός.definition: Relating to or exercising supreme power or authority; characteristic of a ruler or sovereign state. Describing something that is dominant or commanding in nature.
κυριαρχικός.example_greek: Η κυριαρχική εξουσία του κράτους είναι απόλυτη.
κυριαρχικός.example_english: The sovereign power of the state is absolute.

φτώχεια.definition: The state of being extremely poor and lacking money or material possessions. It refers to the condition of having insufficient income or resources to meet basic needs.
φτώχεια.example_greek: Η φτώχεια είναι ένα σοβαρό κοινωνικό πρόβλημα.
φτώχεια.example_english: Poverty is a serious social problem.

ανασφάλεια.definition: The state of being unsafe, uncertain, or lacking confidence and stability. It can refer to physical danger or emotional/psychological uncertainty.
ανασφάλεια.example_greek: Η ανασφάλεια στην περιοχή αναγκάζει τους κατοίκους να παραμένουν στα σπίτια τους.
ανασφάλεια.example_english: The insecurity in the area forces residents to stay in their homes.

προταφανής.definition: Extremely obvious or clearly visible; something that is unmistakably apparent or easy to perceive.
προταφανής.example_greek: Η προταφανής αλήθεια ήταν ότι δεν ήθελε να έρθει.
προταφανής.example_english: The obvious truth was that he didn't want to come.

αναπόρευκτος.translation: impassable, not able to be traversed
αναπόρευκτος.definition: Unable to be passed through or traveled on; describing a path, road, or route that is blocked or impossible to navigate.
αναπόρευκτος.example_greek: Το βουνό ήταν αναπόρευκτο λόγω των χιονιών.
αναπόρευκτος.example_english: The mountain was impassable due to the snow.

μοίρα.definition: One's predetermined fate or destiny; also can mean a share or portion of something.
μοίρα.example_greek: Πιστεύει ότι η μοίρα του είναι να γίνει πλοίαρχος.
μοίρα.example_english: He believes his fate is to become a ship captain.

μάταιο.definition: Lacking purpose, result, or effect; ineffective or useless. Can also mean characterized by excessive pride in one's appearance or abilities.
μάταιο.example_greek: Οι μάταιες προσπάθειες του δεν έφεραν κανένα αποτέλεσμα.
μάταιο.example_english: His futile efforts brought no result.

διαφοροποίηση.definition: The process of making or becoming different or distinct; the act of distinguishing between things or creating variety in products, services, or approaches.
διαφοροποίηση.example_greek: Η διαφοροποίηση των προϊόντων είναι σημαντική για την επιτυχία της εταιρείας.
διαφοροποίηση.example_english: Product diversification is important for the company's success.

αγανάκτηση.definition: A feeling of anger or displeasure caused by something considered unjust or offensive. It expresses strong disapproval or irritation.
αγανάκτηση.example_greek: Η αγανάκτηση του κοσμού ήταν εμφανής μετά την ανακοίνωση.
αγανάκτηση.example_english: The public's indignation was evident after the announcement.

φοβίζω.definition: To cause someone to feel fear or anxiety; to intimidate or alarm someone through words or actions.
φοβίζω.example_greek: Τα δυνατά θόρυβα φοβίζουν το παιδί.

ιερεμιάδα.definition: A prolonged, sorrowful complaint or mournful lamentation; a tedious expression of grief or grievances. The term derives from the biblical prophet Jeremiah and his book of lamentations.
ιερεμιάδα.example_greek: Άκουσα μια ιερεμιάδα για τα προβλήματα του διαχειριστή.
ιερεμιάδα.example_english: I heard a lengthy complaint from the manager about the problems.

μοιροράτρης.definition: A person whose fate or destiny is determined; someone who is fated or doomed. This is a rare or archaic literary term combining 'moira' (fate) with 'ratris' (one who strikes or determines).
μοιροράτρης.example_greek: Ο μοιροράτρης δεν μπορούσε να ξεφύγει από το πεπρωμένο του.
μοιροράτρης.example_english: The fate-ordained person could not escape his destiny.

τετελεσμενός.definition: Describing something that has been brought to completion or fulfillment. It carries a sense of finality and perfection, often with connotations of destiny or fate having been fulfilled.
τετελεσμενός.example_greek: Το έργο είναι τετελεσμένο και δεν υπάρχει τίποτα να προσθέσουμε.
τετελεσμενός.example_english: The work is completed and there is nothing more to add.

άγνοια.definition: The state of not knowing or being unaware of something; absence of knowledge or information about a particular matter.
άγνοια.example_greek: Η άγνοια δεν είναι δικαιολογία για τη δική του συμπεριφορά.
άγνοια.example_english: Ignorance is not an excuse for his behavior.

τρομοκρατία.definition: The use of violence and intimidation against civilians to create fear for political or ideological purposes.
τρομοκρατία.example_greek: Η διεθνής κοινότητα καταπολεμά τη τρομοκρατία με συντεταγμένες προσπάθειες.
τρομοκρατία.example_english: The international community fights terrorism with coordinated efforts.

ελίτ.definition: A select group of people considered to be the best or most superior in a particular field or society. The upper class or privileged group.
ελίτ.example_greek: Η πολιτική ελίτ της χώρας αποφάσισε νέες πολιτικές.
ελίτ.example_english: The political elite of the country decided on new policies.

επιβάλω.definition: To force someone to accept or comply with something; to make something necessary or mandatory. Can also mean to be urgent or pressing.
επιβάλω.example_greek: Ο νόμος επιβάλλει νέους κανόνες για την ασφάλεια.
επιβάλω.example_english: The law imposes new safety regulations.

νεοφιλελεύθερης.definition: Relating to or advocating neoliberalism, an economic and political ideology that emphasizes free markets, deregulation, and privatization. It is a modern form of liberalism that emerged in the late 20th century.
νεοφιλελεύθερης.example_greek: Οι νεοφιλελεύθερες πολιτικές προκάλεσαν αντιδράσεις στη κοινωνία.
νεοφιλελεύθερης.example_english: Neoliberal policies provoked reactions in society.

πρόσχημα.definition: A false reason or excuse used to hide the real purpose or intention behind an action. Something presented as a cover for one's true motives.
πρόσχημα.example_greek: Χρησιμοποίησε την ασθένεια ως πρόσχημα για να μην πάει στη δουλειά.
πρόσχημα.example_english: He used illness as a pretext to not go to work.

ισότημα.definition: The state or condition of having equal value, worth, or social standing. It refers to parity or equivalence between persons or things.
ισότημα.example_greek: Η ισότημα των δύο υποψηφίων ήταν ο λόγος που δεν μπορούσαν να αποφασίσουν.
ισότημα.example_english: The equal standing of the two candidates was the reason they could not decide.

ισημερινός.definition: Relating to or located at the equator; characterized by equal day and night length. Can also refer to the equinox period.
ισημερινός.example_greek: Η ισημερινή περιοχή έχει τροπικό κλίμα.
ισημερινός.example_english: The equatorial region has a tropical climate.

ημίχρονο.definition: The break period between the two halves of a sports match, or the period itself that represents half of the total playing time.
ημίχρονο.example_greek: Στο ημίχρονο του αγώνα, η ομάδα μας ήταν μπροστά με 2-0.
ημίχρονο.example_english: At half-time of the match, our team was ahead 2-0.

εικαστικός.definition: Relating to visual arts such as painting, sculpture, or other forms of artistic expression that create visual representations.
εικαστικός.example_greek: Οι εικαστικές τέχνες περιλαμβάνουν ζωγραφική και γλυπτική.
εικαστικός.example_english: Visual arts include painting and sculpture.

παραγωγικός.definition: Capable of producing or generating output, results, or profit. Related to production or productivity in economic or linguistic contexts.
παραγωγικός.example_greek: Αυτή η παραγωγική επιχείρηση παράγει χιλιάδες προϊόντα κάθε μέρα.
παραγωγικός.example_english: This productive company manufactures thousands of products every day.

εύρωστος.definition: Having good physical health and strength; vigorous and sturdy in constitution. Often used to describe someone with a robust build or excellent health.
εύρωστος.example_greek: Ο παππούς μας είναι ένας εύρωστος άντρας παρά την ηλικία του.
εύρωστος.example_english: Our grandfather is a robust man despite his age.

απολυταρχισμός.definition: A political philosophy that rejects all forms of hierarchical authority and government, advocating for a society based on voluntary cooperation and mutual aid.
απολυταρχισμός.example_greek: Ο απολυταρχισμός είναι μια ιδεολογία που αντιτίθεται σε κάθε μορφή εξουσίας.
απολυταρχισμός.example_english: Anarchism is an ideology that opposes every form of authority.

μάζα.definition: A large quantity or amount of something; in cooking, a mixture of ingredients formed into a cohesive substance; in physics, the quantity of matter in an object.
μάζα.example_greek: Η μάζα του ψωμιού είναι έτοιμη για ψήσιμο.
μάζα.example_english: The dough for the bread is ready for baking.

άδειος.definition: Containing nothing; not filled or occupied. Can refer to physical spaces, containers, or positions.
άδειος.example_greek: Το δωμάτιο ήταν άδειο και σκοτεινό.
άδειος.example_english: The room was empty and dark.

άλογο.definition: A large domesticated mammal with hooves, a mane, and a tail, commonly used for riding or pulling vehicles.
άλογο.example_greek: Το άλογο τρέχει γρήγορα στο λιβάδι.
άλογο.example_english: The horse runs quickly in the meadow.

άνδρας.definition: An adult male human being. Also used to refer to a husband or partner in a relationship.
άνδρας.example_greek: Ο άνδρας μιλά με τη γυναίκα του.
άνδρας.example_english: The man is talking with his wife.

άνεμος.definition: The natural movement of air, especially when strong or noticeable. A flow of air from high to low pressure areas in the atmosphere.
άνεμος.example_english: The wind is blowing hard today.

άνοιγμα.definition: A hole, opening, or gap in something; the act or result of opening.
άνοιγμα.example_greek: Το άνοιγμα της πόρτας ήταν δύσκολο.
άνοιγμα.example_english: The opening of the door was difficult.

άνοιξη.definition: The season of the year between winter and summer, characterized by warming temperatures and blooming flowers. In the Northern Hemisphere, it typically runs from March to May.
άνοιξη.example_greek: Την άνοιξη, τα λουλούδια ανθίζουν στον κήπο.
άνοιξη.example_english: In spring, the flowers bloom in the garden.

άρτος.definition: A staple food made from flour, water, and yeast, baked until firm. In Greek culture and Orthodox Christianity, bread holds significant symbolic and practical importance.
άρτος.example_greek: Αγόρασα φρέσκο άρτο από το φούρνο.
άρτος.example_english: I bought fresh bread from the bakery.

άσχημος.definition: Not attractive or pleasing in appearance; lacking beauty or good looks.
άσχημος.example_greek: Αυτό το σπίτι έχει άσχημη αρχιτεκτονική.

ίδιος.definition: Belonging exclusively to a person or thing; indicating possession or unique characteristics. Can also mean 'the same' or 'identical'.
ίδιος.example_greek: Αυτό είναι το δικό μου ίδιο αυτοκίνητο.

αέρας.definition: The invisible mixture of gases that surrounds the Earth and that living things breathe; also refers to a gentle wind or breeze.

αίθουσα.definition: A large room or hall used for meetings, gatherings, exhibitions, or other public purposes. In modern Greek, it commonly refers to a lecture hall, auditorium, or reception room.
αίθουσα.example_greek: Η συνέντευξη θα γίνει στην αίθουσα του ξενοδοχείου.
αίθουσα.example_english: The conference will take place in the hotel's hall.

αίσθημα.definition: A subjective experience or emotion; a physical or emotional sensation felt by a person.
αίσθημα.example_greek: Έχω ένα περίεργο αίσθημα για αυτή τη κατάσταση.
αίσθημα.example_english: I have a strange feeling about this situation.

αβγά.definition: The oval reproductive body produced by female birds, especially chickens, containing an embryo and nutrients, used as food.
αβγά.example_greek: Αγόρασα τρία αβγά από το μανάβη.
αβγά.example_english: I bought three eggs from the grocer.

αγάπη.definition: A deep feeling of affection and care for someone or something. In Greek tradition, it often refers to unconditional, selfless love.
αγάπη.example_greek: Η αγάπη είναι το πιο δυνατό συναίσθημα.
αγάπη.example_english: Love is the most powerful emotion.

αγαπώ.definition: To feel deep affection or love for someone or something. A regular verb expressing the emotion of love or strong liking.
αγαπώ.example_english: I love my family very much.

αγκώνας.definition: The joint between the upper arm and forearm; the bend or angle of the arm.
αγκώνας.example_greek: Χτύπησα τον αγκώνα μου στο τραπέζι.
αγκώνας.example_english: I hit my elbow on the table.

αγορά.definition: A place where goods are bought and sold; a public space for commercial transactions. In ancient Greece, it also referred to the central public square of a city-state.
αγορά.example_greek: Πηγαίνω στην αγορά για να αγοράσω λαχανικά.
αγορά.example_english: I go to the market to buy vegetables.

αγοράζω.definition: to acquire something in exchange for money; to make a purchase of goods or services.
αγοράζω.example_greek: Κάθε Σάββατο αγοράζω λαχανικά στην αγορά.
αγοράζω.example_english: Every Saturday I buy vegetables at the market.

αγροτικός.definition: relating to or characteristic of the countryside, farms, or agricultural activities. Often used to describe rural areas, farming communities, or agricultural traditions.
αγροτικός.example_greek: Ζει σε μια αγροτική περιοχή της Θεσσαλίας.
αγροτικός.example_english: He lives in a rural area of Thessaly.

αγόρι.definition: A young male child or a male person who has not yet reached adulthood.
αγόρι.example_english: The boy is playing in the park.

αδέσποτος.definition: Describes something or someone that has no owner, master, or responsible person; often used for stray animals or abandoned things.
αδέσποτος.example_greek: Βρήκαμε έναν αδέσποτο σκύλο στο δρόμο.
αδέσποτος.example_english: We found a stray dog on the street.

αδελφή.definition: A female sibling; a woman who shares the same parents or one parent with another person.

αδελφός.definition: A male sibling; the son of the same parents or parent as another person.
αδελφός.example_greek: Ο αδελφός μου είναι δύο χρόνια μικρότερος από εμένα.
αδελφός.example_english: My brother is two years younger than me.

αδερφή.definition: A female sibling; the female child of the same parents.

αδερφός.definition: A male who shares the same parents or parent as another person. A male sibling.
αδερφός.example_greek: Ο αδερφός μου είναι δύο χρόνια μεγαλύτερος από εμένα.
αδερφός.example_english: My brother is two years older than me.

αεροπλάνο.definition: A powered aircraft with wings and engines, designed for transporting passengers or cargo through the air.
αεροπλάνο.example_greek: Το αεροπλάνο απογειώνεται στις οκτώ το πρωί.
αεροπλάνο.example_english: The airplane takes off at eight in the morning.

αθλητής.definition: A person who competes in sports or athletic events, especially professionally or at a high level of skill.
αθλητής.example_greek: Ο αθλητής κέρδισε το χρυσό μετάλλιο στους Ολυμπιακούς Αγώνες.
αθλητής.example_english: The athlete won the gold medal at the Olympic Games.

αθλητικά.definition: Relating to or characteristic of athletics, sports, or physical competitions. Can function as an adjective modifying nouns (neuter plural nominative) or as an adverb describing manner.
αθλητικά.example_greek: Τα αθλητικά παπούτσια είναι πολύ άνετα.

αιώρα.definition: A suspended seat or platform hung from above by ropes or chains, used for swinging back and forth as a form of recreation or exercise.
αιώρα.example_greek: Τα παιδιά παίζουν στην αιώρα στο πάρκο.
αιώρα.example_english: The children play on the swing in the park.

ακίνητος.definition: Not moving or unable to move; stationary. Also used to refer to real estate or fixed property.
ακίνητος.example_greek: Το ακίνητο περιουσίας του περιλαμβάνει ένα σπίτι και ένα γραφείο.
ακίνητος.example_english: His immovable property includes a house and an office.

ακατάστατος.definition: Describing a state of disorder, chaos, or lack of stability; characterized by confusion and disarray.
ακατάστατος.example_greek: Το σπίτι του ήταν σε ακατάστατη κατάσταση.
ακατάστατος.example_english: His house was in a chaotic state.

ακμή.definition: The highest point or most flourishing period of something; the peak of development, success, or intensity.
ακμή.example_greek: Ο καλλιτέχνης έφτασε στην ακμή της καριέρας του.
ακμή.example_english: The artist reached the peak of his career.

ακολουθώ.definition: to go or come after someone or something; to pursue or accompany. Also means to understand or comprehend something.
ακολουθώ.example_greek: Ο Γιάννης ακολουθεί τον δρόμο προς το σπίτι.
ακολουθώ.example_english: Yiannis follows the road towards home.

ακούω.definition: To perceive sound through the ears; to listen attentively to someone or something.
ακούω.example_english: I listen to music every day.

ακριβός.definition: Having a high price or cost; also means exact, accurate, or precise. It describes something that is either financially expensive or characterized by exactness and attention to detail.
ακριβός.example_greek: Αυτό το ρούχο είναι πολύ ακριβό.

ακριβώς.definition: In a manner that is exact and without error; used to emphasize accuracy or to confirm agreement with a statement.
ακριβώς.example_greek: Αυτό είναι ακριβώς αυτό που ήθελα.
ακριβώς.example_english: That is exactly what I wanted.

ακροατήριο.definition: A group of people gathered to listen to a speaker, performance, or presentation; also refers to a room or hall where an audience sits.
ακροατήριο.example_greek: Το ακροατήριο άκουσε προσεκτικά την ομιλία του καθηγητή.
ακροατήριο.example_english: The audience listened carefully to the professor's lecture.

ακροατής.definition: A person who listens to something, such as a speech, lecture, or broadcast. Can refer to an individual listener or a member of an audience.
ακροατής.example_greek: Ο ακροατής άκουγε με μεγάλη προσοχή τη διάλεξη.
ακροατής.example_english: The listener was paying close attention to the lecture.

ακόμα.definition: An adverb indicating continuation of a state or condition, or used for emphasis. It can mean 'still' (continuance), 'yet' (negation with time), or 'even' (emphasis).
ακόμα.example_greek: Ακόμα δεν τέλειωσα τη δουλειά μου.
ακόμα.example_english: I still haven't finished my work.

ακόμη.definition: An adverb indicating continuation of a state or action, or emphasis. It can mean 'still' (continuing to the present), 'yet' (up to a point in time), or 'even' (for emphasis).

αλάδι.definition: A liquid fat extracted from plants, seeds, or animals, used for cooking, lighting, or lubrication.
αλάδι.example_greek: Το αλάδι είναι απαραίτητο για το μαγείρεμα.

αλάτι.definition: A white crystalline mineral (sodium chloride) used to season food and preserve it.
αλάτι.example_greek: Το αλάτι είναι απαραίτητο στη μαγειρική.

αλήθεια.definition: The quality or state of being true; conformity with fact or reality; honesty and sincerity.
αλήθεια.example_greek: Η αλήθεια είναι πολύ σημαντική στη ζωή.
αλήθεια.example_english: Truth is very important in life.

αλεπού.definition: A wild carnivorous mammal of the dog family, known for its reddish fur, bushy tail, and cunning behavior.
αλεπού.example_greek: Η αλεπού ήταν πολύ έξυπνη και έκλεψε το κοτόπουλο.
αλεπού.example_english: The fox was very clever and stole the chicken.

αλιεία.definition: The activity or industry of catching fish, or the act of fishing. Also refers to a place where fish are caught.
αλιεία.example_greek: Η αλιεία είναι μια παραδοσιακή επαγγελία σε πολλά παραθαλάσσια χωριά.
αλιεία.example_english: Fishing is a traditional occupation in many coastal villages.

αλλά.definition: A coordinating conjunction used to introduce a contrasting clause or statement that contradicts or modifies what was previously said.
αλλά.example_greek: Θέλω να πάω στην παραλία, αλλά βρέχει.
αλλά.example_english: I want to go to the beach, but it's raining.

αλλάζω.translation: to change, to alter, to exchange
αλλάζω.definition: To make something different or to become different; to replace one thing with another or to undergo transformation.
αλλάζω.example_greek: Πρέπει να αλλάξω τα ρούχα μου.
αλλάζω.example_english: I need to change my clothes.

αλλαγή.definition: The act or process of making something different; a transformation or modification from one state to another.
αλλαγή.example_greek: Η αλλαγή του καιρού είναι αναπόφευκτη.
αλλαγή.example_english: The change of weather is inevitable.

αλληλογραφία.definition: The exchange of letters between people; written communication through letters. Also refers to a collection of correspondence or the act of corresponding by mail.
αλληλογραφία.example_greek: Η αλληλογραφία του με τον φίλο του διήρκεσε για χρόνια.
αλληλογραφία.example_english: His correspondence with his friend lasted for years.

αλλοδαπός.definition: Describing someone or something that comes from another country or is of foreign nationality. It can also mean strange or unfamiliar.
αλλοδαπός.example_greek: Ο αλλοδαπός επισκέπτης ήταν πολύ ευγενικός.
αλλοδαπός.example_english: The foreign visitor was very polite.

αλογο.definition: A large domesticated mammal with hooves, used for riding, transportation, or farm work.
αλογο.example_greek: Το αλογο είναι ένα όμορφο ζώο.
αλογο.example_english: The horse is a beautiful animal.

αλουμίνιο.definition: A lightweight, silvery-white metallic element that is highly resistant to corrosion and widely used in industry and manufacturing.
αλουμίνιο.example_greek: Το αλουμίνιο χρησιμοποιείται για την κατασκευή αεροπλάνων.
αλουμίνιο.example_english: Aluminum is used in the construction of airplanes.

αλφάβητο.definition: The set of letters used in writing a language, arranged in a conventional order. In Greek, it refers to the Greek alphabet consisting of 24 letters.
αλφάβητο.example_greek: Τα παιδιά μαθαίνουν το αλφάβητο στο σχολείο.
αλφάβητο.example_english: Children learn the alphabet at school.

αλφαβητικός.definition: Relating to or arranged according to the order of the letters of the alphabet. Used to describe things organized in alphabetical sequence.
αλφαβητικός.example_greek: Η λίστα είναι σε αλφαβητική σειρά.
αλφαβητικός.example_english: The list is in alphabetical order.

αμάξα.definition: A motorized vehicle used for transportation of people and goods; in older usage, a horse-drawn carriage or wagon.
αμάξα.example_greek: Η αμάξα του είναι κόκκινη και πολύ γρήγορη.
αμάξα.example_english: His car is red and very fast.

αμάξι.definition: A wheeled vehicle, typically horse-drawn, used for transporting goods or passengers. In modern Greek, it can also refer to a car or automobile.
αμάξι.example_greek: Το αμάξι κινείται αργά στο δρόμο.
αμάξι.example_english: The carriage moves slowly on the road.

αμέλεια.definition: The state or quality of being neglectful or careless; lack of proper attention or care toward something or someone.
αμέλεια.example_greek: Η αμέλεια του γιατρού είχε σοβαρές συνέπειες.
αμέλεια.example_english: The doctor's negligence had serious consequences.

αμίαντος.definition: A naturally occurring fibrous silicate mineral that is heat-resistant and was historically used in construction and industrial materials.
αμίαντος.example_greek: Ο αμίαντος χρησιμοποιήθηκε ευρέως στις κατασκευές του εικοστού αιώνα.
αμίαντος.example_english: Asbestos was widely used in twentieth-century construction.

αμαζόνα.definition: In Greek mythology, a member of a legendary nation of female warriors. The term can also refer to a strong, warrior-like woman or to a female river dolphin.
αμαζόνα.example_greek: Η Υπολίτα ήταν η βασίλισσα των Αμαζόνων.
αμαζόνα.example_english: Hippolyta was the queen of the Amazons.

αμεληθώ.definition: First person singular future simple passive form of the verb αμελώ (ameló), meaning to be neglected or disregarded in the future.
αμεληθώ.example_greek: Φοβάμαι ότι θα αμεληθώ αν δεν κάνω καλή δουλειά.
αμεληθώ.example_english: I'm afraid that I will be neglected if I don't do good work.

αμερικανικός.definition: Relating to or belonging to the United States of America or its people, culture, or characteristics.

αμμώδης.definition: Composed of, resembling, or containing sand; having the qualities or appearance of sand.
αμμώδης.example_greek: Η αμμώδης παραλία ήταν πολύ όμορφη.
αμμώδης.example_english: The sandy beach was very beautiful.

αμνός.definition: A young sheep, especially one less than a year old. In religious contexts, it symbolizes innocence and sacrifice.
αμνός.example_greek: Ο αμνός είναι ένα πολύ καλό και ήσυχο ζώο.
αμνός.example_english: The lamb is a very gentle and quiet animal.

αμοιρία.definition: The state of being deprived of something good or fortunate; misfortune or bad luck. It can also refer to the lack or deprivation of a particular benefit or advantage.
αμοιρία.example_greek: Η αμοιρία του ήταν που δεν κέρδισε το λαχείο.
αμοιρία.example_english: His misfortune was that he didn't win the lottery.

αμοντάρω.definition: A transitive verb meaning to disassemble or take apart something that has been assembled or mounted. It is the opposite of 'μοντάρω' (to assemble).
αμοντάρω.example_greek: Αμοντάρω το ράφι από τον τοίχο.
αμοντάρω.example_english: I am taking the shelf off the wall.

αμυντικός.definition: Relating to or intended for defense; serving to protect or guard against attack.
αμυντικός.example_greek: Η αμυντική στρατηγία της χώρας ήταν πολύ ισχυρή.
αμυντικός.example_english: The country's defensive strategy was very strong.

αμφίδεξτρος.translation: ambidextrous, able to use both hands
αμφίδεξτρος.definition: Able to use both the right and left hand with equal skill and ease. The term comes from the Greek 'amphi' (both) and 'dextros' (right/skillful).
αμφίδεξτρος.example_greek: Ο αθλητής είναι αμφίδεξτρος και μπορεί να παίξει με τα δύο χέρια του.
αμφίδεξτρος.example_english: The athlete is ambidextrous and can play with both of his hands.

αμφίπλουρος.definition: An adjective describing something that has two sides or affects both sides equally. In medical contexts, it refers to conditions affecting both sides of the body symmetrically.
αμφίπλουρος.example_greek: Η αμφίπλευρη πνευμονία είναι σοβαρή κατάσταση.
αμφίπλουρος.example_english: Bilateral pneumonia is a serious condition.

αμφιβολία.definition: A state of being uncertain or undecided about something; hesitation or lack of clarity regarding a particular matter.
αμφιβολία.example_greek: Υπάρχει αμφιβολία για το αν θα έρθει στη συνάντηση.
αμφιβολία.example_english: There is doubt about whether he will come to the meeting.

αμφορέας.definition: A large ceramic jar with two handles used in ancient Greece and Rome for storing and transporting wine, oil, or other liquids. It typically has a narrow neck and pointed base.
αμφορέας.example_greek: Ο αρχαιολόγος ανακάλυψε μια παλιά αμφορέα στο ανασκαφικό χώρο.
αμφορέας.example_english: The archaeologist discovered an old amphora at the excavation site.

αμόνι.definition: A heavy iron block with a flat or slightly curved top surface, used as a base for shaping metal with a hammer.
αμόνι.example_greek: Ο σιδεράς χτυπούσε το μέταλλο πάνω στο αμόνι.
αμόνι.example_english: The blacksmith was hammering the metal on the anvil.

αμύδρα.definition: In a manner that is not clear, distinct, or bright; with little light or clarity. Describes something perceived or expressed in an unclear or indistinct way.
αμύδρα.example_greek: Το φως χτυπούσε αμύδρα στο πρόσωπό του.
αμύδρα.example_english: The light struck his face dimly.

αμύνομαι.definition: A middle voice verb meaning to defend oneself, protect, or ward off. It can also mean to defend someone or something against an attack or threat.
αμύνομαι.example_greek: Ο στρατός αμύνεται κατά του εχθρού.
αμύνομαι.example_english: The army defends itself against the enemy.

αμώμητος.definition: Free from blame or fault; without defects or flaws; beyond criticism or reproach.
αμώμητος.example_greek: Ο δικαστής διατήρησε μια αμώμητη φήμη σε όλη την καριέρα του.
αμώμητος.example_english: The judge maintained a blameless reputation throughout his career.

ανάβαση.definition: The act of climbing or ascending upward; a movement from a lower to a higher place or position. The term is also used historically to refer to a military expedition or journey inland.
ανάβαση.example_greek: Η ανάβαση του Ιησού στον ουράνιο θρόνο είναι σημαντικό γεγονός για τους χριστιανούς.
ανάβαση.example_english: The ascent of Jesus to the heavenly throne is an important event for Christians.

ανάβασμα.definition: The act of ascending or climbing upward; a rise or elevation. It can refer to a physical climb, a musical ascent, or figurative upward movement.
ανάβασμα.example_greek: Το ανάβασμα του βουνού ήταν πολύ δύσκολο.
ανάβασμα.example_english: The climb up the mountain was very difficult.

ανάβω.definition: to ignite or start a fire, light, or electrical device; to switch on a light or appliance.
ανάβω.example_english: I turn on the light in the living room.

ανάγκαιος.definition: Required or needed; essential or unavoidable. Something that cannot be done without or must happen.
ανάγκαιος.example_greek: Η εκπαίδευση είναι ανάγκαια για την ανάπτυξη του ατόμου.
ανάγκαιος.example_english: Education is necessary for the development of the individual.

ανάγκη.definition: A condition or circumstance that is necessary or unavoidable; something that must be done or obtained.
ανάγκη.example_greek: Έχω ανάγκη από ένα ποτήρι νερό.
ανάγκη.example_english: I need a glass of water.

ανάγνωση.definition: The act or process of reading text or written material. It can also refer to a particular interpretation or version of something.
ανάγνωση.example_greek: Η ανάγνωση είναι ένα σημαντικό μέρος της εκπαίδευσης.
ανάγνωση.example_english: Reading is an important part of education.

ανάγνωστρα.definition: A woman who reads, especially one who reads aloud or professionally. The feminine form of ανάγνωστης (anágnóstis).
ανάγνωστρα.example_greek: Η ανάγνωστρα διαβάζει το βιβλίο με έμφαση.
ανάγνωστρα.example_english: The female reader reads the book with emphasis.

ανάγριος.definition: Describing something or someone that is wild, untamed, or having a fierce nature. Often used to characterize animals or people with aggressive or uncontrolled behavior.
ανάγριος.example_greek: Ο ανάγριος λύκος κυνήγησε το πρόβατο.
ανάγριος.example_english: The wild wolf chased the sheep.

ανάγω.translation: to lead up, to trace back, to attribute
ανάγω.definition: A transitive verb meaning to lead or guide upward, or to trace something back to its origin or cause. It can also mean to attribute something to a particular reason or source.
ανάγω.example_greek: Η ιστορία του αναγόμενη στον 5ο αιώνα.
ανάγω.example_english: The history traced back to the 5th century.

ανάδυμος.definition: Describing something that rises or emerges from the sea; often used in Greek mythology to describe deities or beings that emerge from water.
ανάδυμος.example_greek: Η Αφροδίτη ήταν ανάδυμος από τα κύματα της θάλασσας.
ανάδυμος.example_english: Aphrodite was rising from the waves of the sea.

ανάδυτα.definition: An ancient Greek bathing establishment or swimming pool, typically a public facility. The term refers to rooms or areas designated for bathing and swimming in classical Greek architecture.
ανάδυτα.example_greek: Τα ανάδυτα ήταν ένας δημοφιλής χώρος στην αρχαία Ελλάδα.
ανάδυτα.example_english: The bathing rooms were a popular place in ancient Greece.

ανάπαυση.definition: A period of time when someone stops working or being active to recover energy or relax. It can also mean a break or interruption from activity.
ανάπαυση.example_greek: Χρειάζομαι μια ανάπαυση μετά από μια δύσκολη ημέρα.
ανάπαυση.example_english: I need a rest after a difficult day.

ανάστημα.definition: The measurement of a person's physical height from head to feet; the vertical extent of a standing body.
ανάστημα.example_greek: Έχει ψηλό ανάστημα και είναι πολύ εντυπωσιακός.
ανάστημα.example_english: He has tall stature and is very impressive.

ανέμωνη.definition: A flowering plant of the genus Anemone, known for its colorful cup-shaped flowers. Also refers to sea anemones, marine animals.
ανέμωνη.example_greek: Η ανέμωνη είναι ένα όμορφο λουλούδι της άνοιξης.
ανέμωνη.example_english: The anemone is a beautiful spring flower.

αναέργαστος.definition: Not worked on or processed; in a natural or raw state without labor or refinement applied to it.
αναέργαστος.example_greek: Το αναέργαστο ξύλο είναι δύσκολο να χρησιμοποιηθεί.
αναέργαστος.example_english: Unworked wood is difficult to use.

αναέρευς.definition: Describing a space or environment that lacks air or oxygen; not containing air.
αναέρευς.example_greek: Το δωμάτιο ήταν αναέρευς και δύσπνοο.
αναέρευς.example_english: The room was airless and suffocating.

αναέρευτος.definition: Not explored or investigated; remaining unknown or unexamined. An adjective describing something that has not been thoroughly searched or studied.
αναέρευτος.example_greek: Η αναέρευτη περιοχή του δάσους περιμένει τους περιπετειώδες ερευνητές.
αναέρευτος.example_english: The unexplored area of the forest awaits adventurous researchers.

αναγκάζω.translation: to force, to compel, to oblige
αναγκάζω.definition: To compel someone to do something against their will; to force or oblige someone to act in a particular way.
αναγκάζω.example_greek: Δεν με αναγκάζει κανένας να κάνω κάτι που δεν θέλω.
αναγκάζω.example_english: No one forces me to do something I don't want to.

αναγνωρίζω.definition: To identify someone or something as being familiar or known; to admit or accept the existence or validity of something.
αναγνωρίζω.example_english: I didn't recognize him at first.

αναγνώριση.definition: The act of recognizing or acknowledging someone or something; the state of being recognized or accepted as valid.
αναγνώριση.example_greek: Η αναγνώριση του έργου του ήταν μεγάλη.
αναγνώριση.example_english: The recognition of his work was great.

αναγνώρισμα.definition: A document or mark used to identify a person or thing; an official credential or badge that proves identity or recognition.
αναγνώρισμα.example_greek: Πρέπει να έχετε το αναγνώρισμά σας μαζί σας.
αναγνώρισμα.example_english: You must have your identification document with you.

αναγνώστης.definition: A person who reads or is skilled in reading. In ancient Greece, it also referred to a professional who read aloud to audiences.
αναγνώστης.example_greek: Ο αναγνώστης διάβασε το βιβλίο με μεγάλη προσοχή.
αναγνώστης.example_english: The reader read the book with great attention.

αναγράφω.translation: to write down, to inscribe, to record
αναγράφω.definition: A verb meaning to write or inscribe something, typically officially or formally. It can also mean to record or register information in writing.
αναγράφω.example_greek: Το όνομά του αναγράφεται στο μνημείο.
αναγράφω.example_english: His name is inscribed on the monument.

αναγραφή.definition: The act of recording, registering, or inscribing something in an official register or document. It can also refer to the inscription or text itself that is written or recorded.
αναγραφή.example_greek: Η αναγραφή του ονόματός του στο μητρώο ήταν υποχρεωτική.
αναγραφή.example_english: The registration of his name in the registry was mandatory.

αναδείκνυμι.definition: A verb meaning to bring to light, reveal, or make evident; also to appoint or designate someone to a position. In ancient Greek, it was commonly used in political contexts to mean 'to elect' or 'to choose.'
αναδείκνυμι.example_greek: Η έρευνα αναδείκνυει νέα στοιχεία για το ιστορικό γεγονός.
αναδείκνυμι.example_english: The research reveals new evidence about the historical event.

αναδεικτικός.definition: Serving to demonstrate, indicate, or reveal something; showing or pointing out clearly. In grammar, refers to demonstrative pronouns or adjectives that point to a specific person or thing.
αναδεικτικός.example_greek: Αυτή η αναδεικτική προσέγγιση δείχνει καθαρά τα προβλήματα.
αναδεικτικός.example_english: This demonstrative approach clearly shows the problems.

αναδηλώνω.translation: to declare again, to proclaim anew
αναδηλώνω.definition: A verb meaning to declare, announce, or proclaim something again or anew. It can also mean to reveal or make known publicly.
αναδηλώνω.example_greek: Ο πρόεδρος αναδήλωσε τα αποτελέσματα των εκλογών.
αναδηλώνω.example_english: The president declared the election results again.

αναδιοργάνωση.definition: The process of organizing something again or differently; a fundamental restructuring of an organization or system.
αναδιοργάνωση.example_greek: Η αναδιοργάνωση της εταιρείας ήταν απαραίτητη για την βελτίωση της παραγωγικότητας.
αναδιοργάνωση.example_english: The reorganization of the company was necessary to improve productivity.

αναδιοργανώνω.definition: To organize again or differently; to restructure or reorganize something that was previously organized.
αναδιοργανώνω.example_greek: Η εταιρεία αναδιοργανώνει τις υπηρεσίες της.
αναδιοργανώνω.example_english: The company is reorganizing its services.

αναδιπλάσιος.definition: An adjective meaning doubled or increased twofold; often used in mathematics or formal contexts to describe something that has been doubled or multiplied by two.
αναδιπλάσιος.example_greek: Ο αναδιπλάσιος αριθμός του δέκα είναι είκοσι.
αναδιπλάσιος.example_english: The doubled number of ten is twenty.

αναδιπλασιάζω.definition: A verb meaning to double something or to increase twofold. It combines the prefix 'ana-' (again/back) with 'diplassiázo' (to double).
αναδιπλασιάζω.example_greek: Ο επιχειρηματίας αναδιπλασίασε τα κέρδη του σε ένα χρόνο.
αναδιπλασιάζω.example_english: The businessman doubled his profits in one year.

αναδρομή.definition: The act of looking back at or recounting past events; a reference to something that happened previously in a narrative or conversation.
αναδρομή.example_greek: Η αναδρομή στο παρελθόν του χαρακτήρα ήταν πολύ συγκινητική.
αναδρομή.example_english: The character's flashback to his past was very moving.

αναδρομικός.definition: Describing something that applies or refers to the past, or takes effect from an earlier date than originally established. Often used in legal or administrative contexts.
αναδρομικός.example_greek: Η αναδρομική ισχύ του νόμου δημιουργεί πολλές συζητήσεις.
αναδρομικός.example_english: The retroactive effect of the law creates many discussions.

αναδόχευση.definition: The act of taking on responsibility for a project, task, or obligation; a contractual agreement to perform work or provide services.
αναδόχευση.example_greek: Η αναδόχευση του έργου ήταν μια μεγάλη πρόκληση για την εταιρεία.
αναδόχευση.example_english: The undertaking of the project was a major challenge for the company.

αναδόχευτος.definition: An archaic or rare adjective meaning something that has not been undertaken, guaranteed, or assumed responsibility for. It combines the negative prefix 'ανα-' with the root related to 'δοχή' (acceptance/guarantee).
αναδόχευτος.example_greek: Το έργο παρέμεινε αναδόχευτο για χρόνια.
αναδόχευτος.example_english: The project remained unundertaken for years.

αναδόχευτρα.definition: A woman who undertakes or contracts to do something, particularly in reference to a woman who assumes responsibility for a task or project.
αναδόχευτρα.example_greek: Η αναδόχευτρα ανέλαβε την κατασκευή του κτιρίου.
αναδόχευτρα.example_english: The female contractor undertook the construction of the building.

αναδύνω.definition: To come up or emerge from below, to surface or rise. Often used literally (coming up from water) or figuratively (emerging into prominence).
αναδύνω.example_greek: Ο ήλιος αναδύνει από το βουνό.
αναδύνω.example_english: The sun emerges from behind the mountain.

αναμμένος.definition: Describes something that is on fire, burning, or illuminated. It is the past participle of the verb ανάβω (anávo), meaning to light or ignite.
αναμμένος.example_greek: Το φως ήταν αναμμένο όλη τη νύχτα.
αναμμένος.example_english: The light was on all night long.

ανατολή.definition: The direction where the sun rises, or the eastern part of a region. Also refers to the act or time of sunrise.
ανατολή.example_english: The sun rises in the east.

ανατολικά.definition: In or toward the east direction; an adverbial form indicating movement or location toward the eastern part of an area.
ανατολικά.example_greek: Το σπίτι βρίσκεται ανατολικά της πόλης.
ανατολικά.example_english: The house is located east of the city.

αναψυκτικό.definition: A non-alcoholic beverage served cold, typically carbonated and sweetened, intended to refresh and quench thirst.
αναψυκτικό.example_english: I want a soft drink with ice.

ανεμώχελυδα.definition: A decorative device mounted on a roof or high point that rotates to show wind direction, typically shaped like a rooster or arrow.
ανεμώχελυδα.example_greek: Η ανεμώχελυδα στην κορυφή του σπιτιού περιστρέφεται με τον άνεμο.
ανεμώχελυδα.example_english: The weathervane on top of the house rotates with the wind.

ανεψιός.definition: A male child of one's parent's sibling; a male cousin. This term specifically refers to a male cousin in Modern Greek.

ανοίγω.definition: to make something open or accessible by moving a door, window, or cover; to unlock or unfasten something.
ανοίγω.example_greek: Ανοίγω την πόρτα για να μπει ο φίλος μου.
ανοίγω.example_english: I open the door for my friend to enter.

ανοιγμένος.definition: In a state of being open; having been opened. Used to describe something that is not closed or has been unlocked.

ανοιχτός.definition: Not closed; accessible or available. Can describe a door, shop, mind, or opportunity that is not restricted or sealed.
ανοιχτός.example_greek: Το κατάστημα είναι ανοιχτό από τις 9 το πρωί.
ανοιχτός.example_english: The shop is open from 9 in the morning.

αντάμωση.definition: A coming together of two or more people; an encounter or meeting between individuals.
αντάμωση.example_greek: Η αντάμωση μας ήταν πολύ ευχάριστη.

αντίθεση.definition: A situation in which two things are strikingly different from or incompatible with each other; the state of being opposed or contradictory.
αντίθεση.example_greek: Υπάρχει μεγάλη αντίθεση ανάμεσα στις δύο απόψεις.
αντίθεση.example_english: There is a great contrast between the two views.

ανταγωνισμός.definition: A state of competition or conflict between two or more parties; the condition of being opposed or hostile to one another.
ανταγωνισμός.example_greek: Ο ανταγωνισμός μεταξύ των δύο εταιρειών ήταν πολύ έντονος.
ανταγωνισμός.example_english: The competition between the two companies was very intense.

αντανάκλαση.definition: The return of light, sound, or other waves from a surface. Also means the act of reflecting or thinking deeply about something.
αντανάκλαση.example_greek: Η αντανάκλαση του φωτός στο καθρέφτη είναι πολύ καθαρή.
αντανάκλαση.example_english: The reflection of light in the mirror is very clear.

αντικείμενο.definition: A thing or item that is tangible or can be perceived; the focus or subject of attention, discussion, or action.
αντικείμενο.example_greek: Το αντικείμενο της μελέτης είναι η ιστορία της Ελλάδας.
αντικείμενο.example_english: The subject of the study is the history of Greece.

αντιπροσωπεία.definition: A group of people appointed or authorized to represent others, or the act of representing someone. It can refer to a formal delegation, diplomatic mission, or representatives acting on behalf of an organization or country.
αντιπροσωπεία.example_greek: Μια αντιπροσωπεία της Ελλάδας συμμετείχε στη διάσκεψη.
αντιπροσωπεία.example_english: A Greek delegation participated in the conference.

ανυπομονησία.definition: The state of being unable or unwilling to wait calmly; lack of patience or tolerance.
ανυπομονησία.example_greek: Η ανυπομονησία του το οδήγησε να κάνει λάθη.
ανυπομονησία.example_english: His impatience led him to make mistakes.

ανυψωτικό.definition: Capable of raising, elevating, or stimulating something physically, mentally, or emotionally. Often used to describe substances or experiences that have an invigorating or stimulating effect.
ανυψωτικό.example_greek: Αυτή η μουσική έχει ανυψωτικό αποτέλεσμα στο κοινό.
ανυψωτικό.example_english: This music has an uplifting effect on the audience.

ανώνυμος.definition: Not identified by name; of unknown or unacknowledged authorship or origin.
ανώνυμος.example_greek: Ο ανώνυμος συγγραφέας έγραψε ένα θαυμάσιο μυθιστόρημα.
ανώνυμος.example_english: The anonymous author wrote a wonderful novel.

αξία.definition: The monetary worth or importance of something; the degree to which something is valued or esteemed.
αξία.example_greek: Η αξία του σπιτιού αυξήθηκε πολύ.
αξία.example_english: The value of the house increased a lot.

αξιοσέβαστος.definition: An adjective describing someone or something that deserves respect, admiration, or veneration; dignified and honorable.
αξιοσέβαστος.example_greek: Ο αξιοσέβαστος καθηγητής είχε μεγάλη επιρροή στους φοιτητές του.
αξιοσέβαστος.example_english: The venerable professor had great influence over his students.

αξών.definition: A straight line around which an object rotates or is symmetrically arranged; in anatomy, the second cervical vertebra of the spine.
αξών.example_greek: Η Γη περιστρέφεται γύρω από τον άξονά της.
αξών.example_english: The Earth rotates around its axis.

αορατικός.definition: Not visible or perceptible to the eye; unable to be seen. Often used in philosophical or spiritual contexts to describe something intangible or beyond perception.
αορατικός.example_greek: Η αόρατη δύναμη της φύσης είναι αορατική αλλά πολύ ισχυρή.
αορατικός.example_english: The invisible force of nature is imperceptible but very powerful.

απάντηση.definition: A response or reply to a question, request, or statement. The act of answering or the words spoken in response.
απάντηση.example_greek: Η απάντησή του ήταν πολύ σαφής.

απάστακτος.definition: Not shaken or moved; remaining firm and unmoved, either physically or figuratively in one's convictions or position.
απάστακτος.example_greek: Ο απάστακτος στάχυς ήταν σύμβολο της σταθερότητας.
απάστακτος.example_english: The unshaken grain was a symbol of steadfastness.

απάστολος.definition: One of the twelve disciples of Jesus Christ, or more broadly, a messenger or envoy sent on a mission.
απάστολος.example_greek: Ο Πέτρος ήταν ένας από τους δώδεκα απόστολους του Ιησού.
απάστολος.example_english: Peter was one of the twelve apostles of Jesus.

απάτη.definition: The act of deliberately deceiving someone or misleading them for personal gain; dishonest or fraudulent behavior intended to trick or cheat.
απάτη.example_greek: Η απάτη δεν θα περάσει απαρατήρητη.
απάτη.example_english: The deception will not go unnoticed.

απέναντι.definition: A preposition or adverb indicating position opposite or facing something; can mean 'across from' or 'in front of'. Often used with the genitive case.
απέναντι.example_greek: Το σχολείο είναι απέναντι από το νοσοκομείο.
απέναντι.example_english: The school is opposite the hospital.

απέχθεια.definition: A strong feeling of dislike or hostility toward someone or something; the state of being hated or despised.
απέχθεια.example_greek: Η απέχθεια μεταξύ των δύο αδελφών ήταν έντονη.
απέχθεια.example_english: The enmity between the two brothers was intense.

απαίτηση.definition: A firm request or assertion of a right; something that is insisted upon as necessary or due.
απαίτηση.example_greek: Η απαίτηση του εργοδότη ήταν πολύ υψηλή.
απαίτηση.example_english: The employer's demand was very high.

απαγόρευση.definition: The act of prohibiting or forbidding something; an official order that prevents something from being done or used.
απαγόρευση.example_greek: Η απαγόρευση του καπνίσματος ισχύει σε όλα τα δημόσια κτίρια.
απαγόρευση.example_english: The smoking ban applies to all public buildings.

απαλή.definition: Having a smooth, soft texture or quality; gentle and delicate to the touch.
απαλή.example_greek: Το δέρμα του μωρού είναι πολύ απαλό.
απαλή.example_english: A baby's skin is very soft.

απαλός.definition: Having a soft, smooth, or delicate texture or quality. Often describes something gentle to the touch or emotionally tender.
απαλός.example_greek: Το μωρό έχει πολύ απαλό δέρμα.
απαλός.example_english: The baby has very soft skin.

απαλότητα.definition: The quality or state of being soft, tender, or delicate to the touch. It can also refer to gentleness or mildness in manner or expression.
απαλότητα.example_greek: Η απαλότητα του δέρματος του μωρού είναι εντυπωσιακή.
απαλότητα.example_english: The softness of the baby's skin is impressive.

απανθρωπία.definition: The quality or state of being inhumane, cruel, or lacking human compassion and kindness towards others.
απανθρωπία.example_greek: Η απανθρωπία του πολέμου προκάλεσε τεράστιο πόνο.
απανθρωπία.example_english: The inhumanity of war caused immense suffering.

απαντώ.definition: to respond to a question or statement; to give an answer to someone who has asked something.
απαντώ.example_greek: Απάντησε στην ερώτησή μου με ειλικρίνεια.

απαράλλακτος.definition: Completely identical or exactly the same; having no difference or variation from something else. Also means unparalleled or incomparable.
απαράλλακτος.example_greek: Τα δύο αδέρφια είναι απαράλλακτα στην εμφάνισή τους.
απαράλλακτος.example_english: The two brothers are identical in their appearance.

απαρτία.definition: A system of institutionalized racial segregation and discrimination, historically associated with South Africa. The term is used in Greek to refer to any form of systematic racial or social separation.
απαρτία.example_greek: Η απαρτία ήταν ένα σκοτεινό κεφάλαιο της ιστορίας της Νότιας Αφρικής.
απαρτία.example_english: Apartheid was a dark chapter in the history of South Africa.

απαρχή.definition: The beginning or commencement of something; in ancient contexts, the first portion of a harvest or spoils offered to the gods as a token or sacrifice.
απαρχή.example_greek: Η απαρχή της νέας εποχής ήταν πολύ ενθουσιώδης.
απαρχή.example_english: The beginning of the new era was very enthusiastic.

απατάω.translation: to deceive, to cheat, to defraud
απατάω.definition: To deliberately mislead or trick someone; to act dishonestly towards another person for personal gain.
απατάω.example_greek: Δεν πρέπει να απατάς τους φίλους σου.
απατάω.example_english: You should not deceive your friends.

απείραντος.definition: Without limits or boundaries; extending indefinitely in space or time. Used to describe something immense or unlimited.
απείραντος.example_greek: Ο απείραντος ουρανός με γεμίζει με δέος.
απείραντος.example_english: The infinite sky fills me with awe.

απλά.definition: In a simple or uncomplicated manner; used to indicate that something is straightforward or without difficulty.

απλώνω.definition: To stretch out or spread something over a surface or area; to extend something that is folded or compressed.
απλώνω.example_greek: Απλώνω τα ρούχα στο σχοινί για να στεγνώσουν.
απλώνω.example_english: I spread the clothes on the line to dry.

αποβάλλω.translation: to cast off, to shed, to discard
αποβάλλω.definition: A transitive verb meaning to throw away, discard, or shed something (such as leaves, skin, or an unwanted item). It can also mean to reject or abandon something.
αποβάλλω.example_greek: Το δέντρο αποβάλλει τα φύλλα του το φθινόπωρο.
αποβάλλω.example_english: The tree sheds its leaves in autumn.

αποβάσιμος.definition: Capable of being navigated or traversed by a ship; describing a waterway or route that is suitable for maritime passage.
αποβάσιμος.example_greek: Το κανάλι είναι αποβάσιμο για μικρά σκάφη.
αποβάσιμος.example_english: The canal is navigable for small boats.

αποβάσκανος.translation: free from evil eye, protected from envy
αποβάσκανος.definition: An adjective describing someone or something that is protected from or free of the evil eye (vaskania), malicious envy, or bad luck caused by envious looks. It can also mean fortunate or blessed.
αποβάσκανος.example_greek: Είναι αποβάσκανος άνθρωπος που δεν τον πειράζει η κακία των άλλων.
αποβάσκανος.example_english: He is a fortunate person whom the malice of others does not affect.

αποβάσκανση.translation: removal of evil eye or curse
αποβάσκανση.definition: The act or process of removing or counteracting the evil eye (vaskania) or a curse believed to have been cast upon someone, often through ritual or magical means.
αποβάσκανση.example_greek: Πήγε στη μάγισσα για αποβάσκανση γιατί πίστευε ότι της έχουν κάνει το μάτι.
αποβάσκανση.example_english: She went to the witch for removal of the evil eye because she believed someone had cast it on her.

αποβάσμα.definition: The act of disembarking or landing from a ship or aircraft; also refers to a landing place or beach where troops or passengers disembark.
αποβάσμα.example_greek: Η αποβάσμα των στρατιωτών έγινε στην παραλία το πρωί.
αποβάσμα.example_english: The soldiers' landing took place on the beach in the morning.

αποβάτης.definition: A person who gets off or disembarks from a ship, vehicle, or aircraft; a passenger who leaves a means of transportation.
αποβάτης.example_greek: Ο αποβάτης κατέβηκε από το πλοίο με προσοχή.
αποβάτης.example_english: The passenger disembarked from the ship carefully.

αποβέβηκα.definition: First person singular perfect tense of the verb αποβαίνω (apovaíno), meaning to land, disembark, or go ashore.
αποβέβηκα.example_greek: Αποβέβηκα στο νησί χθες το πρωί.
αποβέβηκα.example_english: I disembarked on the island yesterday morning.

αποβία.definition: The act of going ashore or disembarking from a ship or boat; the place where one lands or comes ashore.
αποβία.example_greek: Η αποβία των επιβατών έγινε με ασφάλεια.
αποβία.example_english: The disembarkation of passengers took place safely.

αποβίβαση.definition: The act of getting off or unloading from a ship, boat, or aircraft; a military or civilian landing operation.
αποβίβαση.example_greek: Η αποβίβαση των επιβατών ήταν γρήγορη και ομαλή.
αποβίβαση.example_english: The disembarkation of passengers was quick and smooth.

αποβίβασις.definition: The act of disembarking or coming ashore from a ship or vessel. A formal or military term for the process of passengers or troops leaving a boat.
αποβίβασις.example_greek: Η αποβίβασις των στρατιωτών έγινε στην παραλία.
αποβίβασις.example_english: The disembarkation of the soldiers took place on the beach.

αποβίωσις.definition: The act of dying or departure from life; death. This is a formal or literary term used primarily in historical, religious, or formal contexts.
αποβίωσις.example_greek: Η αποβίωσις του βασιλέα ήταν ένα σημαντικό γεγονός για το κράτος.
αποβίωσις.example_english: The death of the king was a significant event for the state.

αποβίωτος.definition: Describing a person who has died or passed away; no longer living.
αποβίωτος.example_greek: Ο αποβίωτος πατέρας του ήταν γιατρός.
αποβίωτος.example_english: His deceased father was a doctor.

αποβαίνω.definition: To have a particular outcome or result; to end up in a certain way. Also means to disembark or get off a ship.
αποβαίνω.example_greek: Η απόφασή του αποβαίνει πολύ επωφελής για όλους.
αποβαίνω.example_english: His decision turns out to be very beneficial for everyone.

αποβατήριον.definition: A place where passengers or cargo are landed from a ship; a dock, quay, or pier used for boarding and disembarking vessels.
αποβατήριον.example_greek: Το αποβατήριον ήταν γεμάτο ναυτικούς και εμπόρους.
αποβατήριον.example_english: The landing place was full of sailors and merchants.

αποβατικό.definition: A military vessel designed to transport troops and equipment and land them on shores, particularly used in amphibious operations.
αποβατικό.example_greek: Το αποβατικό πλοίο μετέφερε τους στρατιώτες στην παραλία.
αποβατικό.example_english: The landing craft transported the soldiers to the beach.

αποβατικός.definition: Related to landing from a ship or aircraft; pertaining to the act of disembarking or getting ashore. Commonly used in military contexts to describe operations or vessels used for landing troops.
αποβατικός.example_greek: Η αποβατική επιχείρηση ήταν πολύ επικίνδυνη.
αποβατικός.example_english: The landing operation was very dangerous.

αποβεβλημένος.definition: Describes someone or something that has been rejected, dismissed, or cast aside. It is a formal or literary term often used to describe a person rejected from society or a thing discarded.
αποβεβλημένος.example_greek: Ο αποβεβλημένος νέος προσπάθησε να ξεκινήσει ξανά τη ζωή του.
αποβεβλημένος.example_english: The rejected young man tried to start his life anew.

αποβημοσίωση.definition: The act or process of removing something from public access or taking it out of publication. This is a formal administrative or legal action to withdraw published material from circulation.
αποβημοσίωση.example_greek: Η αποβημοσίωση του άρθρου έγινε λόγω σφαλμάτων.
αποβημοσίωση.example_english: The depublication of the article was done due to errors.

αποβιάζω.definition: A verb meaning to force someone away, to compel by force, or to constrain. It can also mean to violate or assault.
αποβιάζω.example_greek: Δεν θα με αποβιάσει να κάνω κάτι που δεν θέλω.
αποβιάζω.example_english: He will not force me to do something I don't want.

αποβιοθανάτωση.definition: The act of intentionally ending the life of a person or animal to relieve suffering, particularly in medical contexts or for animals.
αποβιοθανάτωση.example_greek: Η αποβιοθανάτωση είναι ένα σύνθετο ηθικό και νομικό ζήτημα.
αποβιοθανάτωση.example_english: Euthanasia is a complex ethical and legal issue.

αποβλέπω.translation: to aim at, to aspire to
αποβλέπω.definition: To direct one's gaze or attention toward something; to have as one's goal or intention; to aim at achieving something.
αποβλέπω.example_english: He aims to become a doctor.

αποβλήτη.definition: Material that is discarded or thrown away as no longer useful or wanted; waste products or refuse.
αποβλήτη.example_greek: Η αποβλήτη από το εργοστάσιο ρυπαίνει το περιβάλλον.
αποβλήτη.example_english: The waste from the factory pollutes the environment.

αποβλήττω.translation: to look away, to glance aside
αποβλήττω.definition: A verb meaning to look away from something or to cast one's gaze in another direction. It can also mean to disregard or ignore someone.
αποβλήττω.example_english: She looks away from the mirror.

αποβλήτω.definition: To throw away, discard, or reject something; to cast off or abandon.
αποβλήτω.example_greek: Δεν πρέπει να αποβλήτουμε τις παλιές ιδέες χωρίς να τις εξετάσουμε πρώτα.
αποβλήτω.example_english: We should not reject old ideas without examining them first.

αποβοάω.definition: To cry out loudly or shout in a loud voice. It is a compound verb formed from 'απο-' (apo-, away/out) and 'βοάω' (voáo, to shout).
αποβοάω.example_english: The man shouts out for help.

αποβοή.definition: A loud cry or shout; an outcry or clamor, especially one expressing protest or distress. In ancient Greek contexts, it can refer to a loud acclamation or public outcry.
αποβοή.example_greek: Η αποβοή του πλήθους ήταν τόσο δυνατή που ακούστηκε σε όλη την πλατεία.
αποβοή.example_english: The outcry of the crowd was so loud that it could be heard throughout the entire square.

αποβολή.definition: The expulsion or loss of a fetus before viability; also refers to the rejection or dismissal of something.
αποβολή.example_greek: Η αποβολή είναι ένα δύσκολο γεγονός για πολλές γυναίκες.
αποβολή.example_english: Miscarriage is a difficult event for many women.

αποβολιμαία.definition: Extremely offensive, disgusting, or morally reprehensible; worthy of being rejected or cast out. An archaic or literary term expressing strong disapproval.
αποβολιμαία.example_greek: Η συμπεριφορά του ήταν αποβολιμαία και απαράδεκτη.
αποβολιμαία.example_english: His behavior was abominable and unacceptable.

αποβολιμαίον.definition: A place where waste, refuse, or dung is deposited; a dunghill or receptacle for filth.
αποβολιμαίον.example_english: He threw the garbage into the refuse pit.

αποβολιμαίος.definition: An adjective describing something that is extremely unpleasant, repugnant, or deserving to be cast away or rejected.
αποβολιμαίος.example_greek: Αυτή η συμπεριφορά είναι αποβολιμαία και δεν μπορούμε να την ανεχθούμε.
αποβολιμαίος.example_english: This behavior is abominable and we cannot tolerate it.

αποβολιμαίως.definition: In a manner that is extremely disgusting, repugnant, or worthy of abhorrence; in an abominable way.
αποβολιμαίως.example_english: He behaved abominably toward the visitors.

αποβολοσ.definition: The expulsion or loss of a fetus before viability; a spontaneous or induced termination of pregnancy. Also used more generally to mean rejection or expulsion.
αποβολοσ.example_greek: Η αποβολός είναι ένα σοβαρό ζήτημα στην υγειονομική περίθαλψη.
