αποβολοσ.example_english: Miscarriage is a serious matter in healthcare.

αποβουλή.definition: A wicked or malicious intention; a scheme or plot designed to harm someone. It refers to deliberately harmful purpose or conspiracy.
αποβουλή.example_greek: Η αποβουλή του ήταν να καταστρέψει την επιχείρηση του ανταγωνιστή του.
αποβουλή.example_english: His evil intent was to destroy his competitor's business.

αποβουλία.definition: A deliberate plan or act of betrayal; a conspiracy or treacherous scheme against someone. It refers to a hidden plot designed to harm or undermine another person.
αποβουλία.example_greek: Η αποβουλία του κατά του βασιλιά ανακαλύφθηκε πριν εκτελεστεί.
αποβουλία.example_english: The conspiracy against the king was discovered before it was executed.

αποβοφάω.translation: to hiss or boo off the stage
αποβοφάω.definition: A verb meaning to drive away or dismiss someone by hissing or booing, often used in theatrical contexts to indicate audience disapproval.
αποβοφάω.example_greek: Το κοινό αποβόφησε τον ηθοποιό που έδωσε μια κακή παράσταση.
αποβοφάω.example_english: The audience booed the actor who gave a poor performance.

αποβοώ.definition: To go ashore or disembark from a ship or boat. It means to leave a vessel and reach land.
αποβοώ.example_english: The passengers are disembarking at the port.

αποβρασμάτων.definition: Plural genitive form of αποβράσματα, referring to material removed by scraping, filing, or abrasion; shavings or waste fragments.
αποβρασμάτων.example_greek: Τα αποβράσματα του σιδήρου είναι επικίνδυνα για τα μάτια.
αποβρασμάτων.example_english: Iron filings are dangerous for the eyes.

αποβόχειρα.definition: A farewell handshake or gesture of goodbye; the act of shaking hands when parting from someone.
αποβόχειρα.example_english: He gave him a farewell handshake and left.

αποσκευή.definition: Bags, suitcases, and other containers used to carry personal belongings when traveling.
αποσκευή.example_greek: Έχασα την αποσκευή μου στο αεροδρόμιο.
αποσκευή.example_english: I lost my luggage at the airport.

αποσύρνω.definition: To pull, drag, or draw something away from a place or position. Can also mean to withdraw or retreat.
αποσύρνω.example_greek: Ο άνδρας αποσύρνει το καρότσι από το δρόμο.
αποσύρνω.example_english: The man drags the cart away from the road.

αποτελέσματα.definition: The consequences or effects that follow from an action or process; the final product or conclusion of something.
αποτελέσματα.example_greek: Τα αποτελέσματα της εξέτασης ήταν πολύ καλά.
αποτελέσματα.example_english: The exam results were very good.

αποχαιρετισμός.definition: The act of saying goodbye or bidding farewell to someone; a departure phrase or gesture expressing one's farewell.
αποχαιρετισμός.example_greek: Ο αποχαιρετισμός ήταν συγκινητικός και γεμάτος δάκρυα.
αποχαιρετισμός.example_english: The farewell was moving and full of tears.

αποψε.definition: During the evening or night of today; this evening.
αποψε.example_english: We will go to the cinema tonight.

αποψη.definition: A personal belief, judgment, or way of thinking about something; an expression of what someone thinks or believes about a matter.
αποψη.example_greek: Ποια είναι η δική σου αποψη για το θέμα;
αποψη.example_english: What is your opinion on this matter?

απρόσμενος.definition: Not expected or anticipated; occurring by surprise. Describes something that happens contrary to one's expectations.
απρόσμενος.example_greek: Η απρόσμενη επίσκεψή του ήταν μια χαρούμενη έκπληξη.
απρόσμενος.example_english: His unexpected visit was a pleasant surprise.

απόγευμα.definition: The period of the day from noon or early afternoon until evening, typically from around 12 PM to 6 PM.
απόγευμα.example_english: In the afternoon we went to the park.

απόδημος.definition: Describing someone who is away from their home or native place; absent or living abroad.
απόδημος.example_greek: Ο απόδημος Έλληνας ήθελε να επιστρέψει στην πατρίδα του.
απόδημος.example_english: The Greek who was away from home wanted to return to his homeland.

απόδοση.definition: The effectiveness or quality of performance of a person, machine, or system; the ability to produce results or output.
απόδοση.example_greek: Η απόδοση των μαθητών βελτιώθηκε φέτος.
απόδοση.example_english: The performance of the students improved this year.

απόλυση.definition: The act of terminating someone's employment or ending a relationship. It can also mean the release or discharge from an obligation or duty.
απόλυση.example_greek: Η απόλυση του υπαλλήλου ήταν αιφνίδια.

απόμακρος.definition: Located or situated at a great distance; remote or far from a particular place or reference point.
απόμακρος.example_greek: Ζούμε σε ένα απόμακρο χωριό στα βουνά.
απόμακρος.example_english: We live in a remote village in the mountains.

απών.definition: Not present in a place or at an event; lacking presence. The opposite of present or here.
απών.example_greek: Ο Γιάννης ήταν απών από το μάθημα χθες.
απών.example_english: Yannis was absent from the lesson yesterday.

αράδιασμα.definition: The act or result of arranging things in order or rows; an orderly arrangement or display of items in a sequence.
αράδιασμα.example_greek: Το αράδιασμα των βιβλίων στο ράφι ήταν πολύ όμορφο.
αράδιασμα.example_english: The arrangement of books on the shelf was very beautiful.

αρβυλη.definition: A type of high boot or half-boot, especially one worn by actors in ancient Greek and Roman drama, or a laced boot reaching to the middle of the leg.
αρβυλη.example_greek: Ο ηθοποιός φορούσε αρβύλες στη σκηνή.
αρβυλη.example_english: The actor wore buskins on the stage.

αργά.definition: In a manner that is not fast; at a reduced pace or speed. It can also mean 'late' when referring to time.
αργά.example_english: I walk slowly on the road.

αργία.definition: The state of being lazy or inactive; a lack of willingness to work or exert effort.
αργία.example_greek: Η αργία δεν είναι καλή συνήθεια.
αργία.example_english: Laziness is not a good habit.

αργυρό.definition: Made of, resembling, or containing silver; having the color or luster of silver.
αργυρό.example_greek: Το αργυρό κολιέ ήταν πολύ όμορφο.
αργυρό.example_english: The silver necklace was very beautiful.

αργό.definition: At a slow pace or speed; not quickly. Often used to describe the manner or rate at which something happens.
αργό.example_english: We walk slowly in the park.

αργύρω.translation: to silver, to coat with silver
αργύρω.definition: A verb meaning to cover or coat something with silver, or to make something silvery in appearance. It can also mean to turn silver or become silver-colored.
αργύρω.example_greek: Ο τεχνίτης αργύρωσε το παλιό κούπα με μια λεπτή στρώση ασημιού.
αργύρω.example_english: The craftsman silvered the old cup with a thin layer of silver.

αριάδνη.definition: In Greek mythology, Ariadne is the daughter of King Minos of Crete, famous for helping Theseus escape the Labyrinth by giving him a ball of thread. The name is also used as a common feminine given name in Modern Greek.
αριάδνη.example_greek: Η Αριάδνη βοήθησε τον Θησέα να φύγει από τον Λαβύρινθο.
αριάδνη.example_english: Ariadne helped Theseus escape from the Labyrinth.

αριθμητικός.definition: Relating to or based on arithmetic and numbers. Used to describe things pertaining to mathematical calculations or numerical operations.
αριθμητικός.example_greek: Η αριθμητική εξέλιξη είναι σημαντική στα μαθηματικά.
αριθμητικός.example_english: Arithmetic progression is important in mathematics.

αριθμό.definition: A mathematical value or symbol representing a quantity; also refers to a numeral or digit used in counting and calculations.

αριθμός.definition: A mathematical symbol or value used for counting or measuring. Can also mean a numeral, digit, or numerical sequence.

αριστερά.definition: Indicating direction or position towards the left side; the opposite of right. Can function as both an adverb (describing direction) and an adjective (describing position).
αριστερά.example_english: Turn left at the next traffic light.

αριστο.definition: An adjective denoting the superlative degree of quality, indicating something of the highest standard or excellence. It can also refer to aristocratic or noble.

αριστοκρατία.definition: A form of government ruled by a small group of people considered the most worthy or noble, or the privileged class of such people in a society.
αριστοκρατία.example_greek: Η αριστοκρατία ήταν ισχυρή στην αρχαία Ελλάδα.
αριστοκρατία.example_english: Aristocracy was powerful in ancient Greece.

αριστος.definition: Superlative form meaning the best or most excellent in quality or merit. Can also refer to the aristocratic class in ancient Greek society.
αριστος.example_greek: Αυτός είναι ο άριστος μαθητής της τάξης.
αριστος.example_english: He is the best student in the class.

αριστοτέλης.definition: Ancient Greek philosopher (384–322 BCE) and student of Plato, founder of the Lyceum school. One of the most influential thinkers in Western philosophy.
αριστοτέλης.example_greek: Ο Αριστοτέλης ήταν ένας από τους μεγαλύτερους φιλοσόφους της αρχαιότητας.
αριστοτέλης.example_english: Aristotle was one of the greatest philosophers of antiquity.

αρκαδία.definition: A region in the Peloponnese, central Greece, historically known for its pastoral beauty and idyllic landscape. In literature and art, it symbolizes an idealized rustic paradise.
αρκαδία.example_greek: Η Αρκαδία είναι μια ιστορική περιοχή της Ελλάδας με όμορφα τοπία.
αρκαδία.example_english: Arcadia is a historical region of Greece with beautiful landscapes.

αρκετά.definition: To a degree or extent that is adequate or satisfactory; used to indicate that something is sufficient or considerable in amount or quality.
αρκετά.example_greek: Έχω αρκετά χρήματα για να αγοράσω αυτό που θέλω.
αρκετά.example_english: I have enough money to buy what I want.

αρκευμαι.definition: A passive/middle voice verb meaning to be satisfied with something or to be enough/sufficient. It can also mean to content oneself or to make do with.
αρκευμαι.example_greek: Αρκούμαι με λίγα χρήματα για να ζήσω.
αρκευμαι.example_english: I am satisfied with little money to live.

αρκουδα.definition: A large carnivorous mammal with a thick furry body, found in forests and mountains. In Greek culture, it also has folkloric and symbolic significance.
αρκουδα.example_greek: Η αρκουδα είναι ένα μεγάλο και δυνατό ζώο.
αρκουδα.example_english: The bear is a large and powerful animal.

αρμάδα.definition: A large group of ships organized under one command, typically a naval fleet or squadron of vessels used for military or commercial purposes.
αρμάδα.example_greek: Η ισπανική αρμάδα ήταν ισχυρή τον 16ο αιώνα.
αρμάδα.example_english: The Spanish fleet was powerful in the 16th century.

αρμάς.definition: A person who drives a chariot or cart, especially in ancient times. The term is also used historically to refer to skilled drivers or charioteers in military or sporting contexts.
αρμάς.example_greek: Ο αρμάς οδήγησε το άρμα με ταχύτητα.
αρμάς.example_english: The charioteer drove the chariot at high speed.

αρμάσ.definition: A set of straps and fittings used to control and support animals (especially horses), or military equipment and weapons worn by soldiers.
αρμάσ.example_greek: Ο ιππέας έβαλε το αρμάσ στο άλογο.
αρμάσ.example_english: The horseman put the harness on the horse.

αρμένιος.definition: Relating to Armenia, its people, culture, or language. Can also be used as a noun to refer to an Armenian person.
αρμένιος.example_greek: Ο αρμένιος πολιτισμός είναι πολύ παλιός.

αρμα.definition: A wheeled vehicle pulled by horses, used in ancient times for warfare, racing, or transport. In modern Greek, it can also refer to a cart or wagon.
αρμα.example_greek: Ο πολεμιστής ανέβηκε στο άρμα και έφυγε γρήγορα.
αρμα.example_english: The warrior climbed onto the chariot and left quickly.

αρμενία.definition: A country in Western Asia, located in the South Caucasus region, bordered by Turkey, Azerbaijan, Georgia, and Iran.
αρμενία.example_greek: Η Αρμενία είναι ένα όμορφο και ιστορικό έθνος.
αρμενία.example_english: Armenia is a beautiful and historic nation.

αρμενικός.definition: Relating to Armenia, its people, language, or culture.
αρμενικός.example_greek: Η αρμενική γλώσσα είναι πολύ ενδιαφέρουσα.
αρμενικός.example_english: The Armenian language is very interesting.

αρμενιστρια.definition: A woman who steers or controls the helm of a ship or boat. It is the feminine form of αρμενιστής (armeniστής).
αρμενιστρια.example_greek: Η αρμενίστρια κατευθύνει το πλοίο με δεξιότητα.
αρμενιστρια.example_english: The female helmsman steers the ship skillfully.

αρμενοχριστιανος.definition: An adjective describing something or someone related to Armenian Christianity, combining the terms for Armenian and Christian.
αρμενοχριστιανος.example_greek: Η αρμενοχριστιανη κοινοτητα εχει βαθες ριζες στην περιοχη.
αρμενοχριστιανος.example_english: The Armenian Christian community has deep roots in the region.

αρμολογία.definition: The craft or technique of joining pieces of wood together precisely without nails or screws, using joints and fitting methods. Also refers to the joints themselves in wooden structures.
αρμολογία.example_greek: Η αρμολογία είναι μια παραδοσιακή τέχνη που απαιτεί δεξιότητα και υπομονή.
αρμολογία.example_english: Joinery is a traditional craft that requires skill and patience.

αρμονία.definition: A state of accord or agreement between different elements; in music, the combination of different notes played together to create a pleasing sound.
αρμονία.example_greek: Η αρμονία μεταξύ των μελών της ορχήστρας ήταν τέλεια.
αρμονία.example_english: The harmony among the orchestra members was perfect.

αρμονικός.definition: Relating to or characterized by harmony; in agreement or compatible with something else. In music, relating to the overtones or frequencies that create harmony.
αρμονικός.example_greek: Η αρμονική σχέση μεταξύ των δύο μουσικών φθόγγων ήταν πολύ ωραία.
αρμονικός.example_english: The harmonic relationship between the two musical notes was very beautiful.

αρμόδιος.definition: Describing a person who is qualified or responsible for something, or describing something that is suitable and fitting for a particular purpose or person.
αρμόδιος.example_greek: Ο αρμόδιος υπάλληλος θα σας βοηθήσει με την αίτησή σας.
αρμόδιος.example_english: The responsible official will help you with your application.

αρμόζει.definition: Third person singular present tense of αρμόζω, meaning to be suitable, appropriate, or fitting for someone or something.
αρμόζει.example_greek: Αυτό το χρώμα αρμόζει πολύ στο δέρμα σου.
αρμόζει.example_english: This color suits your skin very well.

αρμόζω.translation: to suit, to fit, to befit
αρμόζω.definition: To be appropriate or fitting for someone or something; to match or harmonize with.
αρμόζω.example_greek: Αυτό το χρώμα αρμόζει πολύ στο δέρμα σου.
αρμόζω.example_english: This color really suits your skin.

αρμόνησις.definition: The state of being in harmony or agreement; a harmonious arrangement or coordination of elements. In music, the combination of sounds that creates a pleasing effect.
αρμόνησις.example_greek: Η αρμόνησις μεταξύ των μελών της ομάδας ήταν εξαιρετική.
αρμόνησις.example_english: The harmony among the team members was excellent.

αρμόνι.definition: A pleasing combination of elements; the state of being in accord or agreement. In music, the combination of different notes played simultaneously.
αρμόνι.example_greek: Η αρμόνι μεταξύ των χρωμάτων είναι απίστευτη.
αρμόνι.example_english: The harmony between the colors is incredible.

αρμόνιον.definition: A small wind instrument or keyboard instrument that produces sound through vibrating reeds when air passes through them, commonly used in folk music and popular performances.
αρμόνιον.example_greek: Έπαιζε το αρμόνιον στο τραγούδι του.
αρμόνιον.example_english: He played the harmonica in his song.

αρμόνιουμ.definition: A small keyboard instrument similar to an organ, with a bellows and reeds, commonly used in churches and homes during the 19th and early 20th centuries.
αρμόνιουμ.example_greek: Το αρμόνιουμ ήταν δημοφιλές μουσικό όργανο στο 19ο αιώνα.
αρμόνιουμ.example_english: The harmonium was a popular musical instrument in the 19th century.

αρναία.definition: A young female sheep, typically less than one year old. The feminine form of αρνί (lamb).
αρναία.example_english: The female lamb plays in the meadow.

αρναίος.definition: An adjective describing something that pertains to lambs or is lamb-like in nature. It is derived from the noun 'αρνί' (lamb).
αρναίος.example_greek: Το αρναίο κρέας είναι πολύ νόστιμο.

αρναίστρια.definition: A female lamb or young ewe; the feminine form of αρνάκι (small lamb) or αρνί (lamb). Used to refer to female lambs or sheep in general.
αρναίστρια.example_greek: Η αρναίστρια έδωσε δύο μικρά αρνάκια.
αρναίστρια.example_english: The ewe gave birth to two little lambs.

αρναιστής.definition: A person who denies or rejects something, particularly used in philosophical or religious contexts to describe someone who denies a specific doctrine or claim.
αρναιστής.example_greek: Ο αρναιστής της εξέλιξης υποστήριζε εναλλακτικές θεωρίες.
αρναιστής.example_english: The denier of evolution supported alternative theories.

αρναϊκός.definition: An adjective describing something that pertains to or resembles lambs (αρνί), or characteristic of lambs. It can also mean innocent or gentle like a lamb.
αρναϊκός.example_greek: Το αρναϊκό του χαμόγελο ήταν πολύ γλυκό.
αρναϊκός.example_english: His lamb-like smile was very sweet.

αρσενικό.definition: A highly toxic chemical element (As) commonly used in pesticides and poisons. It can also refer to a male animal or a masculine substance in chemistry.
αρσενικό.example_greek: Το αρσενικό είναι πολύ επικίνδυνο για την υγεία.
αρσενικό.example_english: Arsenic is very dangerous to health.

αρχίζω.definition: To commence or initiate an action, process, or event. It is one of the most common verbs in Modern Greek for expressing the start of something.
αρχίζω.example_greek: Αρχίζουμε να δουλεύουμε στις εννέα το πρωί.
αρχίζω.example_english: We start working at nine in the morning.

αρώματα.definition: Plural form of αρώματα (aroma). Pleasant smells or fragrances, often referring to spices, perfumes, or natural scents.
αρώματα.example_greek: Τα αρώματα των λουλουδιών ήταν πολύ δυνατά.
αρώματα.example_english: The fragrances of the flowers were very strong.

ασάντζ.definition: A sum of money paid in advance, typically as a deposit or preliminary payment before the completion of a transaction or service.
ασάντζ.example_greek: Έδωσα ένα ασάντζ για το σπίτι που θέλω να αγοράσω.
ασάντζ.example_english: I gave an advance payment for the house I want to buy.

ασανσέρ.definition: A mechanical device used to transport people or goods vertically between different floors or levels of a building.
ασανσέρ.example_greek: Το ασανσέρ του κτιρίου είναι εκτός λειτουργίας.
ασανσέρ.example_english: The building's elevator is out of service.

ασθενής.definition: Lacking physical strength or energy; in poor health or condition. Can refer to physical weakness or a person who is ill.
ασθενής.example_greek: Ο παππούς μας είναι πολύ ασθενής και χρειάζεται φροντίδα.
ασθενής.example_english: Our grandfather is very weak and needs care.

ασφάλεια.definition: The state of being safe from danger or harm; protection against risk; also refers to insurance coverage or a safety device.
ασφάλεια.example_greek: Η ασφάλεια είναι πολύ σημαντική στο σπίτι.
ασφάλεια.example_english: Safety is very important in the home.

ατάξια.definition: A state of lack of order, organization, or proper arrangement; disorder or disarray. It can refer to physical disorder, lack of discipline, or general confusion.
ατάξια.example_greek: Η ατάξια στο δωμάτιο ήταν εμφανής.
ατάξια.example_english: The disorder in the room was evident.

ατίμαστος.definition: Not honored or respected; treated with contempt or disrespect. Often used to describe someone who has lost their honor or dignity.
ατίμαστος.example_greek: Ο ατίμαστος πολεμιστής αναγκάστηκε να φύγει από την πόλη.
ατίμαστος.example_english: The disgraced warrior was forced to leave the city.

αταξία.definition: A state of disorder, lack of organization, or confusion. In medical contexts, it can refer to ataxia, a loss of coordination in movement.
αταξία.example_greek: Η αταξία στο γραφείο κάνει δύσκολη την εργασία.
αταξία.example_english: The disorder in the office makes work difficult.

αταξιακός.definition: An adjective describing something that pertains to or is characterized by lack of order, discipline, or organization; chaotic or disorderly in nature.
αταξιακός.example_greek: Η αταξιακή συμπεριφορά του μαθητή ήταν προβληματική.
αταξιακός.example_english: The student's disorderly behavior was problematic.

ατιμάζω.translation: to dishonor, to insult, to disrespect
ατιμάζω.definition: To treat someone with disrespect or contempt; to cause someone to lose honor or dignity.
ατιμάζω.example_greek: Δεν θα επιτρέψω να ατιμάσεις την οικογένειά μας.
ατιμάζω.example_english: I will not allow you to dishonor our family.

ατμόσφαιρα.definition: The layer of gases surrounding the Earth or another celestial body. It can also refer to the general mood or environment of a place.
ατμόσφαιρα.example_greek: Η ατμόσφαιρα του καφενείου ήταν πολύ φιλική.
ατμόσφαιρα.example_english: The atmosphere of the café was very friendly.

αυγό.definition: The oval or round reproductive body produced by female birds, especially chickens, containing a yolk and white surrounded by a shell.
αυγό.example_greek: Αγόρασα τρία αυγά από το σουπερμάρκετ.
αυγό.example_english: I bought three eggs from the supermarket.

αυτί.definition: The organ of hearing and balance located on the sides of the head. It consists of the outer ear, middle ear, and inner ear.

αυτοκίνητο.definition: A motor vehicle with four wheels designed to carry passengers on roads. It is powered by an internal combustion engine or electric motor.

αυτόματος.definition: Operating by itself without human intervention; functioning independently through mechanical or electronic means.
αυτόματος.example_greek: Η πύλη του γκαράζ είναι αυτόματη.

αυτός.definition: A third-person singular masculine pronoun used to refer to a male person or object, or when used with articles/nouns it means 'this' or 'that'. Can also mean 'himself' for emphasis.

αφορίζω.definition: To formally exclude someone from a community, organization, or religious group; to cast out or excommunicate.
αφορίζω.example_greek: Η εκκλησία αφόρισε τον ιερέα λόγω της παραβάσεως.
αφορίζω.example_english: The church excommunicated the priest due to his transgression.

αχλάδι.definition: A sweet, juicy fruit with a characteristic pear shape, typically green or brown when ripe, that grows on pear trees.
αχλάδι.example_greek: Αγόρασα ένα φρέσκο αχλάδι από τη λαϊκή αγορά.
αχλάδι.example_english: I bought a fresh pear from the farmers market.

αχός.definition: A sound reflected back from a surface, or the repetition of a sound caused by reflection. In Modern Greek, it can also mean the fame or reputation that spreads about someone.
αχός.example_greek: Ακούσαμε τον αχό της φωνής του στο βουνό.
αχός.example_english: We heard the echo of his voice in the mountain.

αψίμαχος.definition: An adjective meaning unable to be defeated or conquered in battle; invincible. It combines the prefix 'α-' (not) with 'ψίμαχος' (related to fighting), describing someone or something that cannot be overcome in combat.
αψίμαχος.example_greek: Ο αψίμαχος πολεμιστής νίκησε όλους τους αντιπάλους του.
αψίμαχος.example_english: The invincible warrior defeated all of his opponents.

αψιθιά.definition: A strong, aromatic alcoholic spirit distilled from wormwood and other herbs, traditionally green in color and popular in European countries.
αψιθιά.example_greek: Ο ποιητής ήπιε αψιθιά καθώς έγραφε τα ποιήματά του.
αψιθιά.example_english: The poet drank absinthe while he wrote his poems.

αύριο.definition: The day following today; the next day in the future.
αύριο.example_english: Tomorrow we will go to the beach.

βάση.definition: The lowest supporting part of something; the fundamental principle or foundation upon which something is built or established.
βάση.example_greek: Η βάση της στήλης είναι πολύ γερή.
βάση.example_english: The base of the column is very sturdy.

βέσπα.definition: A stinging insect of the order Hymenoptera, typically having a slender body with yellow and black markings and a venomous sting.
βέσπα.example_greek: Μια βέσπα με τσίμπησε στο χέρι.
βέσπα.example_english: A wasp stung me on the hand.

βίσκοτο.definition: A hard, dry baked confection made from flour, sugar, and butter, often eaten as a snack or with tea.
βίσκοτο.example_greek: Πήρα ένα βίσκοτο με το καφέ μου.
βίσκοτο.example_english: I took a biscuit with my coffee.

βαγόνι.definition: A passenger or freight car that is part of a train, pulled by a locomotive on railway tracks.
βαγόνι.example_english: The train carriage was full of passengers.

βαφή.definition: The process or result of dyeing something, or a dye substance used to color material. In ancient contexts, it can also refer to a dipped or immersed item.
βαφή.example_greek: Η βαφή του υφάσματος έγινε με φυσικά χρώματα.
βαφή.example_english: The dyeing of the fabric was done with natural colors.

βαψιμο.definition: The act or process of dyeing fabric or painting a surface with color. It can refer to the dyeing of textiles or the application of paint to an object.
βαψιμο.example_greek: Το βαψιμο του υφάσματος διαρκεί πολλές ώρες.
βαψιμο.example_english: The dyeing of the fabric takes many hours.

βδομάδα.definition: A period of seven consecutive days, typically from Monday to Sunday or Sunday to Saturday.

βελόνα.definition: A small, thin, sharp pointed instrument used for sewing or medical purposes. It typically has an eye at one end for threading.
βελόνα.example_greek: Χρησιμοποίησα μια βελόνα για να ράψω το ρούχο.
βελόνα.example_english: I used a needle to sew the clothing.

βενζίνη.definition: A flammable liquid hydrocarbon mixture used as fuel for internal combustion engines, particularly in automobiles.
βενζίνη.example_greek: Πρέπει να γεμίσω βενζίνη στο αυτοκίνητό μου.
βενζίνη.example_english: I need to fill up gasoline in my car.

βεντάλια.definition: A device with rotating blades or a hand-held implement used to create air movement for cooling or ventilation purposes.
βεντάλια.example_greek: Άνοιξα τη βεντάλια γιατί έκανε πολύ ζέστη.
βεντάλια.example_english: I turned on the fan because it was very hot.

βερίκοκο.definition: A small orange-yellow stone fruit with sweet flesh, similar to a peach but smaller. It grows on apricot trees and is commonly eaten fresh or dried.
βερίκοκο.example_greek: Αγόρασα φρέσκα βερίκοκα από τη λαϊκή αγορά.
βερίκοκο.example_english: I bought fresh apricots from the farmers' market.

βιβλιοθήκη.definition: A building or institution where books and other materials are kept and made available for public use or borrowing.
βιβλιοθήκη.example_greek: Πηγαίνω στη βιβλιοθήκη για να δανειστώ βιβλία.
βιβλιοθήκη.example_english: I go to the library to borrow books.

βιομηχανία.definition: The manufacturing of goods and products on a large scale using machinery and factories. The economic sector involving industrial production and manufacturing.
βιομηχανία.example_greek: Η βιομηχανία της χώρας αναπτύσσεται γρήγορα.
βιομηχανία.example_english: The country's industry is developing rapidly.

βοήθημα.definition: A means of help or assistance provided to someone in need. It can refer to physical aid, support, or relief offered during difficult circumstances.
βοήθημα.example_greek: Το κυβερνείο παρείχε βοήθημα στους πληγέντες της πλημμύρας.
βοήθημα.example_english: The government provided aid to the victims of the flood.

βοηθώ.definition: To provide aid or support to someone in need. It is commonly used to express the action of helping or coming to someone's assistance.
βοηθώ.example_greek: Πάντα βοηθώ τους φίλους μου όταν έχουν πρόβλημα.
βοηθώ.example_english: I always help my friends when they have a problem.

βουνό.definition: A large natural elevation of the earth's surface, typically rising at least 600 meters above surrounding terrain.
βουνό.example_greek: Το βουνό είναι πολύ ψηλό και όμορφο.
βουνό.example_english: The mountain is very tall and beautiful.

βουστάσιο.definition: A building or structure used for housing cattle and other livestock. It is a farm building designed to shelter and contain animals.
βουστάσιο.example_greek: Ο αγρότης έχτισε ένα μεγάλο βουστάσιο για τα βοοειδή του.
βουστάσιο.example_english: The farmer built a large cattle shed for his livestock.

βοφό.definition: An exclamation used to express disapproval, boos, or to scare someone. It is an interjection commonly used to show dislike or mockery toward a performance, person, or idea.
βοφό.example_greek: Το κοινό φώναξε βοφό κατά τη διάρκεια της παράστασης.
βοφό.example_english: The audience booed during the performance.

βούτυρο.definition: A dairy product made from churned cream or milk fat, typically used in cooking and as a spread.
βούτυρο.example_greek: Χρησιμοποιώ βούτυρο για να φτιάξω κέικ.
βούτυρο.example_english: I use butter to make a cake.

βράδυ.definition: The period of time after sunset and before bedtime, or the darker part of the day. It can also refer to nighttime in general.
βράδυ.example_greek: Το βράδυ πηγαίνουμε για βόλτα στην πλατεία.
βράδυ.example_english: In the evening we go for a walk in the square.

βράχος.definition: A large natural formation of stone; a cliff or rocky outcrop.
βράχος.example_greek: Ο βράχος ήταν πολύ μεγάλος και επικίνδυνος.
βράχος.example_english: The rock was very large and dangerous.

βόσκημα.definition: An animal raised for farming purposes, especially one that grazes. Collective term for cattle, sheep, goats, or other livestock.
βόσκημα.example_greek: Τα βόσκηματα του αγρότη βόσκονταν στο λιβάδι.
βόσκημα.example_english: The farmer's livestock grazed in the meadow.

βότανο.definition: A plant used for medicinal, culinary, or aromatic purposes; generally refers to herbaceous plants with soft stems.
βότανο.example_greek: Το βότανο της μέντας είναι πολύ αρωματικό.
βότανο.example_english: The mint herb is very fragrant.

βύσμα.definition: A device used to close or seal an opening, such as a plug for an electrical socket or a stopper for a bottle.
βύσμα.example_greek: Χρειάζεται ένα βύσμα για τη σκέπασμα του φιάλης.
βύσμα.example_english: A plug is needed to cover the opening of the bottle.

γάιτάνι.definition: A long, flexible cord made of twisted fibers, used for binding, tying, or securing objects.
γάιτάνι.example_greek: Πήρε το γάιτάνι για να δέσει το κιβώτιο.
γάιτάνι.example_english: He took the rope to tie the box.

γάλα.definition: A white liquid produced by mammals to feed their young, commonly consumed as a beverage or used in cooking and dairy products.
γάλα.example_greek: Πίνω ένα ποτήρι γάλα κάθε πρωί.
γάλα.example_english: I drink a glass of milk every morning.

γάστρα.definition: A round cooking pot or container, typically made of metal or ceramic, used for cooking food on a stove or in an oven.
γάστρα.example_greek: Έβαλα το νερό στη γάστρα για να βράσει.
γάστρα.example_english: I put the water in the pot to boil.

γέλιο.definition: The sound or expression of mirth and amusement; the act of laughing.
γέλιο.example_greek: Το γέλιο των παιδιών ήταν μεταδοτικό.

γέννηση.definition: The act or process of being born; the emergence of a new individual into the world.
γέννηση.example_greek: Η γέννηση του παιδιού ήταν μια ευχάριστη στιγμή για την οικογένεια.
γέννηση.example_english: The birth of the child was a joyful moment for the family.

γαιδάρος.definition: A domestic animal similar to a horse but smaller, with long ears and a stubborn nature, commonly used as a beast of burden.
γαιδάρος.example_greek: Ο γαιδάρος είναι πολύ δυνατό ζώο.
γαιδάρος.example_english: The donkey is a very strong animal.

γαλήνη.definition: A state of peace, calmness, and absence of disturbance. In ancient contexts, it refers to calm weather at sea.
γαλήνη.example_greek: Η γαλήνη της νύχτας ήταν γαλήνια.
γαλήνη.example_english: The calm of the night was peaceful.

γαλακτοκομία.definition: The business or practice of raising dairy cattle and producing milk and milk products such as cheese and yogurt.
γαλακτοκομία.example_greek: Η γαλακτοκομία είναι μια σημαντική βιομηχανία στην Ελλάδα.
γαλακτοκομία.example_english: Dairy farming is an important industry in Greece.

γαλιά.definition: A white liquid produced by mammals for feeding their young, commonly consumed as a beverage or used in cooking and dairy products.
γαλιά.example_greek: Πίνω ένα ποτήρι γαλιά κάθε πρωί.
γαλιά.example_english: I drink a glass of milk every morning.

γαλόπουλα.definition: A large domesticated bird commonly raised for its meat, especially around Thanksgiving and Christmas.

γαμήλιος.definition: Relating to marriage or weddings; connected with the state of matrimony or wedding ceremonies.
γαμήλιος.example_greek: Η γαμήλια τελετή ήταν πολύ ωραία.
γαμήλιος.example_english: The wedding ceremony was very beautiful.

γαμπρός.definition: A man on his wedding day, or a man married to one's daughter or sister.
γαμπρός.example_greek: Ο γαμπρός ήταν πολύ ευχαριστημένος στη μέρα του γάμου.
γαμπρός.example_english: The bridegroom was very happy on his wedding day.

γανώματα.definition: Plural form of γάνωμα, referring to glossy or shiny coatings applied to surfaces, such as enamel paint, glaze, or varnish.
γανώματα.example_greek: Τα γανώματα του αυτοκινήτου είναι πολύ λαμπερά.
γανώματα.example_english: The enamels of the car are very shiny.

γαρίφανο.definition: A flowering plant with pink, red, white, or purple blooms, commonly used in bouquets and decorations. The word derives from the French 'girofle.'
γαρίφανο.example_greek: Τα κόκκινα γαρίφανα ήταν πολύ όμορφα στο βάζο.
γαρίφανο.example_english: The red carnations were very beautiful in the vase.

γαρνιτούρα.definition: Decorative elements added to food for presentation, or ornamental trimming on clothing or furniture.
γαρνιτούρα.example_greek: Η γαρνιτούρα του πιάτου ήταν πολύ όμορφη.
γαρνιτούρα.example_english: The garnish on the plate was very beautiful.

γαύρος.definition: Displaying excessive pride or arrogance; inclined to boast about one's achievements or qualities in an unpleasant way.
γαύρος.example_greek: Είναι πολύ γαύρος για τις επιτυχίες του.
γαύρος.example_english: He is very proud about his successes.

γείτονας.definition: A person who lives next door or nearby; someone living in an adjacent property or neighborhood.
γείτονας.example_greek: Ο γείτονάς μας είναι πολύ φιλικός.

γεμάτος.definition: Containing or holding as much as possible; completely filled or loaded with something.
γεμάτος.example_greek: Το σακάκι του ήταν γεμάτο με χρήματα.
γεμάτος.example_english: His jacket was full of money.

γεν.definition: A unit of heredity consisting of a sequence of DNA that codes for a specific trait or characteristic in living organisms.
γεν.example_greek: Το γεν είναι υπεύθυνο για το χρώμα των ματιών.
γεν.example_english: The gene is responsible for eye color.

γενέθλια.definition: The anniversary of the day on which a person was born, typically celebrated with gifts, cake, and festive activities.
γενέθλια.example_greek: Τα γενέθλιά μου είναι στις 15 Ιουνίου.

γενέθλιο.definition: The anniversary of the day on which a person was born, typically celebrated with gifts and festivities.
γενέθλιο.example_greek: Το γενέθλιό μου είναι στις 15 Μαΐου.

γενέτης.definition: A male parent or ancestor; one who generates or procreates. An archaic or formal term for father.
γενέτης.example_greek: Ο γενέτης του παιδιού ήταν άγνωστος.
γενέτης.example_english: The father of the child was unknown.

γενειάδα.definition: The hair that grows on a man's chin and lower face, or a long pointed beard. It can also refer to a goatee or similar style of facial hair.

γενετήσιος.definition: Relating to birth or a birthday; used to describe celebrations or events connected with someone's birth.
γενετήσιος.example_greek: Το γενετήσιο πάρτι του ήταν πολύ διασκεδαστικό.
γενετήσιος.example_english: His birthday party was very fun.

γενετική.definition: Relating to genes or heredity; connected with the study of genetics and inherited characteristics.
γενετική.example_greek: Η γενετική έρευνα αποκάλυψε νέα στοιχεία για την ασθένεια.
γενετική.example_english: Genetic research revealed new information about the disease.

γενικά.definition: In most cases; as a rule; without specific details or exceptions. Used to indicate a general statement or overview rather than specific particulars.
γενικά.example_greek: Γενικά, το καλοκαίρι είναι θερμό στην Ελλάδα.
γενικά.example_english: Generally, summer is hot in Greece.

γενικώς.definition: In a general manner; commonly; without specific details or exceptions. Used to indicate a broad or overall statement rather than a particular case.
γενικώς.example_greek: Γενικώς, το καιρό είναι καλό αυτή την εποχή.
γενικώς.example_english: Generally, the weather is nice at this time of year.

γεννήτης.definition: A person or device that generates or produces something; one who begets or creates.
γεννήτης.example_greek: Ο γεννήτης της ιδέας ήταν ένας νεαρός επιστήμονας.
γεννήτης.example_english: The creator of the idea was a young scientist.

γεννήτρια.definition: A machine or device that produces electrical energy by converting mechanical energy, or a female generator in technical contexts.
γεννήτρια.example_greek: Η γεννήτρια παράγει ηλεκτρικό ρεύμα για το σπίτι.
γεννήτρια.example_english: The generator produces electrical current for the house.

γενναίος.definition: Possessing or showing courage and bravery in the face of danger or adversity. Also means noble or generous in character.
γενναίος.example_greek: Ο γενναίος πολεμιστής δεν φοβήθηκε τον εχθρό.
γενναίος.example_english: The brave warrior was not afraid of the enemy.

γεννητικός.definition: Relating to generation, reproduction, or procreation; in grammar, referring to the genitive case. Used to describe things connected with birth, creation, or the genitive grammatical case.
γεννητικός.example_greek: Ο γεννητικός όργανος είναι σημαντικός για την αναπαραγωγή.
γεννητικός.example_english: The reproductive organ is important for reproduction.

γεννώ.definition: To bring forth offspring; to cause something to be created or generated. Used for biological reproduction or the creation of abstract things.
γεννώ.example_greek: Η μητέρα γεννά ένα υγιές μωρό.
γεννώ.example_english: The mother gives birth to a healthy baby.

γιαγιά.definition: The mother of one's parent; a female grandparent. A term of endearment commonly used in Greek families.
γιαγιά.example_greek: Η γιαγιά μου μένει στην Αθήνα.

γιος.definition: A male child in relation to his parents. The masculine form of offspring.
γιος.example_greek: Ο γιος μου είναι δέκα χρονών.
γιος.example_english: My son is ten years old.

γράφω.definition: to mark letters, words, or symbols on a surface; to compose written work such as a letter, book, or article.
γράφω.example_greek: Γράφω ένα γράμμα στη φίλη μου.
γράφω.example_english: I am writing a letter to my friend.

γρήγορα.definition: In a rapid manner; at high speed or with quick movement.
γρήγορα.example_greek: Πήγαινε γρήγορα στο σχολείο γιατί έκανες καθυστέρηση.
γρήγορα.example_english: Go quickly to school because you're late.

γυμναστήριο.definition: A facility equipped with exercise machines and equipment for physical training and fitness activities.
γυμναστήριο.example_greek: Πηγαίνω στο γυμναστήριο τρεις φορές την εβδομάδα.
γυμναστήριο.example_english: I go to the gym three times a week.

γυναίκα.definition: An adult female human being. Also used to refer to a wife or female partner in a relationship.

γόνατο.definition: The joint between the thigh and lower leg in humans and animals. It is a crucial joint that allows bending and straightening of the leg.
γόνατο.example_greek: Έπεσε και χτύπησε το γόνατό του.
γόνατο.example_english: He fell and hit his knee.

δίνω.definition: To transfer something to someone; to provide or offer something to another person.
δίνω.example_greek: Δίνω ένα δώρο στη φίλη μου.
δίνω.example_english: I give a gift to my friend.

δεξιά.definition: Referring to the right side or direction; the opposite of left. Can function as an adjective modifying a noun or as an adverb indicating direction.

δευτέρα.definition: The second day of the week in the Gregorian calendar, following Sunday and preceding Tuesday.
δευτέρα.example_english: On Monday I go to school.

διαβάζω.definition: to look at and understand written or printed words; to study by reading. Can also mean to cross over or traverse.
διαβάζω.example_english: I am reading an interesting book.

διακοπές.definition: A period of time off from work or school for rest and leisure. Can also refer to interruptions or breaks in continuity.
διακοπές.example_greek: Πέρασα τις διακοπές μου στο νησί.
διακοπές.example_english: I spent my vacation on the island.

διαφορετικός.definition: Not the same as another; having distinguishing characteristics that set it apart from something else.
διαφορετικός.example_greek: Αυτό το χρώμα είναι διαφορετικό από εκείνο.
διαφορετικός.example_english: This color is different from that one.

δουλειά.definition: A task, job, or piece of work that needs to be done. Can refer to employment, labor, or any activity requiring effort.
δουλειά.example_greek: Έχω πολλή δουλειά στο γραφείο σήμερα.
δουλειά.example_english: I have a lot of work at the office today.

δουλεύω.definition: to perform work or labor; to serve as a slave or servant. In modern Greek, it primarily means to work at a job or profession.
δουλεύω.example_greek: Δουλεύω σε ένα γραφείο στο κέντρο της πόλης.
δουλεύω.example_english: I work in an office in the city center.

δωμάτιο.definition: An enclosed space within a building, typically a bedroom, living room, or other functional space.
δωμάτιο.example_greek: Το δωμάτιό μου είναι πολύ μικρό.

δόντι.definition: The dative singular form of δόντι (dónt), meaning tooth. Used when a tooth is the indirect object of a verb or follows certain prepositions.
δόντι.example_english: I have a pain in my tooth.

δύσκολος.definition: Requiring considerable effort or skill to accomplish; not easy. Can also mean difficult in temperament or behavior.
δύσκολος.example_greek: Αυτό το μάθημα είναι πολύ δύσκολο.

ελαφρύς.definition: Having little weight or not heavy; also meaning simple or not difficult to do.

εστιατόριο.definition: An establishment where meals and drinks are served to customers in exchange for payment.
εστιατόριο.example_greek: Χθες πήγαμε σε ένα πολύ καλό εστιατόριο.
εστιατόριο.example_english: Yesterday we went to a very good restaurant.

εύκολος.definition: Not difficult; requiring little effort or ability to accomplish or understand.
εύκολος.example_greek: Αυτή η άσκηση είναι πολύ εύκολη.

ζάχαρη.definition: A sweet crystalline substance obtained from sugar cane or sugar beets, used as a sweetener in food and beverages.
ζάχαρη.example_greek: Θέλω δύο κουταλιές ζάχαρη στον καφέ μου.
ζάχαρη.example_english: I want two teaspoons of sugar in my coffee.

ζεστός.definition: Having or producing heat; at a high temperature or comfortably warm.

ημέρα.definition: A period of 24 hours, or the time between sunrise and sunset when it is light outside.
ημέρα.example_greek: Η ημέρα είναι πολύ όμορφη σήμερα.
ημέρα.example_english: The day is very beautiful today.

θέλω.definition: To have a desire or intention to do something; to express willingness or preference.
θέλω.example_greek: Θέλω να πάω στην παραλία αύριο.
θέλω.example_english: I want to go to the beach tomorrow.

θυμάμαι.definition: A deponent verb meaning to remember or recall something from memory. It expresses the action of bringing past events or information back to mind.
θυμάμαι.example_greek: Θυμάμαι την πρώτη μέρα του σχολείου.
θυμάμαι.example_english: I remember the first day of school.

θυμωμένος.definition: Feeling or showing anger; in a state of irritation or displeasure. Often used to describe someone who is upset about something.
θυμωμένος.example_greek: Ο Γιάννης είναι πολύ θυμωμένος επειδή έχασε το παιχνίδι.
θυμωμένος.example_english: John is very angry because he lost the game.

κάθομαι.definition: to be in a seated position or to occupy a seat. It is a middle voice verb in Modern Greek.
κάθομαι.example_english: I sit on the chair and read.

κίτρινο.definition: A color adjective describing something that is the color of lemons or the sun. It is commonly used to describe objects, clothing, or other visible things.
κίτρινο.example_greek: Το κίτρινο αυτοκίνητο είναι πολύ όμορφο.
κίτρινο.example_english: That yellow car is very beautiful.

κακός.definition: Of poor quality or undesirable; morally wrong or evil; causing harm or suffering.

καλοκαίρι.definition: The warmest season of the year, occurring between spring and autumn, typically from June to August in the Northern Hemisphere.
καλοκαίρι.example_greek: Το καλοκαίρι πάω πάντα στη θάλασσα.
καλοκαίρι.example_english: In summer I always go to the sea.

καλός.definition: Possessing positive qualities; pleasing to the senses or mind. One of the most common adjectives in Greek, used to describe people, things, or situations positively.

καρέκλα.definition: A piece of furniture with a seat, back, and four legs, designed for one person to sit on.
καρέκλα.example_english: She sits on the chair and reads.

καφές.definition: A beverage made from roasted and ground coffee beans, typically served hot. Also refers to a coffee shop or café.
καφές.example_english: I want a coffee with sugar.

κεφάλι.definition: The upper part of the human body containing the brain, eyes, ears, nose, and mouth. Also used metaphorically to mean a leader or the top of something.

κλείσιμο.definition: The act or process of closing something, or the state of being closed. Can refer to closure of a business, door, or event.
κλείσιμο.example_greek: Το κλείσιμο του καταστήματος γίνεται στις εννέα το βράδυ.
κλείσιμο.example_english: The closing of the store happens at nine in the evening.

κοιλιά.definition: The front part of the human body between the chest and legs; the stomach or abdominal region.

κοντός.definition: Of small height or length; situated at a short distance. Can describe physical dimensions or proximity.
κοντός.example_greek: Ο άντρας είναι κοντός και έχει μαύρα μαλλιά.
κοντός.example_english: The man is short and has black hair.

κουζίνα.definition: A room or area in a building where food is prepared and cooked.
κουζίνα.example_greek: Η κουζίνα μας είναι πολύ μικρή.

κρέας.definition: The flesh of an animal used as food. It is a neuter noun in Modern Greek.
κρέας.example_english: I bought meat from the butcher shop.

κρασί.definition: An alcoholic beverage made from fermented grapes. It is a common drink in Greek culture and cuisine.
κρασί.example_greek: Το κρασί είναι πολύ καλό σε αυτό το εστιατόριο.
κρασί.example_english: The wine is very good at this restaurant.

κρεβάτι.definition: A piece of furniture used for sleeping, typically consisting of a frame with a mattress.
κρεβάτι.example_greek: Το κρεβάτι μου είναι πολύ άνετο.

κρεμμύδι.definition: A round or bulbous vegetable with a papery skin, pungent flavor, and layers of edible flesh, used widely in cooking.
κρεμμύδι.example_english: I use onion in my salad.

κρύος.definition: Having a low temperature; cool or chilly. Used to describe weather, sensations, or metaphorically, a cold demeanor.
κρύος.example_greek: Το νερό είναι πολύ κρύο σήμερα.
κρύος.example_english: The water is very cold today.

κυριακή.definition: The first day of the week, considered a holy day in Christian tradition. In Greece, it is a day of rest and religious observance.
κυριακή.example_english: On Sunday we go to church.

κόκκινο.definition: The color red; having the hue of blood or fire. Used to describe objects, qualities, or appearances.
κόκκινο.example_greek: Το κόκκινο αυτοκίνητο είναι πολύ γρήγορο.
κόκκινο.example_english: The red car is very fast.

κόρη.definition: A young female person or a daughter. In ancient Greek, it also referred to a statue of a young woman or a maiden in a spiritual sense.
κόρη.example_english: The girl plays in the park.

κόστος.definition: The amount of money required to buy or do something; the expense or price of an item or service.
κόστος.example_greek: Το κόστος του εισιτηρίου είναι πολύ υψηλό.
κόστος.example_english: The cost of the ticket is very high.

κότα.definition: A female chicken, typically kept for laying eggs or meat production. The term specifically refers to the female of the species, as opposed to a rooster.

λάδι.definition: A greasy liquid obtained from olives or other plants, used for cooking, lighting, or lubrication.
λάδι.example_greek: Το λάδι είναι πολύ σημαντικό στην ελληνική κουζίνα.
λάδι.example_english: Oil is very important in Greek cuisine.

λέγω.definition: To express words or communicate thoughts orally. It is one of the most common verbs in Greek, used to introduce speech or convey information.
λέγω.example_greek: Τι λες; Λέγω ότι είναι καλό.
λέγω.example_english: What are you saying? I'm saying that it's good.

λαχανικό.definition: A plant or part of a plant that is used as food, typically as a savory accompaniment to a main meal rather than as a sweet course.
λαχανικό.example_greek: Αγοράζω φρέσκα λαχανικά από την αγορά κάθε εβδομάδα.
λαχανικό.example_english: I buy fresh vegetables from the market every week.

λεπτό.definition: A unit of time equal to 60 seconds, or one sixtieth of an hour.
λεπτό.example_greek: Περίμενε με λίγα λεπτά, θα έρθω σε ένα λεπτό.
λεπτό.example_english: Wait for me a few minutes, I'll come in a minute.

λεπτός.definition: Having a small thickness or width; slender. Can also mean refined, subtle, or keen in perception.
λεπτός.example_greek: Αυτό το ύφασμα είναι πολύ λεπτό και κομψό.
λεπτός.example_english: This fabric is very fine and elegant.

λευκό.definition: Of the color white; the lightest color, the opposite of black.
λευκό.example_greek: Το λευκό φόρεμα είναι πολύ όμορφο.
λευκό.example_english: The white dress is very beautiful.

λυπημένος.definition: Feeling or showing sadness, grief, or distress. Used to describe someone who is emotionally unhappy or troubled.
λυπημένος.example_greek: Ήταν λυπημένος που δεν μπόρεσε να έρθει στο πάρτι.
λυπημένος.example_english: He was sad that he couldn't come to the party.

μάτι.definition: The organ of sight in humans and animals. It is used literally to refer to the physical eye or figuratively in expressions.

μήλο.definition: A round fruit with red, green, or yellow skin that grows on apple trees. It is commonly eaten fresh or used in cooking and desserts.
μήλο.example_greek: Αγόρασα τρία μήλα από την αγορά.
μήλο.example_english: I bought three apples from the market.

μήνας.definition: A period of time consisting of 28 to 31 days, used as a division of the calendar year.
μήνας.example_greek: Ο Ιανουάριος είναι ο πρώτος μήνας του χρόνου.
μήνας.example_english: January is the first month of the year.

μαύρο.definition: The color black; dark in appearance or lacking light.

μεγάλος.definition: Of considerable size or extent; greater than average in dimension or importance.
μεγάλος.example_greek: Έχει ένα μεγάλο σπίτι στην εξοχή.
μεγάλος.example_english: He has a big house in the countryside.

μητέρα.definition: A female parent; a woman who has given birth to or is raising a child.
μητέρα.example_greek: Η μητέρα μου είναι πολύ καλή.

μικρός.definition: Of limited size or extent; not large. Can also mean young in age or insignificant in importance.
μικρός.example_greek: Το μικρό σπίτι είναι πολύ άνετο.
μικρός.example_english: The small house is very comfortable.

μπλε.definition: A color adjective describing something that has the hue of the sky or sea. It is indeclinable in Modern Greek and does not change form based on gender, number, or case.

declension.note: This adjective is indeclinable in Modern Greek and does not change form regardless of gender, number, or case.

μπορώ.definition: to have the ability or permission to do something; to be capable of performing an action

μύτη.definition: The protruding facial feature above the mouth used for breathing and smelling.

νέος.definition: Recently made, produced, or acquired; or being in an early stage of life or existence.

νερό.definition: A transparent, colorless liquid that is essential for life, consisting of hydrogen and oxygen.

νιώθω.translation: to feel, to sense, to perceive
νιώθω.definition: To experience an emotion, physical sensation, or intuitive awareness. To have a feeling or impression about something.
νιώθω.example_greek: Νιώθω ευχαρίστηση όταν είμαι με τους φίλους μου.
νιώθω.example_english: I feel happy when I am with my friends.

ντύνομαι.translation: to dress, to wear, to get dressed
ντύνομαι.definition: A middle/passive voice verb meaning to put on clothes or to get dressed. It can also mean to wear or be dressed in something.
ντύνομαι.example_greek: Κάθε πρωί ντύνομαι γρήγορα για να πάω στη δουλειά.
ντύνομαι.example_english: Every morning I get dressed quickly to go to work.

νωρίς.definition: At or during an early time; before the usual or expected time.

νόστιμος.definition: Having a pleasant flavor; delicious or appetizing to the taste.

ξέχνω.definition: to fail to remember something or someone; to cease to think about or recall information.

ξυπνάω.definition: To stop sleeping and become conscious; to wake someone from sleep. Can be used as both transitive (to wake someone) and intransitive (to wake up).
ξυπνάω.example_english: I wake up every morning at seven.

ξυπνώ.definition: To regain consciousness from sleep or to cause someone to stop sleeping. Can be used both transitively (to wake someone up) and intransitively (to wake up oneself).
ξυπνώ.example_greek: Ξυπνάω κάθε πρωί στις 7 το πρωί.
ξυπνώ.example_english: I wake up every morning at 7 o'clock.

πέμπτη.definition: The fifth day of the week, occurring between Wednesday and Friday.
πέμπτη.example_greek: Την Πέμπτη θα πάμε στο σινεμά.
πέμπτη.example_english: On Thursday we will go to the cinema.

πίνω.definition: to consume a liquid by swallowing it. A common verb describing the action of drinking beverages such as water, wine, coffee, or other drinks.

πίσω.definition: Indicates direction or position towards the rear or posterior part of something; can mean physically behind or in the past.
πίσω.example_english: Go back and wait for me.

παίρνω.translation: to take, to get, to catch
παίρνω.definition: A common transitive verb meaning to take something into one's possession, to grab, or to receive. It is one of the most frequently used verbs in Modern Greek.
παίρνω.example_greek: Παίρνω ένα φλιτζάνι καφέ το πρωί.
παίρνω.example_english: I take a cup of coffee in the morning.

παιδί.definition: A young human being, typically from birth to early teens. Also used to refer to a son or daughter of any age in relation to their parents.
παιδί.example_english: The child is playing in the park.

παλιός.definition: Having existed or been in use for a long time; not new or recent. Can refer to objects, people, or things that have aged.

παντελόνι.definition: A garment worn on the lower body, covering the legs. It is typically worn as everyday clothing and can be made of various materials.
παντελόνι.example_english: He put on his brown trousers.

παπούτσι.definition: A covering for the foot, typically made of leather or other material, worn on the outside of a stocking or sock.
παπούτσι.example_english: I bought a pair of new shoes.

παππούς.definition: The father of one's parent; a male grandparent. A term of familial relationship used to refer to one's paternal or maternal grandfather.
παππούς.example_greek: Ο παππούς μου μένει στο χωριό.
παππούς.example_english: My grandfather lives in the village.

παράθυρο.definition: A window, typically a side window or secondary window in a building or vehicle. The word combines 'para' (beside/beside) and 'thiro' (window).
παράθυρο.example_greek: Κοίταξε έξω από το παράθυρο του αυτοκινήτου.

παρασκευή.definition: The sixth day of the week, coming after Thursday and before Saturday. In Greek culture and Orthodox tradition, Friday is also associated with religious significance.
παρασκευή.example_greek: Την Παρασκευή πηγαίνω στο σινεμά με τους φίλους μου.
παρασκευή.example_english: On Friday I go to the cinema with my friends.

παρόμοιος.definition: Having a close likeness or resemblance to something else; of the same kind or nature.
παρόμοιος.example_greek: Αυτά τα δύο σπίτια είναι παρόμοια στη δομή τους.
παρόμοιος.example_english: These two houses are similar in their structure.

πατέρας.definition: A male parent; the male head of a family or household.

παχύς.definition: Describing something or someone that has considerable thickness, width, or body weight; bulky or obese.
παχύς.example_greek: Ο παχύς άνδρας περπάτησε στο πάρκο.
παχύς.example_english: The fat man walked in the park.

περπατώ.definition: to move on foot at a normal pace; to go for a walk or stroll.
περπατώ.example_english: I walk in the park every morning.

πλένομαι.definition: A middle/passive voice verb meaning to wash or bathe oneself. It can also mean to be washed in a general sense.
πλένομαι.example_greek: Κάθε πρωί πλένομαι με κρύο νερό.
πλένομαι.example_english: Every morning I wash myself with cold water.

πλένω.definition: to clean something by applying water and typically soap; to wash clothes, dishes, or a person's body.
πλένω.example_greek: Πλένω τα χέρια μου με σαπούνι.
πλένω.example_english: I wash my hands with soap.

πληρώνω.definition: to give money in exchange for goods or services, or to settle a debt or obligation.
πληρώνω.example_english: I pay the bill at the restaurant.

πορτοκάλι.definition: A citrus fruit with a tough, thick orange-colored skin and sweet, juicy flesh inside. It is commonly eaten fresh or used to make juice.
πορτοκάλι.example_greek: Αγόρασα τρία πορτοκάλια από το μανάβικο.
πορτοκάλι.example_english: I bought three oranges from the greengrocer.

πουκάμισο.definition: A garment worn on the upper body, typically with sleeves and buttons or a similar fastening.
πουκάμισο.example_greek: Φορούσε ένα λευκό πουκάμισο στη δουλειά.
πουκάμισο.example_english: He wore a white shirt at work.

πράσινο.definition: The color green; describing something that has the color of grass, leaves, or the spectrum between blue and yellow.
πράσινο.example_greek: Το πράσινο δέντρο είναι πολύ ψηλό.
πράσινο.example_english: The green tree is very tall.

πρέπει.definition: An impersonal verb expressing necessity, obligation, or what is appropriate or fitting. It is typically used with the infinitive or subjunctive mood.

προετοιμάζω.translation: prepare in advance, get ready beforehand
προετοιμάζω.definition: A transitive verb meaning to prepare something or someone in advance before a particular event or action. It combines the prefix 'προ-' (before) with 'ετοιμάζω' (to prepare).
προετοιμάζω.example_greek: Πρέπει να προετοιμάσουμε τα έγγραφα πριν την συνάντηση.
προετοιμάζω.example_english: We need to prepare the documents in advance before the meeting.

προσέχω.translation: to pay attention, to be careful
προσέχω.definition: To direct one's attention toward something; to be cautious or careful about something. Often used with the meaning of 'watch out' or 'beware.'
προσέχω.example_english: Be careful! There's a car coming.

πρωί.definition: The early part of the day from sunrise until noon. The time of day when the sun rises and begins to light the sky.

πωλώ.definition: to exchange goods or services for money; to offer something for sale to others.
πωλώ.example_english: I am selling my old car.

πόδι.definition: The lower limb of the human body from the ankle downward, or the corresponding part in animals used for standing and walking.
πόδι.example_english: I have pain in my foot.

πόρτα.definition: A movable structure that opens and closes an entrance to a room, building, or vehicle.

ρούχο.definition: An article of clothing or a piece of cloth worn on the body. Usually used in the plural form (ρούχα) to refer to clothes in general.
ρούχο.example_english: I bought a new piece of clothing yesterday.

σάββατο.definition: The seventh day of the week, falling between Friday and Sunday. In Greek culture, it is typically considered the first day of the weekend.
σάββατο.example_greek: Το σάββατο πηγαίνω στο σινεμά με τους φίλους μου.
σάββατο.example_english: On Saturday, I go to the cinema with my friends.

σήμερα.definition: on this present day; during the current day from morning to night.

σκέφτομαι.definition: to form thoughts or ideas in one's mind; to think about something or someone. It is a middle voice verb expressing mental activity.
σκέφτομαι.example_greek: Σκέφτομαι ότι αύριο θα είναι μια όμορφη μέρα.
σκέφτομαι.example_english: I think that tomorrow will be a beautiful day.

σπίτι.definition: A building where people live; a dwelling place or residence.
σπίτι.example_greek: Το σπίτι μου είναι κοντά στη θάλασσα.
σπίτι.example_english: My house is close to the sea.

στέκομαι.definition: To be in an upright position on one's feet; to remain stationary in a standing position.
στέκομαι.example_greek: Στέκομαι στη σταθμό και περιμένω το λεωφορείο.
στέκομαι.example_english: I am standing at the bus stop and waiting for the bus.

σταματώ.definition: To cease movement or action; to come to a stop or cause something to stop moving or functioning.
σταματώ.example_english: The car stopped at the traffic light.

στεγνός.definition: Free from moisture or water; not wet. Can also mean impervious to water or sealed against moisture.
στεγνός.example_greek: Το ρούχο είναι στεγνό μετά το σιδέρωμα.
στεγνός.example_english: The clothing is dry after ironing.

στόμα.definition: The opening in the face through which food enters and sound is produced. In anatomy, the oral cavity.
στόμα.example_greek: Άνοιξε το στόμα του για να φάει.
στόμα.example_english: He opened his mouth to eat.

συζυγος.definition: A person who is married to another; a husband or wife. The term is used for both genders in Modern Greek.

σχολείο.definition: An educational institution where children and adolescents receive formal instruction from teachers in various academic subjects.
σχολείο.example_greek: Το σχολείο ανοίγει στις οχτώ το πρωί.
σχολείο.example_english: The school opens at eight in the morning.

τελειώνω.translation: to finish, to complete, to end
τελειώνω.definition: To bring something to completion or conclusion; to stop doing something. It can be used transitively (with an object) or intransitively (without an object).
τελειώνω.example_greek: Τελείωσα τη δουλειά μου στις πέντε το απόγευμα.
τελειώνω.example_english: I finished my work at five in the afternoon.

τετάρτη.definition: The fourth day of the week, occurring between Tuesday and Thursday.
τετάρτη.example_greek: Την Τετάρτη θα πάω στο γυμναστήριο.
τετάρτη.example_english: On Wednesday I will go to the gym.

τοματα.definition: The plural form of τοματο (tomato), a red fruit commonly used in cooking and salads.
τοματα.example_greek: Αγόρασα τρία κιλά τοματα από τη λαϊκή αγορά.
τοματα.example_english: I bought three kilos of tomatoes from the farmers' market.

τράπεζα.definition: A piece of furniture with a flat surface supported by legs, or a financial institution that provides banking services.
τράπεζα.example_greek: Κάθισαν γύρω από την τράπεζα για να φάνε.
τράπεζα.example_english: They sat around the table to eat.

τρέχω.definition: to move quickly on foot with steps faster than walking. To hurry or rush in a particular direction.
τρέχω.example_greek: Ο αγόρι τρέχει προς το σχολείο.
τρέχω.example_english: The boy runs toward the school.

τρίτη.definition: The third day of the week in the Greek calendar, occurring between Monday and Wednesday.
τρίτη.example_greek: Την Τρίτη θα πάμε στο σινεμά.
τρίτη.example_english: On Tuesday we will go to the cinema.

τραπέζι.definition: A piece of furniture with a flat top supported by legs, used for placing objects on or eating at.
τραπέζι.example_english: The table is in the living room.

τρώω.definition: to consume food by putting it in the mouth and swallowing it. One of the most common verbs in Greek.
τρώω.example_english: I eat an apple every morning.

τσάι.definition: A hot beverage made by steeping dried leaves of the tea plant in boiling water, or the plant itself.
τσάι.example_greek: Θα ήθελα ένα φλιτζάνι τσάι, παρακαλώ.
τσάι.example_english: I would like a cup of tea, please.

τυρί.definition: A dairy product made from milk that has been curdled and pressed into solid form, commonly used in cooking and as food.
τυρί.example_greek: Αγόρασα ένα κιλό τυρί από την αγορά.
τυρί.example_english: I bought a kilogram of cheese from the market.

υγρός.definition: Containing, covered with, or characterized by moisture or water; having the quality of a liquid rather than solid or gas.
υγρός.example_greek: Ο δρόμος είναι υγρός επειδή έβρεξε.
υγρός.example_english: The road is wet because it rained.

φίλος.definition: A person with whom one has a bond of mutual affection, excluding sexual or family relations. A person who is friendly and supportive.
φίλος.example_greek: Ο φίλος μου είναι πολύ καλός άνθρωπος.
φίλος.example_english: My friend is a very good person.

φαρμακείο.definition: A retail establishment where pharmaceutical drugs and medicines are dispensed and sold, typically under the supervision of a pharmacist.
φαρμακείο.example_greek: Πήγα στο φαρμακείο για να αγοράσω φάρμακα.
φαρμακείο.example_english: I went to the pharmacy to buy medicine.

φθινόπωρο.definition: The season of the year between summer and winter, characterized by cooling temperatures and falling leaves.
φθινόπωρο.example_greek: Το φθινόπωρο είναι η ωραιότερη εποχή του χρόνου.
φθινόπωρο.example_english: Autumn is the most beautiful season of the year.

φοβισμένος.definition: Feeling or showing fear; in a state of being frightened or anxious about something.
φοβισμένος.example_greek: Ο μικρός είναι φοβισμένος από τα σκυλιά.
φοβισμένος.example_english: The little boy is afraid of dogs.

φρούτο.definition: The sweet or savory reproductive body of a seed plant, typically edible and containing vitamins and natural sugars.
φρούτο.example_greek: Αγοράζω φρούτα στην αγορά κάθε εβδομάδα.
φρούτο.example_english: I buy fruit at the market every week.

φτάνω.translation: to reach, to arrive, to be enough
φτάνω.definition: to come to or get to a place; to be sufficient in quantity or quality for a purpose.
φτάνω.example_greek: Φτάνω στο σχολείο στις οχτώ το πρωί.
φτάνω.example_english: I arrive at school at eight in the morning.

φτηνός.definition: Low in price; costing little money. Can also mean of poor quality in informal usage.
φτηνός.example_greek: Αυτό το προϊόν είναι πολύ φτηνό.

φόρεμα.definition: A garment worn by women or children, typically covering the body from shoulders to legs. It is a common piece of clothing.
φόρεμα.example_greek: Το φόρεμα είναι πολύ όμορφο και κόκκινο.
φόρεμα.example_english: The dress is very beautiful and red.

χέρι.definition: The body part at the end of the arm that is used for holding and grasping things. It has five fingers and is used for many everyday activities.
χέρι.example_greek: Έσκισα το χέρι μου κατά λάθος.
χέρι.example_english: I tore my hand by mistake.

χαρούμενος.definition: Feeling or expressing joy and contentment; in good spirits and pleased about something.
χαρούμενος.example_greek: Ήμουν πολύ χαρούμενος όταν άκουσα τα καλά νέα.
χαρούμενος.example_english: I was very happy when I heard the good news.

χειμώνας.definition: The coldest season of the year, occurring between autumn and spring. In Greece, winter typically runs from December to February.
χειμώνας.example_greek: Ο χειμώνας είναι πολύ κρύος φέτος.
χειμώνας.example_english: Winter is very cold this year.

χθες.definition: On the day before today; referring to the previous day.

χρειάζομαι.definition: A middle voice verb meaning to need, require, or be in need of something. It is commonly used to express necessity or lack of something.
χρειάζομαι.example_english: I need a cup of coffee.

χρόνος.definition: The indefinite continuous sequence of existence and events that occurs in an apparently irreversible succession from the past through the present to the future.

ψάρι.definition: An aquatic vertebrate animal with fins, gills, and scales that lives in water. Commonly used as food.

ψηλός.definition: Having greater than average height or distance from bottom to top. Used to describe people, objects, or measurements that extend upward considerably.
ψηλός.example_greek: Ο Γιάννης είναι πολύ ψηλός άντρας.
ψηλός.example_english: John is a very tall man.

ψωμί.definition: A staple food made from flour, water, and yeast that is baked. A basic carbohydrate food common in Mediterranean cuisine.
ψωμί.example_greek: Αγόρασα ένα φρέσκο ψωμί από το φούρνο.
ψωμί.example_english: I bought fresh bread from the bakery.

ωραίος.definition: Possessing beauty or attractiveness; pleasing to the eye or mind. Commonly used to describe people, objects, or situations that are aesthetically pleasant or nice.
ωραίος.example_greek: Αυτό το σπίτι είναι πολύ ωραίο.

ώρα.definition: A unit of time equal to 60 minutes, or more broadly, a period of time or a suitable moment for something.

ἀναδύνω.definition: A Greek verb meaning to come up or emerge from below, to rise to the surface. It is a compound of ἀνα- (ana-, up) and δύνω (dýno, to dive or sink).
ἀναδύνω.example_greek: Ὁ ἥλιος ἀναδύνει ἐκ τοῦ ὁρίζοντος.
ἀναδύνω.example_english: The sun emerges from the horizon.

αποβολός.definition: Describes someone who has been cast away, rejected, or excluded from society. In classical and religious contexts, it refers to something that has been thrown away or discarded.
αποβολός.example_greek: Ο αποβολός αγάπησε και συγχώρεσε όσους τον απέρριψαν.
αποβολός.example_english: The castaway loved and forgave those who rejected him.

αποπέμπω.definition: To send away or dismiss someone; to reject or rebuff. Often used in formal or official contexts to indicate the removal or discharge of a person from a position or situation.
αποπέμπω.example_greek: Ο δικαστής αποπέμπει την αγωγή του.

αποριμένος.definition: Describing someone who is in difficulty, unable to proceed, or bewildered; stuck in a situation without knowing what to do.
αποριμένος.example_greek: Ήμουν αποριμένος όταν άκουσα τα νέα.
αποριμένος.example_english: I was perplexed when I heard the news.

αρκούδα.definition: A large mammal with thick fur, strong limbs, and a short tail, belonging to the family Ursidae.
αρκούδα.example_greek: Η αρκούδα είναι ένα μεγάλο και δυνατό ζώο.
αρκούδα.example_english: The bear is a large and powerful animal.

γεννέτησιος.definition: Relating to or capable of generation, reproduction, or procreation. An archaic or formal adjective describing something connected with birth or creation.
γεννέτησιος.example_greek: Η γεννέτησιος δύναμη της φύσης είναι αξιοθαύμαστη.
γεννέτησιος.example_english: The generative power of nature is remarkable.

γιγαντιαίος.definition: Extremely large in size; resembling or characteristic of a giant. Used to describe something that is exceptionally big or impressive.

γιός.definition: A male child in relation to his parents. The masculine noun used to refer to a boy or man as the offspring of his mother and father.
γιός.example_greek: Ο γιός μου είναι δέκα χρονών.
γιός.example_english: My son is ten years old.

είλημμένος.definition: Describing something that is wound, coiled, or twisted in a spiral form. Often used to describe hair, vines, or objects with a helical structure.
είλημμένος.example_greek: Τα είλημμένα μαλλιά της ήταν πολύ όμορφα.
είλημμένος.example_english: Her twisted hair was very beautiful.

εμπιστεύω.definition: To have confidence in someone or something, or to place something in someone's care with confidence.
εμπιστεύω.example_greek: Εμπιστεύομαι τον φίλο μου με τα μυστικά μου.
εμπιστεύω.example_english: I trust my friend with my secrets.

εξοικειωμένος.definition: Adjusted or adapted to something; having become familiar or comfortable with a situation, environment, or practice through repeated exposure or experience.
εξοικειωμένος.example_greek: Είμαι εξοικειωμένος με το κλίμα της πόλης.
εξοικειωμένος.example_english: I am accustomed to the city's climate.

έσοδος.definition: Money or resources received, typically from business operations, investments, or other sources of income.
έσοδος.example_greek: Τα έσοδα της εταιρείας αυξήθηκαν αυτό το τρίμηνο.
έσοδος.example_english: The company's revenues increased this quarter.

ευρωστος.definition: Strong and healthy in body; characterized by physical strength and vigor. Can also mean well-built or solidly constructed.
ευρωστος.example_greek: Είναι ένας ευρωστος άνδρας με δυνατά χέρια.
ευρωστος.example_english: He is a robust man with strong hands.

εφαλτήριον.definition: A platform or board used for jumping or diving, particularly in athletics or swimming. In ancient Greek, it referred to a board used by acrobats and jumpers.
εφαλτήριον.example_greek: Ο αθλητής χρησιμοποίησε το εφαλτήριον για να κάνει το άλμα του.
εφαλτήριον.example_english: The athlete used the springboard to perform his jump.

κατατέθει.definition: Third person singular present tense of κατατίθημι (katatíthimi), meaning to deposit, file, or lodge something formally, such as a complaint or document.
κατατέθει.example_greek: Ο δικηγόρος κατατέθει την αίτηση στο δικαστήριο.
κατατέθει.example_english: The lawyer files the petition in court.

κινητήριος.definition: Relating to motion or movement; serving as a motive force or engine. Used to describe mechanical or physical forces that cause movement.
κινητήριος.example_greek: Ο κινητήριος μηχανισμός του αυτοκινήτου είναι πολύ ισχυρός.
κινητήριος.example_english: The motor mechanism of the car is very powerful.

κρύο.definition: The state or sensation of low temperature; the absence of warmth or heat.
κρύο.example_greek: Το κρύο του χειμώνα είναι πολύ δυνατό φέτος.
κρύο.example_english: The cold of winter is very intense this year.

πήχυς.definition: The part of the arm between the elbow and the wrist; also an ancient unit of length measurement approximately 18 inches or 45 centimeters.
πήχυς.example_greek: Ο πήχυς του είναι πολύ δυνατός.

πικρόχολη.definition: A state of bitterness, anger, or resentment; metaphorically derived from the ancient Greek concept of bile as the humor responsible for anger.
πικρόχολη.example_greek: Η πικρόχολη του απέναντι στο αδικό γεγονός ήταν αιτιολογημένη.
πικρόχολη.example_english: His bitterness toward the unjust event was justified.

σύννεφη.definition: A visible mass of water droplets or ice crystals suspended in the atmosphere. It appears white or gray and floats in the sky.
σύννεφη.example_english: The sky is full of clouds.

ντομάτα.definition: A round or oval red fruit with edible flesh and seeds, commonly used in cooking and salads. It is the edible berry of the plant Solanum lycopersicum.
ντομάτα.example_greek: Αγόρασα φρέσκες ντομάτες από την αγορά.
ντομάτα.example_english: I bought fresh tomatoes from the market.

τυφλά.definition: In a blind manner; without sight or without careful consideration; recklessly or without awareness.
τυφλά.example_greek: Δεν πρέπει να αγοράζουμε τυφλά χωρίς να σκεφτόμαστε.
τυφλά.example_english: We should not buy blindly without thinking.

υπονομευτής.definition: A person who engages in sabotage or undermines something deliberately, especially through covert or subversive means.
υπονομευτής.example_greek: Ο υπονομευτής προσπάθησε να καταστρέψει το έργο.
υπονομευτής.example_english: The saboteur tried to destroy the project.

χρωστώ.definition: To be under obligation to pay or repay money or goods to someone. It can also mean to be obligated or duty-bound to do something.
χρωστώ.example_english: I owe a lot of money to the bank.

άποψη.definition: A personal belief, judgment, or perspective on a matter. An expression of what someone thinks about something.
άποψη.example_greek: Η άποψή μου είναι ότι πρέπει να δουμε και τις δύο πλευρές.
άποψη.example_english: My opinion is that we should see both sides.

γαϊτάνι.definition: A thin flexible cord or rope made of twisted fibers, used for tying, binding, or securing things.
γαϊτάνι.example_greek: Το γαϊτάνι είναι δεμένο γύρω από το κουτί.
γαϊτάνι.example_english: The rope is tied around the box.

αλαδιά.definition: The state or quality of being slovenly, unkempt, or messy in appearance or habits; lack of cleanliness or orderliness.
αλαδιά.example_greek: Η αλαδιά του σπιτιού του ήταν απαράδεκτη.
αλαδιά.example_english: The messiness of his house was unacceptable.

αγαντάρω.definition: A verb meaning to wait for someone or something, or to expect an outcome. It is commonly used in Modern Greek to express the action of waiting.
αγαντάρω.example_greek: Αγαντάρω τον φίλο μου στην πόρτα.
αγαντάρω.example_english: I am waiting for my friend at the door.

αναζωογονητικός.definition: An adjective describing something that restores life, vitality, or energy; capable of reviving or bringing back to life. Formed from the prefix 'ανα-' (ana-, again) and 'ζωογονητικός' (life-giving).
αναζωογονητικός.example_greek: Το αναζωογονητικό ποτό του έδωσε νέα ενέργεια.
αναζωογονητικός.example_english: The revitalizing drink gave him new energy.

ανακατάληψη.definition: The act of recapturing or retaking something that was previously lost or taken away, or the recovery of something. It can also refer to the resumption of control or possession.
ανακατάληψη.example_greek: Η ανακατάληψη της πόλης ήταν ένας σημαντικός στόχος της στρατιωτικής επιχείρησης.
ανακατάληψη.example_english: The recapture of the city was an important objective of the military operation.

αναλημματικός.definition: Describing something that provides relief, comfort, or mitigation of pain or distress; serving to alleviate symptoms or burden without curing the underlying cause.
αναλημματικός.example_greek: Η θεραπεία είναι αναλημματική και όχι θεραπευτική.
αναλημματικός.example_english: The treatment is palliative and not curative.

αναπληρώνω.translation: to fill, to make up for, to substitute
αναπληρώνω.definition: To fill something completely or to compensate for a lack or absence. Can mean to replace someone in a position or to make up for a deficiency.
αναπληρώνω.example_greek: Ο δάσκαλος αναπληρώνει το χαμένο χρόνο με επιπλέον μαθήματα.
αναπληρώνω.example_english: The teacher makes up for the lost time with additional lessons.

αναπόδεικτος.definition: Not proven or demonstrated; lacking evidence or proof. An adjective describing something that has not been substantiated or verified.
αναπόδεικτος.example_greek: Η κατηγορία είναι αναπόδεικτη και δεν έχει βάση.
αναπόδεικτος.example_english: The accusation is unproven and has no basis.

αρνηθώ.translation: I will refuse, I will deny
αρνηθώ.definition: First person singular future simple form of the verb αρνούμαι, meaning to refuse, deny, or reject something.
αρνηθώ.example_greek: Θα αρνηθώ να δεχθώ αυτές τις όρους.
αρνηθώ.example_english: I will refuse to accept these terms.

αντίτιμο.definition: A payment or compensation given in return for something; a price or fee paid as exchange or recompense.
